oil slick off the french coast
the next item is the commission statement on the oil slick off the french coast.     124094.99245313447
mrs de palacio will open the debate on behalf of the commission.     30798.527663196808
mr president, i shall be speaking on behalf of the ppe-de group, but also as an elected representative for brittany, and i am therefore directly concerned and shocked by these events.     6269.663236677611
together with mrs grossetête and the ppe-de group, we tabled a motion for a resolution.     4258.817553119738
i am pleased to see that we have today arrived at a compromise motion.     9896.476775151154
the erika shipwreck is a truly european issue, firstly because it is disfiguring and polluting some of the most beautiful beaches in europe, with extremely serious consequences for tourism, people who earn their living from the sea and environmentalists, and also because the underlying issue is the regulation and monitoring which ought naturally to be on a european scale.     2243.7321490132063
a disaster of this scale could almost certainly not have occurred along the american coastline, and why?     2171.612000376872
because the americans managed to learn their lesson from the exxon valdez disaster and in 1990 to draw up the oil pollution act, which makes the parties concerned aware of their responsibilities, as it makes it possible to hold the charterer liable, and is more restrictive, and, more especially, more carefully monitored thanks to various regulations and, in particular, the existence of the american coastguard.     3979.4812435394133
if, i repeat, we had had regulations of this type, then this disaster would surely not have occurred.     2028.4379692252874
this is why we think that the applicable legislation must be reviewed, particularly the 1992 protocol which has exempted the shipowners, in this case the oil tanker charterer, from any liability.     4340.59009672385
well, if the charterer is exonerated, then of course he is less concerned with the inspections which oil companies are responsible for.     1781.0069993007855
the regulations must therefore be reviewed, and i thank you, commissioner, for highlighting the inadequate features of current european regulations.     2367.9706542207923
moreover, as you said, monitoring must above all be stepped up: that of the flag state, that of the classification societies.     2889.494477845683
you told us that rina was an italian classification register recognised by the commission.     7034.721229335252
what are the conditions for this recognition?     3620.2007156388054
what guarantees are there of its reliability?     6989.419567545138
you are going to commission a fact-finding mission.     6369.44740902399
we await the outcome with great interest.     16919.431671807753
inspections by the maritime authorities and supervision within european ports must also be tightened up.     2912.1250329669397
there is a paris memorandum in existence, let me remind you, which stipulates a minimum for inspection, that is, one in four of the vessels putting into european ports must be inspected by the maritime authorities of the country concerned.     4778.896201384948
this obligation is not respected either in france or in many other european countries.     1621.7071564780001
why not?     387.0239955793162
what conclusions has the commission already drawn or what conclusions is it going to draw?     5215.172301027489
i also think it essential to step up the level of inspection that the charterer is responsible for, in this case, the oil company.     2439.531186406077
if the charterer' s liability entails a financial commitment, then doubtless the inspection will be enforced to a greater extent.     2868.14579387748
finally, we need some coordination of national maritime authorities in order to achieve some sort of european facility comparable to the coastguards who supervise the coasts of the united states.     6296.600587715427
firstly, i would like to thank the environment colleagues in my group who approached this particular problem jointly with their transport team.     6957.72438081544
this disaster is both a transport and environmental one and is not confined to one area.     1607.7843235222463
here we are here again in this parliament discussing yet another maritime disaster.     1314.539960556863
i could reel off the list of disasters that have afflicted this industry in my 10 years in this parliament but i only have three minutes.     2754.54441324151
surely it is now about time that not only the member states but that toothless tiger that is known as the international maritime organisation, and last but not least, the oil companies and tanker owners take on their responsibilities by acting to stop these ecological and human tragedies repeating themselves year after year.     1723.4805904650789
how many more erikas do we have to endure before the powers that be deliver to us a proper port state control that is effective throughout the whole of the european union, not just certain areas of it?     1982.5064546141314
how much more pollution do we have to endure before the industry delivers us tankers with segregated tanks and double holds?     3266.978440866003
how many more seabirds are to die before shipping companies end the practice of cleaning their tanks at sea which, as our resolution points out, causes far more pollution than any erika-type disaster?     3307.3683470630604
while i recognise that some progress has been made in areas like port state control and minimal crewing standards, we are still faced with what our late colleague ken stewart used to call 'ships of shame sailing in and out of our european union waters: rust buckets badly crewed'.     1910.5046588167115
we need the member states to act quickly and decisively.     2801.9070601823373
we need our governments to put up the financial resources needed to give us effective control and we need them also to stop dragging their feet when it comes to maritime safety.     3082.9036457380953
the erika cost no human lives but it may have ruined many human livelihoods.     17302.327558077675
just as the sea empress, just as the braer did.     322.581278243776
it is absolutely certain that all three left us with an ecological disaster.     1072.3580733452477
my group's sympathy goes out to the people of brittany who have seen their area despoiled by this disaster, just as my group's sympathy went out to the people of west wales and to the shetland islands on previous occasions.     5228.051194653305
is it not about time that we stopped expressing sympathy and started issuing enforcement notices to get rid of these ships of shame in favour of safe seas or we will be back again, later on this year, discussing a new motion for a resolution when yet another flag-of-convenience tanker sadly goes down spewing its crude oil all over the sea.     3357.5493507988094
the time for resolutions has passed.     6569.264799654847
what is now needed is action.     1071.8477691847177
sadly, i fear, the council will yet again fail to act and we will be back in the future discussing yet another maritime and ecological disaster.     2245.9227680469453
mr president, every time there is a disaster of this kind people say it must never happen again.     2756.912772961223
in reality we can never stop accidents happening at sea, but it is incumbent upon us all to learn the lessons whenever an incident of this kind takes place and apply those lessons to reduce risks in future.     4760.71925334318
the "erika" incident was serious especially for the people of brittany most closely affected, but it was devastating for the wildlife of much of europe.     3452.814689627927
some say that it is the worst accident that has ever befallen the bird life of this part of europe.     1909.1068037514517
the royal society for the protection of birds believes that as many as 400,000 birds, perhaps mainly guillemots, may have died.     4183.315420157984
the pictures many of us will have seen of oil-soaked birds being put down by veterinary officials were both distressing and tragic.     3041.1830555851257
we all want the highest standards for ships whatever flag they may be flying.     3132.3307656541615
we need to enforce the regulations and ensure that the "polluter pays" principle is applied so that financial penalties and some incentives are used to put pressure on both ship operators and oil companies to ensure that best practice is introduced.     3464.489036480362
i agree with every word the commissioner said in her remarks, but the question is, how long will it take to ensure that measures are introduced to deal with the problem in the way she proposes?     3485.4546273338838
as a politician she needs to remind her officials how difficult it would be were she to have to come back to this parliament in a year, were a similar incident, just as devastating, to take place, if some of the measures she has proposed today were simply still remaining as fine words and she had not had the chance to put them into practice.     2250.5051504082107
the thought of an incident of this kind occurring in the near future should concentrate her mind and the minds of her officials wonderfully.     12218.362952937867
mr president, commissioner, i have brought you a small gift.     2854.463467766531
here is a cake of oil sent by someone living on the french island of noirmoutier who wrote to me as follows: "with every tide, the beach is covered with heavy fuel oil which has leaked out of the erika.     2718.466660727073
with every tide, the volunteers, members of the armed forces and firemen remove great pancakes of this thick, sticky, black tar.     5059.928819679603
when will we see a return to cleanliness and the end of this devastating accident?"     2380.5263646417566
indeed this disaster involving the erika, like that of the russian vessel in turkey, moreover, is unacceptable and intolerable at a time when the ultimate hi-tech technology is available.     2782.9654976702377
it is all the more unacceptable for such a thing to occur twenty years after the amoco cadiz disaster, despite our saying at the time and many times since, "never again!"     1443.7700486571591
it is, of course, the responsibility of policies, and therefore our responsibility, to guarantee the safety of sea transport.     2287.287731162598
we absolutely have to guarantee our citizens that a disaster of this type will never happen again.     2376.068649101491
but, listening to you, commissioner, we are somewhat frustrated because anyone already involved in these matters knows that in 1992, i believe, the commission and parliament drew up a very interesting text which already contained all the proposals included in the resolution drawn up by the transport committee.     1816.8679029548591
indeed, we must say and keep saying that the erika is one disaster too many.     1080.641315669237
this is why it is essential, as a matter of urgency, for the european union to undertake a radical review of its directives on sea transport to make them more restrictive and to establish clear and specific regulations determining the responsibilities of the owners of the cargoes.     2918.6305197489064
we should be aware, for example, that shell and british petroleum refused to use the erika to transport their oil.     2902.093761152687
why, in these circumstances, is the liability of total as charterer not recognised?     3964.8700559839162
your proposals must also include making double hulls mandatory and enforcing observance, at last, of the ban on degassing at sea.     4939.205279261332
a european inspection body must be established which can monitor the condition of vessels effectively and efficiently.     2756.879166094165
in addition, it is also essential for the european union to undertake to reform the imo, as a matter of urgency.     4738.558770615244
what point is there in issuing restrictive directives if, once they are at sea, most vessels do just what they wish?     4278.523379954636
finally, a compassionate thought for all the volunteers, nature lovers and bird lovers, who spontaneously and generously are coming through to the aid of the oil-covered birds, organising rescue operation with the means available.     1904.5163285494834
i can testify to this remarkable work.     29296.74221523878
you are no doubt aware that approximately 200 000 birds are going to die as a result of this oil slick, which is a major ecological disaster, practically unprecedented in scale.     2486.65540934142
in relation to this, you are no doubt also aware how difficult it is nowadays to preserve natural species as well as natural spaces.     8984.755039907448
and, on this subject, commissioner, you did not mention how the commission intends to contribute to the restoration of the natural environment and the natural habitats.     2330.355174570811
once again, there will be no clearly defined party responsible.     1790.4317600707843
in the meantime, it is nature that is taking the rap, as always.     1063.0390238567923
mr president, my group asked that this debate be set up in order to provide parliament with an opportunity to express its solidarity with the people directly affected by this ecological disaster, affected both in terms of their economic activities and in terms of their attachment to their natural environment.     3477.6720587930254
let me, in this connection, welcome the presence in the visitors' gallery of mrs javette-le besque, spokesperson for the oil slick action group "marée noire" , made up of men and women from the french department of morbihan.     2310.27213452923
many volunteers from france and other european countries have expressed their solidarity by lending a hand to the victims in the field.     3769.155869441844
as the european parliament, we can express our solidarity primarily by taking action in order to ensure the immediate release of emergency aid to the families affected by the oil slick and, at the same time, demanding tightening up of the quality criteria, on the european and international scale, of the regulations and standards governing the safety of sea transport, stipulating much more frequent inspection and sanctions that are a far greater deterrent to offenders.     3175.295467735843
our proposals are particularly concerned with the age of oil tankers.     1423.8106057144266
among those inspected and found to be in contravention of regulations last year, 15 were at least 20 years old and some were 30 and more.     3615.863651333874
this is no longer tolerable.     59970.933822025916
next, we have flags of convenience.     19319.647194623692
according to the international transport federation, over 40% of the ships wrecked in 1998 were sailing under flags of convenience, the symbol of profit and the exploitation of human beings at the expense of safety.     7224.381231349137
this is no longer tolerable.     59970.933822025916
finally, there is the lack of transparency.     4394.142577384261
everything is done to make the lines of responsibility, the identity of the owners and real decision-makers as impenetrable as possible.     2480.8777933198044
this is no longer tolerable.     59970.933822025916
in all these respects we must ensure real, significant change, including setting a time limit for the switch to new standards, including the mandatory double hull, on pain of being banned from the ports or the territorial waters of member states.     3516.333787683219
we must also ensure increased strictness both in issuing sea-worthiness certificates and in monitoring the condition and the maintenance of vessels.     6925.846926050067
finally we must ensure that all the parties responsible for disasters contribute to repairing the damage caused.     2404.743021321165
i am thinking specifically of total-fina in the present instance.     7190.512610882943
this is an approach, mr president, which the european union can develop with regard to the international maritime organisation.     2172.0141379489532
in so doing, it will have proved its usefulness to public opinion which, in this instance, is awaiting clear, tangible action.     6357.674583495765
mr president, commissioner, as a member for the french atlantic coast, for vendée, i should first like to express the disgust of the people who are the victims of the erika oil slick in the face of these disasters, caused not by a natural disaster such as the hurricane which has also just struck us, but by a crime.     1730.3029440886264
in this emergency, in this ordeal, we have seen a magnificent display of solidarity: local solidarity, national solidarity, and intergovernmental solidarity.     5552.543828408711
what the stricken populations are waiting for now, those who have lost everything, particularly among those who made their living from the sea and from tourism, those people whose businesses have been compromised for several years to come, is not for the polluters to repair the damage they have caused, but for the gears to now be set in motion to ensure that their present misfortune serves to prevent similar crimes affecting other people in future.     1951.3274429180078
quite clearly, we are now paying the price for our lack of concern.     2321.6234571712716
because our states, with the blessing of the community, did not deem it useful to have its own commercial fleet and allowed meticulous shipbuilding know-how to disappear, we no longer have access to the clear traceability of vessels and we can see veritable coffin ships navigating our waters under flags of convenience, drifting according to the short-term interests of the multinationals.     6233.144791945559
it is absolutely essential to put an end to this floodtide of irresponsibility, irresponsible charterers, untraceable shipowners and lenient certification companies.     7409.983701291731
so today we find ourselves in a state of utter confusion.     3817.8576935884007
obviously, such matters should be handled at world level, but the current international regulations of the imo are far from adequate and are far too loose, and neither our own countries nor the community have tried determinedly to make them stricter, despite previous disasters.     2988.993396402261
admittedly, there is the international oil pollution compensation fund, iopcf, but it waters down the issue of liability and sets its ceiling at a quite inadequate level, which must be reviewed.     2994.319385156884
the question of flags of convenience must be reviewed within the imo.     26668.766087240714
it is up to the members states and the community to take the necessary initiatives to achieve this.     1501.9597786317584
let me remind you that the erika was sailing under a flag of convenience, that of a state applying for membership of the european union.     4059.011256851818
in addition, there are community directives, but they are seldom applied, if at all, due to the insufficient number of national inspectors.     6459.207507421358
this omission must be remedied as a matter of urgency.     11551.825678390918
a directive on maritime safety has been in preparation for many years, but it is making very slow progress.     1899.8140120465468
the commission is displaying intolerable inertia and unacceptable shortcomings.     12873.92882925564
the date when the commission is supposed to present its communication devoted to this key issue is put off time and again.     3200.1794564488773
it is now scheduled for july.     4266.656906571118
this must be brought forward.     1862.2466442538578
the terms of the regulations to be enforced must be particularly clear and strict.     1541.0185757468605
i shall mention three issues that our group feels are priorities.     3984.2385155299958
firstly, the liability of owners of oil cargoes must be clearly established and the victims of pollution incidents must be able to claim on this liability unequivocally.     5480.187230183047
the best form of prevention we can offer for the future is the sure knowledge we can offer charterers that they will be heavily penalised in civil, criminal and financial terms if they are not extremely vigilant regarding the matter of the safety of the vessels they select.     3883.086011535552
secondly, the requirement of the double hull as a condition for access to community waters must be enforced as soon as possible and cease to be put off indefinitely.     4931.479633207994
thirdly, a strict age limit on vessels to be given access to community waters must be established in the near future.     2481.3692691012716
this could be of a maximum of 15 years of age.     1543.4522894416364
if we do not obtain a framework of community regulations which is sufficiently clear, strict and trenchant, then those member states that wish to do so must be allowed to take the necessary steps to protect their people and their territory, in the same way that the united states succeeded in learning the lessons of the exxon valdez disaster by making double hulls compulsory and banning all vessels more than 20 years old from their waters.     2654.739906012598
thus, the erika was refused access to american waters.     5060.893747682721
if the european community had done the same, a great disaster would have been avoided.     1063.3256572123714
let us ensure, mr president, commissioner, that this time we really do learn the lesson, even after the media hype and the shock of this house have died down.     2263.2084535462086
mr president, torrey canyon, olympic bravery, haven, amoco cadiz, gino, tanio, so many names we remember with horror.     4381.654964015019
and now the erika.     417.8937036782514
whose turn is next?     5995.465185097289
twenty one years after amoco cadiz, this umpteenth oil slick, the seventh since 1967, shows the irresponsibility of our successive rulers.     12591.517757030584
the atlantic west coast once again is paying a high price for their inability to react, for their capitulation to the multinationals.     5695.253961867761
it is hard to understand why the french and the europeans allow something which the americans refuse to accept, and why europe, normally so quick to pass environmental legislation, has chosen to overlook the matter of maritime safety.     5208.460421534378
and this is the result.     1674.9568860868949
the erika, a vessel flying a maltese flag, a floating rust bucket, classed amongst the most dangerous type of oil tankers, has contaminated more than 400 km of our coastline, a case of pollution even worse than that caused by the amoco cadiz.     3866.0306601537345
as an elected representative for the loire-atlantique region, unfortunately, i can testify to this.     9021.491258565651
there is nothing natural about these recurring disasters, they are the result of fate.     5962.410868652426
they are the result of human thoughtlessness.     1708.3650815278868
what we have here is an ecological disaster.     952.2817379287503
only the people cut off from the harsh reality of the grass roots could have any doubt about that.     2239.5325596112916
it is also an economic disaster for all the people who make their living from the sea and from tourism, fishermen, oyster farmers, shellfish farmers, people who make a living from the salt marshes, shopkeepers, etc.     1484.6357457420042
the erika disaster must be the last in the series.     8477.911255490593
first of all, we must shed some light on this shipwreck.     7822.655188649232
why do we not appoint a parliamentary committee of inquiry or get members of parliament involved in the team of experts which has just been announced by the commission?     3226.282699430517
next, as a matter of urgency, we muss pass legislation, firstly, regarding identification of the exact characteristics of goods transported.     9346.83660427397
in fact, according to the experts, the oil the erika was carrying was supposed to sink to the bottom and should never have reached the coast.     4961.376167853728
we know what actually happened.     4759.868199617556
next, reliable technical inspection must be established like the technical inspection compulsory in france for vehicles more than five years old.     4270.297345367181
we must regulate the use of flags of convenience, make double hulls mandatory for the transport of polluting or dangerous materials, work on technological innovations in order to be able to process or recover hydrocarbons at sea.     6853.8076664839855
this would be a minimum requirement, i believe, on the dawn of the third millennium.     6000.773170135514
non-compliant vessels must be denied access to european waters, the responsibility of polluters, shipowners or charterers must be determined in line with the principle of the "polluter pays" , maritime supervision must be strengthened to prevent any degassing, thorough and reliable inspection of tankers must be required, an appropriate "natural disasters" budget line must be re-established for member states and, in the meantime, community aid released on an exceptional basis and the french departments affected by the disaster should be made eligible for structural funds.     4145.760876617247
i have also tabled a resolution on this subject on behalf of the edd group.     15662.894843045899
mr president, in the course of the last few years there has been a succession of serious tanker disasters world-wide and the countermeasures taken have either been ineffective or not worth mentioning.     1337.2179144034294
it is particularly bad this time, not least because it has affected a major european state; an incident which could happen again at any time.     1742.9757625864268
we urgently require a directive if we are to reduce these risks.     2849.8719152139493
the 15 clearly do not suffice.     5302.497204492523
these guarantee - making no claim to be exhaustive - a minimum of 3 things.     6562.205010980693
no tanker or freighter fit only for the scrap heap must ever put into any harbour within the european union again.     2216.5597981131205
all those involved, including the transport agent, are responsible for any ensuing damage, and these individuals are to provide satisfactory assurances.     2139.8713865839545
this is the only way of affording the victims the opportunity to make their claims for compensation.     3174.107688112241
however, we must aim higher in the long-term, let there be no mistake about that.     2649.9033593229055
in other words, we need real cost-effectiveness for our entire energy supply system.     2413.656708503505

allow me to say first that what we are talking about is an environmental catastrophe, which also impacts on the economy and threatens livelihoods.     5502.64824333152
so what are we to do now?     621.5888801564776
i have no intention of talking about past events; rather i want to look to the future.     7552.901017949931
what are we to do now?     789.119816125245
i will tell you a few things we should do.     2391.6493043472265
of course, we need double-hulled vessels.     13728.265014176679
that goes without saying, but it is something that will only work through in the medium and long term.     1139.9121778549104
so what do we need to happen immediately?     1354.0471895624692
we need technical testing of ships, that is obligatory technical testing every two years, and no ship to be operated without the accompanying certificate.     11703.686679083892
we need this at european level and internationally; we need something like a tüv, an organisation that undertakes technical testing of motor vehicles in germany, which is compulsory every two years.     4487.71741057692
if you do not have a certificate then you are not allowed to operate the vehicle.     3034.6894881826124
this is what we need for ships.     2371.4707998764416
thirdly, we need to carry out checks on these safety and insurance certificates in the ports, that is in every port of the european union.     3569.949924928996
any failure to produce these certificates must lead to the ship being laid up and prohibited from leaving the port, regardless of all the port charges.     7193.209622534697
we must reach agreement here, in all the european union' s ports, from marseilles, through to rotterdam, to wilhelmshaven, cuxhaven, and wherever ships drop anchor.     16683.82708282547
fourthly, we need the shipowner to bear liability, and not just to the tune of a derisory usd 12 million, but to the value of at least usd 400 million, to be covered per insurance certificate.     4289.599590716909
we also need the country whose flag the ship flies to bear liability.     5965.5417269805575
we need to be sure that if need be, the country whose flag the ship is flying will accept liability.     3491.172506695638
this would be a marvellous exercise in supervision!     5416.974816596071
i can assure you that those countries awarding flags would then take great care to avoid having to accept liability.     9367.055376758444
in the fifth place, we need there to be a guarantee that these demands will apply to all ships using the ports and waterways of the european union, and incidentally, that includes the candidate countries.     2481.805248113888

where is the sense of crisis which generations before us had?     3231.3559709060273

mr president, we shall vote in favour of the gue/ngl group resolution because it denounces total-fina' s responsibility in this ecological disaster and i endorse the proposal that flags of convenience and the use of superannuated vessels should be prohibited, or that double hulls should be mandatory for oil tankers.     2825.0260782317114

how is it possible not to be outraged when a bank refuses to divulge the names of the erika' s owners on the grounds of professional confidentiality, without governments reacting?     7618.281897579484
the crux of the problem is that all governments, and indeed all the european institutions, acknowledge the right of major corporations such as total-fina and its ilk to maximise profits whether it be harmful to their employees or to the environment.     3087.8917732628565
they accept the professional confidentiality of business and banks, even though this same confidentiality serves to conceal what are essentially criminal actions.     8171.697846545997
in these circumstances, even the best resolutions will remain little more than appeals to principle which are powerless to limit the great corporations' capacity to cause damage.     3842.281388585313
mr president, i am in favour of tightening up the legislation on port state control and on classification societies.     4927.912277170349
however, we must stick to the facts.     6174.784170494788

the answer cannot lie in the number of investigations but rather in guaranteeing the rigour with which the controls are undertaken.     4687.168038261573
i have heard in the course of the discussions that have taken place in the last few weeks that the member states do not have enough money to undertake the controls.     2345.039575556625
i cannot accept this.     6308.7547674146535
if the member states decide, in agreement with ourselves, that 25% of ships are to be monitored on the basis of port state control, then they must also make the necessary personnel available.     2371.1632212270415
the controls must also be carried out in such a way that the inspector does not just go on board and check whether there are any fire extinguishers missing, rather there must be materials testing, for this ship obviously broke up on account of material fatigue.     2254.0100593716907
this can only be detected if controls undertaken are more rigorous.     1865.5976121976455

we, as the committee on transport, are prepared to discuss the commission communication on the question of safety at sea and, of course, we are likewise prepared to discuss, at a later date, the legal implications of this communication.     3742.3898968122653

first, the commission was right to concentrate its investigation on the liability of rina, the italian shipping register, because we need to know if community law was applied.     7825.005594635304
that should be the starting point for our efforts.     1827.1904285387116
secondly, apart from the shipowners' liability, it would be a good idea in such cases to examine the liability of the charterers, for example the oil companies which are also responsible for causing ecological disasters like this and, of course, for restoring the damage.     1731.5586884338586
once we have done that, our reaction to the ecological disaster is fully justified.     1472.0107373629494

mr president, commissioner, i believe that the unfortunate incident involving the erika should in fact, as others have said this afternoon, constitute a decisive watershed which will see the end to this type of accident in the european union where, since 1967, there have been seventeen accidents involving large oil tankers; more than one every two years.     2489.1293067839206
the economic and social damage, which we have spoken about today, in terms of the loss of jobs and fishing and tourist resources, is so great that they fully justify decisive and thorough action on the part of the community institutions.     1919.6581001192774
commissioner, i would also like to thank you for the speedy response of your department to this event and the measures which are being adopted and implemented.     2288.969372644093
i trust that these measures will, in a few months, give rise to a clear and convincing legal instrument - a directive, possibly - which will put an end, once and for all, to these 21st century pirates, who deprive us all of the wealth and beauty of the sea.     3118.6567465413204
i would like to comment on one of the measures to be taken, which was mentioned by the commissioner and some of my colleagues.     1647.9261738636326
i am referring to the double hull, which consists of two layers of steel, with containers divided into two tanks.     19262.94058847698
commissioner, there are many experts who consider that the double hull is not sufficiently safe and propose the so-called 'ecological hull' in which case, in the event of an accident, the seawater penetrates the containers and, as a result of pressure, the oil is transferred to other tanks.     1715.1749322114024
commissioner, i believe that it is time to opt for the safest technically possible measures.     11219.773272337528
it is therefore not sufficient to merely reproduce the legislation in force in other countries.     2087.8712721577995
i believe that we can and must improve the existing legislation in this regard.     2174.456183132544
any cost-benefit analysis which truly takes into account all the harm occasioned to persons and the environment by these accidents, is bound to support this approach.     3240.002510364187
mr president, commissioner, i think that first we should all congratulate ourselves on the procedure that has been adopted and which will lead us, after each of our respective political groups has rallied together and expressed its feelings, finally to produce a joint resolution.     2439.1325415003726
i think that given the dramatic circumstances, and as a matter of principle, it would be ill-advised and inappropriate to hold several votes.     4364.03106124375
on the other hand, parliament is issuing a resolution in one vote, for which we can see the agreement amongst the various speakers who have preceded me.     2605.418238455445
i think that this is an opportunity to put parliamentary pressure on the doubts or evasions of a few governments and i think that we are providing the commission with a powerful political act for the preparation of a european maritime area.     2022.0333240205903
i think that it is quite obvious, and this is the first conclusion that can be drawn from the erika disaster, that public opinion would find it hard to understand that we regulate chocolate but do not regulate maritime transport.     2910.900434209835
public opinion would find it hard to understand that we talk about a common judicial area, common airspace, a common rail area and a single market, but not a common maritime area.     1579.5597150150365
i therefore believe that this is a project which must be opened up with the absolute will to see it followed through and followed through in concrete terms.     1914.8726429097587
the commissioner has suggested three main chapters which need to be drafted and brought into line with our legislation, in which we can set standards.     2464.066894238704
i have also noted, to my great satisfaction, that even the groups advocating greater national sovereignty, or the eurosceptic groups are calling for europe to regulate, and i think that this is in fact the right level to take action.     3302.887072167379
we sometimes regulate things which could, by and large, remain subsidiary.     8983.237767472241
in this area we must respond to public opinion.     4823.97794355617
this is extremely important and everyone should feel concerned because we are, when all is said and done, on the global scale merely a peninsula.     2002.6668590030467
therefore, once our legislation has been brought into line, we must follow this up immediately with appropriate law.     2533.136067968894
we must also put in place systematic controls and finally apply the 'polluter pays' principle of responsibility which is, of course, supposed to be a deterrent.     5663.9158848069155
i have almost finished and i would like to say that i have personally requested a public hearing of the committee on transport, which would enable us to provide an immediate follow-up to the erika case and to suggest possible future considerations.     2710.5404513972785
i hope that all political groups will support us in our request for a public hearing.     1677.5403064846453
mr president, the oil tanker erika, the ownership of which is veiled by accommodation addresses on malta and probably in italy and greece and which was hired by total-fina for the transport of oil, foundered off the breton coastline, with disastrous consequences.     1903.2261579946892
the implications for the environment and for the flora and fauna of the european maritime environment are enormous.     1857.4040756430134
the cause of the disaster must be sought in the weakened structure of the oil tanker.     1700.0035709640495
so people are doubtful about the safety of ships which carry dangerous or polluting goods.     7529.237553212162
the international maritime organisation has issued international rules in this connection.     8438.796238706687
states can carry out port inspections.     57981.942423785025
in europe, legislation is stricter and it is compulsory to inspect 25% of the incoming vessels according to the port-state control directive.     5477.629545485525
however, it seems that none of the member states meets this figure due to a lack of inspectors.     8232.63780741274
it is clear that there is no lack of legislation.     2939.1580255158246
in my opinion, the commissioner is quite right.     1418.8996891120412
where the scheme falters is in the enforcement of existing legislation.     2208.8853760269803
but how are we now going to enforce this if there is indeed a lack of inspectors, esteemed european commission?     4195.995670993703
can the commission provide an assurance that directive 93/75 concerning minimum requirements for vessels bound for or leaving community ports and carrying dangerous or polluting goods will be implemented correctly in all member states?     4351.6202690286145
should its implementation not be more strictly monitored?     2922.585327706033
would it not be beneficial, in the short term, following the rotterdam model, to inspect according to a points system in which, for example, account is taken of the ship' s age, whether it is single or double-hulled or whether it sails under a flag of convenience.     5666.851522149937
in short, to give priority to older sub-standard ships over ships which meet all quality requirements.     6086.943900572581
the erika was built at a japanese shipyard and is single-hulled.     5875.15864994994
there are four more sister ships in service at present.     1330.3833751628056
construction of this type of ship was halted at the time because the risk of rusting was especially high.     4402.029641482047
some of them also sail under the maltese flag.     21346.770460130258
are we waiting for the next accident to happen?     6407.38720645944
would sanctions not be appropriate, as the commissioner stated, for the classification society?     1087.4820765614284
rina is currently the talk of the town.     16437.831292166942
malta is entering the accession negotiations.     32037.424360933193
in my opinion, the european union can only accept malta if it is guaranteed that the maltese flag will sail without an accident in future.     5515.556086553906
i would be grateful if you could be disciplined since we are running out of time, and this delay is going to limit the time available for questions to the commission.     2749.644597142221
mr president, just before i went down into the chamber, i received an e-mail with a quite emotional description from a swedish woman who had chosen to spend new year' s eve on the french coast in brittany instead of staying at home and celebrating with her relatives.     9226.86911418874
like many other hundreds of volunteers, she had seen the destruction, washed birds and cleaned up after the people responsible who were not to be found on the coast on those evenings and nights when they really should have been required to be there.     2158.108842495955
like so many others, i too wonder where the people responsible are.     2110.929937875693

(the president cut the speaker off)
i would like to thank mrs grossetête and her colleagues for bringing this motion before parliament.     7072.872902221018
the environmental disaster of the sinking of the erika off brittany and the spillage of 10 000 tonnes of oil has been widely reported in the british media.     2133.2799949709142
although britain and france have had their differences recently, i can assure you that there is considerable sympathy in my country for all those affected.     3081.613243972648
tv pictures of the damage done to your coastline and wildlife, in particular birds and the already dwindling fish stocks, have brought back memories of similar british disasters, such as the torrey canyon in 1967, and have prompted many british volunteers to help.     3126.6941075445484
i salute these joint efforts to repair the damage.     10146.86666123811
this is clearly a problem for all eu coastlines which will require imaginative solutions.     6016.869116867321
instead of allowing the costs of these disasters to fall on the communities most affected and insurance markets such as lloyds of london, we must develop new technologies to retrieve from the sea much more than 10% of the lost oil.     1228.4650745428362
there is currently little financial incentive for this when the insurance market picks up the tab.     1078.1169960442282
ultimately, the polluter must pay.     3153.935951008788
in addition we must build on the council directive dealing with enforcement of international standards for ship safety and pollution prevention by ensuring that the inspection goal of 25% of ships calling at eu ports is met and that a high standard of inspection is delivered.     3707.012230158708
moreover, although i am not opposed to shipowners registering their vessels in their country of choice, i believe there is a need for stricter application of international regulations.     9297.014974581918
rather than banning flags of convenience, which would violate the right of every sovereign state to have a merchant fleet, under the 1995 eu directive on port state control, national maritime authorities must impose more effective sanctions on flag states which fail to carry out their international treaty obligations.     10581.003699209674
i sincerely hope that, particularly during the french presidency later this year, the commission and the council will look carefully at all these options, and i heartily commend this resolution to parliament.     2711.1994537766304
mr president, it feels quite strange to be standing here again today, as i have been doing at regular intervals for almost ten years, and all because of an accident caused by human hand and by a lack of human competence, and not by force majeure.     2113.2033649125005
the victims are waiting outside.     22455.780170299084
they want answers.     82821.28764641189

mr president, firstly i would like to express my very sincere thanks, not only for the initiatives of the different groups, of mrs grossetête and the group of the european people' s party (christian democrats) and the european democrats, but also for the initiatives of mr wurtz and the confederal group of the united left/nordic green left, who proposed this debate, which has led to a positive and important discussion.     4311.53794453084
before the end of march, i propose to present a communication in which i will possibly present legislative texts, that is to say, amendments of specific directives so that discussions may begin in the council and in parliament.     2273.240714459968
i would like to tell you that, in fact, it is not just an environmental problem - it is a very important environmental problem - but it is also a very important social problem.     1239.9092734972194
there are men and women who depend on fishing, gathering shellfish and the service or tourist industries in these coastal areas.     1661.4072988087116
they are environmentally sensitive areas, but they are also sensitive from the point of view of social development and territorial equilibrium.     2445.896067684931
and therefore we must take the utmost care to prevent the recurrence of a similar situation.     2952.9690918575216
mrs roth-behrendt said that nothing had ever been done.     3323.7614028618955
in fact, i believe that things have been done by the commission, but there is still more to do.     1340.4304888600477
this was demonstrated by the americans who, after the exxon valdez disaster, within one year, adopted enormously rigorous and tough legislation, which threatens, as i said earlier, to divert ships unacceptable to american ports towards europe.     5217.34026797648
in europe, after the amoco cadiz, or the urquiola on the spanish coast, in 1976, or the torrey canyon, in the same year, or the many other cases there have been, we really began to legislate after 1994 and 1995, particularly in the last few years.     1806.5781903482632
during these years, the emphasis has mainly been on the safety of passenger transport.     2613.152446824591
that is the reality.     2304.3527444767865
in my opinion, we now have to make a significant and urgent effort to confront new problems, also deriving from american legislation, which emphasise the safety of the transport of dangerous goods in the maritime sector.     2396.1545734854917
i have referred to a series of issues which i will lay out in more detail, if you wish, in an appearance before a special committee on this matter, or when i present the actual initiatives in the coming months.     5382.215416539307
my intention is - and i insist - for us to begin discussions after the end of march, also coinciding with the council of ministers, and, of course, before the end of the six-month period of the portuguese presidency, for us to have texts to discuss.     3608.6956556790315
flags of convenience are one problem, but are not the only one.     6450.9860812187635
the romanian flag is not a flag of convenience but, nevertheless, that country has an extremely high number of incidences at inspections; more than the countries with flags of convenience.     13753.10548455974
malta and cyprus have asked to join the community.     5327.476478830807
we must be demanding on this issue and we are negotiating to this end.     3712.446738333613
this will oblige us to reconsider the registers in the european union, and to deal with the notorious problem, which will no doubt arise again, of the aim of a community register.     4293.412315274904
i believe that this will be difficult but, in any event, we should carry out a review of the registers of the european union countries.     3771.4514875404025
as for the inspections, very much a key question, the first thing we have to consider is how the current legislation has worked, as mr hatzidakis has said.     2326.8001411652826
we already have legislation.     1830.2092179555418
according to the information which the commission services have given me, this legislation is not adequately applied in many member states due to a lack of personnel, means and inspectors.     3272.396822607296
the problem is not that only 25% are inspected, but rather how we select, how we find, the ships which constitute the highest risk, through the age of the ships and the risk history of the flags.     2956.0742945389607
unfortunately, the 25% often consists of flags which are expected to fulfil the requirements: in this way inspections are quicker and the work is carried out more easily.     3654.504404232745
therefore, more than making amendments, we will have to implement measures to control what is already being done, apart from some supplementary requirements with regard to reviews, especially in relation to the age of the different vessels.     2949.0137215565655
and that is why we have solas (the international convention for the safety of life at sea) and marpol (the international convention for the prevention of pollution from ships), two conventions of the international maritime organisation which will eventually have to be made mandatory in all member states of the union and whose application will have to be monitored.     1791.6820946381697
with regard to the question of union inspectors, i believe that the principle of subsidiarity justifies the idea that these inspections be carried out by the member states, which does not mean that the commission does not have to ensure that the states carry out their duty adequately.     6321.047192011778
lastly, i would like to stress the issue of liability.     10122.18839269335
not only in terms of maximum sums insured, which i believe should be similar to those in the united states.     3676.6098333153736
we have set a figure of usd 180 million; in the united states they are talking about usd 1 billion as a maximum ceiling for covering claims payments.     15575.945032979622
i believe we have to increase the current amount so as to move closer to levels in the united states, but that we also have to reconsider the sums insured on vessels and, therefore, the liability of shipowners, and also to hold liable those who charter ships, which is to say the owners of the cargo.     3128.923415541433
unless we require that the owners of the cargo be held liable, we will never, in my opinion, resolve these problems.     4284.625675660073
i will end now.     2804.2089160794035
i will not expand further.     5171.8891928605735
we will have the opportunity to do so on other occasions.     2241.3776611526646
but, obviously, as some speakers have said - and i thank all of you for your totally positive and relevant interventions - we cannot allow a situation where, within one, two or three years, we have to say once again that we have not done what we should have done.     1445.3689928715514
as for me, i can say, after having debated it in the college of commissioners, that the commission is prepared to present the necessary legislative measures, amendments and directives to parliament and the council, in order to equip ourselves with the greatest possible level of safety.     4011.538092164666
the political will of this parliament is required - and i can see that i can count on this - as well as that of the council of ministers.     1636.740616317784

storms in europe
the next item is the commission statement on the storms in europe.     87846.07800435965
mr barnier has the floor on behalf of the commission.     50820.387969564166
commissioner, i would like to thank you for the ideas you have put forward, particularly on the very specific proposals concerning the people affected and who are expecting a lot from the european union, in terms of the help that we can offer them.     1945.7536542589646
in the days immediately following this terrible storm i wrote to you myself with a request for you to ask the french authorities to redraw the boundaries of objective 2 so that all areas devastated by the storms in france and elsewhere could benefit from objective 2.
i think that you must have done so, because you have not mentioned it.     3047.509871889269
in fact, we all know that anyone who does not live within the perimeter of an objective 2 region will find it all but impossible to obtain aid.     2653.1152885636748
we should therefore put this situation right immediately.     2462.587594036016
you also know that this will not only be a short-term problem, but also a medium and long-term one.     810.2181878670134
let me explain.     45721.32497453589
i was in the region of lorraine at the time of this terrible storm which destroyed around 20% of deciduous forest.     5161.256161403846
indeed, for some 'forestry' districts, which have lost 20% of their trees, this represents a huge loss.     3854.858332968398
we know, for example, that it takes between 150 and 200 years for a tree to reach maturity, so the loss these towns will suffer will not be spread over one, two or five years, but over many more.     3193.122620713035
the towns concerned estimate that it will be spread over 40 years.     3682.5719719583703
i think that consequently it will be extremely difficult indeed to compensate these rural towns through subsidies for losses in their financial revenues.     1937.4512124608405
i think that this is something which we should continually bear in mind so that we do not lose sight of it in our various policies.     2636.8389126978477
it is true that the problem of the forestry sector is an extremely complex one.     3698.3889326032286
indeed, you spoke of releasing funds for timber storage on the grounds that its price must not depreciate.     4566.245636129238
on the other hand, districts which have not been affected by the storms will also suffer, as the national forestry office has decided to freeze cuts in forestation for four years.     3098.2016339621387
those districts which have not suffered losses will therefore see their incomes decrease in any case.     5305.984004838998
i am telling you all of this to illustrate how complex the problem is, and i thank the commission once again for extending their analysis of this situation as far as it could.     2756.9107858610278

eur 11 billion.     30441.394785415217

mr president, commissioner, europe has undergone a tough ordeal at the start of this millennium.     8166.721693303096
i would first like to express my heartfelt condolences to the families in mourning as a result of the appalling weather conditions that devastated europe in december.     5629.738086211385
these storms were an unprecedented ecological disaster for our forests.     10530.489079665134
i hope you do not mind if i make particular mention of the forests in my region, lorraine, which have suffered enormous destruction.     6729.070256596804

i am certainly not forgetting the economic dimension of this matter, as the timber industry has been devastated and a whole population is suffering the tragic effects of this.     4355.9928800010775
the community action programme for civil protection, established by a council decision on 9 december, was launched on 1 january 2000.
i ask member states to grasp this opportunity.     5393.090068858735
this programme must work at full capacity.     4070.024550121328
although it may have been effective in certain areas - and i do not doubt the sincerity of commissioner barnier' s words - unfortunately it is still in its infancy when it comes to forestry.     5917.27526551478
our priority, as we wait for community-level funds to be freed up, is for material aid in terms of partnership exchanges between member states.     9016.450923613529
it is therefore a matter of urgency to improve forest roads, and to repair them as quickly as possible.     4102.667713764295
lending forestry equipment and making personnel qualified in forestry management available are also covered by this measure.     9874.648627740244
our next challenge will be to prevent the occurrence of phytosanitary problems which could result from having to abandon large amounts of wood in the forest, and from the pollution of ground water through having to store such large quantities of wood.     5587.114052141965
finally, it is essential that we slow down the logging of standing wood, in order to give priority to the purchase of windfalls.     3914.3466014687115
these timber sales should be supported by strong media coverage in member states.     24716.98319737218
this aid for gathering timber is, of course, only the first stage of an aid package, which will take the form of aid for reforestation and aid for the development of rural areas.     4362.575614392408
i ask the commission to incorporate this into a study of how the situation should be managed now that the emergency is over.     2234.5004492724684
this is a very sad opportunity for europe to renew its forestry heritage in line with the role that this plays in wildlife and nature conservation and in our economies.     7218.060461096084
it is therefore a time for solidarity and cooperation between member states in the face of an ecological and economic calamity.     2093.217079127831
if this solidarity is to have been worthwhile, it is up to europe to propose priority actions for safeguarding the timber industry.     2910.951568961999
hurricane "lothar" should prompt us not to abide solely by the principle of undertaking repairs once the damage is done - something which is discussed at a very superficial level here -, but to switch to the precautionary principle, under the terms of which potential perpetrators are brought to book too.     7803.868363443367
current programmes must be accelerated.     25785.302567679966
for example, as they stand, the commission' s programmes will preclude us from meeting our obligations under kyoto.     21861.375881511718
the trade in emissions rights is immoral to my mind and rather than solving the problem, it shelves it.     1182.8951429310225
the entire taxation system must be given an environmental perspective in the medium term.     2784.8572096052762
we must work more rapidly towards achieving the targets stipulated in the white paper for renewable energy sources, as this would bring about a massive reduction in greenhouse gases.     5974.416051639573
the new commission has not put forward nearly enough on this subject, and what it has put forward is woefully inadequate!     2117.026353523115

the wind triumphed over electricity pylons, roofs, traffic signs and, ultimately over the forest.     10977.654542538858
we have heard that in a short space of time the storm left us with approximately 120 million cubic metres of solid timber, and this is only an estimate.     5136.952424460045

all this is well-intentioned advice.     4940.002315101212

what we need the commission to do as a matter of urgency is to approve national aid, so as to avoid the competition issue being raised again at a later date.     2376.8506913811952

mr president, commissioner, the storms that took place at the turn of the millennium should galvanise us into thinking about certain problems.     3414.4433141374298

food safety
the next item is the communication on food safety and a statement by the commission.     25887.075260648246
mr president, i am very pleased to take this first available opportunity of outlining to parliament the main lines of the commission's white paper on food safety, adopted on wednesday last, 12 january, in association with my colleague, mr liikanen.     7080.204059503939
at my hearing last september, i promised speedy delivery of this white paper.     53160.81594611393
i am happy that we have been able to deliver so quickly.     9513.780570584546
the white paper represents three to four months of intensive work since the new commission was appointed last september.     7168.429009438065
it takes on board the extensive consultations of the past couple of years since the commission's green paper on food law was published.     13031.304893411783
equally it reflects our experiences of recent food alerts in such areas as dioxin and sludge, as well as the bse crisis.     11364.987583121201
the white paper also reflects the concerns of this parliament which you have outlined to both president prodi and myself on the numerous occasions we have debated food safety in this house since the commission was appointed.     5502.95143377568
i do not need to remind you that consumer confidence in europe's food safety regime has been badly affected by the crises and alerts of recent years and months.     5279.157471443013
the commission is fully committed to restoring that confidence by putting in place the most up-to-date and effective food safety regime in the world.     2217.807577253757
when i launched the white paper last week i said that the shopping trolley is one of the most potent weapons on the face of the earth.     8908.788519015916
the most discerning decisions are made by the consumers of europe.     1670.1094409917155
if their confidence is damaged this is reflected in shopping decisions.     17603.324133623508
this in turn has a dramatic effect on farmers, producers and industry generally.     3589.6023804686383
in an industry worth some eur 600 billion annually, even a slight dip in confidence levels can have significant effects.     3377.5409654035366
between the agro-food sector and the farming sector there are over 10 million employees.     1617.6298828386878
high levels of confidence are necessary to boost job numbers and competitiveness.     8377.349883080688
this crisis of confidence has also had the unfortunate but inevitable effect of eroding the trust of consumers in the systems and institutions at national and european level that should monitor and assure the highest standards of food safety.     5011.999960039432
in saying all of this, i would like to make it clear that europe nevertheless has one of the best food industries in the world and also one of the safest food control systems.     1975.0596475806049
the challenge is to make the system the very best.     1444.4771553026974
the overall objective therefore of the white paper on food safety is to put in place the necessary legislation and structures that will guarantee the highest possible level of health protection for consumers arising from the consumption of food.     4349.751381001162
we are setting out a challenging and ambitious agenda for change.     11539.857034240764
the commission will need the full support of parliament if we are to achieve our ambitions on schedule.     4697.192317787305
we will also need the fullest support from the council and other key stakeholders.     2178.4823700580605
the white paper on food safety outlines a comprehensive range of actions needed to complement and modernise existing eu food legislation.     24358.114845039603
all of these measures are designed to make it more coherent, understandable and flexible.     3889.6666079626066
we want to promote better enforcement of that legislation and to provide greater transparency to consumers.     3760.40931820033
the detailed action plan on food safety in the white paper sets a precise timetable for action over the next three years.     4012.730990948082
over 80 measures are envisaged.     5005.881922557602
our objective is to put in place a coherent and up-to-date body of food law by the end of 2002.
we are also envisaging the establishment of a european food authority by 2002 as an essential complement to the new food safety regime.     4379.745485132798
this idea will be the focus of much scrutiny and debate.     3393.093330755996
it has already attracted much comment, including reactions from members of parliament.     9881.453968301434
the chapter of the white paper devoted to the establishment of a european food authority is clearly designed to elicit views and comments.     17576.535524975727
we are seeking views by the end of april on what we envisage.     15897.953698258452
i will return to this process of consultation in a few moments.     6602.394407522224
the commission believes that major structural change is required in our system of food safety to ensure the twin objectives of assuring the highest standard of food safety and restoring consumer confidence.     2762.723668480624
why should a european food authority be an essential part of that structure of change?     2877.2239823038794
the first key concern is independence.     4141.326844935434
key stakeholders, including consumers, are seeking a system that is independent and perceived to be independent of all vested interests.     7517.797968238436
we must also ensure excellence and transparency.     4363.157638705204
we have made a lot of progress over the past couple of years since the reformed system of scientific advice was adopted as a consequence of the bse crisis.     10156.074305325143
however, the commission believes that we must go further.     2675.4186937258005
we must create a permanent and truly independent, excellent and transparent system of risk assessment.     2878.6784353389326
the key task of the authority will be risk assessment in the area of food safety.     3150.8790204221327
we envisage that the work of the existing five scientific committees devoted to food safety will be transferred to the authority.     5249.158901856721
they may not be transferred in their present form or structure - this is an issue on which we will elicit views before making our definitive proposals on the authority's establishment.     7804.841440882959
however, if we were merely to propose a simple moving of deckchairs, this would clearly not be enough.     903.1513268541889
as the white paper makes clear, the new authority must be a value-added structure.     7546.984298049973
i am of the view that the existing system of scientific advice needs to be strengthened.     3471.141689815546
within the authority i envisage much stronger scientific and other support for the independent scientists.     6621.680496824909
i am also envisaging that the authority will be much more proactive than our current regime - anticipate rather than react, identify issues before they become crises.     11032.088842224925
this proactive approach should become the hallmark of the authority.     19059.449941330284
to be proactive the white paper identifies a number of new areas that would be embraced by the authority.     8398.686647495204
these include a comprehensive information gathering and surveillance function, the coordination of scientific information in the eu, and building up strong networks with food safety agencies and bodies in member states.     3793.2013770602252
as part of its remit we also envisage the authority operating an enhanced rapid alert system for food and feed concerns.     20228.317508951925
the commission has decided that it is neither appropriate nor feasible to devolve risk management power to the authority.     5079.242007554108
we believe that decisions in the risk management area should properly remain the preserve of the commission, parliament and council.     2385.869624736478
i make no apology for this approach; i firmly believe that it is the correct one.     4691.0820028388425
certainly there are those who would argue that we should give law-making powers to such an authority.     2843.8618792632747
i do not accept those views and reject them with some degree of passion.     9549.759222365383
it was only last year that the treaty was amended to give parliament a much greater role in the legislative process.     2073.809085121196
to give such a role to an authority at this stage would be, in my view, a retrograde step and represent a dilution of democratic accountability.     7543.916904574951
i will be very interested to hear the views of parliament itself in relation to this matter.     9011.599571344153
there are also those who argue that the commission could effectively ignore the advice of the new authority.     4104.11203769565
i also reject this line of argument.     9128.684551515966
how could a commissioner for health and consumer protection reject or ignore well-founded independent scientific advice in relation to food safety?     10709.045713945427
would this be in the interests of europe's citizens?     5844.603407123748
in my view, most definitely not unless such rejection of the scientific arguments were soundly based, rationally argued and fully justified.     8942.42364569628
it seems difficult to envisage such a situation evolving.     22426.78445029707
i can assure you here today that the commission, in exercising its risk management functions, will fully take account of the scientific advice of the authority.     4608.294330220017
i have already said that the authority will be charged with developing networks with national food safety agencies and bodies in the member states.     3423.5139916281682
this is a major task.     1955.5261290897793
we must develop greater certainty in the science that underpins food safety in the european union.     10090.523005569274
the authority must become the authoritative source of scientific advice and information on food safety issues.     7581.804977076262
this situation will not come about by the very creation of the authority but will evolve over time as confidence is gained in the authority itself.     3770.9227891065498
i do not believe we can be prescriptive in the area of science and advice based on science.     9429.970899293578
however, with the evolution of dynamic networks with national scientific bodies and agencies, the authority will become pre-eminent on the european stage.     4086.5602580803743
i am also anxious to hear the views of parliament in this regard.     10251.022131753816
as an integral part of a value-added structure, the white paper proposes that the authority would have a major role in risk communication: to disseminate complex scientific information in a consumer-friendly way; to be the obvious and indispensable port of call for the most up-to-the-minute data on risk; to be highly visible; to tell the good news story about food; to be proactive.     4759.004745317513
the white paper also contains very important proposals in respect of controls.     8422.642251535382
this is a hugely important component of the system of checks and balances to ensure that member states and operators are complying with community legislation.     5073.428392394073
i want to see a genuine internal market operating in the control arena.     5779.788967815326
in this context we are also proposing to strengthen significantly the control function carried out by the food and veterinary office located in dublin.     8960.061510423719
this revised community framework would have three core elements: operational criteria set up at community level, community control guidelines, and enhanced administrative cooperation in the development and operation of control.     2909.853879198518
as part of our proposals in this area - which i expect to bring forward at the end of this year - i will be examining whether additional powers in addition to infringement procedures need to be given to the commission.     5220.9078004069115
these could include withholding community financial support or recouping funding already committed to a member state.     983.1098717463033
these proposals must be seen as part of our mission to have the highest food safety standards in the world, boost consumer confidence and increase markets for farmers and producers in the union.     3549.3655644065
in addition to the proposals for a new european food authority and an enhanced control system at community-level, the white paper proposes an action plan with a wide range of measures to improve and bring coherence to the community's legislation, covering all aspects of food products from farm to table.     5909.321990087013
it sets out over 80 separate actions that are envisaged over the period ahead and intends to close identified loopholes in current legislation.     6178.889294627859
the new legal framework will cover animal feed, animal health and welfare, hygiene, contaminants and residues, novel food, additives, flavourings, packaging and irradiation.     10869.737702223785
it will include a proposal on general food law which will embody the principles of food safety such as responsibility of feed manufacturers, farmers and food operators, traceability of feed, food and ingredients, proper risk analysis through, for instance, risk assessment - that is, scientific advice and information analysis - risk management - that is, regulation and control - risk communication, and application of the precautionary principle if and when appropriate.     6184.025343990684
as regards the precautionary principle, i might add that the commission is currently finalising a communication which i expect to be adopted very soon.     2424.4243036843945
i look forward to having a useful exchange of views this afternoon with the members of parliament who would clearly have preferred to do this last week if there had been an appropriate parliamentary forum available.     3601.5447788561846
however, having regard to my contacts with the presidents of the relevant committees, i recognise that this was not feasible.     4855.346615103905
but i know too that we will have many further opportunities in the coming months to consider the white paper's proposals on the authority.     5576.173468731334
we now have a number of months to have the necessary debate on the commission's ideas on the white paper on the establishment of a european food authority.     6078.315934074382
parliament will have a key part to play in this debate.     2768.2343366150208
parliament played a crucial role in europe's response to the bse crisis.     14261.460961361892
it has especially active since then in bringing citizens' concerns about food safety issues to the fore.     6807.789837191876
i expect that parliament's contribution to the debate on the authority can be as incisive and constructive.     8421.43498429606
while we have a number of months to the end of april to debate the issue and garner our views, i recognise fully that this is also a very tight timeframe.     3105.390095059742
i would therefore ask parliament to make appropriate arrangements to ensure that its views can be formulated as quickly as possible.     3772.993986641272
it is essential that the commission should have the benefit of parliament's contribution to the shaping of what would be a key component of raising consumer health protection to a new plane and thereby restoring consumer confidence in the european union's food safety regime.     4587.45821720085
the european food authority will be a key ingredient in the eu's food safety regime in the years ahead.     6647.245346124576
it is essential that we get the mix right.     2971.8758316951516

mrs lynne asks whether the authority will have sufficient teeth.     47601.91913762992

there would have to be an interaction at scientific level.     5874.738144018583

we must attempt to avoid that and establish the structures so as to ensure that there is a proper dissemination of information between scientists, that there is full consultation and discussion and that the authority at community level has the opportunity and is mandated to seek the advice and opinion of independent scientists in all member states and indeed maybe even beyond, where experts are to be found elsewhere.     3433.581421829891

mrs myller then asked me about the location of the authority.     2875.261108641566

i recognise that the feo is located in dublin and, although i come from that part of the world myself, i have to accept that it is not the centre of europe!     2704.4712094974743
but the fvo is in quite a different situation from the food safety authority.     5597.077083922512
