question no 28 by (h-0781/99):
subject: construction of nuclear plant in earthquake-prone turkey the two recent earthquakes in turkey registering more than 7 on the richter scale raise tremendous doubts about turkey's persistence with the construction of costly nuclear reactors at akkuyu, while energy reserves produced by the atatürk dams are exported to third countries and the eu spends a slice of its budget on repairing the damage wrought by the earthquakes.     63289.17326279712
turkey's nuclear plans ?     139603.53006352045
which ignore the risks to the population and ecosystems of turkey and the surrounding region ?     6579.385739050031
prompt the suspicion that there are underlying plans previously determined by turkey's political and military leaders to acquire nuclear technology enabling them to build nuclear weapons, as evidenced by their attempts to buy reactors of canadian origin of the type acquired by india and pakistan.     45511.631989624955
what steps will the commission take to prevent nuclear accidents and the proliferation of nuclear weapons in a country that wishes to join the eu but spends enormous amounts on nuclear programmes while receiving financial aid from the eu budget?     12494.27022288961
mr verheugen has the floor on behalf of the commission.     51345.390934846684
the commission is following with interest the planned construction of a nuclear power plant in akkuyu, turkey and recognises the importance of ensuring that the construction of the new plant follows the highest internationally accepted nuclear safety standards.     9598.723960819185
according to our information, the decision on the selection of a bidder has not been taken yet.     4417.710079054387
the commission acknowledges the fact that turkey has signed and ratified the convention on nuclear safety and recognises that the responsibility for granting licences and regulating the siting, construction, commissioning, operation and decommissioning of nuclear power plants in turkey rests entirely with the turkish atomic energy authority.     14255.693581659143
the commission does not have a mandate to impose any limits on decisions taken by any country in terms of energy production, including the nuclear option.     5614.858339729571
as commissioner wallström indicated during the hearings in the european parliament in september 1999, the commission will bring up the issue of nuclear safety and radiation protection in any relevant meeting with the turkish government in the future and i am happy to inform you that i will have a meeting with the turkish foreign minister in a couple of days and will certainly raise the issue.     7801.002589454423
in particular, the commission is aware of the public concerns about the recorded seismic activity in the area of the ecemis fault line located in the vicinity of the proposed plant site.     4801.518073380232
according to information received from the international atomic agency, the design of the plant takes into account the possibility of earthquakes stronger than any ever recorded in the area and particularly more than ten times stronger than the one registered in june 1998 in adana.     7931.020736390165
large design margins are being provided in order to ensure safe operation of the plant according to the environmental conditions of the site.     7818.121999361328
the commission is also aware of concern about the possible intention of using the plant to produce weapons-grade material.     9480.711012256228
it acknowledges the fact that turkey has signed and ratified the treaty on the non-proliferation of nuclear weapons and has subsequently concluded a comprehensive safeguards agreement with the international atomic energy agency.     16389.291190964705
thank you for your reply.     7678.71670801256
i should like to make the following points: turkey is now a candidate country.     5165.778842331448
this plan is designed to increase its energy potential by 2%.     6881.074494550242
at the same time, it also wishes to acquire cadou type reactors from canada, which we now know have already been used for nuclear weapons in pakistan and india.     11564.354235283547
in this sense, there is therefore a serious risk that some idiot will decide that the new geopolitical balance of power in the caucasus calls for a nearby country with weapons grade nuclear technology.     6475.012553282394
so much for the political aspect.     1196.1194265329766
now to the technical aspect.     1290.9257834506718
the safety factor in this type of project, and i am speaking as an engineer, has nothing to do with increasing safety levels in relation to the risk of collapse by a factor of ten or twenty.     10661.434108446723
in cases such as this, where there is uncertainty, simulators are used.     12477.89929603129
however, we cannot used simulators in cases relating to nuclear energy.     15335.015615936796
in this sense, all high seismic risk areas must be excluded a priori for nuclear installations such as this.     16170.82073457831
therefore, and given that the relationship between the european union and the commission and turkey has now changed, it would be a good idea if turkey discussed certain matters relating both to the safety of the area in general and the peaceful development of turkey within the european union.     2645.776977796981
a couple of weeks ago we had a debate in parliament on the nuclear safety issue with special regard to the candidate countries.     9456.912931209814
i have explained the position of the commission.     2985.6353626495506
you have to accept the fact that there is no acquis communautaire on nuclear safety.     8111.307504885849
so what we did was use political means to convince some candidate countries that we have to have decommissioning plans for some reactors that are considered not to be upgradable.     5245.299877463282
in the case of turkey it is different.     2606.9958498715964
the plant is not yet there.     1577.9476698319004
i have already said that turkey is part of the npt and of the convention on nuclear safety.     1423.630242485963
if we see in the development of that nuclear power plant that there are concerns about safety we will discuss it with turkey.     3594.1825450367155
if the conclusion is that turkey is planning to build a nuclear power plant that does not meet normal european safety standards is concerned then we would do the same as we did with lithuania, slovakia and bulgaria.     3391.8551115646637
mr president, the commissioner said that turkey has signed the non-proliferation treaty, it has signed the nuclear safety treaties: why should there be any doubt at all but that turkey's nuclear plant should not be just as safe as any in the community, and would the commissioner be prepared to take a rather more robust position with mr souladakis on this matter?     5747.226981619457
i think it is the right of a member of parliament to raise his own concerns and fears.     2501.9810895066744
i do not have these concerns.     2460.2955729835467
i believe that turkey completely recognises the standards and the criteria of the convention on nuclear safety and the non-proliferation treaty, but there is no doubt that there are concerns in the public in europe and i think it is quite right to discuss them here in parliament.     2780.3547545853235
question no 29 by (h-0786/99):
subject: depleted uranium weapons has the commission conducted any studies of the potential effects upon eu states of transboundary pollution arising from the use of depleted uranium weapons in the kosovan conflict?     27325.0464522201
if not, why not?     171.31057647871606
mrs wallström has the floor on behalf of the commission.     95470.69631016137
thank you, mr bowe, for your question.     33031.93827675825
the european commission has monitored the environmental effects of the conflict from the beginning of the nato action.     5459.077279326959
as early as last june the commission financed a first study.     3637.8940721101276
it was carried out by the regional environment centre for central and eastern europe and concluded that there had been no large-scale ecological catastrophe.     2177.2479871875257
none of the subsequent evidence or analysis has overturned that original assessment.     6146.564320635201
the commission has also been closely associated with the production of the recent report published by the united nations environment programme - balkan task force.     4406.285173265139
this is the most detailed and comprehensive report to date on the environmental effects of the kosovo conflict and i recommend it to those who have not yet studied it.     2688.437288656402
the use of depleted uranium weapons was one of the many issues considered and this report is now widely available also on the net.     4036.0362678469646
the balkan task force was hampered by the fact that little or no information was available on the actual use of these weapons during the conflict.     7906.611640357265
no indications of contamination were found in kosovo during the btf fact-finding mission.     17796.298570906456
however, this does not exclude the possibility that areas in kosovo are contaminated by depleted uranium.     18129.100616409727
from a desk assessment coupled with a fact-finding mission the report concludes that the risks, if any, are limited to an area around the target.     4190.163039506465
future actions will take place within the stability pact for south-eastern europe.     11332.336985801048
a special regional environmental reconstruction plan is also being developed.     2067.6741392610766
it will form the framework for emergency assistance for combating war damage if such action should be needed.     3206.715496318697
first of all, mr president, can i thank the commissioner for that very useful response.     1339.9545971619984
clearly the commission has given some consideration to this problem and i am pleased that these reports which have been produced so far have looked carefully at the issue.     2499.429299259122
but i would like to point out that the concern with depleted uranium weapons is about the way in which they are used.     5167.176939969437
this uranium becomes air-borne, is inhaled and could now be effectively carried by members of the population in kosovo with rather more long-term effects than it has been possible to establish so far.     3810.5907496900923

question no 45 by (h-0006/00):
subject: compliance with directive 96/71/ec concerning the posting of workers the posting of workers directive has still not been implemented in denmark, even though the deadline has passed.     13404.886256422782
the draft legislation before the folketing does not contain any rules governing rights of employed persons under collective agreements.     14040.85060795178
pursuant to article 3(8) of the directive, terms and conditions of employment must be in keeping with the 'collective agreements which have been concluded by the most representative employers' and labour organisations at national level and which are applied throughout national territory'.     3738.9943116771597
however, such use of an agreement outside its specific scope cannot be imposed on the two sides of industry without a legal basis.     3563.3811685306505
there are therefore two possible solutions: either the directive is inapplicable in denmark, or denmark is required by the directive to introduce universally applicable agreements.     5014.645646800332
can the commission confirm that the directive concerning the posting of workers is inapplicable in denmark as far as 'collective agreements ... declared universally applicable' (cf.     9217.898293897082
article 3(1)) are concerned, given that such universally applicable agreements do not exist under danish law?     5265.484228724091
if not, will it explain how the directive is to be complied with in this area?     2323.28720144359
the european union posting of workers directive stipulates that the compulsory rules concerning the terms and conditions of employment applicable in the host country must also apply to workers posted to that country.     5598.421561462589
what it says it that one of two things may apply, i.e.     2043.5875106908056
either the legislation in the host country or the collective agreements which have been declared to be the rules universally applicable to a specific sector.     2116.790572799496
because denmark does not have a system for declaring collective agreements to be universally applicable rules, provision must be made in the implementing legislation so that, apart from current conditions of employment stipulated in legislation, the hours in universally applicable collective agreements, concluded by the most representative organisations, also apply to posted workers.     4350.025684844975
in other words, to put it simply, denmark has a choice: it can either introduce legislation or it can use the legislative process to choose a collective agreement and legally activate it.     2721.4186391389167
discussions are being held between the european commission and denmark, and denmark is expected to notify the commission of the transposition of this directive into its national legislation.     4495.359262818912
the deadline by which it had to answer the commission' s questions expired on 6 december 1999 and we have not received a reply.     7897.4152162746
we are waiting to see what the next move will be.     4319.424657062265
thank you for a very clear answer, the gist of which - if the interpretation is correct - is that the posting of workers directive entails an obligation on the part of the danish state to establish a system of universally applicable agreements.     6033.920255098653
it is a very clear answer, but it is also an answer, may i point out, which puts danish organisations, the danish government and the danish parliament in a very, very difficult situation politically, for it is a well-known fact that there are a number of basic problems associated with the relationship between the danish model, which is based quite obviously upon collective agreements, and the continental model, which presupposes legislation.     3432.7592919306267
the disagreement and the correspondence to which you refer, commissioner, concern, first and foremost, another directive, namely the working time directive, but now we can foresee another letter of formal notice and further treaty infringement proceedings looming because the danish government does not intend, or has expressly stated that it does not wish, to implement legislation and establish universally applicable agreements.     6061.529515192782
firstly, no attempt is being made to change the system in denmark or in any other country.     2424.8535433843113
as i said earlier, there are always problems interpreting the directives of the european commission both because they have a very general framework and because systems are very different from one country to another.     1530.5256480754538
as far as your question is concerned, this problem does not only apply to denmark.     3441.4200064065453
it is not only denmark which has matters pending.     2756.794742197716
five countries have transposed this into national legislation and the rest are at the discussion stage.     3824.805219830558
what now needs to be done, and this was the reason for the consultations and discussions between the commission and the danish government, and with other governments, is to find the best way forward, so that something which is decided at european level by all the member states, and which constitutes a general framework, can also include workers posted to denmark from other countries.     2463.620850953656
and this is the direction in which we expect the danish and the other nine governments to move.     2246.9012139903252

1997 discharge
the next item is the report (a5-0004/2000) by mrs van der laan, on behalf of the committee on budgetary control, on giving discharge to the commission in respect of the implementation of the general budget of the european communities for the 1997 financial year (section i - parliament, ii - council, iii - commission, iv - court of justice and v - court of auditors) [sec(1998) 520 - c4-0350/1998, sec(1998) 522 - c4-0351/1998, sec(1998) 519 - c4-0352/1999]
the commissioner, mrs schreyer, is not here yet, but i hope, and expect, that she will arrive in the next few minutes.     46444.07100280214
nevertheless, i recommend that we start, in the hope that the commissioner will be able to follow the debate, and particularly the rapporteur' s speech, from her office, if that is where she still is.     6102.381836773451
mr president, i am sure that mrs schreyer must have a very good reason for not being here because her absence would be inexcusable otherwise.     3564.314074294968
i would like to start by thanking my colleagues for their cooperation on this report, it would not be what it is today without that spirit of cooperation.     2879.872427698163
mr president, early last year, the 1997 discharge was postponed because this parliament could not possibly grant discharge to a resigning commission which could not enter into any commitments for the future.     5735.080423483947
in its resolution, this parliament stated that discharge could not be granted until we had received serious, far-reaching proposals for reform from the new european commission.     2128.3881235712656
this report, therefore, appears at a crucial time, on the eve of mr kinnock' s reforms.     17195.20542930068
it is an excellent opportunity for this parliament to introduce far-reaching reforms to these plans.     3528.749012898133
in the period leading up to this, it already appeared, on the basis of the initial drafts, that the commission had made very important pledges.     1742.4406549097775
we asked for a whistle-blower' s regulation, and this is now in place.     1735.119223220198
parliament wanted financial inspection and audit functions to be separated.     18778.06873102758
this has now been done.     1090.1586924854125
parliament requires a code of conduct for commissioners and cabinets.     26447.627567479183
this is in place too.     1373.9146938998572
parliament asked the commission to waive its excessive privileges.     4730.832325263122
it did this as well.     2986.903701039784
the commission has also entered into commitments to cooperate with parliament in terms of sem 2000.
fundamental changes will also be considered for the technical assistance offices.     3685.108160798364
these are sound, first steps which illustrate that, if this parliament so wishes, changes are not only possible but can also be translated into action quickly.     2951.644548641738
we want more than that, however.     871.6405144441942
the commission should now produce an ambitious and far-reaching reform programme.     4692.828044411384
this is not only necessary for proper public administration, it is a conditio sine qua non of regaining the trust of the european citizen.     5123.65474762553
we now demand from the european commission clear pledges concerning the following points.     3529.9721408903465
firstly, parliament must have complete access to all commission documents.     3186.7994589340337
this, however, presupposes that we set up an internal scheme quickly in order to be able to guarantee the confidentiality of sensitive documents.     10422.944991562525
in the context of providing information, i would like to draw the commission' s attention to the fact that we are very concerned about the present draft plans on public access to documents.     5967.162415167495
if the current draft is correct, then this is a huge step backwards in comparison to today.     3965.5924123173827
we must put an end to the situation in which financially powerful organisations with representation in brussels are able to access information whilst the ordinary citizen is not.     10994.982447442042
neither can we tolerate a situation in which a public body holds the copyright to public documents.     5438.050349916441
we would also like a clear job description for each european official so that an official with conscientious objections can more easily object to tasks which are unethical or unlawful.     3538.7068708428724
furthermore, it should be the case that, if the court of auditors detects a mistake not just one year but two years running, the management should be held responsible for it, and this will have an effect on chances of promotion.     3636.626648097589
ultimately, we also need to achieve better cooperation, of course, between the european court of auditors and its national counterparts.     5056.5769869956885
this parliament has also asked the commission to give an initial outline of the external aid policy reforms on 31 march of this year.     4914.371864486753
it should no longer be the case that, although europe is an economic power, we have no political influence because, when the chips are down, we cannot offer effective aid to areas which desperately need it.     2589.801157444455
i would quote gaza as an example.     41838.75048642403
it is unacceptable that the commission completed the construction of a hospital in 1996 and that, as yet, it has been left unused.     1435.0717280259466
mr president, since 1996, the discharge has acquired a heavy, political significance.     10476.0019805658
it is one of the most powerful weapons parliament has and must therefore be deployed with care.     2610.559185361946
this is why we will most probably grant the discharge tomorrow.     4830.196472147275
however, we are not surrendering this weapon without placing a time bomb in its place.     7824.832353668491
indeed, the 1999 discharge will not be given until all financial irregularities highlighted by the court of auditors have been cleared up.     8408.689140801276
finally, this discharge report is naturally addressed to the commission.     3749.093210695479
but this does not detract from the fact that the european parliament should also put its own house in order.     4043.2397030331995
as long as we have no status, we are not credible as a force for reforming the union.     2413.30091391225
the reforms of the european institutions are necessary in order to be able to continue europe' s development process.     13695.425103007738
we cannot have a decisive and fair europe unless it is also open and democratic.     2269.3577492377385
all institutions must now join forces in order to work together towards building this type of europe.     7056.962384504252
mr president, the committee on industry decided to go ahead and draw up a report on the discharge for 1997 although we were not specifically asked to do so.     3207.346548542666
we went ahead because we felt we should start this parliament in the way in which we mean to go on, that is, by making sure that we take good care of taxpayers' money in europe.     3542.881100131378
during the course of our work on this report it became clear that there are persistent problems in the spending areas under the control of our budget.     3208.2540653487495
they were not unique to 1997 and two strands seem to run through them.     5110.419281952367
the first is a tendency for the commission to embark on very ambitious programmes, particularly in third countries, without sufficient assessment of the practicalities of implementation and proper resourcing.     3652.603264351054
the second involves serious managerial shortcomings in the commission, in particular in relation to coordination across departments and management of external contracts.     7083.33838582722
i know that all the institutions bear some responsibility for the increasing workload of the commission and for some of the lack of resources.     3178.1550443835827
that cannot excuse everything that we came across.     10097.813173523908
citizens of europe expect the european institutions to be properly managed and they are right to do so.     2171.2313383068804
that is why i want to echo the comments from my colleague about the importance of the reform process which has been promised to the people of europe by mr prodi and mr kinnock.     4483.0907707792685
from what i have seen of the reform process, it looks good.     3223.899163451677
i saw some of mr kinnock's papers today, i heard some of what he had to say.     2597.6914845386204
i have every confidence that if we and the politicians of europe support him we will see the kind of reform we need.     2479.1584264845624
but we need that reform process.     1631.6836518797436
many of the general points raised in our committee's report have been covered in mrs van der laan's report.     5351.504334698291
it is an excellent report and we should all congratulate her on it.     1101.4463439957797
it seems to hit on all the right points without just being like some of the old reports, a series of details.     5994.29988735191
it groups them together and that is very important.     819.729413471168
there are two issues i would like to draw your attention to.     6770.132789084927
one is nuclear safety in eastern europe.     4968.627826362804
we have to get this right.     2275.6616372325834
the committee of independent experts said the commission was not managing this properly.     1840.5754321679171
we have to remedy that.     4023.24638367616
the second point is about scrutiny mechanisms.     4867.463916697402
we need from the commission material we can use to help us scrutinise spending.     4833.898998247221
we need proper information, given in a proper way and we all have to take this whole process a lot more seriously than in the past.     2300.6661164196416
it has been seen as a bureaucratic process to be done as quickly as possible in as little time as possible.     1738.9368135234558
i hope colleagues in this house will support the grounds for discharge for 1997 and, at the same time, that the commission will push ahead with the reform process which is long overdue.     4116.763219538573
only in this way can we create a new culture in the commission and at the same time get public confidence restored.     2023.8539985624984
mr president, i am sure the commission will be relieved to hear that the 97 discharge is unlikely to have the same impact as the 96 discharge which, as you are all so very well aware, led to the forced resignation of the santer commission.     6358.54076785202
the socialist group will be voting to grant discharge.     7792.036907266158
i am sure you will be relieved to hear that as well.     7582.558261727926
but that is not to say that we are satisfied, and that everything in the garden is rosy.     1954.1844102147131
it is clear that a radical overhaul of the commission is long overdue.     3589.087343858757
it indicates, however, that we acknowledge that steps are being made in the right direction.     3282.083453467491
i just want to outline some of the issues that we, as socialists, have put down as amendments.     5221.56377654153
we hope these will be carried because they are important in the way that they will impact on future reform.     2759.0439708674617
first of all, the immunity of officials: this should be lifted if and when requested by a national prosecutor.     4213.036403738945
we need to make it much easier to prosecute officials who are guilty of fraud and corruption.     6965.624467234648
it is critical to note that the commission has too often failed to act on the reforms recommended by the court of auditors.     10166.227043130904
the court's report is there for a reason, our response to it is there for a reason and it is important that it is followed through.     1292.3465025868227
i have just heard now that an audit progress board is going to be set up by the commission.     2000.75980528347
even if we hear nothing else, we know that is going in the right direction in terms of the reforms that we want to see.     2935.8882884699347
too often we have put forward recommendations and they have not been acted upon even though you have said very often that you will act on them.     1376.5994035587241
we want to see that follow-through to a much greater extent in future.     2802.401378355127
the other issue is access to confidential documents.     2953.349478999197
we have had problems in the past in terms of our responsibility in carrying out discharge because we have not had access to the documents we should have had.     2321.554084861751
we understand that we also have a responsibility here, that if we are given documents we must ensure that confidential documents will indeed be kept confidential.     2079.6977241174695
we have put down an amendment to that effect.     1432.2104842028052
one issue which is referred to in the van der laan report is the whole question of the gaza hospital.     10821.562836914858
the situation there is totally unacceptable.     2440.554472630727
we will not put up with it for much longer and we are looking for immediate action on that issue.     1277.8126312274737
i would like to congratulate lousewies van der laan.     30321.379306440183
i do not usually bother congratulating people but i think she has produced a very cogent report and deserves our thanks.     4628.761662530138
mr president, commissioner, if we are honest, we find ourselves in a somewhat unusual situation.     2152.458233229182
we are discussing last year' s discharge, but also the commission' s responsibility.     87289.98428214263
i would like to raise the problems which are still piled up high on our desks.     1428.8207071473294
the assessment of whether or not the discharge will be granted also depends to some extent on where the emphasis is placed.     3488.4031260355778
it is about a commission which is no more.     928.1632000407966
there is now a new commission.     1193.2558010271828
then it is logical to grant discharge, because how can the current commissioner be blamed where 1997 is concerned?     2666.1299951781
the problems remain and this gives rise to doubts.     5952.962338392299
we have to take a decision now regarding the commission' s good intentions, but there is still no structural outline for these good intentions.     7168.580578440296
mr kinnock will present his proposal next month.     9499.204628974006
we are all very much anticipating this, but the facts are still missing at this time when we already have to decide whether or not to grant discharge.     3072.720379910543
it is clearly a dilemma with which the rapporteur has also struggled.     1153.6962524647417
this dilemma stretches beyond the areas which i have already listed.     3454.956546585408
take, for example, the pledges made by the commission.     4510.390804077057
they look good in themselves.     3851.33815175986
i have read a few documents written by mr kinnock and we have every confidence in them.     4525.243542847347
but i will give two examples from which it is not as evident that the good intentions which the commission has now expressed will lead to a good outcome: the public nature and confidentiality of documents.     2199.4743111502776
the previous speaker already said something about this.     1926.4795619045967
a document is circulating at the moment - not at a low level, but at a high level within the commission - which, instead of enhancing the public nature of documents, is having the opposite effect.     3383.105493522631
this is an illustration of empty pledges not necessarily leading to good results.     8540.398373371161
this also applies to whistle-blowers.     41175.29206505395
mr kinnock has also devoted some fine-sounding phrases to this subject but, at the same time, it is entirely unclear, at this moment when we have to make a decision, what, for example, happens with whistle-blowers who want to get something off their chest and cannot do this internally but who want to address the outside world - the press or parliament.     5132.144347705306
we have still not had a response to crucial questions of this type.     1644.1881508322926
so there is doubt as to whether these pledges of the commission contain enough substance at this crucial time when decisions need to be made.     3301.0343070907684
this also applies, for example, to the very real projects which the committee on industry has introduced.     2868.978967049958
in my opinion, the commission and mr kinnock should come with good intentions and with sound plans on personnel policy and financial management, but each commissioner who is now responsible for an area which has had serious shortcomings in the past should come with sound plans in order to improve the situation and not with general, empty proposals.     2858.5605756452956
at the moment, our group still feels sympathy for, and patience with, the commission because it cannot be held responsible for a large proportion of the mistakes made in the past, but this patience has a limit.     3620.243927138627
clear progress must be visible.     8254.761983092716
at present, we trust that the commission will produce these sound proposals, but it is not a foregone conclusion that it will do so.     1732.8327760744887
finally, mr president, the 1996 discharge was the beginning of the end of the last commission.     4205.254437862263
i express the hope that - in fact, i urge the present commission to ensure that - the 1997 discharge is the beginning of a true reform of financial policy by the commission.     4041.571447748286
otherwise this discharge will not have been of any value.     3217.316947899851
in assessing the question of whether or not to grant the commission discharge, the decision must be based upon what actually occurred during the financial year concerned, in this case in the course of 1997.
in our group, we find it hard to see how the financial administration for 1997 was in any crucial way better than that for 1996.
in that year, we voted against granting discharge.     3202.639375378521
as a result, we shall vote against granting discharge for 1997, too.     8280.212589391891
we think that this picture of ours is confirmed by the examination carried out by the court of auditors.     19851.931427515992
it is both good and necessary that reforms have been promised.     2255.5358455158776
so far, the promises which have been made are still, however, to be fulfilled, especially where transparency is concerned.     1818.4033139532132
we are therefore going to vote in favour of the demands for reforms which are presented in the resolution, but against the granting of discharge.     6689.374858468874
mr president, first of all, i have only positive comments to make on mrs van der laan' s very expert work on this report.     7423.575706641996
the union for a europe of nations group cannot vote for approving the accounts for 1997.
the report on so-called discharge contains a comprehensive and extremely critical survey of the accounts.     4892.877601316641
we support these critical remarks, and i must therefore state that it would seem quite absurd, against this background, to vote in favour of approving the accounts.     8025.641969767844
it has not been possible for the court of auditors to issue an auditor' s statement to the effect that the arrangements covered by the accounts are lawful, and we should regard it as extremely problematic if we, as members of this parliament, were to vote in favour of accounts without having any guarantee of the legality of the arrangements concerned.     4633.659936338761
the majority have made their approval of the accounts conditional upon the new commission' s implementing a series of reforms, so as to ensure that what we are familiar with from the previous commission' s period of office is not repeated.     11197.370027427285
again, i must say that we are concerned here with an extremely unfortunate confusion of the old commission' s accountability for 1997 and the new commission' s accountability for the future.     7914.470175775562
we do not think that the new commission, under any circumstances, could incur liability for the past.     4112.537359973597
we think it is wrong to talk about the commission' s liability as an institution.     17745.52912084601
the mistakes up until 1999 are attributable to those who had responsibility at that time, and we still have no way of knowing whether the new commission can do any better.     2282.9352838478408
by means of this extraordinary procedure, parliament is stopping itself from placing liability for the arrangements in 1997 squarely where it belongs, namely with the previous commission.     4977.923547261406
it was the 1996 accounts which led to the downfall of the previous commission, and the 1997 accounts are just as incriminating.     3352.587740640613
there is no reason why, against this background, we should agree to grant discharge.     11217.562595288846
as far as the decision to balance the accounts is concerned, we shall abstain from voting, and where, finally, the motion for a resolution is concerned, we shall attach most importance to the many correct instructions contained in this and vote in favour of it.     4785.95671896192
mr president, the decision relating to discharge for the 1997 financial year was postponed because the erstwhile commission, to whom it was to be granted, had stepped down before the appointed time and was only continuing in office in a caretaker capacity.     4807.349034569268
mrs van der laan' s report - on which she has lavished a great deal of hard work, and for which we are indebted to her - proposes that we should grant discharge to the commission for 1997.
one might ask how it is that the current commission is to receive the discharge for its predecessor' s budgetary management - mr camre just mentioned this - particularly as the discharge for the previous year, 1996, was denied.     4447.30359783333
that is just the way things are, however.     1424.3742683271912
in taking over the mandate, the new commission has to assume responsibility not just for the achievements of the past but also for the mistakes made.     3390.583321952651
since, on account of the college system, discharge can only be granted to the commission as a whole or, alternatively, denied or postponed, it is no longer of any consequence that four former commissioners who belonged to the previous commission that has yet to be discharged, crop up again as members of this body.     6833.541890495728
this question ought to have been asked when the new commission was appointed.     3254.7866353651507
if parliament votes in favour of the committee on budgetary control' s proposal this week and grants discharge, then the commission must not take this to be a blank cheque.     14114.22991553744
for it is the third section of mrs van der laan' s report that is the most important, to my mind, namely the motion for a resolution.     4963.062164392923
the comments contained within, under eight headings, are an integral part of the discharge, the implementation of which forms the basis of our decision.     9077.688570117007
in the course of the discharge procedure for the coming years - that for 1998 is already under way - parliament will have to examine, as a matter of urgency, whether or not it was too quick to bestow premature praise on the commission for 1997.
we will be in a position to judge just as soon as the commission presents its reform programme.     3596.494248663381
we will be able to use the discharge procedure for 1998 to check whether efficiency, transparency and accountability, and likewise a sincere willingness to provide information, are being displayed vis-à-vis the discharge authority.     18368.056653458745
mr president, we all recall that parliament decided to postpone the discharge for the 1997 financial year pending commitments from the new european commission regarding internal reform.     5475.487148273456
in response to this the commission has entered into various commitments and has certainly adopted many reform measures.     3396.8686907823176
it is fair to say that the new president, mr prodi, and his team are certainly committed to implementing the financial control requirements as laid down by this parliament.     2076.857125455253
however, the reform of the european commission must now be taken in the context of the debate that is going to take place in the run-up to the forthcoming intergovernmental conference and the reform of various eu policies and initiatives.     1863.854297840178
existing eu treaties will be amended so as to ensure, for example, that the enlargement process can succeed.     3951.552687307633
i have no doubt further reform of eu institutions will be analysed in this debate.     5484.514245036607
but from the perspective of small member states it is important that, as the european commission is reformed, it must be done in a way that ensures that small member states continue to have representation on the commission.     3832.305443929225
mr president, the commission is now to obtain its discharge for 1997, but in reality it does not deserve it.     3331.784913738842
1997 was a matter for the old commission and, therefore, the new commission does not think that it can accept liability.     1927.492688668732
it is true that implementation of the budget for 1997 fell within the old commission' s remit.     22458.301359295197
the new commission has, on the other hand, undertaken to carry out a cleaning up exercise following former scandals, and i must admit that i am not impressed at all.     6544.6285766752935
the old mindset which involved sweeping things under the carpet and protecting one' s friends unfortunately still exists.     20374.502406873056
there are some who think that it is more in our interests to let bygones be bygones and make a fresh start.     5789.83966902726
i do not, however, think we can make a fresh start if we do not tidy things up properly.     4410.720686482211
i am referring here especially to the earlier scandals involving echo.     17822.43508361011
i am very indignant about the fact that it is so difficult to get any documents handed over on this matter.     6088.405170206486
i am the rapporteur for echo on the committee on budgetary control, and i shall have a lot of difficulty carrying out my work if the commission will not give me the necessary information.     3814.945146876777
from the outside, it looks as if the commission has something to hide.     2726.1484935472427
my investigations also unfortunately suggest that this could be the case.     2146.4524845448846
the commission is not putting all its cards on the table and is in that way repeating ancient practice on the part of the commission.     4133.418618254897
it was this practice which led to the commission' s downfall.     48997.215381694026
i can therefore fully support the demand to give parliament unconditional right of access to documents.     2979.2519103237973
otherwise, we cannot carry out our work.     10699.11542366824
thank you, commissioner schreyer.     6421.866044566696
the debate is closed.     4906.850855896859
the vote will take place tomorrow at 12 p.m.
response to second report of committee of independent experts
the next item is the report (a5-0001/2000) by mr van hulten, on behalf of the committee on budgetary control, on action to be taken on the second report of the committee of independent experts on reform of the commission.     19518.564585139815
mr president, this time last year the european parliament set up a committee of independent experts chaired by mr middelhoek to investigate allegations of fraud, nepotism and corruption in the european commission.     3989.8966612212403
on 15 march the committee published its first report concluding that: "it is becoming difficult to find anyone who has even the slightest sense of responsibility".     4777.640146617495
within a few hours of the presentation of the report president santer announced the resignation of his entire team.     8355.819258634448
the resignation marked the end of a bitter struggle between an increasingly confident parliament and a european commission mired in allegations of scandal.     13934.773614858022
since then the brussels landscape has changed beyond recognition; a new, reinvigorated parliament has been elected and a new commission confirmed in office.     5887.568989792696
speaking in this house on 21 july president-designate prodi undertook to take full account of the second report of the committee of independent experts on commission reform, which contains 90 detailed recommendations and which we are debating today.     4623.348086111051
the new commission has already taken important steps to move away from the way it used to function.     2978.7850470157173
a code of conduct for commissioners and their private offices has been adopted.     6609.566321626228
in a symbolic but significant gesture commissioners have voluntarily renounced their entitlement to the tax-free purchase of alcohol, tobacco, petrol and consumer goods.     46466.54392179583
new rules have been drawn up and implemented governing the appointment of senior officials.     6483.85730754864
the number of departments has been reduced.     3673.720521918272
the commission, in my opinion, has shown a clear and unprecedented commitment to change and for this they are to be congratulated.     2998.653070071153
the overall aim of the reforms must be to create a strong, honest european public administration equipped to carry out its tasks in an effective and efficient manner; an administration in which officials are provided with the means to carry out their tasks and are held fully accountable at all levels; an administration that recognises and rewards merit and encourages officials to develop their full potential.     4954.50865680953
in order to achieve this, action is required in four areas.     5007.155638037831
first, financial management and control within the commission must be improved.     949.3100703203386
one of the main problems is the lack of a functioning system of financial control.     1511.5491645538266
the commission's dgs must be made fully responsible for their own expenditure, including financial control.     3005.314076149166
a new independent audit system service must be set up.     1962.3183282872537
dgs must publish their own annual accounts so as to enable a clear identification of problem areas and set annual targets for reducing fraud and irregularities.     10786.342625784337
in return for this greater degree of autonomy, managers must be made fully and personally responsible for their actions.     5963.038228681013
it is clear that the transition to such a new system will take time.     1368.2434689224349
changes to the financial regulation will be required and parliament must have its say on those changes.     1969.4207718514804
but while the commission must ensure that it respects the treaty and the financial regulation in the transitional phase, this must not be an excuse for inertia.     1939.7846028815793
urgent change is required today.     2905.607441776464
second, the fight against fraud, mismanagement and nepotism must be strengthened, firstly by creating a culture in which they cannot thrive.     12354.638419339655
this requires a clear example to be set by commissioners and senior staff as well as adequate training and secondly, by reinforcing the existing mechanisms for dealing with fraud.     5852.319343326333
olaf, the commission agency set up earlier this year must be placed under the direction of an independent european public prosecutor whose job will be to prepare for prosecution by national criminal courts, criminal offences committed against the financial interests of the union by members and officials of the european institutions.     3808.886321689208
a proposal can be made, a proposal should be made, on the basis of article 280 of the treaty, by the middle of this year.     3239.132693182575
third, standards in european public life must be upheld.     4011.6868862014553
the political crisis which led to the downfall of the commission earlier this year clearly demonstrated the need for unambiguous and enforceable rules of conduct.     6925.436359281384
a number of codes have since been introduced.     11681.929594387686
they must be assessed by parliament and should be made legally binding.     2873.0507946060798
the european institutions should follow the example of a number of countries, most notably the united kingdom, and institute a committee for standards in public life, with a mandate to give advice on professional ethics and rules of conduct in the european institutions.     6034.051862820107
whistle-blowers who act in good faith must be protected.     36475.69323226292
at the end of last year mr kinnock announced new measures for the protection of whistle-blowers.     15108.613775904
they must be implemented without delay.     5544.717840421578
although such measures can never be an alternative for good management, they must be an escape valve when something goes wrong.     1753.8217204263935
crucially, reforms must not be limited to the commission.     3365.3289887259175
parliament must consider itself the need for improvements to its internal rules, administrative procedures and management practices.     2801.6146383303835
finally, the commission's human resource policy must be modernised.     3654.6059986394966
it is clearly no longer suited to the requirements of a modern, multinational organisation.     2992.7767300509845
the social dialogue has often acted as a brake on reform and its overhaul is long overdue.     10258.756236219559
a career with the european institutions must become more attractive.     3105.017224576577
too many young, new officials are leaving their jobs after just a few years.     2552.028861415806
merit must be recognised and rewarded, specific skills training should be a sine qua non for promotion to a higher grade.     12940.871518677988
the promotions procedure must be made fairer and more transparent.     1473.7798094459117
last but not least the pay and benefits package must be reviewed.     2068.1423466127762
it must become more flexible and more responsible to labour market conditions.     1845.8451483685142
it must be rid of some of its more outdated elements; and it must deal with the legitimate concerns of the general public who cannot understand why european civil servants should be paid an expatriation allowance indefinitely in a europe of open borders, or pay a level of taxes that is often well below that of member states.     4391.7041173014095
commissioner kinnock will, tomorrow, present his communication on reform.     2880.109252065778
this communication must contain a clear timetable.     6544.8741100958405
with a new commission and a new parliament up and running the momentum for reform is now as strong as it ever has been and probably ever will be.     1064.5777971059554
enlargement of the union is just a few years away.     1758.2187683645664
now is the time for europe to put its own house in order; to instil in its institutions - as the independent experts might have said - a sense of responsibility.     2647.2937762894053
in june of last year europe's voters gave a clear signal that they are fed up with endless stories about mismanagement and nepotism.     36495.33302791651
there is a very simple way to deal with those stories.     3544.53029908509
let us get rid of mismanagement and nepotism.     157508.13631784287
mr president, i do not want to be misunderstood - i sincerely hope this does not happen - and i should therefore like to start by saying that i am, of course, in favour of combating fraud and firmly support the appropriate, necessary reforms.     4386.583997763187
this is not a straightforward issue and calls for a much more wide-ranging analysis, but i shall restrict myself to raising only a few points, in order to make people more aware, if possible, of what we are talking about.     3112.9091788032724
all the experts can provide information and news, formulate opinions and give advice, but, they have no political or elective responsibility, and rightly so.     2813.4813271571957
i nevertheless believe that politicians should look into what can be gleaned from an expert report and what cannot be taken en masse in a spirit which, on some occasions, i have felt in the past and still feel to be self-destructive.     5085.403179180048
i believe - and i shall only pick up on a few ideas - that parliament alone, and no one else, can exert an influence over its members.     5126.057345957554
if this were not the case, the european parliament' s authority and representativeness would be seriously undermined in the coming years, and this institution would not move forward, as it should.     3971.9863597576946
it is just as important for no-one to be able to or have to refuse the european parliament or its committees any documentation.     3743.7894291634743
members of parliament must possess the qualities of morality, discipline and discretion over delegated issues innately, and these certainly cannot be imposed on them by anyone else.     8776.614872650636
i would point out that there is no european legal system, but that a different legal system exists in each member state.     1324.995333931094
we run the risk of delegating issues relating to the same offences which will then be punished in different ways.     8022.246059093402
i agree that an investigation system should be introduced, but i am also absolutely convinced that we must recognise the right to equal respect for dignity and, in any case, the equal need for the right to a defence.     3112.6701926709543
i am opposed to european community officials being accused of criminal acts at the drop of a hat: whistle-blowing is a method that has no place in the third millennium.     8756.756487629584
in conclusion, mr president, parliament must introduce a principle of democracy: we must implement reforms which allow parliament to grow and expand its powers, not go into reverse.     6431.430367199251
this debate on the van hulten report brings to a close one of the most traumatic periods for the european institutions since their creation in 1957.
the refusal of the european parliament to grant discharge and the definitive refusal for the 1996 budget; the motion of censure tabled in this house a year ago for differing reasons; and the first report of the committee of independent experts on the reform of the commission which led to the mass resignation of the commission itself, are now part of european folklore.     3476.3767102151946
those of us involved in these historic events are well aware that none of the upheaval would have been caused had we not acted with parliamentary power to press for changes in the way in which the commission operated.     3612.6911846904327
remember that the council of ministers, absent again this evening from our debate, approved the 1997 discharge - not the 1996 - on the very day the commission itself resigned on 15 march.     2585.011559936873
now we come to the second report of the committee of independent experts which we already had the opportunity to welcome and briefly debate last september.     2288.7847145141304
our greatest concern in the epp is to ensure that public confidence in the european commission is restored.     3524.3783314563625
further steps to build a strong europe will be to no avail if the european peoples perceive that there is no adequate system of democratic accountability for over-zealous officials.     3017.8935164860095
under no circumstances will we concede ground which we have won over the past few months, which we believe to be in the interests of openness and transparency.     6270.5441648202295
we are therefore concerned to see the flurry of commission announcements in recent weeks by commissioner kinnock suggesting policy proposals of various kinds to be put in a white paper shortly.     6469.3626491725645
while conveying a desire to proceed rapidly, it also gives the impression that the commission is in transmission rather than listening mode.     10169.238706832448
our concern is strengthened if the rumour reported a few days ago is true - namely that the commission wants to limit severely the access of parliament to information.     6649.520955080747
this was, after all, one of the causes of the downfall of the last commission.     3357.75882454232
have the lessons not been learnt?     8194.156603346964
the knowledge that the framework relations between the european parliament and the european commission still remain to be negotiated led to our disagreement with the rapporteur when we debated his report in committee.     3259.8557891745263
we could, in no way, agree with his view that it would be demeaning for parliament to set out detailed instructions as to what we wanted the commission to take up in its reform package.     2351.720521796818
the less precise, mr van hulten, we are in our resolutions, the more room it gives the commission and your former colleagues in the council to do what they like.     4198.1247584842085
we believe the vast number of recommendations of the wise men' s report should be implemented.     10572.77510234633
we have, as the epp-ed group, submitted all the recommendations of the wise men' s report in committee and many of them have now been put into the report, entirely changing the nature of the van hulten report in committee.     2849.0985180380067
we have resubmitted a few amendments which fell in committee, in particular, our desire to see the codes of conduct revised specifically to include the reference to merit and managerial capacity which you, commissioner, accepted - when we had our hearings last september - should be included in these codes of conduct, particularly when considering appointments and promotion.     6468.563881909973
looking to the future, we know that we are at the beginning of a long process of continuing reform in the european commission.     2361.8920271455045
we want in particular to see the hard-working and highly competent norm for commission officials acknowledged in the outside world - a reputation that has been darkened by the inappropriate conduct of a few individuals.     2928.353407626111
commissioner, you will be aware from your presence in the last commission why the crisis occurred.     2023.343833258606
in a nutshell: there were programmes being run for which there were insufficient staff resources available.     3968.4847826135256
we urge you to take the opportunity to establish the real staffing needs of the commission based on the essential activities for which it is responsible.     3031.4735866369697
our position was made very clear on this topic in the 2000 budget.     2777.5422481943488
we will be vigilant over the next five years to ensure the reforms now being suggested are fully implemented and will support efforts made to modernise institutions.     2976.7110479261773
but, equally, we will not hesitate to withdraw our support financially or otherwise should steps be taken which do not correspond to the openness promised by commission president romano prodi prior to his nomination.     11977.225223243135
let us hope that we can avoid institutional upheavals by having an ongoing dialogue which assumes from the outset that parliament will be an equal partner in deciding the outcome of commission reform.     6389.005262293966

mr president, this report is positive but, actually, we need more than this.     1907.2054410615335
fraud, mismanagement and nepotism do not come from nowhere.     26735.159297219147
they are most likely to occur if there is little democratic control on cash flow.     11865.203748303573
via the structural funds, a great deal of the european budget is being pumped around the system.     4975.918686067952
this is only useful as long as there is a sense of solidarity where rich member states contribute to both the revenue and development of poorer member states.     4643.657513621143
but there is also funding which is being pumped via brussels back to the same rich member states.     4622.473670840228
districts and regional authorities consider this as their own money but they can only get their hands on this by investing vast amounts of money and manpower in lobbying and negotiating.     5771.596529602602
after each incident of improper use of this money, and certainly after fraud, the call for stricter control is more pronounced.     6878.995078891043

mr president, a year has not yet passed, but it is already clear that parliament is going to evaluate the first and second reports differently.     2756.292654880199
the first report was widely publicised, formally debated and used - just like the controversy and news leaks which preceded it - to make mincemeat, first of the president of the commission, and then of the majority of the european commissioners, even though they had no connection with fraud, mismanagement or nepotism.     4051.986033480207
reading now what happened then, not even a year ago, it becomes clear that that first report was intended to do anything but serve the cause of truth or reform, as is maintained today, so much so that this second report - which, on the other hand, could have provided much more salient points - was commissioned with the precise mandate not to address specific cases, since there is no intention to follow up the offences detailed in this report.     2778.2243341858443
it is of no concern to the major groups in parliament, nor to the majority of the trade unions, who are busy discussing the defence of european public administration, but, in practice, are occupied with the corporate protection of their own members, putting the wide powers afforded to them to dubious use.     3658.4063476040305

allow me to start with the most important demand.     3232.273481209036
we do not want financial control to be abolished.     3811.6318496403646
the financial controller should still be able to make checks before funding commitments or payments are made, not in every case, but wherever uncertainties or risks arise.     9397.149823906231

i do not have exact figures, but the community' s budgetary discipline and the associated implementing provisions apply to almost 100 different areas of responsibility of the financial controller, to his or her independence and the tasks assigned to this office.     8560.840899053046
this cannot be ignored or evaded, certainly not on account of soft law, as was once suggested at a meeting of our committee.     7022.755455824177
irrespective of such legal considerations, it would be an unforgivable mistake, under the circumstances, to scrap financial control in the traditional sense at the very moment when those responsible for such matters in the commission are, at long last, no longer out on a limb but set to become part of a chain of functioning supervisory and investigatory mechanisms.     4581.611689843284
as we see it, there will be a dovetailing of three mechanisms in the future: a system of independent prior approval by the financial controller, concomitant and follow-up control by the internal audit service - also known as the audit service - which has yet to be set up, and finally, there will be the targeted tracking-down of irregularities by olaf, the new anti-fraud office.     3053.788118034689

mr president, the santer commission came to grief because financial control failed all down the line.     5192.200849444278
therefore, the future of this new commission will depend in no small measure on the extent to which reforms are swiftly undertaken here and financial control is back in working order.     3122.0032019368814
as far as this is concerned, a number of people have already referred to the fact that the commission intends to drastically improve and consolidate its follow-up checks and that these checks should be carried out on a completely independent basis, with no sweeping of matters under the carpet in future.     5965.006066908705
naturally, this is only to be welcomed.     4187.745779426849
what i do not understand is why this has to come at a price, as it were, that is, of having to dispense with independent - and i stress, independent - prior approval.     2490.838295453834
up until now, the commission' s payments could only be made when the authorising officer signed the appropriate order and the financial controller gave his approval in the form of a visa.     10117.292722235985
and so it is the "two key" principle that applies here.     1344.5134485301235
a single key is to suffice in future.     2712.663657232735
the financial controller is no longer to make advance checks, if all goes to plan as regards the reforms currently under discussion within the commission.     3738.2353631961028
if you will pardon me for saying so, mr kinnock, what you are proposing is a little like abolishing the police because they were unable to prevent crimes.     6168.196603219184
what we really need to focus on, however, is making the checks more effective.     1577.250488911743
this could be achieved by no longer insisting, in future, that the financial controllers furnish every single payment transaction with their approval stamp.     18310.73890291002
it is precisely those who feel the need to control everything that end up controlling nothing at all.     2333.7472927884364
therefore, in future, prior approval should take place in a targeted manner, that is, only in cases of uncertainty or risk.     4993.146981495104
the officials responsible for financial control should be deployed on a decentralised basis, that is, in the operational directorates-general, amongst those of their colleagues that spend the money, so that they are immediately available when problems arise and so as to render the checks less ponderous and time-consuming.     2532.0368923795822
however, the financial controllers must work independently.     3673.249542813907
that is the crucial difference between our plans and those of the commission, when it talks in terms of decentralisation.     3921.3981476319786
it is obviously the commission' s intention to make the financial control officials subordinate to the individual directorates-general, but this is precisely what we do not want.     4509.232690450706
surely we have learnt this much from the events surrounding the leonardo affair, when the internal examiners in the relevant directorate-general issued warnings, but these were neither heeded nor passed on.     10903.053712636303
therefore, independence is prerequisite for effective checks.     19181.666908226038
that is the position which a clear majority of the committee on budgetary control subscribes to.     1740.7881641507802

though time forbids me, mr president, from commenting in this debate on each element in the resolution before the house, i readily give the assurance that the details will be treated as an important input into our reform proposals throughout this consultation and indeed to our work in other relevant areas.     4523.460401078081
turning to the main themes of the resolution i offer the following observations.     6532.467781853127
the need for transparency is rightly stressed, not least because greater transparency in the way in which the commission operates will improve the efficiency and will also demystify what the commission does.     2024.1270036238914
this is essential for an executive administration that must be accountable, not just to this house, but more generally to the european public.     1529.2348134190072
naturally, sensible safeguards are required to protect specially sensitive information, but the cases where these are needed should be kept to the minimum possible.     4376.155857406822
i have repeatedly emphasised that, indeed not just as a commissioner, but in 25 or 30 years of campaigning.     1816.0231758682871
that most certainly is the intention of the commission.     1541.9112749094188
financial management and control is obviously a crucial area of reform.     1318.9759766596144
as the house knows and has repeatedly said, the scale and scope of the union's financial interventions have grown immensely in the past decade without a proportionate increase in staffing or an adjustment in procedures.     7685.723011389806
several members have made that point again in the course of today's debate.     3687.2327544458435
we share the view, forcefully expressed by the committee of independent experts, and repeated in this draft resolution that the time has come for a thorough overhaul of our rules and procedures.     3034.1485191266397
the means of doing that are set out in the reform strategy and the commission will present its proposals for a radical recasting of the financial regulation in april.     2614.181907668609
parliamentary support in pursuing that essential course for change will be absolutely vital.     2624.461906331491
i share the view expressed by mrs theato that it would not be acceptable in any way at all for us to seek to operate new arrangements without a change in the law.     3748.185440759144
change in the law is fundamental.     2386.817206154967
there are preparations which can be made and they are specified and set out with the full reassurances in the reform strategy, but enactment of change in the legislation is obviously of fundamental importance to the operation of the new system.     2690.4897227973183
essentially - and with legal change - the commission will systematically move away from the current centralised system of prior approval of each financial transaction by the financial controller and instead strengthen the internal control systems within spending departments so that directors-general are better equipped to take responsibility for decisions with an impact on the european union budget.     4851.278465623437
in addition, and to achieve better assurances than those provided under the present system, the new system of decentralised controls must be complemented by setting up an internal audit service - "the second key" as herr bösch said, established in a way that is closely consistent with the recommendations of the committee of experts.     4742.3880094708675
that service will open on 1 may this year.     1972.6446388821691
it will be headed by a professionally qualified member of the audit profession and its independence must and will be guaranteed through a new provision to be added in the financial regulation.     2458.7011720496457
the new service will report to me and it will be accompanied by an audit progress board chaired by my colleague, mrs schreyer, who i am delighted to see in the chamber this evening.     7061.734441024039
as budgets commissioner, here responsibilities already include relations with the court of auditors.     20618.274093355853
this audit progress board will ensure effective and rigorous follow-up to internal audits.     7357.08168249031
i want to emphasise strongly that changing our control systems will definitely not mean relaxing them.     8631.177697439654
the proposed change will make the systems more effective, both in terms of inputs and in terms of measured and accountable outcomes.     6460.695095168802
i can also specifically assure the house that we are not going for what is sometimes called "a big bang approach".     4495.888608787898
financial control' s ex ante visa for each spending department will only be relinquished as and when the internal control system in a department is shown to be fully adequate.     6508.726384671746
i am somewhat surprised that paragraph 10 of the draft resolution does not appear to address the fundamental criticisms of the current centralised financial control function made in both reports of the committee of independent experts.     3941.5711092971446
that committee was very clear about the need to abolish the centralised ex ante visa.     13629.534657512166
it was clear too on the need to separate internal audit from financial control.     3158.3389802325455
in addition, i do not believe that paragraph 10 fully reflects the opinion of the court of auditors in 1997.
naturally the court will be able to give its considered opinion on the proposed changes to the financial regulation but it is useful to recall mr karlsson' s comments to this house last month.     10331.068036162711
"the commission's internal control" he said "is not forceful enough in preventing incorrect operation.     4841.226271150856
for instance, the financial controller granted a positive a priori visa in most of the cases of mismanagement or irregularities recently uncovered.     12918.748514536175
at the same time, the internal audit function is carried out in an uncoordinated way by several bodies, notably the same financial controller, the inspectorate general and by some units operating directorates-general."     5078.097572887021
the centralised ex ante approval system was doubtlessly originally designed to ensure carefulness but, over the years, it has had the perverse effect of reducing the extent to which managers feel responsible for their decisions.     3102.6433391474616
i do not believe that there is any real disagreement between us on this.     5598.324522925001
i take it from paragraph 10 that the underlying preoccupation of honourable members is that there should be a carefully managed transition.     2908.943631186393
that will most certainly be the character of the change as honourable members will see when they read the reform strategy document.     5399.19233871546
our objective, the committee' s objective, the court of auditors' objective is not to abolish financial control, it is to get rid of and improve upon centralised financial control.     23414.936653398716
before leaving this area i would add that we agree with the rapporteur that the existing internal audit function must be maintained pending the establishment of the new independent internal audit service in a matter of months.     4853.657403938237
turning quickly to the section of the resolution on fighting fraud, corruption, mismanagement and nepotism: the major new proposal of the report concerns reporting by commission officials of perceived wrong-doing.     6524.457824433219
as i made clear in the hearings last september and on other occasions, we are at one in the belief that there is value in defining the best possible mechanisms for this purpose although obviously we all hope that it will rarely be needed.     3574.6198978391612
since june of last year, the olaf regulation has given better guidance to staff about reporting possible irregularities.     5403.396403808789
we propose to complement those provisions by defining the rights and obligations for officials to report suspected wrong-doing through internal channels but not exclusively within the same hierarchical line.     7692.992157248099
the possibility of using specified external channels will also be addressed.     7658.893528849627
we are seeking to implement best practice.     18149.952703826126
serious response to reports, confidentiality at early stages and career protection will be assured for people who report wrong-doing in good faith and in ways that do not compromise investigations by untimely disclosure.     11787.686387025093
as a corollary, there will be safeguards for officials who are the subject of false allegations.     4015.0533333689505
a communication later this year will give full details.     5563.393413738644
i do not think parliament will be disappointed by what we will propose.     4495.0587494798565
i am persuaded, however, by the suggestion in paragraph 34 of the report on using external bodies to enforce existing provisions on financial liability.     8761.307772600863
we already plan to reform the existing disciplinary procedures to ensure thoroughness, fairness, consistency and professionalism.     12135.204094444825
and we will propose the establishment of an interinstitutional disciplinary board - something else on which we will need the support and understanding of parliament.     3953.5666633754436
a communication in june will set out the full proposal for change.     2755.845886181926
standards in public life is the next main section of the report before the house.     2464.583762223711
with the introduction of a series of codes of conduct the current commission has begun to develop an explicit ethical framework.     17253.954573579613
this will be taken a step further with a proposal in june for an interinstitutional agreement on a committee on standards in public life.     2766.843920546746
that is in line with the draft resolution.     2554.381084030311
an important role for the committee will be to give advice on ethics and standards and to supervise common and separate codes of conduct for the institutions.     5272.204742502936
i welcome parliament's support for that.     4614.183555244175
we are also currently examining how we can most effectively implement the proposal for a classification system for documents, made in paragraph 50 of mr van hulten's report.     3932.751646516382
the draft resolution rightly recalls the commission's accountability to this parliament.     2789.4499027374086
president prodi and vice-president de palacio and other colleagues have demonstrated the commission's practical commitment to that.     2212.5555144419136
i hope that we will soon be able to agree a code of conduct on relations between our institutions which will include updated rules on access to documents.     8516.016577028895
the point was very properly raised by mr elles and referred to by mrs thors.     25045.79420610926
i am sure they are aware that in mid-december we officially received the draft framework agreement with parliament.     4647.3584128366265
we are now waiting for parliament to decide when it wishes to begin negotiations on the text.     5292.428635505519
we are very happy to proceed as rapidly as possible.     4249.117525559452
the report rightly emphasises the central importance that human resources policy must have in reform.     5101.638827524648
i am glad to say to mr van hulten and mr haarder that the detailed recommendations on recruitment, training, appraisal and the appointment to management positions are very much in line with our own thinking and our own proposals.     8165.320757371631
i also want to move towards a linear career system because the current category system is no longer adapted to the needs of our institutions.     5295.052728039636
it certainly blocks the advance and mobility of people with proven capabilities.     19219.37996473264
members, who, in the course of this debate, have properly praised commission officials, who in the great majority and typically are of high integrity, hard-working and of great capability, are absolutely right in the comments that they have made.     5252.891629592536
while the reform strategy white paper will clearly outline our ideas on these and other points, detail and precision are obviously essential.     11193.1294407223
a series of communications will therefore follow in the coming months.     10865.425734198274
each of them will be available for reflection and response by this house.     4702.198999545954
it is, meanwhile, very obvious that we will need to work closely with parliament, as an institution, on central matters of common interest, notably pay and pensions and the revision of the staff regulations.     4012.2083409875945
on this latter point we are reflecting on whether it might be useful to adopt a framework regulation which makes common provision on leading issues like pay, conditions, staff representation rights and so on, but enables the different institutions to operate implementing rules on other matters.     4303.547738044816
