decisions on asylum should take months - not years.     15097.075351132715
the second area is that of freedom: freedom to move and reside wherever one wishes in the union; freedom of information; freedom to vote for all who have european citizenship - and that includes not just member state citizens but third country nationals.     2880.429585703012
let our citizens know that our common policies in justice and home affairs are about freedom, not just about repression.     4204.6520646236295
the last area i want to mention is convergence of civil and criminal justice systems.     4736.682549876742
eurosceptics claim that this is a threat to sovereignty, the end of the nation-state, and so on.     8594.950217296471
but the case of the suspect wanted for questioning in connection with the murders of three women in france, including british student, isabel peake, who was thrown off a train, shows why we need mutual recognition.     21905.732890612326
arrested and released in madrid, he is now being extradited after arrest in lisbon.     8952.367450417583
whatever the eurosceptics say, it is in all our interests to get cooperation in such matters.     4208.797018258129
finally, as has already been said, it is essential, and i hope that the igc will cover this, to move to codecision with the european parliament in these areas, with democratic and judicial scrutiny.     2071.957361190924

the two identical-sounding questions which are under discussion are, of course, underpinned by a mantra of the kind which, in recent years, has increasingly transformed politics into a type of ideology - fine-sounding, but pernicious.     10224.763135737063
and our current eu mantra is afsj: area for freedom, security and justice.     6276.153792511165
behind this lies another ambition on the part of the commission and of the council and of the overwhelming majority of this house, namely to introduce such an area.     3157.113113817817
and who is not in favour of security, freedom and justice?     2513.8923007259264
the problem is just that this is not something the eu can introduce by means of legislative decrees and other supranational resolutions.     1839.2842574479369
freedom, security and justice are the root system of any society, reflecting its history, corporate experience and political development.     9250.196950529495
a system of this kind is not something the eu can introduce without society' s sustaining damage as a result.     5542.891673464181
but it is precisely here that we find the real rationale behind the mantra concerning security, freedom and justice.     5194.501558110297
the agenda is not designed to secure justice for the citizen.     6059.15563011647
that is already secured by means of the various national legal systems.     2035.1790868792334
the agenda is designed to transfer vital parts of society' s criminal law, policy on crime and administration of justice to the eu' s control.     16878.263065984574
it is concerned with increased integration which, as part of a duplicitous package, involves increased repression and control.     3459.0261963558787

charges brought against loyalists in 1999 totalled 193, while those against republicans totalled 97.
since january 2000, there have been six shootings by loyalists and two by republicans, loyalists have been involved in six serious assaults, one leading to another murder, while republicans have engaged in five serious assaults.     26844.45361064274
mr president, that situation cannot continue and needs remedying.     1604.9121515857018
mr president, the treaty of amsterdam has set an important objective for the union.     5933.062187884691
this is a task which all meps, the council and the commission must take on during this legislature: the creation, as has been said already, of an area of freedom, security and justice.     2507.467186169055
the tampere council, the motor and architect of this objective, has proposed certain goals, but five years, the time scale laid down for the implementation of chapter iv of the treaty, is too long to wait for the urgent solution which some problems require.     3647.740419987636
my first point is that parliament must not be excluded from the important decisions to be taken in this area and that its participation in the decision-making process must be guaranteed, especially with regard to this project which affects the citizens, as the commissioner has explained very well.     1971.8436191700343
my second point is that we must make rapid progress in the adoption of a common asylum system, adopting common procedural rules and, above all, putting an end to the current confusion between migration for political reasons and migration for economic reasons.     4287.927527385217
the recent laws on aliens adopted in my country, spain, and in belgium, are a reminder that there is an urgent need for the communitisation of immigration policy.     6420.482170213284
my third and final point relates to the external activities of the union in the field of immigration and asylum.     4809.746405792956
we must not and cannot give the impression that the union exclusively wishes to defend itself from an avalanche of refugees and economic migrants.     7948.765529191691
we must opt for a policy of cooperating in the development of our neighbouring countries in the east and the mediterranean area, but we must do so with rigour, with economic means and in close collaboration with public institutions, which must cooperate in the protection of those citizens whose most basic rights are violated or who wish to emigrate in order to meet their most essential needs.     4201.1167958210435
finally, in relation to the charter of fundamental rights, the citizens of europe need to have a vision of citizenship.     13864.536737241517
the euro, employment, even security, are not enough.     1631.8689853773926
they need a 'european soul' , as a distinguished spanish professor once said.     6362.083644439747
mr president, unlike some of the previous speakers, i want to go back to the words of our portuguese ministers and mr vitorino.     3986.1095824679865

as with all music it needs to be a fine tune and properly orchestrated.     3424.9629458179843

as described this afternoon it will go a long way towards drowning out some of the unpleasant tunes we heard during the european election and over the last few months.     3636.0040326331196

anyone who is english or british or follows cricket knows what a scoreboard is.     4921.102501958348

the first is that we need a mature dialogue between the council and the european parliament.     1589.3358659862479

two hundred years ago it was liberty, equality and fraternity, and things went along very well until the governments of the left raised equality above the others.     3985.7691073391816

we will examine questions to the council.     3858.3878376950242
question no 1 by (h-0780/99):
subject: construction of nuclear plant in earthquake-prone turkey the two recent earthquakes in turkey registering more than 7 on the richter scale raise tremendous doubts about turkey's persistence with the construction of costly nuclear reactors at akkuyu, while energy reserves produced by the atatürk dams are exported to third countries and the eu spends a slice of its budget on repairing the damage wrought by the earthquakes.     69278.04836606861
turkey's nuclear plans b which ignore the risks to the population and ecosystems of turkey and the surrounding region b prompt the suspicion that there are underlying plans previously determined by turkey's political and military leaders to acquire nuclear technology enabling them to build nuclear weapons, as evidenced by their attempts to buy reactors of canadian origin of the type acquired by india and pakistan.     28202.50902487008
what steps will the council take to prevent nuclear accidents and the proliferation of nuclear weapons in a country that wishes to join the eu but spends enormous amounts on nuclear programmes while receiving financial aid from the eu budget?     12292.740104430062
mr president, the council wishes to point out that turkey is a signatory to the convention on nuclear safety, the objectives of which should allay the fears expressed by mr souladakis.     5666.090546272427
the aim of this convention, which came into force on 24 october 1996, is precisely to achieve and maintain a high standard of nuclear safety at world level by means of national measures and through international cooperation.     3753.3699378354095
it further aims to ensure that nuclear installations are equipped with continuing protection against potential radiological risks, so as to protect individuals, society at large and the environment against the harmful effects of the ionising radiation that can be emitted by such installations.     8247.13262366428
the convention, as you will be aware, also covers the prevention of accidents which have radiological effects and the limitation of such effects when incidents of this kind occur.     6366.858820171448
i would also like to remind members that, as a candidate for membership of the european union, turkey will - and this is a pre-accession condition, which is an important point - sooner or later, as part of its own pre-accession strategy, have to adopt a set of policies geared towards adopting the entire acquis communautaire in good time, and including therefore all provisions already in force on nuclear safety.     3212.1710492577045
thank you for your answer.     6850.169677784485
however, i would just like to add the following.     3152.1442126462066
turkey is only looking to increase its energy potential by 2% with this unit.     3627.9754581778116
nevertheless, rumour has it that it plans to buy candu reactors of the type acquired by pakistan and india which were used for nuclear weapons.     28443.068370159042
in this respect, the issue requires closer examination, because the sensitive situation in the caucasus region could heighten people' s suspicions.     12712.321203879093
my second point concerns the safety of these plants.     4688.29071917028
in areas of high seismic risk, it is not enough just to have secure, earthquake-proof buildings.     12658.30061606033
speaking as an engineer, i can tell you that we carry out tests to investigate the effects of certain risk factors in extreme circumstances.     16068.11332115075
however, we cannot carry out tests on nuclear plants which are in operation; it is just not possible.     5718.941290457929
with this in mind, it goes without saying that nuclear plants a priori should not be built in areas prone to earthquakes.     15641.198443961002
in this respect, and because turkey is situated on the periphery of the european union, we should be helping it to become a more secure, peaceful and cooperative country.     5415.055040200893
that is our role and this is the point of my question.     1804.402439660376
i recognise the relevance of the arguments that mr souladakis has put forward.     15541.016053886167
this is, of course, a highly sensitive issue.     1973.2771208536994
furthermore, it will be evident that this is a matter that does not only affect turkey, in view of that country' s proximity to the existing territory of the european union.     4283.377810848218
it also affects other states neighbouring the eu with which we maintain relations.     2785.9728580276924
we understand your concerns and will certainly take account of them, particularly during the discussions that we will be having with turkey to define that country' s own pre-accession strategy agenda.     4443.809430977014
this question will naturally be one of our key concerns, and the european commission will not fail to take this into account.     2556.067811409018

i would like to say that transparency does not only apply from the community institutions "outwards" , it also applies between the institutions.     1855.778456165885
by this i mean that there are aspects of the form and procedure of the community institutions' workings which are not accessible to the other institutions, and not only as regards the commission' s relationship with parliament, but also as regards the commission' s relationship with the council.     4633.798338609185
as a matter of fact, at the last conference, this issue was raised and discussed on several occasions.     4798.104832322474
the european parliament will have the opportunity to take part in the preparatory group for the next intergovernmental conference.     3237.0136979813906
the matter is still open.     2387.680523915976

we have no doubts about this and we think that the members taking part in this preparatory group will have an opportunity to raise this issue again.     3379.48190836222
i believe that the commission' s proposal should make provision for greater transparency in terms of the way the various institutions function, but i think that there is always room for improvement in this matter.     6061.477438516744
for our part, we are prepared to study any proposals made in this area.     5301.022688618423
it is a very important question indeed which jonas sjöstedt and others have asked.     3361.8618100161952

we are very anxious indeed that the eu, too, should move in this direction, and this is also stated in the treaty of amsterdam.     3320.420543777133
one or two years ago, parliament adopted a report by mrs lööw which is extremely important in this context.     2177.676298486798
the report warned that the forthcoming process would lead to restrictions upon the transparency of the member states.     6985.959440350213
we can now see that there is perhaps some reason for this warning from parliament.     2420.5900776472977

i listened very carefully to what mr seixas da costa had to say on transparency and the intergovernmental conference.     7155.262923729724
my concern is, if i have understood mr seixas da costa correctly on the interesting points he made as regards transparency and the igc, whether the portuguese presidency will take on the commitment to tackle the issue of widening the agenda of the intergovernmental conference.     7839.124206456436
after all, examining the issue of transparency in the ways the institutions operate should never be hidden away under any old heading or confined to some lobby or other - though i know full well the importance of these lobbies.     5249.841840651016

mr dimitrakopoulos, transparency is naturally an issue of concern to the institutions.     7284.2243053638595
this intergovernmental conference will, at the outset, concentrate on improving the way the institutions operate, particularly taking account of the desire we all share for them specifically to become more democratic, more transparent and more efficient.     3629.485962663091
all of this must nevertheless be done whilst respecting a broad framework - that of the overall acceptability of any solutions we may devise to achieve these three aims.     4932.403188879125

the question is whether it is premature or not to start a new initiative on the subject of transparency.     2498.2702295875447

as far as we are concerned, they will be.     1706.5947207201862

question no 5 by (h-0785/99):
subject: proposal for a regulation laying down the total admissible catches for specific fish populations for the year 2000, and the case of anchovies in particular the latest reports from the international council for the exploration of the sea indicate that the current level of anchovy stocks in ices zone viii is critical.     4966.3206503379915
have the council of fisheries ministers and the commission assessed the impact of transferring the portuguese quota for ices zones xi and x and cecaf zone 34.1.1 to france (5000 metric tonnes per year overfishing) on the ices zone viii anchovy stocks and their responsibility for the current situation of the anchovy stocks and the possible social and economic impact on this fisheries sector in the future?     3915.3895658551933
does the council believe that it is permissible to consolidate future overfishing which violates the originally established tac of 33 000 metric tonnes per year, given the current evidence for the critical state of anchovy stocks?     14109.707397685504
what measures does the council intend to adopt, and when, with a view to taking proper account of the critical state of anchovy stocks, the aforesaid quota transfer and the principle of relative stability?     5051.90211209906
mr president, the portuguese presidency is extremely anxious to give a comprehensive answer to this question, particularly because it broaches an issue which concerns portugal in a positive way, and i shall explain how.     2947.6615510004117
the council is aware of the critical level of anchovy stocks in the bay of biscay to which mr ortuondo larrea refers.     9670.144659331689
nevertheless, at the last fisheries council of 1999, held on 16 and 17 december, the member states involved in this area of fishing, namely france, spain and portugal, considered that applying the precautionary principle which sets a tac reduced from 5 000 tonnes to 2 000 tonnes, as the commission proposed, was excessively cautious.     4349.19842145107
a compromise solution was therefore reached in order to achieve a balance between the need to reduce the biological risks, that is the species of fish being affected, and the socio-economic problems caused by fishing limits.     3291.779366692574
this was achieved by fixing the tac at an intermediate level of 16 000 tonnes instead of the 33 000 set in 1999.
it was also stipulated that this would be revised in the light of new scientific information on preserving species, of which we hope to see examples in the first six months of this year.     4728.699820199839
as for the southern stocks of anchovy in ices zone ix, the tac was fixed at 10 000 tonnes for the whole of 2000 as opposed to 13 000 tonnes in 1999.
fishing quota transfers between portugal and france were reduced proportionally from 5 008 tonnes in 1999 to 3 000 tonnes in 2000, for fishing in french waters.     6555.422954249698
i would like to state that this transfer will not increase the pressure of fishing on anchovy stocks overall, over the whole community fishing area.     2535.921913021308
in accordance with the principle of relative stability, 90% of stocks of anchovy in the bay of biscay are allocated to spain and only 10% to france.     7632.117887676273
without this transfer from portuguese waters, the level of the bay of biscay tac would have to be multiplied by ten in order to grant adequate catches to france.     12970.550505712716
that is why i think that this is quite positive from portugal' s point of view.     5083.383863120673
this situation would obviously be even more damaging to the level of stocks than the risk referred to by mr ortuondo larrea, which we also understand.     3868.657574896346
mr president-in-office of the council, i must say that your comments have not satisfied me at all.     2939.9348342021954
i understand that portugal is affected by this.     2422.3721078811263
however, it seems to go against all reason and intelligence that 80% of the anchovies, which, until 1995, were being fished in the portuguese fishing grounds, through an agreement between that member state - portugal - and france, should now be fished in the bay of biscay and that the council of ministers wishes to maintain that this has no effect on the anchovy stocks in the bay of biscay or the cantabrian sea.     4223.559898018332
it has been shown, as we have been claiming since 1995, that this move is appalling, and, if portugal and france wish to reach an agreement, that is fine by us, but these anchovies should be fished in portuguese fishing grounds and not in the bay of biscay.     5184.464723198418
today, scientific studies show that the anchovy population in the bay of biscay is in danger of extinction, and now there will be measures to restrict the amount of anchovy which can be fished in that bay.     4746.9299172630335
i do not know if you are aware, representatives of the council of ministers, of your responsibility over these last few years, and in 2000 as well, for the plight of the families who live on anchovy fishing in the north of the iberian peninsular.     1998.0613361921403
i would like to say, mr ortuondo larrea, that in its judgement of 5 october 1999, the european court of justice ruled that this transfer, which was approved, was in accordance with the principles laid down in council regulation 37/60/92 and, specifically, with its article 9(1), which stipulates that member states may exchange all or part of the fishing rights that they have been allocated.     6567.9672928457485
the court also stated that the principle of relative stability had not been violated since the anchovy quota allocated to spain in zone viii had been maintained at 90% and france' s at 10%.     5228.599688836136
apart from this, mr ortuondo larrea, the court is of the opinion that the transfer effected between portugal and france does not violate the principle of exploiting living sea and freshwater resources in a rational and responsible way, on the grounds that the fishing pressure in zones viii and ix neither increases nor adversely affects the overall quota of these resources allocated to spain.     9962.005436316285
accordingly, mr ortuondo larrea, the council reiterates its opinion that without this transfer, greater account would be taken of france' s possibilities for anchovy fishing in the bay of biscay.     17998.849005269803
the council therefore reiterates its opinion that fishing pressure would be greater and more damaging to population numbers than the solution which was eventually chosen.     4380.749176651005
in concrete terms, 3 000 tonnes represent 57.5% of portugal' s fishing opportunities in 2000 against 5 008 tonnes or 73.9% in 1999.
these numbers represent, in our opinion and in the opinion of the council, a real improvement in terms of conservation in comparison with the level of 80% laid down in council regulation 685/95.     5153.535945206575

your question switched to the subject of arms, a broader policy issue which i do not think falls within the scope of this question but which the council will, of course, address in the future, if it sees fit and if it decides to put it in a specific context.     3363.009026761154
is the council aware of the social problems caused by the export of food by the eu to developing countries at prices below the cost of production?     3366.0366691030545
does the presidency intend to do anything about this?     9155.758364180901

mr marinos, we are not adopting a purely economic approach to managing the portuguese presidency, and therefore, the cultural dimension is, i am sure you will appreciate, one of our main concerns.     4401.308386734906

we also intend to initiate a new debate, a more innovative one, on the issue of europe' s film heritage, given that this must be one of the most important factors in the development of a european cultural identity and of a european audiovisual economy.     3583.0534519530306
the presidency will also concentrate on discussions about creating a digital television system in europe, which will be the subject of a conference that we shall be organising during the portuguese presidency, in conjunction with the commission, in february 2000.
but, mr marinos, in the eu context, culture is an issue which is repeatedly raised in connection with igc agendas.     6829.026545902196

mr president, i join others in wishing portugal every good wish for a successful presidency.     7504.210643426509
i notice that you said this particular issue was not currently on your agenda for the next igc but if some people wished it, you would consider it.     3383.0346427091167

capital tax
the next item is the joint debate on:
the oral question (b5-0004/2000) by mr désir and others, to the council, on the council position on the idea of a capital tax
the oral question (b5-0005/2000) by mr désir and others, to the commission, on the commission position on the idea of a capital tax.     4935.670665544155
madam president, mr president-in-office of the council, commissioner, during the asian crisis in 1997, as with those which hit mexico in 1995 or the ems in 1993, everyone could see the damage caused to the economies of entire countries by financial speculation, particularly currency speculation.     4329.712425912107
between eur 1 500 and 2 000 billion are traded every day on the financial markets.     8247.99573508422
in three or four days, this can equal the total figure of annual global production, or global gdp, of approximately eur 6 000 billion.     5166.964014062072
the bulk of these financial transactions are purely speculative and do not involve any trade in goods or investments.     8969.023825207109
yet the erratic movement of these massive sums of capital can, in a few hours, cause the collapse of a currency or the economy of a country, plunging its whole population into recession.     4777.684486500849
in view of this situation, renewed interest has been expressed in the proposal by james tobin, winner of the nobel prize for economics, to create a tax on currency transactions.     9042.391697104256
the rate of this would be very low so that it did not affect trade in goods or investments, but it would act as a light brake on the wheels of speculation, thereby checking the multiplication of short-term transactions.     8291.289207146945
note that this proposed tax, which seems to be producing strong emotions in some people, would be the lowest tax in the world and the lowest in the history of global taxation.     4165.238037518001
yet it would represent the recapture of the areas seized from democracy by the financial sphere.     19548.15727382258
one reason for the acceptance of this proposal, and of the campaigns by ngos such as attac, solidar or others which have popularised this proposal, is that it would release resources which could be allocated to development programmes in the poorest countries, in areas such as education or health.     3623.6582486844836
it could act as a kind of asset redistribution in a world where there is both increasing wealth and increasing poverty.     5977.6741032307045
many personalities and institutions have come out in favour of this tax such as the brazilian president, fernando cardoso, the finnish government, the canadian parliament last march and also lionel jospin in 1995.
this question has been debated in several national parliaments of the eu.     3205.031574645766
the common response has been that this tax could not be applied solely at national level and that the right platform for tackling this is the european union.     2094.9857532619735
this is the reason for the oral question to the council and commission which we have tabled with 37 other members.     1065.720688291682
i am delighted with the compromise motion for a resolution which we have reached with the gue/ngl, greens/efa, eldr and pse groups.     12067.10064386802
this motion asks the commission to present a report to parliament within six months on the feasibility of this tax.     5300.366394592444
it also asks for an examination of the pressures and financial sanctions which could be applied against countries which encourage tax evasion or which maintain tax havens.     6270.806635688915
one of the most common objections is that such a tax would be too complex, but this could be said of any tax.     1859.3495544391958
if this argument had been accepted in the past, then no taxes would ever have been created.     1631.375004734427
the motion also asks that a position document, drawn up by the commission and council, should be submitted before the next annual meeting of the imf.     2587.4815494315553
the european union must take the initiative and must also, as demanded in the motion, propose this initiative to the g7 because it is primarily a political question.     2495.273593584857
if the union takes the initiative, this will have a ratchet effect because this debate is occurring all over the world, including in the usa and japan.     2289.6592947384124
a desire is being expressed everywhere with increasing force that the world should not be ruled by traders but by the people, their parliaments, their governments and their democratic institutions.     5555.433379132152
europe must play a role in this new international regulation and i am extremely happy that today, with this debate and this motion, this question is now on the agenda of european construction.     2428.766784180169
madam president, i do not have a great deal to add on this subject.     6401.078318775083
the council has not yet discussed the possibility of introducing a tax on movements of capital as professor tobin suggested.     4858.459434372716
this is a creative initiative, which we know has been welcomed by different industries across the world and by various sides in european politics, but the commission has not yet put forward any proposal or initiative on this issue.     3200.21845584566
as it falls to the commission to propose any such initiative, the council has not yet been able to adopt a position on this issue.     3323.3167441810447
madam president, the revenue basis of the tobin tax is said to be made up of very short-term exchange transactions.     6091.21286793451
such transactions, according to the argument, bear very little relation to fundamental economic variables.     22487.613719228884
this has also just been underlined by mr désir.     3992.440551828131
therefore, according to proponents, such as mr désir, an internationally applied tobin tax would, in principle, reduce speculative transactions and, hence, the volatility of exchange rates, which would lead to an improvement in economic prosperity.     15210.313591821481
taking into account the considerable volume of short-term financial streams, even a low nominal tobin tax would yield respectable sums of tax revenue.     29971.24124757742
in reality, the motive underlying the increased interest in the tobin tax and other sources of international finance in the mid-nineties was non-economic.     6525.018414368364
indeed, it was fuelled by the potential of this tax to generate income for international, public programmes at a time when demand for such funds was rising quickly and funding was increasingly harder to come by.     3345.3275426430487
the commission recently observed renewed interest in a global tax, such as the tobin tax, as a means of achieving socially responsible globalisation, so to speak.     5467.069581611031
mr désir too has mentioned this growing interest on all sides.     6388.215004505518
but if the tobin tax were to be applied unilaterally in order to prevent attacks on a specific currency, then this tax could not be effective and it could harm the internal financial market.     4190.132663254828
put more strongly, in the long term it is possible that for large numbers of funds, the tobin tax will be evaded by shifting foreign exchange rate transactions to off-shore tax havens.     7159.266606540423
in that case, the tax could lead to a net loss of total economic prosperity.     3921.4619484456584
so the tobin tax can only be effective if there is a sufficient number of industrialised countries which are prepared to participate.     7897.314933985552
finally, even if the tobin tax is designed to curb speculations with currencies, there is still the risk that even non-speculative streams would be affected, and that is not the intention.     6009.341566782871
for the reasons set out above, i cannot see a good reason for introducing a european tobin tax.     4183.378844754586
at any rate, the commission is against any attempt to restrict capital movement within the european union.     5840.914115912654
in more direct terms, to the extent that this measure could be deemed an indirect restriction on capital streams, this would contravene the treaty of rome.     21179.122093078182
the correct speculative approach, it seems to me, is to remove the real causes which lead to financial chaos, rather than attempting to suppress its symptoms by introducing obstacles to the operation of the markets.     9059.478955003335
madam president, commissioner, i should like once again to say a few words on the modus operandi and the ideological tactics behind this oral question.     8608.1278198637
also because i know how many social democrats in this house share our criticism, and not just the economic and financial policy experts.     2689.4823549643643
we know what the problem is: instability on the markets as the result of speculation.     4869.122465287399
but the people asking the question need to know that not every instance of instability, as the commissioner has just so impressively told us, is the result of speculation.     3559.4672969890817
the tobin tax would not have been able to prevent the crises in the eea in 1992 and 1993 or the crises in the south-east asian currencies in 1997.
the initiators of this question do not propose a solution; instead they are calling for a new burden, they are calling for more bureaucracy, for a measure which runs counter to the market mechanisms and they are taking up plenary' s time with an intergroup opinion which excludes the competent parliamentary committee, contrary to several resolutions by parliament.     3401.56892061457
in my view, and i say this quite clearly, the question is ideologically motivated rather than solution-focused.     3156.329546913947
i consider this to be the wrong way forward for several reasons.     3719.818884217424
why do we reject the tobin tax and consider it so questionable?     7817.2585775096995
first, and the commissioner has also confirmed this, because it places a heavy burden on the capital markets.     4322.221743274627
even a small tax would have serious repercussions on the capital markets, because this sort of speculation tax would reduce the profitability of investments considerably.     6867.447146891726
in addition, this sort of measure would also seriously hamper the development of the financial markets and would stand in the way of the principle of the free movement of capital, which is one of the central tenets of the internal market.     4349.113967722709
secondly, speculative capital is extremely difficult to identify.     11064.242428737769
thirdly, unless they are introduced in all countries at the same time, tax measures are easy to circumvent because there are otherwise too many loopholes.     4702.717500886357
fourthly, technically speaking, this measure is highly impracticable and will result in a great deal of bureaucracy and administration and be impossible to control.     6527.463874563819
the measure which we should be taking is part of the resolution before us here today.     1516.7207129024277
we need stronger banking supervision.     66706.89590099001
we need to clarify the liability for transactions.     29685.820279207037
we need a framework of regulations at international level.     1545.9529489824047
we need to examine national laws in order to check that they comply with the rules of the basle committee and how the dissemination of these rules can be stepped up.     9644.590553894799
i have a serious problem with one of the central points of this resolution which we cannot accept as it stands.     2348.8276742271805
i refer to point 9.
having listened to the commissioner just now, we know that the commission' s knowledge of the facts will result in a 'no' to the tobin tax.     4821.049916584965
if the knowledge of the facts and this debate and the arguments are as we say, and i think that they are right, then to take up another six months of the commission' s time and occasion uncertainty on the financial and capital markets would be irresponsible.     3957.306614429879
the capital markets do not want uncertainty from us, they want clarity; which is why we reject point 9 and call for a split vote.     6562.480391611666
madam president, the debate on a tax on short-term financial actions is not a new one.     2660.4896330942543
we have been discussing the tobin tax proposal for twenty years and yet the question is still topical because we need really concrete and honest answers based on careful studies.     3950.4888875007587
we need to know just how desirable and feasible a tax on short-term transactions really is.     5096.545903686275
this question has now entered a different dimension, a dimension in which usd 1 500 billion roam the world twenty-four hours a day looking for attractive investments, no more than 3% of which have anything to do with the real economy.     3724.7229226847116
we are concerned about stability on the financial markets, not for the sake of the financial markets, but because this has to do with own growth, our own investments and our own jobs in the european union.     1827.0038928931128
we must ask what instruments we can use in order to create the order here which has been missing in the past.     4417.48017496369
we have done everything to help liberalise financial transactions, but so far we have had no working set of rules, as we have learned from the bitter lessons of the repercussions of the asian and latin american crises.     6405.711712830609
it is therefore high time that we thought both about supervisory regulations and about transparency on the financial markets and their information.     1763.7731000753759
it is important that we know who the actors are and what their credit rating is.     4486.537274391967
we must also ask if there is not a need for fiscal justice.     1718.6938394581794
at a time when the tax burden on the work factor is increasing throughout europe and internationally, we must consider how we can achieve efficient taxation of investment income and that includes the question of how to tax short-term financial transactions.     3855.526644461251
i think that we really do need a careful report and conclusions from the commission, so that we as the european parliament can see if we need to ensure that the european union achieves real international solidarity with the usa and japan in making the economy and the financial markets more stable and secure.     1924.5427505759055
this is the intention behind the question and we must then follow the ordinary procedures of reports from committees and plenary debate in order to reach a balanced and really forward-looking proposal.     5413.16160587597
madam president, on behalf of the group of the european liberal, democrat and reform party, i must express my surprise at this debate on the international movement of capital and its possible taxation which, although it does not mention it in the text, appears to be an attempt to revive, as the introduction has done, the issue of the tax proposed some years ago by mr tobin, which our group opposed, clearly and totally, during the last legislature, with a series of arguments clearly and coherently expressed by our president, mr cox.     9110.644990396839
i will have to repeat these arguments, modestly, in this intervention, because the same issue has arisen once again.     4651.641384679043
we do not agree with this type of tax because its application, in the event that it could happen, would not achieve the aims which mr tobin presented at the time and which the left in this house wishes to revive.     4906.662868714495
firstly, there is no evidence that the tax on international capital movements could reduce the dangers of volatility and instability in the international markets.     8398.50259063435
on the contrary, since it is certain that not all countries would accept it, we would open the way to speculation and fraud and that really would create volatility, instability, insecurity, opacity and unfair competition in the field of financial services operating on an international level.     5204.817530757597
furthermore, the idea of obtaining resources to apply them to the more economically needy countries presents such complexity, when we consider their application in practice, that it appears simply impossible.     7362.027555542586
this is a false debate whose aim is purely political, and has no basis or technical viability.     5268.096864365844
furthermore - since we are in this european parliament - if we have still not managed the slightest agreement with regard to the package of fiscal measures proposed by the commission within the european union, how can we expect to achieve a fiscal agreement on an international level?     2768.7531945997616
i suppose that the first thing they say will be, 'let us see whether you are capable of reaching a fiscal agreement within the union' .     8737.772337695795
however, the liberal group has not wanted to close the door to a new analysis of this issue and, therefore, we have accepted a joint motion for a resolution proposing that a study be carried out on the possibility and suitability of imposing this tax on certain areas of international capital movements.     2740.28823682667
i am sure that this study - if carried out objectively, with knowledge of the financial markets and their mechanisms on an international level - will show that it is neither suitable nor possible, not only in terms of its application, but also as a means of achieving the proposed objectives.     3522.8967023828163
in itself, globalisation is positive.     4204.716088210216
the economy has never grown so much nor favoured so many countries on a global level.     2490.711361189354
this is due to progressive opening up and greater freedom in the international trade in goods and services and also a greater capacity for adequate movement of capital.     2353.678435786263
any analysis of economic development in recent years, if objective, will show, through the figures, that they have no political colour or orientation.     1888.0636990619512
as i have said, we are not opposed to such a study.     1403.998931781404
we will support the motion for a resolution in support of this study, but we simply insist that, as this motion for a resolution points out, it is by means of liberalisation and opening up at an international level that we will achieve economic progress, both on the level of states and on a general level.     2670.015016111023
madam president, commissioner, the problem does not lie in the ideology of the debate but in its political nature.     4943.61425548957
as mrs randzio-plath said, we must remember that the reason why certain members want to revive this question is because of the recent international financial crises and their economic, social and environmental consequences which have been felt by all in some countries.     3614.6141589710546
the main objective of this debate is to look at the instruments available to the international community for stabilising the international financial and monetary system.     2295.149454342153
in this respect, mr bolkestein, the motion for a resolution cannot be separated from the issue of the tobin tax.     6051.456614250773
if you look carefully, this proposed tax on capital is just one of several instruments aimed at forcing international investors to behave responsibly on the financial markets.     6954.60860367592
secondly, the motion does not say that we want a solely european initiative.     2649.8936429666965
this is why we explicitly refer to the autumn session of the international monetary fund so that the european union, in other words the commission and also the council, can decide how it will defend the opportunity to stabilise the international financial and monetary system and introduce international taxation.     2828.306107160863
there should be no confusion in this debate.     6124.289590646706
as environmentalists, we do not want an excuse for a motion.     11693.96593487045
although a study produced by the commission would doubtless be interesting, we do not need another to add to those already available.     2239.253379392881
we do need to obtain, from the commission and the council, in other words from each of the governments forming the union, a clear, positive or negative, political stance.     3080.583392349297
they must indicate whether they are ready to defend, within competent public bodies such as the international monetary fund in the first instance, the appropriateness of such a tax within the other instruments needed to stabilise the international monetary system.     5555.886337824048
as for the argument that this initiative would increase uncertainty, very short-term capital movements are specifically intended to play on uncertainty and to bet on changes between currencies or various short-term financial assets.     7894.806425695103
this argument is therefore not relevant.     2313.2559751488957
i will not go into the basic issue any further for everyone has their arguments.     3418.673489896864
however, i would highlight not only paragraph 9 of the motion, but also paragraph 10.
we clearly expect a political stance to be taken with a very firm deadline of the preparations for the autumn session of the international monetary fund.     3236.1824648130923
we therefore demand, if this motion is adopted tomorrow, that the commission and council appear before parliament to tell us what positions they will take on this occasion.     2285.7624863382953
madam president, my group is extremely delighted that this debate is taking place today, particularly as we have spared no effort along with members of other groups to bring this about.     3574.1579317782403
obviously we all have different approaches which is demonstrated by our debate and the motions for a resolution tabled at the beginning.     3682.932997093378
having said this, our group is pleased about the step forward which the compromise motion represents.     4979.697412153017
this invites the commission to present a report within six months.     5631.107957779718
the european parliament can play its role by taking the initiative, even if it does so timidly, on this subject.     2575.3686383747317
however, i agree with the previous speaker that, if paragraph 9 were challenged, this motion would clearly lose its substance.     11070.269070547401
this debate concerns a vital question which increasing numbers of people are legitimately raising.     10632.27106107875
what are the respective places of people and finance in today' s economy?     17827.788615622947
usd 1 800 billion is the volume of money moved every day in the world' s currency markets.     95982.03124105169
this represents over a quarter of the annual global volume of trade in goods and services.     8068.681092906249
by putting finance at the helm, this has reinforced the demands of profitability and toughened the operating conditions everywhere.     15382.07014573567
it has led to a growth in mergers, acquisitions and company restructuring and to gigantic sums being raised with increasing frequency on the financial markets.     6812.215577506751
unemployment and pressure on employees worldwide is increasing.     13231.103056767439
openings and real growth are being hampered and the sudden influx or withdrawal of speculative capital is threatening the economies of many countries, to the point of collapse, as in asia, russia and brazil.     15016.739687654626
faced with this neoliberal globalisation dominated by the financial markets, demand, as our motion recognises, is developing from a different idea of the world in which common law stipulates a duty of solidarity in an increasingly interdependent world.     9908.204879305487
no one can ignore the challenge to civilisation posed by the lack of nearly one billion jobs and the need to make unprecedented efforts in terms of development and access to information.     6941.889999766307
in this respect, all ideas deserve to be considered, including the tobin tax which is important, just like other forms of transaction which may be envisaged.     4944.474239873818
this tax could help to check speculation without penalising the activities of the real economy.     2692.3606336994767
it could release new resources for investment in people and, at a time when the undp estimates the sum needed to eradicate poverty to be usd 40 billion per year, it could allow access for everyone to drinking water and satisfy health needs.     6575.936611961018
when the debate on the ability of politics to influence economics goes global, the establishment of this tax could become one of the symbols of the political desire to recapture the democratic areas seized by the international financial operators.     9802.80916724215
the commissioner has said that a minimum number of industrialised countries would be needed to achieve this.     3417.0234936935994
i would point out that the european union is composed of a large number of industrialised countries and, like mr désir and several other speakers, i feel that the european union which we are forming can take the initiative in this respect.     4461.147239032318
in any case, the european parliament would be honoured to take up the call of the people to control the world in order to achieve a sense of well-being for everyone.     2857.137515304984
madam president, i too am delighted that we are having this debate.     4487.943092961743
i only regret that it has been so long in coming.     1434.4626011324592
we know that each generation has, over the course of time, blindly bought into what could be called a dominant ideology.     7805.287292216055
this is a set of prejudices accepted as such due to a general conformism, but whose dramatic nature can be seen by subsequent generations.     7618.6038382369825
free trade and the constant worship of globalisation are now the dominant ideology or, rather, the big mistake of our fin de siècle.     4099.275380354537
this ideology is as dangerous as the former ideology of the dictatorship of the proletariat in the corridors of the kremlin or, before that, the certainty, elevated to the status of a dogma, that the earth was flat.     9301.773179327833
our world is now dominated by a single logic or, i could say, a single god of money at work instead of people at work.     5177.236546136202
with a figure for trade in goods and services of frf 36 000 billion only being equal to the product of four days' speculation, the financial bubble owes very little to the productive activity of individuals.     3892.15280223545
it is self-perpetuating in a world of ever higher bids which constantly denies the humanity of man, the rich as much as the poor.     6878.331263640249
yet it is the latter who, at the end of the day, are excluded from the majority of the world' s decision-making centres for these are monopolised by a tiny minority referred to recently, and very aptly, by jean-pierre chevènement as the globalised élites.     16529.934063666147
it was natural that these élites would exclude any political concerns from their field of manoeuvre, thus protecting themselves from any democratic control and basically managing to be both illegitimate and irresponsible.     8115.97500629665
however, the people are fighting back, as demonstrated by their reaction during the incredible seattle summit.     6481.014250155358
the leaders of the world, headed unfortunately by the european commission, rushed forward submissively, hoping that their docility at the feet of the master of the world, mr clinton, would be enough to ensure them the scraps from the table.     2109.8037445556574
yet they reckoned without the salutary reaction of the nobodies, both individuals and states.     1377.7200596283992
this reaction was more organised than previously, due in france particularly to the magnificent attac network which is fighting with increasing support to limit free trade in general and to establish taxation of international financial transactions in particular.     4826.019242362533
the creation of this taxation, of either the tobin type or the more realistic type proposed by professor lauré, could prove to be politically opportune, at least due to its symbolism rather than its low rate.     7257.449342059551
this is because it would basically mean that politics was regaining a foothold in an area from which it has been excluded by operators whose profits are proportional to the degree of resignation of states.     7362.647846201072
madam president, although some of the aims of the tobin tax are fairly worthy of support, introducing the tax exclusively within the territory of the european union, as the first area of the world to do so, would sound the death knell for the european currency markets.     4397.124329638717
if the world' s other important currency trading areas were to be excluded from the tobin tax, the fact that tax was liable to be paid in europe would lead to currency dealing moving to those areas.     10642.523539723335
this has already been made clear a number times in this debate.     2971.2627736909267
it is not enough that all the world' s industrialised countries should be included in the scheme, as then currency trading would move to tax havens outside that group of countries.     7917.248256093157
there should therefore be a universal application: it is not enough that the g7 countries or the other industrialised countries are involved.     2550.8987944771548
on the other hand, it would be so difficult to bring the scheme into force everywhere in the world at precisely the same time - and personally speaking, i do not believe we can achieve this - that the tobin tax dream might as well be buried.     3968.6648512255974
furthermore, although there might be international consensus on the introduction of the scheme, the question would remain regarding how the tax revenue would be distributed.     5688.855585645692
the world' s currency dealing centres will hardly agree to the tax revenue being used, for example, for a variety of well-meaning, un-backed purposes throughout the world without a considerable amount of the revenue remaining with them.     14772.912280837765
with regard to the tobin tax as a means of preventing future currency crises, it has to be realised that the causes of a currency being overvalued cannot be averted through taxation.     6897.9802783928935
for that reason, as mr karas also said, we should give more attention to the policies that lead to a certain currency being overvalued in relation to its actual potential value.     8824.892058862897
this again would require measures to regulate the exportation and importation of capital; otherwise it would not be possible to focus on policies in this way.     2160.1821722355758
although it seems a reasonable scheme, the tobin tax is not without its problems, even if there should be agreement as to its implementation, which i do not believe there will.     3245.9142448866983
it has to be remembered that money at present is first and foremost information, and that the global economy will, in the future, rotate more and more on the basis of information and intellectual capital.     1848.052375451921
for that reason, the uninterrupted flow of assets in massive volumes between the world' s financial centres is not merely speculative activity, which has nothing to do with the real economy.     5189.3602044644995
if this 'feedback' mechanism, which gives users the opportunity to make choices, were hampered by measures such as the tobin tax, mistaken decisions might be made that turned out to be so costly in terms of the economic consequences that the tax would never be able to make up for it.     5642.722234886279
hence, as mr karas has stated to his credit, our group is resolutely opposed to the introduction of the tobin tax.     14189.445072002954
madam president, i speak as someone who is in support of a tax on speculative capital movements across currency borders.     13588.062167459364
it seems to me blindingly self-evident that some action is necessary.     1703.4774553513882
in the last 30 years, the volume of currency transactions has increased by 83 times: firstly, total global reserves of all central banks barely amounts to one day of foreign exchange trading; secondly, annual global trade in real goods and services equals just three-and-a-half days of foreign exchange markets out of 350, that is, 1% of capital movements are for trade in goods and services and 99% are for speculation.     4022.4884718238104
the size, unpredictability and irrationality of the global foreign exchange markets have made it harder and harder to manage national and regional economies.     5777.604957493235
computer-controlled selling programmes turned a random blip into an avalanche, burying jobs, lives and industries across the globe.     31374.13715873811
the only arguments against it are: firstly, it would impair the efficiency of the foreign exchange market - a little fanciful in light of the financial feeding frenzies we have seen that have destroyed jobs throughout the world, in east asia, europe and latin america.     3569.242978559004
mr chirac has described those who engage in this as the aids virus of the world economy.     2658.6233429752638
what is necessary is to throw some sand into the wheels of the currency speculators, stockbrokers and option traders.     35303.54750241407
of course, this cannot be done in one country; it cannot be done within the single currency area, but it could be done at international level with global cooperation.     2662.926159616951
europe, japan and the us - the euro, the yen and the dollar, would constitute, in my opinion, such a block.     5814.096703729748
i am disappointed in the epp.     40566.19358998849
i thought at least they read the resolutions they were going to vote on.     7816.78529323976
yes, i am in favour of such a tax, but it is not what we are going to vote on tomorrow.     3006.4753477519903
what we are going to vote on tomorrow is to ask the commission to study the matter, to look at what steps and what conditions would be necessary for the introduction of such a tax.     2705.3571988030753
equally i understand the commissioner failing to endorse such a tax, but i cannot understand why he failed to have the courage to welcome the opportunity to demonstrate the truth of his position by a study of the issue.     6991.95404967221
there is nothing so closed as a closed mind.     4151.420624501398
the tax has the support of 47% of the people in the uk, according to a recent survey by war on want.     5039.519382582852
in france it has had enormous resonance, particularly when linked to a demand to use the proceeds to help the third world out of its debt and poverty.     1122.8865281529818
this issue can no longer be ignored by parliament, the council or the commission.     3984.9592639383036
there is now a weight of public opinion demanding an answer more sophisticated than: "it has not been done before".     2939.7194676237505
in the new millennium, with new global problems requiring new global solutions, they need to give good arguments why it cannot be done now, rather than why it has not been done in the past.     3302.5140162406465
madam president, commissioner, a compromise resolution on the usefulness of a tax on international financial flows would constitute a first step towards the essential amendment of many international rules.     5484.852302372456
i have two or three points to make in this respect.     1740.4844244825554
one of the main demands of the demonstrators in seattle was, rightly, the establishment of this type of tax, and this is not by chance.     6186.854178468601
the proposal of this tax today sends out a strong signal from the rich european countries proving that they want to move away from the culture of egoism.     4312.088570272214
everyone feels that this kind of joint tax is a virtuous measure which does not prevent trade but is aimed primarily at speculation and its perverse effects.     5154.1394656032
the members on the right should note this.     4411.3261854597395
it symbolises the need for solidarity with the countries of the south, particularly those in africa, the caribbean and the pacific which questioned us extensively on this subject in seattle.     4204.7783438571605
in order to fully achieve its objective, the money supply released by such a tax must be redistributed among the poorest countries.     9142.330982003486
this is how we can give sense and morality to the commercial rules and show solidarity with the people of the south.     2409.2210384873993
madam president, we cannot just keep repeating the number which several previous speakers have quoted; we must also mention the fact, commissioner, that 80% of these huge, daily foreign exchange sales have an investment term of no more than eight days.     6341.9178946179845
we cannot maintain a situation in which investments and the creation of jobs are less lucrative than short-term sales of foreign currency and shares.     6355.3589009524185
we cannot maintain a situation in which we allow ourselves to destroy the opportunity to take political action.     4067.192626470495
we cannot maintain a situation in which income from non-economic activities increases more and more quickly.     2608.3890947649
it has been demonstrated in germany that income from monetary assets has almost doubled from 7.6% of overall economic income since tobin developed his idea.     8638.76322946123
we cannot maintain a situation in which the poorest countries of the world are most exposed to the speculative arguments surrounding short-term currency fluctuations.     12200.61455475137
nor do i believe that this is impracticable.     7655.1493043443215
why should it not be possible for the g7 countries, the other eu member states, china, singapore and switzerland to come to an agreement on the introduction of the tobin tax?     2953.22572095652
there must be an overriding economic interest.     5901.900602747044
why should it be impracticable, and it can be differentiated, to impose this tax on spot foreign exchange transactions, all the so-called cash transactions, currency futures and options?     12913.359435039834
why should it be impracticable to set this tax at a rate which does not jeopardise the long-term investments needed by the economy but which finally makes short-term and speculative investments unattractive.     4331.701124023148
numerous very difficult questions are bound to arise, but the only real problem which i can see so far is one of political will and the st. florian principle of shifting responsibility from one agency to another which has now taken hold.     2730.8364063832187
madam president, we are not asking for more bureaucracy, as mr karas put it just a few minutes ago.     4328.385220092425
we are looking for solutions.     4689.218987324026
of course capital markets need clarity - that is actually the idea.     5461.257586092394
the tobin tax will bring some transparency to a very obscure question.     6948.18441625339
"the more progress is made towards trade liberalisation, the more rules are needed", as commissioner lamy said during the millennium round in seattle.     2607.6145485286092
he recognised that the combined impact of the single market and the single currency that characterise the process of the european union's construction is intimately linked to a huge amount of new regulations.     4022.885375713859
now that financing is readily available across national boundaries without any difficulty the same approach will obviously be strictly necessary, all the more so as we consider that an enormous amount every single day is employed in speculative transactions.     4970.67386185988
we citizens of the world cannot afford the current rate of speculation.     8288.339302998611
to continue would be economic suicide.     843.4498573624797
honourable members, commissioner, it is often said that europe has succeeded in stabilising the economic sector, but this is only true if we confine ourselves purely to the public sector.     2677.101948762915
if we look at the private sector, however, we will find not only that this fabled 'financial stability' has been unsuccessful but that we are most probably sitting on a volcano ready to erupt at any moment.     1735.3072950662483
the debt burdens of the private sector and the corresponding excessive inflation of stock exchange securities, the two main market stability indicators, have, for some time, been at danger levels, as the commission report on the economic situation in europe clearly illustrates.     4296.715559270615
the fact that the public accepts such rates and does not just abandon all financial activities in a panic can be explained by overambitious expectations over future gains.     11162.372457873162
speculative activities are growing beyond measure and are, in turn, generating profits which will eventually prove to be merely fictitious.     7268.826783128056
over-optimism will give way to disappointment and fear, which, in turn, will result in a stock market and economic crisis.     4645.755044709477
the danger is too great for us to ignore.     10534.673328345161
we ought to face it regardless of how difficult it may seem.     7357.274385397844
modern economic science has provided an exceptional instrument with the tobin proposal and others, which we will be able to use if we have the political will to make a start.     3718.6465350549506
that is what we are lacking, commissioner: the political will to make a start in tackling speculation.     2445.4174019234533
no longer should there be a place in democracy for the worship of, or for this fatalist approach towards, market forces as adopted by the liberals - and i am sorry that mr gasòliba i böhm has left - as this will only lead to its ruin.     5188.818596731943
madam president, mr bolkestein, the ignorance surrounding this debate is unbelievable.     70903.86985018062
we needed a greek member to finally tell us what this tobin tax means.     12221.884084708448
you reject it flatly because you do not understand the first thing about it.     1546.745074534759
that the right opposes taxes is quite understandable, except that they should never have been allowed to spirit their millions, or rather billions, into switzerland.     11906.728819718299
we all know that!     708.8878893848399
but now i call on you, in all consciousness, to help put a stop to speculative financial transactions and undermining long-term projects with long-term capital.     4572.123629829849
it has never been done in south-east asia, it has never been done in europe, and i can understand why mr karas from austria has left the chamber, given austria' s financial record.     7899.268555167986
they are all tarred with the same dirty brush!     9610.991130534896
we need a tobin tax to give us a fiscal instrument with which we can intervene.     6885.868660562614
madam president, i am totally appalled by the commission' s, i.e.     13672.6848548006
mr bolkestein' s, political ignorance!     359878.558445925
he should perhaps use his position on the tobin tax to ask himself why he is here at all!     4264.55515270325
madam president, i too have found parts of the debate quite bizarre.     17456.391510824775
it is perhaps not by chance that the conservative benches have emptied because that which is represented as market freedom no longer has anything in common with the noble principles of the social market economy which has brought so much prosperity to us here in western europe over recent decades.     3773.2191152110536
if we continue down this path, and this is the ideology, and declare the financial markets to be sacrosanct and even claim that any intervention in the workings of these markets is contrary to progress and contrary to the growth of the economy, then we will quite certainly be on the wrong tack and that could take on quite dangerous proportions.     3777.531338768613
those who own the monetary assets are currently amassing huge fortunes which are again screaming out to be reinvested.     592.9450965574455
we have asset inflation of global proportions.     9467.824753366729
as a result, speculative bubbles continually arise on the financial markets, causing growing economic instability which, in turn, jeopardises the entire economy.     11602.232000618395
and it is precisely the medium-sized undertakings and large numbers of employees who suffer as a result.     5003.468634949349
we can take action against this; it is possible.     2941.7245921544977
the minimum requirements for this would be for a proper study to be carried out at long last, not only by the commission, but by reputable economic institutes throughout europe.     1871.6130488153046
that too is feasible.     1902.3022535188477
and then perhaps everyone will stop bandying about arguments which are asking for trouble!     2525.082201815454
it is not correct that the tobin tax is technically impracticable - it is quite feasible in the age of the computer.     7496.703690909646
the fact that tax havens are frequently nothing but hard disk storage space for large european banks here at home must not be ignored.     9582.129114043548
in short, we must not allow real capital and speculative capital to become so remote from each other, because otherwise we shall be steering towards situations which we experienced on this continent time and again during the last century and which had catastrophic results.     4126.350097254569
madam president, let us indeed tax money speculation: it does not produce anything new.     11263.475338319618
the tax would be targeted at the right place: at the speculators.     8953.042617062967
who is going to defend these speculators, when nurses and teachers are paying top rates of tax?     7953.189711841518
article 73 c of the treaty establishing the european union provides scope for taxation on transactions.     5968.54684749347
those who oppose taxation on currency dealing are in favour of those tax-recycling centres, the tax havens, of which there are 62 in all.     11537.6072196338
the union should make a tax pact, which any countries could initially be party to.     3309.6950033344638
the low rate tobin tax would not cause tax-recycling problems.     25848.747273312136
it is strange to claim that if everyone is not included it would be impossible to bring into effect.     5070.892533333382
the european union was founded through the efforts of just a few countries, and you see how we are today.     1967.610202464453
the truth is the greatest problem is a political one: they do not want to tax capital, but raise personal taxation instead.     3905.9471925681273
in the end tax would politicise the global, undemocratic system of financial power.     3067.75482877827
ladies and gentlemen, we must challenge the monopoly the world' s speculative elite have on planning the world' s economy by remaining outside the tax system.     36812.37413671714
madam president, may i begin by extending a heartfelt thanks to all speakers and, above all of course, mr désir, for the fact that this debate is taking place this evening.     7663.356462975925
whatever one may think about the topic of debate, it is evident that it is an important one.     2802.5231198671195
this is why it has been around for years, and it is satisfying that the commission is being afforded the opportunity this evening to give a response.     3016.094499324917
i would like to start by underlining two remarks made by the member of your parliament, mr gasòliba.     12965.79115189673
he said that globalisation is a positive phenomenon, and i share his view.     4580.9715125462235
i believe that globalisation has helped many countries which, so far, have been on the sideline of the world trade streams to participate in these and be incorporated into the world trade system and, as far as i am aware, no country has ever been worse off by participating in international trade.     3367.8025371180825
in other words, as indeed already remarked by mr gasòliba, globalisation is, in my view, positive, and i am of the opinion that it has contributed to a new international distribution of labour which has been pursued by many people for many years.     3489.4252428184723
a second remark made by mr gasòliba was that there is currently no evidence that the tobin tax will stabilise the exchange rates.     16133.880402629226
i think that he is correct in this regard.     3914.1231297763434
i too am not aware of any reason why a tax on exchange rate transactions would lead to a stabilisation of exchange rates.     9072.587655379799
i am of the opinion that these are two important remarks, and that is why i am reiterating them here.     3073.4288859638996
madam president, could i furthermore point out that none of the speakers this evening has drawn a link between the tobin tax on the one hand and the functioning of the european union on the other?     4993.833195106725
surely we are all familiar with the treaty of rome and know that one of the four freedoms is the freedom of capital movement.     6011.389015277948
madam president, i can assure your parliament that my day-to-day work consists partly of attempting, in conjunction with my offices and colleagues, to completely integrate the financial markets within europe, thus enabling financial transactions to run as easily and smoothly as possible.     14075.476721774117
in my capacity as commissioner for the internal market, i am, in other words, opposed to all measures which hamper international financial transactions.     5403.381189178904
you could hardly expect this commissioner to agree with a tax which would, in fact, impede international economic movement.     3900.1732786011
once again, the tobin tax is, in my view, at odds with the treaty of rome, and this is an important, theoretical objection which has not been considered by any of the speakers this evening.     9006.28256375091
madam president, apart from these - to my mind - quite fundamental observations, there are also practical objections to the tobin tax, as already underlined by mr karas and other speakers.     8327.736425015675
it has unfortunately been observed, i have to say, that the european union has failed to reach agreement internally on specific tax measures.     4182.844913088493
this indicates how difficult these dossiers are.     10444.787093568355
if we have not been able to reach agreement internally, i wonder whether we will then be able to convince the other trade partners.     8659.831601568761
i think that this will be problematic.     6640.756225685067
madam president, mr jonckheer has stated that this could be one of the measures for stabilising the financial markets.     6372.020525244496
i think, however, that instability is merely a symptom and that we should be looking at the underlying causes.     4800.2993203862925
i think that it is preferable to fight the causes rather than attempt to tackle the symptoms when this is probably not possible and when, as already stated, the measure concerned is pretty certainly at odds with the treaty of rome.     11321.040333269775
in order to remove these causes, one has to look at what is happening in the countries in question.     5783.820061493973
is it the case that these countries can boast of what is termed in english 'good governance' ?     14739.214567031828
is it the case that there is low inflation and that there are low interest rates?     2943.782996434298
that the markets for production factors, including the labour market, operate flexibly?     6373.446637084602
that is not always the case.     744.6034769465987
so if we want to look into what causes exchange rate instability, then we need to take into account the elements of good governance and focus on these first.     4464.02697543501
in addition - and this has also been mentioned already, for example by mr gasòliba i böhm, but also by others - there is a need for more supervision.     3383.5639302009154
more supervision nationally - supervision by national governments of national banks - and internationally, for example by means of the imf.     7998.87604904704
so i believe the best way to tackle the causes is by ensuring, on the one hand, that good governance is observed in the countries in question and that, on the other hand, there is more supervision nationally and internationally in order to avoid these lapses.     5045.323379362129
madam president, according to various members of your parliament, more funding should still be made available for major projects.     3396.8909192286956
for example, mr kreissl-dörfler spoke in english about 'long-term capital for long-term projects' - this is an obvious issue.     4122.197244794762
my reply to this is that, firstly, countries should honour their pledges, namely make 0.7% of their gross national product available for development aid and cooperation, so that a great deal more money becomes available than at present for such long-term projects and via a route established for the purpose, namely contributions to development cooperation, and not via an artificial and probably harmful route, namely the tobin tax.     4353.18924457519
madam president, i should like to leave it here.     10402.91379943746
as far as the resolution is concerned, of which i have seen a draft, the commission will await the vote and will, based on this, determine its stance.     2235.446041892218

resumption of the session
i declare resumed the session of the european parliament adjourned on friday, 21 january 2000.
madam president, allow me to remind you that tomorrow will be the second anniversary of the cermis tragedy.     4984.737500679496
two years ago, in cavalese in italy, an american aeroplane from the aviano nato base cut through the cables of a cable car during a low-flying exercise which exceeded safe limits, causing the deaths of more than 20 europeans.     3932.159941613956
since then, the victims' families, who have not had the consolation of justice, since the pilot responsible has not faced criminal charges, have been awaiting solicitous financial compensation from the united states at the very least.     2102.7263242945373
