recourse to the precautionary principle
the next item is the commission statement on recourse to the precautionary principle.     88494.3611042307
i want to start by saying that i am pleased to be presenting this communication on the use of the precautionary principle, and it has been written together with david byrne and erkki liikanen.     10188.03255124326
the precautionary principle is not a new concept.     4753.579891638915
it has been used by the community for some time now in a range of policy areas, environmental, human, animal and plant health; and it is explicitly mentioned in the environmental provisions of the ec treaty since maastricht.     2127.3024915013657
it also appears in a range of international texts, for example, the rio declaration and most recently the bio-safety protocol.     8745.642367603172
the essence of the precautionary principle is clear.     28165.625244954095
it involves taking action in a given policy area when the science is not clear, but where there are reasonable grounds for concern that the potential risks are big enough to require action.     3258.9154491691443
however, the use of the precautionary principle has been increasingly in the spotlight in recent years.     3651.6518388804893
events such as the bse and dioxin crisis have stimulated a growing public debate about the circumstances in which precautionary action is justified and necessary.     6093.413529571838
in view of this growing interest, the commission therefore considered that it would be useful to present a communication to set out its position on the use of the precautionary principle.     3812.675284142071
the basic aim of the communication is twofold.     4211.319751933089
to explain in a clear and coherent way how the commission applies, and intends to apply, the precautionary principle in its management of risk and to set out guidelines for its application based on reasoned and coherent principles.     4343.31968932504
we also hope that the communication will help to build a better common understanding of how to manage risks and to dispel fears that the precautionary principle might be used in an arbitrary way or as a disguised form of trade protectionism.     4509.043190465777
the commission' s starting point in applying the precautionary principle is the need to ensure a high level of protection in the fields of environmental, human, animal and plant health.     8604.257560323074
of course, this objective cannot be used to justify irrational or arbitrary measures, but it does mean that action can still be taken even in situations where the science is unclear.     5980.1035600038995
as such, while the precautionary principle does not involve the politicisation of science, as some have alleged, it does take us to the crossroads between science and politics.     8153.135399332719
the initial decision to apply the precautionary principle depends largely on the level of protection sought and the level of risk that decision-makers are prepared to accept for society.     6080.413389498815
it is therefore political by nature.     1261.8738997360917
however, the measures that may subsequently be taken must obviously comply with the general principles applicable to risk management and the guidelines for applying the precautionary principle are therefore the key element of the paper.     2807.208221788101
measures taken must be proportional to the chosen level of protection - that is, we do not use the sledgehammer to crack a nut.     13449.107342280466
they must be non-discriminatory in their application, that is measures should not differentiate on the basis of geographical origin.     9665.638727403806
and they must be consistent with similar measures already taken.     1665.668091751509
for example, if one product has been approved, similar products should also be approved.     2034.1115834953305
measures are based on an examination of the potential benefits and costs of action or lack of action; that is, the measures should be cost-effective and subject to review in the light of new scientific data and be clear about who is responsible for producing the scientific evidence necessary for a more comprehensive risk assessment, that is the burden of proof.     2589.0267428850425
all these elements have to apply cumulatively.     1107.9098675353696
it is also important to bear in mind that there are a wide range of measures, which can be taken in applying the precautionary principle.     3303.9833207520946
for example, a research programme, public information campaigns, recommendations and so on.     3831.935598338103
applying the precautionary principle does not, therefore, automatically imply a ban.     11697.648293383168
this communication does not pretend to be the final word on this issue; however, it is the first time that the commission has presented a structured presentation of the principle and its operational use.     3301.6989614112426
by setting out in some detail how the commission applies, and intends to apply, the precautionary principle, we hope to be able to clarify the situation at community level and to contribute to the ongoing debate at european and international level.     5841.71397858964
mr president, commissioner, thank you very much for your statement.     672.5811565478152
i have three short questions, the first being as follows.     2077.5029390658688
we have been awaiting a common communication from the commission on the precautionary principle for a very long time now and it was anything but clear in the past as to whether the commission speaks with one voice.     1705.7608952452217
does the document you have presented here today also accord with the opinion of your colleagues the commissioners for industry, foreign trade, competition and the internal market?     2556.0599197536553
i am sure that your answer to this will be "yes" but i would be grateful if you could tell me if these gentlemen are also aware as to what the consequences of this will be?     2535.686852957487
my next question, mrs wallström, is when will you begin to apply this principle to legislation; to legislation pertaining to chemicals or other sectors, for example?     7437.341251796323
lastly, will we be able to recognise when this principle has been applied in legislation?     5743.843761528248
in other words, will each proposal contain an additional sheet stating that checks have been made as to the application of the precautionary principle and the following conclusion has been reached?     2955.5776691179663
.     1000.0
thank you very much, dagmar roth-behrendt, for these questions.     2720.140715928734
of course, this is shared by the whole commission.     3256.6524268153225
this is a common communication which was written by the three of us, david byrne, erkki liikanen and myself, but it has been passed unanimously in the commission today.     4787.920630428744
it received strong support from the rest of the commissioners and it has been through an interservice consultation in the whole of the commission.     2077.880000417834
so we have really worked through this document and i am sure that they are all able to describe the guidelines and the principles as laid down in this document.     4557.079203641019
yes, i would say we already use this way of working with different, difficult issues like a new chemical strategy for example.     3344.4702946288307
and, i am sure that we will also mention it explicitly when we work with this principle.     2286.9216455746787
we have just recently had a case of using the precautionary principle when it came to ban phthalates in soft pvc toys; and of course, we apply it in several different environmental areas.     4371.79210211274
it has been mainly used in the environmental field, but, of course also when it comes to human health.     1688.6391877223416
so, we will try to be very clear about how and when to use it.     1500.456045772843
mr president, commissioner, regarding the relationship between science, on the one hand, and applying the precautionary principle on the other, the communication states that invoking that precautionary principle always requires a political decision if there is a lack of scientific evidence.     2637.9650731512993
needless to say, this accords science a great deal of power.     67827.5816944911
we might ask ourselves, what kind of scientists will supply this documentation and what do they stand for?     6168.208860614967
the communication also states that the precautionary principle can be applied, provided there is a sufficient recognised minority of scientists.     4661.297595422809
i would like to ask, what is, then, the definition of a recognised minority?     2779.4278853475844
how would you describe this?     12184.415143004602
what establishes a recognised minority?     15781.825485912701
if a political decision is involved, i would also like to ask: who takes this political decision?     1810.992308803104
the commission?     927.2863633395799
the council?     492.04252703772124
and to what extent will the european parliament be able to play a role in this?     3129.4718688315843
what is the role of the scientific committees?     2865.340468773589
what, also, will be the role of the food authorities which you are about to set up?     1265.1303556915052
finally, will this whole decision-making process be carried out in a transparent, public way?     2219.2080910609884
these are my three questions.     2142.3692172394403
.     1000.0
yes, mr president, that is true.     3071.2186745370045
there were many and difficult issues and not always crystal clear, but i will try to answer some questions.     5260.646382822592
who will take the decision, who are the decision-makers?     1839.4827463673507
well, that depends on who the legislators are.     2554.6002661004027
this is part of risk management.     2125.3141771790774
they have to look at, for example, the concerns of people in relation to a special issue, and they have to judge from what is known about the scientific evidence in a certain case.     3953.311131479613
however, it is true that it does not need a big majority of the scientific community to be able to use the precautionary principle.     3462.7380782516166
it can be used on evidence from a minority or where science is incomplete.     8699.316802530888
that is, of course, where you strike a balance between this principle as a political tool and science.     5612.425443013411
it is not always easy to describe exactly how this process is done, but there is no question of changing the scientific base.     4757.694932200622
we use the experts to get as much scientific information and facts as possible before we take a decision; and that should also be done in the future.     1531.0834215505572
you have to see this as a tool for risk management.     2114.0069625557167
you have to decide whether you want to put people in danger, for example, or if you want to protect the environment; and you have to look at the science that is available.     3287.178332268349
you have to assess all that and evaluate the science.     10757.088699361628
then you decide to take a measure or not, to take action or not.     1809.3973219196616
so, there is no answer to all your questions but this does not change the system of the scientists that we use today or the system of experts that we use today.     1713.4956503710932
mr president, i have a question concerning an actual, concrete case in which it should be possible to apply the precautionary principle.     3357.8690457545754
it is about brominated flame retardants.     170407.8022588013
it is a fact that these substances are now being discovered.     4617.879299602675
there are increased concentrations of them in both people and the environment.     2385.5667619232263
there are many people who maintain that they entail major risks, while others question how large the risks involved in these substances really are.     5187.08262832179
recently, both sweden and denmark urged the commission in the council of ministers to take the initiative and ban brominated flame retardants.     13155.83615045017
i am wondering, therefore, if you are preparing such a ban and if it would not fit in very well with your approach to the precautionary principle itself to come up with such an initiative.     3153.5098111762773
.     1000.0
(sv) mr president, thank you very much, mr sjöstedt, for this question.     6667.56998922551
the issue of brominated flame retardants is an important one.     26873.783823028687
it was raised quite recently in the environment council by a number of ministers who want the commission to look at what basis there might be for a ban and at what might need to be done.     3246.012449524809
we are in the process of looking into this question and assessing what knowledge we have today and what it is possible to do.     2299.4624192876613
allow me to remind you, however, that use of the precautionary principle does not need to be synonymous with there being a total ban.     4775.997266851043
there may be a series of different measures which can be taken.     5930.177046767697
it ought not, therefore, to be immediately interpreted as a ban.     4106.950252205548
where brominated flame retardants are concerned, there may eventually be a ban, but it is important to say that the precautionary principle permits the whole spectrum of political measures and initiatives to be taken.     8571.836365358877
this issue is extremely topical.     4056.8363035090174
the commission is to do its job and give proper attention to the issue before we come back with an assessment of what needs to be done.     2122.0058461975195
you said that the precautionary principle should not be used as a disguised trade barrier.     4984.324097090473
is the understanding of this point the same on both sides of the atlantic ocean?     4839.004798719993
i think we are going to have problems particularly with hormone-treated beef and genetically modified crops.     21758.664609128227

.     1000.0
(sv) mr president, i am pleased to be able to tell you that, in montreal, we have just signed a protocol concerning bio-safety.     23547.72046881897
we succeeded there in agreeing, at an international forum, as to the definition of the precautionary principle.     11214.075643093123
i consider it to be a breakthrough that we succeeded in concluding this protocol.     9626.348943989196
i believe it will set the trend for future discussions concerning the precautionary principle.     9808.046347900654
we are going to be able to use this as an example of how the precautionary principle is to be interpreted.     7512.180930090744
it is, moreover, also accepted as an important and effective tool.     2508.273320452419
mr president, i have two questions.     1164.1476337401914
i shall try to be very brief indeed.     5678.7869433035685
the first question concerns the implementation of the precautionary principle.     7867.530645378791
as i have understood it, there has been a certain lack of clarity in some respects as to how this is to be understood.     4066.904168870167
is it the case that a risk assessment is firstly to be carried out, including a cost-benefit analysis?     2492.3847575529285
in that case, i am rather concerned, because the idea was really that a cost-benefit analysis should not be used as a tool for deciding whether the precautionary principle should be introduced.     3660.4135888538094
instead, the precautionary principle should come first.     16559.413917981998
my second question concerns the burden of proof.     8406.972757176927
i remember margot wallström being introduced as commissioner in the committee.     18329.80757814358
at that time, she was talking about the desire for a reversed burden of proof.     10442.86477910608
in other words, a product' s manufacturer is to demonstrate whether or not the product is hazardous.     14091.15589691023
i want to know whether this also applies in the document which the commission has presented now.     2381.252256780392
.     1000.0
(sv) thank you, mrs schörling.     29519.144203983782
these are two important questions, and it is good that i should be given the opportunity to clarify them.     2710.2127433869264
no, one must not begin by carrying out a cost-benefit analysis.     3848.4525548276224
instead, the latter should be based upon an assessment of what it is we know, of what science has to tell us and of how we should view the product concerned in the light of the risks we judge there to exist for the environment or for the health of human beings or animals.     3531.037575436609
when, on the other hand, the decision has been made to take a certain measure, the one chosen ought to be cost-effective so that there is in fact no resort to measures which are wholly unreasonable given their degree of effectiveness.     3086.0601240428978
it is not, therefore, the case that one must begin with a cost-benefit analysis.     1688.9238947804424
the second question concerns the reversed burden of proof.     13842.735380368196
it is perfectly correct that we need to apply this in certain cases.     3452.2952196880688
i have used chemical strategy as a good example of an area in which we need to do this.     2385.234390869543
there, however, we are concerned with that particular area, whereas it may be said that the present communication concerning the precautionary principle is horizontal in the sense that it concerns the full range of specific technical areas that are of political concern.     3782.2590337908114
therefore, the issues of the reversed burden of proof and of what the burden of proof is to look like are not especially discussed in this context.     4022.602322219742
instead, it is a question of political decision-making and of the bases for this.     2394.8042146135867
it is, however, perfectly correct that, when it comes, for example, to chemicals, we must ensure that we obtain a reversed burden of proof.     7323.497468743199
mr president, i believe that the precautionary principle needs to be quite radical in order to work, otherwise we shall always be confused by all the different interpretations possible.     5260.488047221354
i will give an example: a fungicide has been proven to cause babies to be born blind.     21138.24449572872
it is therefore a teratogenic substance.     878.3359890029701
very well, it is a fungicide, it kills off mould.     3196.2980491475546
this teratogenicity has been demonstrated by just one english laboratory, perhaps because it is the only one that has carried out tests.     2748.1945656707576
now, in my opinion, given that this is such a serious risk to health, the precautionary principle lays down that this product should immediately be taken off the market, which new zealand has done, for example.     2611.279798065491
so i want to put this question to you.     2414.286382431056
when human health is at stake, or there is the possibility that human health will be seriously affected, should we not perhaps carry out a cost-benefit analysis?     3242.6592044091426
the cost of a baby being born blind is too high; there are no benefits that can compensate for this.     5312.0883011806645
in short, commissioner, i would therefore like to know whether in this case, the precautionary principle as you understand it, would in any case, require the product to be taken off the market, until such time as another laboratory provides evidence to the contrary.     3980.0920860852134
.     1000.0
(sv) mr president, no one wants more than i do to see a situation in which we are able to apply the precautionary principle in such a way that it is seen as radically protecting people' s health and the environment.     5339.420699984843
obviously, i cannot adopt a stance on this special case and on the particular product which has been mentioned here, but i shall go directly back and see what i can obtain in the way of information about this particular case.     2758.318864426147
the truth, of course, is that a great many member states, and no doubt other nations too, have taken measures to protect their populations' health which amount to using the precautionary principle, even if it has not always been called that.     1759.583790489632
naturally, it is not in the first place a question of cost; there can in fact be an enormous cost to society if the decision to take a particular measure is avoided.     2386.329641489249
nor must it be a case of sitting and calculating the value of a human life over and against the cost of taking a particular measure.     1820.3082144767286
i think, however, it is quite obvious that when it is eventually time to decide upon a particular step to be taken, it is often the case that there are many different courses of action to choose between.     2609.8241031782727
in those circumstances, consideration should be given to what is likely to produce the best result.     4338.389100969253
i cannot forbear telling you about an occasion when the expression cost-effective was used by people whom one cannot perhaps imagine using it, namely when i was on a visit to africa.     4033.1802849323435
at a hospice for aids patients, i met two irish catholic nuns who were caring for dying aids patients.     7713.572199925293
they were the first to say that, each day, they must think about doing what is most cost-effective because resources had to suffice for their poor patients there.     3574.1224476157213
we must be very careful about how we use resources.     2294.012618675045
i believe we can learn something from this.     4482.327117779283
we must, of course, always ensure that we use our resources in the most effective manner and in such a way that they provide the broadest possible help.     1723.4069442769985
as is also explained in the present communication, it is not therefore correct that one must first begin with some sort of cost-benefit analysis.     2612.9777834684696
consideration should be given to what science is offering in the way of knowledge, and this should be used as an important political tool for protecting the environment and people' s health.     3376.123547291928
mr president, it is a rare honour to be given a second opportunity to ask questions!     6282.434863077781
commissioner, i would like to come back again to my first question, when i asked about the other commissioners, for it may have sounded as though i was in jest.     1826.6202367454196

i have good reason for asking this.     2921.1065678960226
as you rightly said, the precautionary principle is needed in precisely those areas where, as yet, there is no scientific evidence.     2951.9638190015307

mr president, i think it is extremely important to point to the present communication' s guidelines for using the precautionary principle as a way of repudiating assertions to the effect that we always want to use the principle for protectionist reasons.     1880.090372341204

intergovernmental conference
the next item is the report (a5-0018/2000) by mr dimitrakopoulos and mr leinen, on behalf of the committee on constitutional affairs, on the convening of the intergovernmental conference [14094/1999 - c5-0341/1999 - 1999/0825(cns)].     138627.16715643863
mr president, i will begin by thanking the portuguese presidency and the commission for all our recent very useful contacts.     3251.996278626574
let me also once more thank my co-rapporteur mr leinen for our excellent collaboration.     7620.7320621929075
the intergovernmental conference that is about to begin is important, both in a general sense and more particularly in view of the imminent enlargement.     3274.492707025884
the work of the conference will determine the structure and method based on which europe will be functioning in the twentieth century.     5706.646278078435
for the european union of the future to be able to function more effectively, more democratically and with complete transparency, clearly what we need is an extensive and in-depth reform of the institutions and the way they work.     2129.630185331348
the first and substantive prerequisite for achieving that reform is the agenda based on which the intergovernmental conference is to begin its work.     4201.290613480067
from the decisions made at the helsinki summit, it emerges that the agenda agreed there is unsatisfactory and does not guarantee the essential and substantive changes required for creating a more functional europe, one that is more effective, more democratic and more transparent.     3255.140348921855
this is because the helsinki agenda is limited to consideration only of the three issues that relate in a fragmentary way to the structure and operation of just two of the european union' s institutional bodies.     4791.6951250070515
i will not repeat which ones, because everyone knows.     7119.570285149576
my conclusion is justified on the one hand by the constant and sometimes very insistent demands of the peoples of europe and on the other hand by the huge scale of the great enterprise in which the european union is already involved, namely enlargement.     2201.427191639357
granted this, the new intergovernmental conference must not fail to include in its agenda issues which, properly addressed, would ensure the correct operation of all and not just some of the european union' s institutions.     6798.558331653002
just as it also must not fail to include everyday issues which, for that very reason, are highly visible to the european public.     2299.527436119018
in its numerous debates and its reports so far the european parliament has openly supported the convening of the intergovernmental conference.     4837.496427565889
at the same time, however, i am asking for it to include issues that improve and complement the reform, i repeat, of all the institutional bodies as well as issues that are of direct concern and interest to the european public such as health, energy, culture, transport and even tourism.     2005.2289683676797
whether or not the agenda will ultimately be widened is still the subject of debate and on this point i would like to extol the political conduct of the portuguese presidency, which has repeatedly made a commitment to the european parliament to make the necessary efforts towards that end.     2282.779540611804
all the same, whether or not more issues will be considered must in no way diminish the importance attributed by every member state to the intergovernmental conference.     5441.965571363046
this is because the institutional framework within which europe will operate in the future is a foundation of european integration and is therefore a matter of the greatest national importance for every one of the union' s member states.     3749.007061780751
in light of what i have said, i ask parliament to approve the opinion which mr leinen and i have put before you, so that on 14 february, the work of the intergovernmental conference can begin and our two representatives, mr brok and mr tsatsos, can proceed with the difficult task that awaits them with the european parliament' s backing.     7808.447994325277
the same applies to the commission and i would again like to thank commissioner barnier for his collaboration with the european parliament and for the very constructive proposals he put before us.     2553.71528874588
mr president, the group of the european people' s party was not satisfied with the conclusions of the european council in helsinki.     6121.538416123271
it seemed to us that the agenda lacked content, because our group was thinking in terms of the treaty and, specifically, the protocol on the union' s institutions.     10287.370321908076
in helsinki, the governments approved the idea that, as long as the european union consists of fewer than 21 states, a minimal reform would be sufficient.     3958.0066772230757
with more than 21, a deeper reform would become necessary.     3456.9297519710476
the european council in helsinki itself approved enlargement involving 13 new member states.     3880.952705450577
it is therefore inconsistent to open accession negotiations with 13 countries while contemplating a minimal reform.     8435.957647402409
we are therefore thinking in terms of the treaty and we want the agenda of the next intergovernmental conference to allow for a deeper reform of the union.     4015.7760584471134
having said this, mr president, this parliament is holding this debate today because we did not want to delay the start of the conference.     2489.6541394810015
in theory, we could have done so.     3289.1900578568575
we could have voted on the 17th of this month and the conference would not have been able to start on the 14th.     3421.21069862808
however, we did not want to do this because we wish to send a clear political signal to the governments and the candidate countries that we want this reform to take place, precisely in order to facilitate enlargement.     4594.4684404564305
mr president, i am very happy to be able to say this to the portuguese presidency because it has earned great respect in this assembly.     7825.437250476536
the portuguese presidency shares many of this parliament' s aspirations and has committed itself to doing everything it can to ensure that the agenda of the intergovernmental conference is widened to include other essential issues.     7478.401841549356
mr president, i would like to point out that this aspiration on the part of the european parliament is not an aspiration to gain benefits for the european parliament itself.     2960.6748524776854
however, the european parliament will be reinforced politically by the treaty resulting from this intergovernmental conference.     5588.871152623628
this is for one very simple reason: according to the agreements made in helsinki, this intergovernmental conference will lead to issues, which are currently decided unanimously, being decided by a qualified majority.     3844.8523951476755
therefore, the number of issues decided by a qualified majority will increase.     5680.8888527332965
it is already established within the community acquis that those legislative issues which are decided by a qualified majority are also subject to codecision with parliament.     2331.1916457044395
therefore, since the qualified majority will be extended, there will also be more codecision with parliament.     2929.360825774594
however, this parliament would not be fulfilling its role as a supranational european institution if it did not consider the political design of the union.     3733.3677370126265
that it is what we are doing.     1020.439736307761
we believe that, in the political design of the union, other issues need to be dealt with.     3419.882702705507
these include, amongst others, the incorporation into the treaty of security and defence issues, which have made much progress recently, but which must be incorporated into the treaty.     5253.543594877689
of course there is also the charter of fundamental rights, and work began on its wording yesterday, which i believe has started very positively.     4226.23980963972
we are going to work seriously to ensure that the europeans come to see being european as an advantage and that european citizens have certain fundamental rights which are connected to the union' s institutions.     4144.43820805854
therefore, mr president, our proposals, which we will specify in another report, together with the proposals of the commission, which has produced an excellent document - and i am happy to be able to say this here to mr barnier - are going to be used as conference documents.     4124.519248968945
we will talk about this on another day.     3813.353446985965
today we must give the green light to this conference, and, for its part, the group of the european people' s party, mr president, is ready to give that green light.     5317.570767453992

we are giving a positive opinion on the convening of the igc precisely because we have been impressed by the attitude of the portuguese presidency which has taken on board our request that the agenda of the igc be widened.     6450.57419066441
the amsterdam leftovers is not a very good term, because they are very important subjects in themselves.     2801.522734623301

remember the igc that led to the single european act.     5241.7428730275005

the parliament, the council presidency and the commission are pulling in the same direction for a wider agenda.     1611.2264218517075
i wish you every success, president of the council, in making sure that the european council agrees to this agenda and that on valentine' s day when you start off the igc it will be under good auspices and you will bring it to a good conclusion, also when the french presidency takes over, by the end of this year.     4458.516324709591

my group, mr president-in-office of the council, noted with satisfaction that the council presidency is far from happy with the current state of affairs.     3430.9878547155745
you can therefore rest assured that the confederal group of the european united left/nordic green left is committed to seeing that the union gets on with the tasks in hand.     6526.503777920127
what we are talking about is nothing more and nothing less than the future of europe, and, in particular, a europe that the citizens truly regard as being theirs, because they are instrumental in shaping it and because the cares and concerns, the problems and issues they face on a daily basis are also taken seriously by politicians.     3024.881675970223
the politics of closed doors and private discussions; all that must be consigned to the past once and for all.     3102.36303289097
that is why we need transparency.     1832.7082758251709
i believe it falls to you, as president-in-office of the council, to see to it that the train does not keep rolling on as before, leaving the people behind on the platforms as if they were ignorant as to the content of the treaties.     2541.000311816281
we believe it is imperative not just to make every available effort to provide the citizens with comprehensive information on how the intergovernmental conference is going, and its outcome, rather they must be directly involved in the overall reform process to a far greater extent.     4408.44702466213
we also take the view that the political decision-makers must at long last change the habit of a life-time and ask the citizens in referenda, once the intergovernmental conference is over, whether or not they go along with the way in which europe is to continue to develop in the future.     3584.699326717287
in this way, europe would truly belong to the citizens and the union would acquire an entirely new quality of democratic legitimacy.     10077.038308861644
there is another central issue relating to democracy that i would like to make clear reference to.     5044.172739085765
as a member of the convention that has now taken up its work on the charter of fundamental rights, i wish to make the following point clear: neither my group nor i are content with making a solemn announcement about the charter.     5339.0844139731025
after all, what will the citizens say if we make a solemn announcement about rights to them but they are unable to take legal action in respect of these rights as individuals?     4789.674025562366
no, to my mind this would only serve to deepen the credibility crisis facing the union.     3978.6342226052507
the poor turnout at the european elections really should have been warning enough for everyone.     3356.008379611349
what we need are visible rights for each and every citizen; the charter of fundamental rights must be made legally binding for all the citizens of the union.     3004.436418994203
together we should work towards this goal in the interests of the citizens, and what i expect to come out of the intergovernmental conference is that the relevant cologne decision will be revised here at the end of the year.     3672.833538588167
of course, we are also talking about effectiveness of decisions and functioning institutions in a union of 27 plus member states.     10890.738332504716
we will not achieve this just by undertaking a mini reform, as the council has resolved to do, and that is why all institutions must come under scrutiny.     1909.6708910558864
we need bold change and, at the same time, we must take care to give the greatest possible attention to equal rights for the major and small states.     2091.922364422663
this is something i would stress, particularly with my being an mep for one of the major member states.     3130.14930251081
i would like to thank the commission for the proposals they have put forward and i am certain that comprehensive discussions with the citizens will ensue here in the european parliament, on all the issues you have raised.     1854.4284769863025
there is one final issue i would like to raise.     5050.678079159665
parliament' s opinion expressly demands amendments to the treaty in the area of economic policy.     11088.583341945163
it is indeed the case that globalisation of national economies, but above all the introduction of the euro and the associated stability pact have led to a situation where we need to do more than analyse decision-making processes.     3851.07077693095
what we need most of all is to have the courage to undertake a critical examination of the union' s policies to date.     8552.698089411844
it is all about creating a socially just europe.     1195.79998547816
a socially just europe is still highly topical, for what we need to do as a matter of priority is place the fight against mass unemployment and poverty firmly at the centre of the union' s policies.     4870.935012209591
hence, to my mind, that includes having the courage to amend article 4 of the ec treaty, which defines the union in traditional neoliberal style as an open market economy with free competition.     12756.52878512382
the same applies to article 105, in my view, for the european central bank must at last be given the political task as enshrined in the treaty, of using its monetary policy to promote sustained growth and employment.     3470.1188404934455
mr president, the union for a europe of nations group considers that the agenda for the forthcoming intergovernmental conference, as set by the helsinki council, i.e.     2720.4653060385344
focussing strictly on the issue of decision making in an enlarged europe, must be adhered to.     4762.585508677336
it must be adhered to in the interests of the fast, effective work of the conference, prior to enlargement.     10025.429834273913
this is why we cannot subscribe to either the opinion of the european parliament or indeed to that of the commission, which both wish to widen this agenda far too much and, what is more, widen it in the wrong direction, i.e.     2822.333887771377
moving towards centralisation and an ever tighter european system.     10301.702369291086
our group has therefore tabled a raft of amendments which, when taken together, form a real alternative resolution.     2712.7773786726198
in them we say that the forthcoming igc must look into a decision-making system that pays greater attention to national sovereignty.     2689.25340286767
we deplore the fact that the commission, in its opinion of 26 january, limits itself to following the routine path of a standardised europe enlarging towards the east, a centralised superstate operating on the basis of majority decisions.     8062.886337907122
the federalists imagine that they are thereby going to create unity through constraint, but this is a totally superficial concept.     12754.127869447544
quite the opposite, in an enlarged europe, mr president, the standardisation imposed by the improper use of majority decisions can lead only to the whole thing exploding.     7534.090005445462
furthermore, the centralised european system, as we can see it developing today and as we can foresee it by extrapolation, erodes nations and, in eroding nations, erodes the national patriotism which forms the basis of our will to defend ourselves.     7452.817575339235
the europe which results from this is not stronger but weaker, as it no longer knows what it is or what it is defending.     2571.932773288916
this is why we have always said that enlargement was possible only if the diversity and freedom of the peoples of europe was clearly recognised, i.e.     2481.864764942438
by adopting flexibility founded upon the respect of national sovereignty.     10229.614314555345
we are delighted today to see the progress this idea has enjoyed in certain circles, one which are still, unfortunately, outside the european parliament and the commission.     3228.335075319516
a flexible europe, is a europe which respects its national democracies, which relies on the support of its nations, its national patriotisms, instead of continually persecuting them.     4846.17692694129
it is this that gives us the determination to defend ourselves against external threats, not the constraining, convoluted decision making procedures which the commission thought it could propose at the forthcoming intergovernmental conference.     3865.502588276239
mr president, i am speaking on behalf of the italian radicals and i would like to say to the president of the council that he has seen what parliament' s opinion is.     8537.621714528812
it is no coincidence that the last intervention is the only one that was completely opposed to further european integration.     2395.8593106058156
i say this without being the slightest bit ironical about the highly respectable position of mr berthu and his group, but it is no coincidence that the european council' s decision is supported by those who are, after all, in parliament because - and this is a highly respectable position - they are fighting against further integration.     5891.694902926225
this is the message that parliament is sending to the council.     1902.1589594346376
i hope that the portuguese presidency - and i add my compliments to those already offered - can convey parliament' s message and convey the result of our mediation work on saint valentine' s day.     28375.00096918961
in any case, tomorrow we shall vote against the igc agenda.     6537.636866262219
this message must be very clear; this is how we explain the way we shall vote tomorrow.     2814.586526394308
we shall give an opinion - which is technically and legally necessary - on convening the igc on 14 february, but we shall give an unfavourable opinion of its content.     9455.259162431179
just consider the facts: we looked at austria a while ago, but we can also look at the stock markets, mr president: given that the euro, that great undertaking, has fallen 16% against the dollar in a year, then perhaps, i will not say a government, but a good head of a family should ask himself whether one can conceivably tell the citizens that the only subjects open to negotiation are the number of commissioners or similar issues.     4568.222192077992
there is clearly a more ambitious goal.     3476.6778367590787
we radicals have tabled amendments which we are submitting to the house in order to consolidate this text, and for example, to request - just for the sake of mentioning articles 6 and 7 - that the very minimum be done, i.e., that the european parliament should also be involved in drawing up the european constitution and that a proposal be made for the european parliament to adopt the amendments to the treaty.     2425.647507018236
we know that there are other topics: for example, many members have subscribed, along with us, to the issue of the seat of the institutions, an issue that we believe should be considered at least at igc level.     3191.27939763456
in conclusion, i hope that the message that we give tomorrow will be strong and wide-ranging for once, so that thanks also to the efforts of the portuguese presidency, this agenda will be revised.     2167.8407869558378
otherwise, the challenge really would be lost, not just for the present but also for many years to come.     1006.1516075210261
mr president, the president of the commission was not being quite honest when he talked last week about the intergovernmental conference.     4337.102191982714
he said that there would always be unanimity on social questions but, in the commission' s draft, plans are quite explicitly being made for majority decision-making on issues relating to social security and on taxes connected with the internal market.     4691.641500004457
this means that it will be possible for major parts of the social systems in the member states to be changed by a majority in brussels, even if a unanimous french national assembly, a british house of commons and a dutch lower house should be against this.     3329.839076445741
and, in this regard, you are going to the heart of democracy as it relates to distribution policy and people' s social conditions, which are what voters wish to influence when they go to the polls.     7413.646597946312
these are matters which we ourselves shall no longer be able to decide upon as voters and no longer be able to change on election day.     9658.603089808073
brussels knows best.     22620.292432882725
mr prodi also announced greater transparency, but his draft regulation signals a clear backward step, and that is not only my assessment, but also that of the european ombudsman.     2690.338772001347
today, the commission is obliged, on a case-by-case basis, to weigh considerations of confidentiality against people' s demands for, and expectation of, transparency and, if the commission' s regulation is adopted, the commission will be entitled to lock away whole categories of documents without having to give them specific consideration.     10946.175996248425
there is also the desire to create a legal entitlement to confidentiality and to lock away documents of a kind which are at present publicly available in a range of member states.     6414.014464500992
under the banner of "progress towards transparency" , a range of documents is being locked away from the general public.     8622.71371324217
it is, of course, thoroughly orwellian.     1781.0945908945362
i want to ask mr prodi never more to comment on a proposal here in the house which is not, at the same time, available for critical scrutiny by the public.     2054.1021711184007
mr prodi secured positive comments for a proposal which would otherwise have been met with criticism, for the new draft regulation presents as progress the fact that it will now be possible to grant right of access to documents in the commission' s possession.     5683.7886140377
there follows, however, a long, long list of exceptions, and there is legal entitlement to confidentiality in regard to those exceptions.     6834.742934270975
the french text contains the word "refuse" and the english text the word "shall" , which means that the commission is to keep secret such information as is today available to the public, for example in my own country.     2345.789353453888
in addition, we have a flexible clause concerning the efficiency of the negotiations and of the institutions, which may be used for any purposes whatsoever.     3631.670407081088
it is therefore ...
(the president cut the speaker off)
mr president, ladies and gentlemen, the portuguese council presidency' s account has given me the distinct impression that we are approaching this intergovernmental conference with well-considered ideas and a high degree of openness.     9453.912248703733
we as a european parliament will use these opportunities to see that our issues are given the attention they deserve.     2387.384792108316
the preparations the commission has made so far are on the right track, although they do not go far enough if the european parliament' s motions are anything to go by.     4055.3123763394324
there is more to be said on that score.     2756.638075745523
it is crucially important for this intergovernmental conference to score a hat trick; that of the power to act, democratic legitimacy and transparency.     6360.85094713727
in the end, it is only by having these three things in place that we will be able to achieve acceptance amongst the citizens.     5069.914050235257
at the same time of course, we need to create the power to act in order to facilitate the enlargement of the european union.     4230.158088730193
if we have been having a different debate here in this chamber today then that only goes to show how important this is.     1379.160467817911
the european union must also have the power to act in the event of a government electing to impose a total blockade.     4842.21029815397
this is an important sign that majority voting is a crucial pre-requisite to the european union' s being able to function come what may.     17953.824467282004
this is particularly important where enlargement is concerned, and legislation and in relation to treaties, which entail changes being made to legislation.     6172.875462441916
of course we also want to extend the european parliament' s corresponding powers in this area.     16093.269783741827
in addition, we as a european parliament are going to have to take action in relation to economic and monetary union, for there is a lack of supervision in this area.     5022.864450530071
the finance ministers behave as if they were in an intergovernmental meeting, when in the ecofin council and in the euro 11 council, which is an unacceptable way to go on.     2153.2366239758426
unlike mrs kaufmann, i am not in favour of extending supervision over the european central bank as i am an advocate of independence for the european central bank.     7486.283546420968
however, there must be an appropriate level of supervision on the political side.     3185.7939242981724
furthermore, the tripartite relationship of commission, council and parliament must be restored to the sphere of foreign and security policy, where hitherto the council was in sole charge far more than should have been the case, especially in relation to the crudely fashioned crisis management measures, for which the commission alone has responsibility.     4734.073495979714
the overall plan failed to do justice to any of this.     2756.959661073929
we must examine whether any changes are needed to this end which would fall within the scope of the intergovernmental conference.     6499.81497381184
the portuguese presidency has agreed to initiate deliberations on this matter, perhaps to extend the mandate.     8765.287683303273
there is another point i would just like to draw your attention to.     6454.190991501131
the debates we are having these days, on a certain european country' s government, for example, show, do they not, that we must place the spiritual, moral, constitutional, community and common welfare-orientated leanings of the european union on a firm footing, and that legally binding fundamental rights must also be incorporated into the treaty for the same reason, as this will be a crucial stabilising factor.     3793.0060943814015
i would urge those who are still very hesitant in this respect to consider whether now is not the time to make this connection and perhaps pluck up the courage we need to make suitable progress in matters of fundamental rights.     3055.6060655457372
the european union needs few, if any, new instruments, and it does not need any new competences.     2147.8947159098107
what it does need are instruments that will enable it to put its competences into practice.     6185.803523915112
for this reason, we must ensure that the instruments have the effect of enabling us to carry out, on behalf of the citizens, the tasks we have already formally been set in the treaty.     4901.491483484821
in my view, that is the most important issue this intergovernmental conference has to concentrate its energies on.     6344.865896663968
if we succeed in making progress here, then we will also be ready for the historic task of enlarging the european union.     3083.5631075945644
this is the crux of the matter.     9807.04182929436
mr president-in-office of the council, i am confident that this will be tackled in a suitably positive manner under your tutelage.     6204.990642229667

(it) mr president-in-office, on listening to the various interventions, you have understood that in the committee on constitutional affairs and also among the leaders of parliament' s political groups, the decision prevailed - although not without some disagreement - to put our faith in the portuguese presidency, instead of creating more difficulties by postponing the start of the intergovernmental conference.     7447.308726986156

we must, therefore, ensure that at the intergovernmental conference we discuss how to consolidate the democratic bases of the union and how to strengthen - and this has been discussed in the house even today, with regard to the situation in austria - the system of principles, values and rights underpinning the union and the role of the political institutions of the union, with regard, among other things, to running the economy.     3427.208768525739

mr president, only a strengthened europe can cope with enlargement.     11586.718425654431
only a strengthened europe is immune to political opportunists who exploit dissatisfaction.     3427.742454135223
that is why sweeping reforms are necessary and, hence, a widened igc agenda.     26553.68000111446
the finnish presidency only listened to minimalist governments and, unfortunately, turned a deaf ear to this parliament.     35229.22542108423
portugal should not take a leaf out its predecessor' s book.     10673.792241597655
it is of the utmost importance that the european parliament and the commission join forces for this igc.     2982.226324587791

of all priorities, this one is the most pressing.     2913.218454161173
what is the commission' s verdict on this?     62287.109979430636

there is no reason whatsoever why representatives directly elected by citizens should be involved in talks at a lower level than the commission.     5465.183950490661
not only do we owe the sweeping reforms to the new member states, but we owe them to ourselves.     12387.947654556532

mr president, there are days when i really cannot understand this parliament.     1970.5437023598195
it seems to take a masochistic pleasure in self-mutilation.     44239.207131757226
after having restricted, on its own initiative, its participation in the igc to two minor seats, parliament is now giving up the right to add to the agenda for this igc.     4626.088955111232
the fact is, whether through weakness or foolishness - nothing to be proud of either way, you will admit - in rushing to give its opinion, parliament is giving up the only lever which the treaty accords it, i.e.     12810.29831408381
demanding a complete igc agenda as a prerequisite before declaring its opinion.     53887.87609524475
