court of first instance
mr president, i would like to begin by congratulating the rapporteur on a report, which, on reading it, gives the impression of simply confirming and accepting the council' s draft decision.     9136.286235786582
however, this report is based on long and effective work which has already produced certain results, such as the incorporation into the finnish presidency' s document of 7 december 1999 of the commitment to widen the agenda of the intergovernmental conference to include a study of the future modification of the organisation, composition and competences of the community courts.     7220.077762580836
for this reason, i would like to express my appreciation for the work carried out by vice-president marinho.     11122.843446331495
mr president, the reform of the community judicial system has become an essential requirement if, in the future, we intend justice to be done within reasonable time scales, and if, in the future, the european union wishes to have a judicial system which is equal to the political project which we have embarked upon.     2977.8246383876053
today - and i must say this with pride and satisfaction - this parliament has had a good opportunity to demonstrate the extent to which we europeans believe that the european political project is much more than just an internal market, and that it is based much more on principles than on economic interests.     1622.1796784618386
behind these principles, mr president, lies the justice system.     3186.071343678999
however, justice delayed is justice denied, and we must reflect on this notion.     8873.517146103271
the working document prepared by the court itself contains alarming information.     19852.724853539014
for this reason, the draft reform, which has been drawn up by the rapporteur, vice-president marinho, is to be welcomed.     5018.263693130663
it is true that it is a stopgap solution, but we must rely on stopgap solutions because, at the moment - as he himself has pointed out - we cannot expect a broad reform of the system from the intergovernmental conference.     7756.798500497237
we have to offer solutions which, while provisional, help to provide a faster and more effective system of justice.     2396.6672370327424
i would like to make two observations during this intervention, which stem from the report itself, given this parliament' s obligation, in accordance with the treaty, to produce a very succinct report, which does not go into depth.     7010.268206471457
these are two of vice-president marinho' s ideas and, i believe, they are seconded by the whole of the committee on legal affairs and the internal market.     1826.3872545518468
the first is that greater resources should be given to judges of first instance, and that they should be given one more référendaire.     1567.4410891779753
the second is that the translation services of the court of first instance and the court of justice should be separated.     14673.73267718935
at the moment, the court of first instance has to wait a very long time for its judgements to be translated.     9002.971243827227
according to the treaty, one of the founding principles of this community is linguistic and cultural plurality and there is no question of our overlooking the possibility of something as important as being able to read a judgement in our own language.     5787.80929665622
since we are talking about the intergovernmental conference, let us consider just two points.     6307.936090033358
the first concerns this parliament.     1363.5439075804536
i believe that it is well worth insisting that we wish to participate more with regard to the court of justice, even in the appointment of judges.     9251.119957522878
above all, we wish to see an extension of the competences of the court of justice, hand in hand with its capacity to fulfil its obligations, in other words, its resources.     13231.593779914463
we wish to see its competences extended, especially within chapter iv of the treaty of the european community and with regard to chapter vi of the treaty on european union and also a review of certain possible options, certain clear limitations of protection in these areas which are of extraordinary importance to our citizens.     5917.287091846646
and i refer once again to the declarations which we have heard in recent days.     3229.7201180099273
mr president, i would like to congratulate the rapporteur and welcome the contents of this report.     5378.496215644937
it has caused me to reflect on two problems which have come to me recently in my own constituency.     2610.5518727532926
both concern cases with decisions pending before the court of justice.     21909.453647159775
the first relates to a large suspension bridge and whether or not tolls on it should be subject to vat.     3581.8582991562935
the decision could have huge implications for our local economy.     2578.374659297733
secondly there is a lady within thirteen weeks of retirement who is desperate for news of a decision that will affect seriously her financial circumstances in old age.     5405.440167679279
these are just two examples of real life problems behind delays in europe' s system of justice.     7679.725352341179
delays in justice can mean personal difficulties and even tragedy.     5474.970272274434
this is not to say that all of our own national systems of justice are perfect, but all too often they may be waiting for a preliminary ruling from the european court.     1572.7561694575788
amongst the statistics there is a worrying upward trend in the time taken to deal with preliminary references.     6325.971941466281
this should not be taken as a criticism of the court or of its staff, rather of the court' s structure and its lack of resources in a growing european union.     7001.112043720675
the proposals in this report are very welcome as an interim palliative, but europe is a legal construction, its courts are central to its proper functioning.     2563.917065448845
in the face of coming enlargement the igc must deal with fundamental reform and restructuring of the court system.     7910.83686500324
otherwise we will all, as elected representatives, be faced with more and more clamour from our citizens who are not getting access to quick and effective justice.     1619.3028196631458
mr president, i too would like to thank the rapporteur for this excellent report.     1787.4456306902202
i also fully endorse his findings, in which, in particular, he suggests increasing the number of consultants for judges at the court of first instance and providing the latter with its own translation service.     6554.995015586238
in point of fact, i consider this to be necessary because we heard, following the committee on legal affairs and the internal market' s visit to luxembourg, that it is of course inevitable, a god-given fact as it were, that the court of justice should take precedence over the court of first instance when it comes to using the joint translation service, and so more often than not, important cases handled by the court of first instance cannot be given the attention they deserve.     6391.907760984922
however i also believe that the reforms must go further than those we are to adopt here.     2154.3687542188773
for example, i believe it is worth considering making the court of first instance that of last instance, if applicable, and making this a final competence, in those spheres where benches of the court, which resemble courts, pass judgement at a preliminary stage - i need only mention the keyword alicante or whatever the plans are for european civil service law.     6977.102405044352
i am also aware that we are probably also going to have to do something about the european commission' s inclination to renationalise competition decisions and transfer them back to national level.     4564.983881938192
it seems to me that we need to give this some thought, as these cases will no longer fall to the court of first instance of course but to the court of justice as draft decisions.     5768.045883048948
we need to consider how we will deal with situations of this kind.     5694.701744508362
where possible, one should also have the opportunity to present draft decisions in competition cases to the specialist chamber within the court of first instance.     7508.94786778687
in addition, we must consider whether it is right and proper - and we have already had cases in which meps, not to mention groups, lodged a complaint against the european parliament - for the court of first instance to be responsible for such cases, when in fact matters of a constitutional nature are affected and it makes more sense for such matters to be brought before the court of justice than before the court of first instance.     4736.267528806812
on a final note, i too believe that olaf requires monitoring under rule of law.     5199.019136491266
as things stand, olaf is in a vacuum and can do as it pleases.     4221.86399350197
it is essential for olaf to be monitored by a court.     5578.69853315671
the only court that could reasonably do so would be the court of first instance.     8704.86615943512
that would be another way of spurring the reform process on.     1810.65451342532
mr president, i would like to begin by joining those who congratulated the rapporteur on this work.     8772.669520933685
the european union is a system based on law.     1224.3754539592385
it must therefore have a system of courts to enforce that law.     2509.1389250298403
furthermore, if the courts cannot deal appropriately and expeditiously with the legal workload placed on them, what happens, as madame palacio vallelersundi has already pointed out, is that justice delayed becomes, as we know, justice denied.     12092.111859417157
the evidence of the court is that that is happening now and it points to steps which can be taken now to alleviate the problem.     2394.2713394828475
however, as mr marinho has pointed out, more is necessary, but that has to wait for the igc,
in my own country, mythology tells of the teeming hordes of faceless brussels bureaucrats but it never mentions the number of european judges.     6261.938301912866
there are less than three dozen at the apex of the european judicial order - hardly an overload given the responsibility of their tasks at the heart of the european legal system.     4315.148917147866
the extent of their importance can be seen in the political implications of the delay in resolving the outstanding anglo/french dispute over british beef which has caused such anger in my country and such frustration with the workings of the union' s disputes resolution procedures.     8396.29638657055
this has been compounded, so some who advise me believe, by the rules of procedure in the french courts which make it well nigh impossible for non-french nationals to proceed against the french government.     3514.8249273682604
certainly it is perceived de facto to be impossible.     5783.510113943488
this contrasts most unfavourably with the united kingdom courts where spanish fishermen successfully brought an action against the united kingdom government in circumstances which were very comparable.     10035.11173515737
what is happening in france, mr president, appears prima facie to be a case of discrimination against other eu nationals on grounds of nationality and as such to be in breach of the treaties.     2494.2842837556445
i would therefore like to ask the commissioner, who was kind enough to make some comments about my remarks in the previous debate, to look into this and to report back to parliament and to me their findings.     2333.3465873266764
i would be grateful if the commissioner could confirm in his final remarks that he will do this.     2670.6450507486743
mr president, on behalf of commission president, mr prodi, i would like to respond as follows.     20366.351418307433
the commission has taken note of the position adopted today by the european parliament regarding the request made by the court of justice and the court of first instance.     6823.3479948648865
the purport of this request is, on the one hand, to transfer the assessment of certain appeals to the court of first instance, such appeals currently being within the exclusive scope of the court of justice and, on the other hand, to increase the number of judges at the court of first instance.     8460.413582300363
may i add that, on behalf of the commission, i listened with great interest to the pleas which have been made just now and that i have a great deal of sympathy for the unease which underlies these pleas.     10440.05796422871
the unease is justified.     12140.121864161201
it has been said more than once: justice delayed is justice denied.     2338.7972771163327
the commission sympathises with these considerations.     1494.5313312130688
in the light of this sympathy, i would like to continue my response as follows.     14085.437936677192
as your parliament is aware, the commission is convinced that, without fundamental reform, the community judicial bodies run the risk - in the short term - of no longer being able to carry out their task within reasonable deadlines.     6423.298731267743
consequently, the commission has asked for the advice of a group of experts regarding the entirety of the reforms which could be implemented in order to enable the court of justice and the court of first instance to maintain the quality and coherence of their administration of justice in forthcoming decades.     9408.463839644368
the commission is aware of the request made by the court of first instance to increase its number of judges, but at the moment, the former is of the opinion that the proposed transfer of competence should be seen in the light of the study which i have just mentioned.     5060.532637467166
in other words, once this study is complete, the commission will issue its opinion as soon as possible.     4503.556512093801
thank you, commissioner.     696.3226540346473
the debate is closed.     4906.850855896859
the vote will take place at 11 a.m. tomorrow.     4974.994163035591
exceptional financial aid to kosovo
the next item is the report (a5-0022/2000) by mr brok, on behalf of the committee on foreign affairs, human rights, common security and defence policy, on the proposal for a council decision providing exceptional community financial assistance to kosovo (com(99)0598 - c5-0045/00 - 1999/0240(cns)).     23019.474544247845
i have been informed that the rapporteur has been delayed for several minutes.     3236.6356341612473
i propose that we start the debate immediately.     3368.586748726509
he will join us and speak as soon as he arrives.     10583.435566112943
i therefore give the floor immediately to mr bourlanges, in his capacity as draftsman of the opinion of the committee on budgets.     11125.400729906334
mr president, this is an important and urgent matter, and parliament is complying with a request from the commission to come to a decision quickly, because today, you must realise, men and women are dying in kosovo quite simply because the temperature is 25 degrees below zero, these people are doing a great deal of work to ensure a minimum of maintenance and they have not been paid.     3166.942200036552
an urgent request has been referred to us, and we approve it.     3688.4640361533807
we have been asked for eur 35 million.     2529.282050426631
we agree that it may be granted and we call on the commission to be extremely vigilant in ensuring, once the decision has been taken, that the amounts are set aside and paid as quickly as possible.     2024.1671386304874
we are talking about macrofinancial aid.     58485.42974135656
there are reactions to this in some quarters since it does not comply with the liberal canons of beauty which stipulate that there must be no financial intervention in order support an administration.     6877.747615092189
this is not our opinion.     2336.7450405799204
our opinion is that it is vital to contribute to setting up an administration in kosovo and that it is by no means absurd to contribute directly to the payment of public employees in this region.     3387.481113747477
indeed that is something extremely useful that we could have done in russia throughout the 1990s to prevent the disintegration of that state.     1948.1544399585582
the second important problem is that we have been asked for payments, the donors have made commitments and, obviously, we are the only ones actually paying.     1995.381302480804
the other parties are not paying.     808.4723568346051
we wish to see an end to this one-sided situation regarding requests for financial aid.     6527.761305588449
we would like the commission to make commitments on the amendments we are proposing in regard to this.     2729.500807290165
we would like to link the granting of all aid to the availability of the amounts due from the other donors.     4582.505551751839
this is not in order to limit or be stingy with our financial contribution to kosovo but, quite the contrary, to ensure that our contribution is being matched by the contributions of other donors.     3468.7550032215677
from that point of view, the proposed facility is a two-phase aid facility and the second tranche must be released as soon as the donors have made clear their interest.     5492.855440079746
finally, we have three questions for the commission in this regard.     1644.9438508546152
firstly, we need the commission to give us, on a regular basis, the list and the amounts that other donors contribute.     2683.0383002852486
when we come to pay, we want to know what the others are paying.     2762.073236827263
secondly, we wish to see an exact progress report on the invitations to tender and the schedule for their implementation.     16850.452125835596
during the budgetary procedure we were told that the funds for kosovo must be voted on as a matter of urgency yet, according to our information, no invitation to tender has been published or initiated to date.     5121.237970668415
this is serious, since it holds up the whole process of reconstruction in kosovo.     3480.9169099533515
finally, and this follows on from what i have just said, we would like the commission to inform the committee on budgets on a very regular basis, monthly, regarding progress on payments made.     5647.707225333597
we have commission documents which speak of firm commitments.     5501.080828845149
it is not firm commitments that we need, just plain commitments would suffice, and we need to know what has actually been paid and, more especially, what has not been paid.     2111.6192109753397
kosovo has suffered far too much from delays in payment.     7614.696343851932
mr president, if we are going to discuss kosovo today, as we have done many times before, then we should also start by taking stock of what has actually already been done in kosovo, or of whether in fact anything has been done at all.     2007.931606668855
what i mean to say is that a few small steps have already been made along the path to normality, and it would be nice if they could at least be listed in the course of such a debate.     1654.9956130036019
as of 9 february, there will be something known as the kosovo transitional council, which will comprise representatives of the political parties, the minorities and civil society, and which is intended to be a quasi interim parliament.     3323.985635666391
this is a positive move and we welcome it, but i believe we need to equip the individuals concerned with an instrument and with strategy planning for what they are actually going to have to prepare, for elections are planned for the autumn.     5181.591748299675
no one really knows what these elections are to lead to and what kind of parliament is to be formed as a result.     2634.3202658779483
no one knows what kind of authority this parliament is going to have vis-à-vis unmik.     7924.511462032743
in other words, there are quite a few imponderables that we are not being informed about and that others are presumably unable to get to grips with properly either.     3505.9051905639594
of all people, it is the albanians, who are now being integrated into the transitional council, who ought in fact to have a little more idea as to what is in store for them.     1635.338260194071
in addition to financial outlay and reconstruction work, urgent attention must also be given to restoring the coexistence of serbs, albanians and other minorities - not forgetting the roma of course - in this country, so that at some point in the near future, on the road to coexistence, it will hopefully be possible for some form of reconciliation to take place.     3635.6688872839522
i would like to list again the things that have actually already been accomplished and that we can be pleased about.     2169.8915111795372
administration-wise there are now 34 tax inspectors.     19874.81533944739
that is excellent news!     3236.3517335462516
there too, people are being encouraged to pay their taxes, for it will not do in the long-term for everyone just to depend on handouts from the eu and from other donors; some of the money for these activities must come from their own resources.     2612.0528974282665
it should also be noted that of the 19 departments, 4 already have administrative leadership, which represents an improvement on the situation there hitherto.     3232.262445093221
there is something else that we are keen to develop and this is very important for a state under the rule of law.     2667.176499964696
we have 130 judges and public prosecutors, who have now been sworn in, and who are ready to take up their work of dispensing justice, with a view to enriching the culture of tolerance there a little, and to at least being able to track criminals down and then secure convictions.     6705.776613141449
another positive development i would like to remind you of is the fact that the old uck is now bound up in the reconstruction of the country.     2282.850391944248
i believe that is a good thing

the eu commission cannot pay for everything.     2151.72958697496

mr president, ladies and gentlemen, please excuse my late arrival, but this other political issue is still on the agenda today, more is the pity.     13430.21639583059
the committee on foreign affairs, human rights, common security and defence policy would advise you to release the eur 35 million, and this piece of advice is given because this money can truly benefit those in need.     8788.871162900736

the next item is the report (a5-0011/2000) by mr langen, on the joint text approved by the conciliation committee for a european parliament and council decision adopting a multiannual programme for the promotion of renewable energy sources in the community - altener (c5-0333/1999 - 1997/0370(cod))
mr president, i would like to thank mr langen - although he is not actually here - for the work he has done to carry through the programme both in parliament and in the council.     7485.893730086346
the project has been long and problematic.     4361.686098303261
in future too, there will be a need to invest heavily in research into the use of renewable energy sources.     2888.686515142653
although there is much that is good about the new directive, it is nonetheless not without its defects.     1881.579985371945
one example is the use of peat.     15793.003967880208
peat cannot be classified as belonging in the same group as fossil fuels.     26571.93375297935
if peat cannot be classified directly as a renewable, non-fossil energy source, it must form its own class, especially as far as the issue of environmental taxation is concerned.     7209.48719965757
it is not right that peat should be judged according to the same criteria as, for example, coal.     6672.776490152237
the development of renewable energy sources is a partial solution to ending the union' s dependence on imported energy.     30528.026344358
research is particularly essential with regard also to the eu' s next round of enlargement.     17379.792540128885
dependence on imported energy affects worst of all many eastern european countries, whose economic structure is still suffering from the dependence created in the times of the soviet union on russian energy.     5981.926520311643
the eu must adhere to the kyoto protocol on climate.     41803.33056500082

this is not satisfactory - i would like to make that quite clear.     2372.4746598129213
however, we agreed to this compromise because it was important to us to enable this programme to be launched very soon indeed.     2050.491592693828
for in the course of the next few years, the proportion of renewable energy sources is to double, accounting for at least 12% of energy consumption.     3343.427785640669
this is the european union' s declared aim.     38890.03258605301
that being the case, since altener is the only eu programme to have the exclusive aim of promoting renewable energy sources, it will certainly be have a key role.     3139.4163010139414
in the first place, this new five-year programme will entail extending the actions in the altener i programme.     6195.90910949834
this will include extending the exchange of information and expertise between operators in the renewable energy sources sector, developing local and regional energy agencies, and establishing new networks for approving financial support, to name but a few examples.     4308.509038805841
to my mind though, what is particularly important about the altener ii programme are the new actions for relieving market saturation by renewable energies and those for implementing, supporting and monitoring the community strategy and the community action plan.     2344.8938445907015
we now have available to us a working paper from the commission' s services on a breakthrough campaign, which is in fact a crucial element of this community strategy.     4800.95749017694

the overall investment costs for this campaign are estimated to be approximately eur 30 billion.     4810.7539080697825
75%-80% of this is to come from private sources, and this will be supplemented by public funds from the member states and from the regions.     2461.7418599625244
in this respect, the altener programme will be able to create new incentives and smooth the way for investment, and help us to realise our ambition, in the interests of environmental protection, the economy and creating new jobs.     6041.748998660266

most of parliament' s amendments have been taken into account and the fact that the programme has seen its total funding rise to eur 77 million must be seen as positive.     3803.682794108104

there is very little in the way of appropriations to meet these targets, however.     2658.773588439844

the next item is the report (a5-0010/2000) by mrs ahern, on behalf of the european parliament delegation to the conciliation committee, on the joint text approved by the conciliation committee for a european and council decision adopting a multiannual programme for the promotion of energy efficiency - save (c5-0334/1999 - 1997/0371(cod)).     17969.322514531625
madam president, i would like to thank the council and commission for a useful and thorough conciliation procedure and one that was good-tempered all round, which is not always the case.     2462.503280100567
i am happy to say that i can regard the end result of conciliation as very satisfactory for parliament as the joint text has incorporated all its amendments either in full or in a re-worded form.     4929.087195043634
the financial amount finally allocated to the programme is also a considerable improvement on the council' s proposal at second reading which we found completely unacceptable and we have made quite a lot of progress there.     5847.721644959097
i therefore propose that this house agree to adopt the proposals on save and the conclusion at the conciliation at third reading.     4373.274536036289
i would like to remind the house that at second reading parliament adopted this report containing eight amendments, including a re-instatement of the commission' s original budget.     7093.052956154745
the commission accepted five of the proposed amendments including the budgetary allocation and i thank the commission for their continued support on the budget during the conciliation procedure as what the council had proposed was unacceptable.     2728.5139800098227
during the procedure, agreement was reached on the studies and actions to devise, implement, complement and assess the community measures.     7490.637999378524
compromise wordings were agreed for five other amendments including legislative and non-legislative measures and the inclusion of local energy centres and, very importantly, the energy audit systems in the review of progress on energy efficiency.     3962.046666777419
i hope that you will all agree that this is important progress.     1688.6698185144562
the issue of the financial envelope was staunchly defended by parliament in the face of the council' s very meagre opening offer.     9280.088496589806
in the event we had to have several meetings before the council finally increased this to a substantial amount.     3402.318968953982
we got an increase of 2 million on their starting figure which was a decisive increase which i can recommend to you and which the commission confirmed that it could implement the programmes under.     3650.4601429102377
this was an important consideration for us.     2836.0692322101763
i would like to say however that it is, and has always been, a very modest budget and therefore the funding for this programme is more symbolic than real.     1552.3118744046003
funding for energy saving still comes primarily from the member states.     5594.529859583283
we have to bear that in mind when approving this programme.     2993.870370715033
if there is more symbolism than reality in what we can achieve at community level, this is a pity because there is a lot of enthusiasm at local level for action, including community actions, on energy saving.     5868.805545600924
where the community can step in is to put the local activators in touch with each other so that they can actually not re-invent the wheel in every region.     1459.3159929555684
we have an important part to play in the community in the european union in this regard.     1496.06519463055
save is the only community-wide programme dedicated to promoting the rational use of energy.     2670.400466925429
it focuses on non-technical elements, helping to build energy efficiency infrastructure, and the purpose of the programme is to create an environment in which investments and energy efficiency will be promoted.     4780.0607973274355
here we need to realise that there is also a market opportunity in industry for energy savings.     2074.818547336806
we have heard a lot about difficulties of competition in renewable energies but energy efficiency saves money for business, saves money all round in fact and therefore it should be non-problematical.     11390.756152644124
it is something that we can all support.     944.8515210746224
i have to say, like motherhood, although we all support it, sometimes we do very little substantially and concretely to help the mothers or the people engaged in energy-saving.     2659.482187424471
we could do rather more, considering that we have made great commitments in reducing co2 and greenhouse gases and to reduce dependence on energy imports.     4635.14644060686
we are not taking the kind of action that the citizens want to see.     2842.75635221789
we are not making the connection so that the citizens can actually do something concretely either at home or in their offices or in industry to support action against global climate change.     3687.0192972245013
if we could communicate this it would be a very interesting thing to do.     2398.3338016872635
i would like once again to thank all who helped in this conciliation process.     4704.195299487093
madam president, ladies and gentlemen, i would once again like to thank mrs ahern for her work as rapporteur on this proposal, since she has contributed, in collaboration with the other members, to the achievement of a very reasonable final result, which in some respects even improves on the proposals of the commission itself and, of course, on what, from a budgetary point of view, the council had initially accepted.     5913.6471201600225
as mrs ahern has explained very well, the budgets of both the save programme and the altener programme, are basically symbolic budgets, since the burden is shouldered by the countries, the states of the union, the regions or even, in some cases, the local authorities.     4152.96638074019
however, despite the fact that the volume is relatively small, it is nevertheless symbolically important that there is a common desire throughout the community as a whole to support this type of action, which contributes to our genuine compliance with the kyoto commitments.     3754.7534441853963
this action also contributes to achieving greater diversification in terms of our energy sources, greater safety in our energy supplies and, in the case of save, to reducing consumption and increasing efficiency in the use of energy, thereby contributing to achieving the objectives set.     4257.608459562186

we hope that this, at the least, will come about during the evaluation process.     3201.259776803714
we sincerely believe in the validity of cultural action, among other things in terms of making a contribution to the social, as well as economic, development of a people.     3057.909644475382
and a fully-fledged europe can compete with the rest of the world and fully rediscover its roots, highlight its common cultural heritage and enhance and restore dignity to cultural and linguistic islands that until now were less well-known.     4808.059347819247
as for the important educational task which the european union must carry out as regards culture, one of the programme' s most obvious limitations is the level of funding - we have heard this several times - which censures the fact that the council at least - certainly not the commissioner - is not completely aware of the importance of cultural action: the document which prioritises the economic factor over social integration also bears witness to this.     4508.51402852849
the overall growth of the european union and the awareness of being european citizens: this is why we believe that the culture 2000 programme, albeit with a scarcity of funds, can make a considerable contribution to this great common goal.     3685.558785699958
madam president, i will add to the unanimity of the evening and say that i support the adoption of culture 2000 and add my thanks to the rapporteur, mr graça moura, who took over from our former colleague nana mouskouri.     6187.700517517041
both of them have done a first-class job.     1713.2667307268014
alongside the debate we had earlier on in the day, culture may not seem that important, but it is; and we have to be careful in the european parliament not to allow the urgent to get in the way of the important.     1655.989180241555
why do i say that culture is important?     1707.5529086541173
well in simple economic terms, europe' s culture adds to genuine prosperity.     29141.29664298006
where would europe' s tourism industry be without the richness of our culture?     24339.85747996503
but, more importantly than that, cultural activities are what make mankind civilised.     9906.096059285002
culture is at the root of our democratic beliefs; and an uncultured society is not going to sustain tolerance and freedom and democracy.     8863.365840037526
cultural diversity is important, and it is under threat.     3116.8219240365597

many people in my country say they see british culture being under threat, for example, from portugal, from germany, from finland, for heaven' s sake.     5307.548298115709
we do drink port wine, and we do like german beer, and we even use finnish saunas, but it is not from europe that culture is under threat.     7281.8834315502345
what i do see right across europe is people drinking coca cola, eating hamburgers, wearing baseball caps, watching hollywood films and often doing all this at the same time.     1615.5761330907026
now i do not believe that protectionism and regulation is the way of defending europe' s culture, but i do believe in lending a helping hand when we can.     6030.56161150857
that is what culture 2000 is about.     1334.5359884916654
so, i say to the council of ministers: keep this programme genuinely under review.     2275.7515369811904
are we doing enough?     2275.755187588183
and, i say to madame reding, thank you for the support and the help you have given so far, keep up the good work, we are on your side.     2939.698251294294
madam president, i too would like to warmly thank the rapporteur, graa moura, and commissioner reding, for their contribution to the creation of this programme.     8040.533424693906
the culture 2000 programme was finalised at the end of last year as a result of conciliation between parliament and the council.     5879.883765198548
the outcome can be considered reasonable, when we take into account the fact that the adoption of the programme called for a unanimous decision to be made in the council.     2637.630626102701

in spite of a budget situation which does not match up to our ambitions, these results will now enable us to approach the future positively and i would like to pick up on what mr gargani has just said, that the commission made a statement regarding a mid-term assessment.     5477.76631338393

all this must lead us to intensify our action in favour of developing a common cultural area, within which our cultures can flourish further in all their unique features, in all their diversity, but may also be mutually enriched and the other citizens of europe may participate fully in them.     5761.068310753574
it is thanks, too, to parliament' s action, preferring a number of small-scale measures to be carried out close to the citizens' cultural roots rather than vast measures on a spectacular scale.     6131.263778628665

i should like this programme to become a practical reality and for culture to be not only a factor of enrichment for our citizens, both in personal as well as in social and economic terms, but a right which it is our job to assert, and also the hallmark of rediscovered interaction within the union.     3826.217037122645
this is what our european 'culture 2000' programme offers.     9116.888659083068

end-of-life vehicles
the next item is the debate on the recommendation for second reading (a5-0006/2000), on behalf of the committee on the environment, public health and consumer protection, on the common position established by the council with a view to the adoption of the european parliament and council directive on end-of-life vehicles [8095/1/1999 - c5-0180/1999 - 1997/0194(cod)] (rapporteur: mr florenz)
mr president, nine million cars are shredded and scrapped every year in europe.     16200.224996693574
even though they can no longer be driven, these nine million cars may still be traded and moved across borders; and not just intra-community borders, but also those outside the union.     5660.011670966516
it is therefore only right that the european union should draw up community rules on how these nine million vehicles every year are to be recycled and processed.     4869.0620348786715
we believe that the directive has a few weaknesses that we in the house should work on to produce a directive which is genuinely ground-breaking.     3493.563957457567
we therefore have a number of amendments.     3423.5498704345287
i personally believe that the scope of the directive goes beyond its objective.     6727.153043073029
i do not think it is necessary for classic cars to be part of the directive.     5566.550215046875
nor do i think that motorcycles should be included, as the distinctive culture of recycling within the motorcycle industry obviates the need for a european directive in this area.     3575.5623226546477
i am not looking for such rigidly high recycling quotas when it comes to special-purpose vehicles either.     7948.771029806582
i want special-purpose vehicles such as ambulances to have high recovery quotas.     7841.744136929185
this is my main concern in this matter.     3015.93332219733
in my opinion, the directive solidly regulates the matter of accountability for how a car is disposed of in europe.     7665.10412436644
this can be left as the commission has proposed with the member states ensuring the appropriate collection points for draining cars.     3313.591416819834
this will involve, for example, disposing of more than 32 million lots of used oil, removing break fluid, etc.     7089.341108236023
an important part of this directive is the issue of what to do with used parts.     2380.120429143393
what should we do with the shredded products?     6625.577790191568
we should not overlook the fact that, although quotas are an important point in recycling and recovery, they are by no means the only point.     5733.747238179593
do not forget that, in the case of cars, 80% of environmental impact comes through driving, 1% through recovery and 19% through construction.     5424.648740612159
quotas are not, therefore, the only parameter in the matter of environmental compatibility, just one of many.     3230.7172084743456
of course we need to aim high, but quotas must not become an end in themselves.     3388.984925499046
we must understand that the bigger picture is important when it comes to the environmental impact of cars.     5546.506889170261
i am far keener to see us move away from today's car, weighing 1,400 kg and driving an average of 200,000 km, to a future car capable of driving the same distance but weighing just 1,000 kg.     11204.426745853038
this would at least mean 400 kg times 200,000 km less transportation.     25150.234516325454
this would represent real environmental progress because it would lead to a large reduction in co2 which, if i have rightly understood the kyoto protocol, is the important factor.     4378.747163309757
we therefore consider that, in future, vehicles which clearly and effectively use lightweight construction methods will produce less co2 and should receive preferential treatment in their combustion quotas.     7002.801743789585
it should not be for each member state, but for you and your staff, commissioner, to decide whether these lightweight construction vehicles (and there is also talk of 3-litre vehicles) should receive preferential treatment.     7929.989390870291
we think they should.     5821.544762442482
may i say something about costs.     2574.324989625249
some say that all the costs should be borne by the manufacturers; that this is only right and in the very best interests of the consumer.     2054.6210736400726
however, this can be seriously questioned as the manufacturers will pass on all the costs to consumers and acquire for themselves a state-sanctioned monopoly on recycling.     4343.084886692809
i can only warn you against consenting to this.     1911.2336713630757
there are amendments which propose splitting the costs fifty-fifty between the manufacturer and the buyer of a new car.     15050.113309821269
the money from this pool, system, or however you want to picture it, would mean that from 2006 all vehicles in circulation could be taken in without cost to the last holder.     5248.924814327515
this too is a particular desire of my group.     2394.659947833131
our proposal to split the costs has a crucial advantage, namely that we won't be an immediate candidate for the court of justice when the directive is adopted.     5038.680861143701
the car industry is concerned about and half expecting repercussions, which is a serious problem which we must address accordingly.     5223.273005270003
this is why i propose splitting the costs between the first holder and the manufacturer.     17062.805274675116
as for a ban on certain materials: of course we need one!     3709.3655162095165
a car has dangerous components which we must ban long-term.     3630.9319586725733
this means that we need a pressure instrument.     3130.476916444426
the commission has put forward various proposals which are too stringent and we have come up with some alternatives.     2352.2379322376264
there are a number of sound amendments.     11682.492796906705
this parliament has been given more authority, so let us use it!     1861.9287643910222
let us have the courage to put together a ground-breaking directive.     11265.747594455783
a directive which we put together just for the sake of it would not be worthy of this house.     3622.129754284709
i ask for your support.     4217.106487350371
mr president, i almost feel like saying, "phew" , now that we have at last come to the second reading on this directive which has given us so much work, and which has clearly also given a great deal of work to the rapporteur, mr florenz.     2052.1562992038116
i do not consider this directive to be at all satisfactory since it does not answer the real question over what is going to be done with vehicles which have already been abandoned and which, today, can only be removed at local authority expense, i.e.     2314.469047107286
the taxpayers' expense.     198579.54942014132
the principle of 'polluter pays' is not being applied.     9256.122444948274
vehicles already abandoned have also been abandoned by community legislation, in that they are not taken into consideration either in the texts on emissions or in this text on end-of-life vehicles.     3590.2443368566023
the only merit of this directive is that it looks to the future.     2001.8731290600865
it has not, however, been possible to solve the problem of vehicles currently being used but which are already old and which cost far more to recycle.     2460.514131597801
on the other hand, we can be fairly confident for the future because we know the efforts that car manufacturers are prepared to make in order to find (a) materials that are easier to recycle and (b) to set up recycling industries.     6769.415026520063
i should also like to stress the need to maintain a flexible approach to recycling, reuse and incineration, as far as the new particularly light materials are concerned which make it possible to reduce vehicle consumption and therefore emissions.     7361.220664397733
this is a problem of compatibility between our directives on end-of-life vehicles and the former directives, including the one on auto-oil, which we voted on a few years ago.     5552.985088139218
we must therefore congratulate the committee on the environment, public health and consumer protection, on having the wisdom, in order to make things perfectly clear, to reintroduce the clause making an exception of the vintage cars which are part of our industrial and cultural heritage.     7404.512442663398
in conclusion, let me say that this directive is not ambitious enough to solve the problems of present car numbers and it is rather hypocritical with regard to the fate reserved for the wrecks which still clutter our forests, lakes and gardens.     7165.629807092585
once again, the principle of 'polluter pays' is not being applied.     6156.441973790257
the taxpayer is still footing the bill.     45868.07210081052
mr president, commissioner, reading the leaflets which have been distributed in recent weeks, one would think that we were talking about the ultimate environmental catastrophe or the death of the car industry in europe.     2073.7620956990095
neither is correct.     20018.966390175796
we simply must realise that the directive on the table is a sound directive.     1816.7439340901894
it means ecological progress for europe, and we can be proud of ourselves if we succeed in enacting it and setting it in motion.     4653.159837686894
all the same, there is one point on which we are in conflict.     1643.2642346029834
in the grand scheme of the directive, it is perhaps a minor point, but nevertheless a disputed one.     2282.140125845604
it concerns the issue of recycling costs.     12620.925553996318
here we find ourselves fundamentally at odds with last year's debate in the council when freedom from costs for the last owner and the issue of financing were lumped together and a question mark placed over whether the last owner should really be free from costs.     5763.133665898275
last february in this house we unambiguously decided (and we social democrats are sticking to this) that the matter of freedom from costs for the last owner is not open to question!     2018.4022826124556
so who will pay for the vehicles which are to be disposed of?     2733.0845541950534
for us this is straightforward: in the case of new vehicles it should be the manufacturer since this will also compel manufacturers to design and build recyclable cars.     11971.083573316655
and what about vehicles which are already on the road?     2000.979861629529
let us look at an example.     13482.42670286164
rover in great britain, if they were responsible for all end-of-life cars, would actually be responsible for 5.8 million cars within the european union and would immediately have to set aside eur 250 million against recycling costs, whereas a manufacturer from korea building similar cars would only have to shell out peanuts, as a representative of deutsche bank put it.     7636.12036586704
such distortions in competition are not about environmental protection; they simply affect investment potential and the jobs of those building cars in europe.     4064.0480509611943
consequently, for end-of-life vehicles we propose setting up a fund to cover the costs of recycling end-of-life vehicles in order to safeguard the principle of freedom from costs.     8276.392411134417
i can understand it when colleagues from countries which do not build cars advocate that manufacturers should pay the full whack and that they are not interested in the problem of distortions in competition when it comes to setting aside funds.     4012.514425092172
however, i would ask these colleagues to show solidarity with the more than 2 million people who make a living from building cars in europe, so that these jobs can be secured in the future.     2631.323288973501
i am in favour of stringent environmental requirements, as you know from the auto-oil programme and the debate on exhaust emissions, but i believe that they should be the same for everyone!     3833.1924996607368
mr president, commissioner, i think we need this directive.     1451.0580537929363
firstly, because it clearly stipulates the environmental objectives.     2170.596433740691
secondly, because it can promote recycling, which is important.     9517.76584580864
thirdly, because it sets out a clear procedure to combat pollution caused by heavy metals.     5205.573074514854
this is also an important point.     1228.6341254956901
the directive affords a european framework, also to member states who already have systems in place and who wish to continue to use these.     2040.0296820799888
so, first of all, we need to keep the recycling percentages at their present level.     7336.75388099798
this will, of course, provide the necessary incentive for technological innovation and for identifying ways of dealing with materials that we do not as yet know how to dispose of.     5731.517870933656
secondly, we need to retain the details as they are included in the common position.     5461.092607592081
as from 2006, therefore, all vehicles should be handed in at no cost to their last owners.     2365.134341827425
needless to say, this is most definitely an essential point which should not be changed in any way.     9506.246599861395
is this too much of a burden?     2239.0244572993674
there is, however, one thing we should not overlook.     4768.858140103895
the directive applies to complete cars, that is to say cars with none of the essential components missing.     14562.712189724198
according to the experts, few of these complete cars are of no value.     6759.115023090205
after all, recycling and the re-use of components form part of a sector which is not necessarily loss-making.     3285.147707469825
quite the reverse, in fact.     3886.434522524534
at present, there is a whole raft of companies which make a living out of them, and a decent one at that.     2047.7351932044035
this directive encourages the whole of this sector.     4979.971309308957
it is a sector which comprises small and medium-sized businesses.     4025.7614318493856
since, in this case, transport costs are high, a system will have to be introduced which will need to be very much decentralised, because transporting an end-of-life vehicle over a distance of more than 100 km is not a profitable activity.     3719.3327158237184
i think it is a good thing that this directive does not need to apply to historic vehicles, and i think we ought to add this provision to it.     2950.044466169761
i think, therefore, that vintage cars ought to be exempt.     53264.15223953478
also, it is a good thing that we should clearly lay the burden of responsibility with the manufacturer.     4128.2596359837535
that is a basic principle which we must uphold.     4235.109207971345
as manufacturers are responsible for the design, they can give a great deal of consideration to the environment at the conception phase.     5445.4711597889755
we must also insist that the cost is borne wholly or largely by the manufacturer, as stipulated in the common position.     7139.272150940513
i find this a well-balanced formula which does not preclude a number of other things.     10413.518820628578
i think we need to retain it.     8141.903444730203
this is why our group will remain as close to the common position as possible and will not alter its essence.     4591.505406239082

in my opinion, we should not jeopardise that common position, because it is a directive which we desperately need for environmental reasons.     2806.9121329588374
i would ask you to support this common position.     2057.4083392589405
our group will, at any rate, do this as far as possible - for environmental reasons and because we have this directive which is well-balanced and encompasses a whole range of issues and difficulties including, for example, the way in which the costs are to be distributed.     2633.3519810731314
mr president, we need to take an important decision today.     2230.5440899509654
does this european parliament support the ecological principle of manufacturer' s liability, that is to say that cars, once they have reached the end of their lives, are the responsibility of the car manufacturer?     8336.026563118037
no, according to the christian democrats in amendment no.     7852.601793140057
38: car manufacturers and car drivers should split the cost 50/50.     52042.07775198567

packaging still accounts for a mountain of rubbish in europe and is a burden upon the environment.     3957.9767560319397

mr president, i am very pleased that environmental protection is now a priority for this parliament, as indeed it is for the citizens of europe.     1622.0175437586522

mr president, this directive deals with the comparatively small but growing problem of abandoned cars and the question of a more structured disposal of all cars at the end of their life.     5586.1191076278155
to that extent, it might be thought desirable, even if it is not a matter of urgent necessity.     4278.725150952059

and should the directive be retrospective to cover every car that has ever been made?     2173.621799009765
the proposal from the council, the common position, is that manufacturers should pay the lot.     4901.1819772112585
that would mean billions of pounds or euros for each of the major companies in each of the countries of the european union.     4543.266225467

because european car manufactures have been operating here for many, many more decades than companies from japan, korea and elsewhere, that would be a much greater burden on the older european companies and a competitive gift to their competitors from elsewhere.     5307.579226067094

if we do reduce it, we remove from manufacturers the incentive to design and produce cars which give rise to less waste.     10839.997519311355
the common position, which was arrived at with great difficulty in the council, which the commission has endorsed and which was also maintained in parliament' s committee on the environment, public health and consumer policy, complies fully with the environmental requirements.     4520.686081503878
i therefore find it incomprehensible and very curious to see amendments from members of the committee on the environment, public health and consumer policy, the objectives of which are to weaken the environmental requirements and significantly reduce car manufacturers' liability.     5044.886405389174
if these amendments are adopted, i think that we shall cause parliament seriously to lose credibility on environmental issues.     3691.9810285057247

what is more, if we reduce manufacturers' liability on this issue, this will also have serious consequences for subsequent matters in other areas, for example the forthcoming directive on scrap from computers and other electronic equipment.     2850.1791926521864

we shall then be able to achieve an environmentally respectable result.     26811.450369362843

now who should pay the costs?     1569.3266269731862
whatever system is adopted, ultimately the cost will be passed on to the consumer.     2900.6773082111454

in the first reading debate it was only the economic and monetary affairs committee which asked this question and it was only myself as the draftsman of the opinion of that committee who posed that question in parliament.     2530.207424220306
that is quite simply: who pays?     827.1305997881735
the council common position reached a reasonable compromise in that the manufacturers should pay a significant part of the cost, not all of it as mr bowis has mistakenly said.     2688.0652440638414
in fact perhaps as little, the lawyers tell us, as 20% or one-fifth of the cost.     2003.445072510254

mr president, there are two issues of principle in the council' s draft directive.     7156.791542777016
the first concerns the manufacturer' s unrestricted liability to take back end-of-life vehicles.     36600.675511309906
there is the risk of creating a monopoly within the car dismantling trade.     8556.543406689776
this particularly applies in areas within the eu where distances are great and where a lot of small companies are involved in dismantling vehicles.     7304.98102326952
i believe that, quite apart from the nature of the issue concerned, the eu' s directives should not treat small companies within the union unfairly.     12472.760365462245
unrestricted liability on the part of manufacturers would also be in danger of creating a situation in which the market for used car parts disappears, for manufacturers would have a greater responsibility to sell new parts.     7615.499331388292
yet the trade in used car parts is important, especially for those who collect and renovate older vehicles.     4471.76384475006
the principle of unrestricted liability on the part of manufacturers is also dubious in terms of the principles governing the market economy.     9702.335994201481
it should be possible for changes to be made to companies and for the latter to be sold and wound up.     2228.083419225716
they should be able to establish themselves in new markets, but also to leave old markets.     6456.974936552241
to bind manufacturers by means of a liability which can extend very far back in time ill accords with a flexible market economy designed to promote development.     10404.57171048634
the second principle in the council' s common position is the retroactive element in the draft directive.     10598.940827052045
it conflicts with established economic and legal principles retroactively to impose upon a manufacturer financial liability for its product.     9853.913205754454
to do so also changes the owner' s liability retroactively.     2792.0441686819922
over the years, a consumer may have altered the product in a number of ways.     7616.11548204059
every country within the eu now has rules governing the scrapping of cars.     17434.79066068192
some may be better than others, and some worse.     1645.9179167841185
in anticipation of this eu directive' s being implemented, eu countries ought however to be individually liable for scrapping vehicles in the best way possible so that the retroactive element in the eu legislation is not applied.     8762.612749286613
this does not conflict with a financing model involving the building up of funds.     3420.9921758630057
mr president, everyone agrees with the obvious environmental and industrial importance of the instrument in question.     3853.83989268223
the common position offers an acceptable compromise but its rather delicate and precarious nature somewhat restricts the opportunities for improvement, and puts the whole directive at risk.     5960.5159268622365
i therefore maintain that parliament cannot force the issue and it would be paradoxical for it to do so in the context of redefining the environmental scope of the instrument.     2946.2777008179246

mr president, this is an extremely bad directive.     1765.427587441804
first of all, as my colleague mr bowis has pointed out, it is retrospective and that is wrong in principle.     8095.027368784529

in this parliament we constantly talk about the need to promote employment and jobs in europe and yet we constantly pass measures which will have the effect of reducing employment.     3057.4498125726923

it is like so much european legislation.     986.8552159792409
it is far too prescriptive.     10836.5314919021
it sets in stone one particular model of recycling.     39176.192512650035
the production line was invented about a hundred years ago, i believe, by henry ford and what we are proposing to do here is to create a sort of nineteenth century production line in reverse - to dismantle vehicles, to take the pieces apart and seek to recycle them.     13078.978453084896
one thing we should stop and think about is the fact that there is a very uncertain market for recycled plastic bumpers of cars.     7429.194123274421
the plastics industry for the most part does not want to take these things back and cannot economically do so.     3925.14912843483
there is already a very successful industry out there which is shredding cars, recovering metals and recovering energy by the incineration of the non-metallic parts.     14019.420468077684
this is a very environmentally reasonable approach.     1923.426933038188
it is just as good environmentally to burn old plastic as to burn new oil for energy recovery.     4797.723275881502
this method of disposal of cars carries no cost and so would eliminate the whole issue of who pays because the "dead" car would actually have a small value going into the recycling and reclamation process.     4583.64778537581
i would oppose this directive on the basis that it is too prescriptive, it ignores what is actually going on in the car recycling market at the moment and it sets in stone methods which are not necessarily the best environmentally, and are certainly very damaging economically.     4993.619705299913
mr president, the common position is sound, but it is up to parliament to improve the common position on end-of-life vehicles.     2583.1273265988248
the social-democratic group has submitted a few important proposals for improvement over the past couple of days.     10156.49536415092
i would especially draw your attention to amendment no 45, in which a choice is made in favour of laying the cost of disassembly and recycling at the door of the manufacturer, for new cars at any rate.     7193.348964488914
consequently, consideration will also be given to recycling during the conception phase and production.     6181.9683253787625

the common position stipulates that manufacturers should not only bear the cost, they should also take back end-of-life vehicles.     3349.4994651351435
this is a big problem in my opinion.     2514.53940560129

if car manufacturers all set up their own dismantling companies, this will necessitate transporting end-of-life vehicles over long distances.     12230.300851329996

if you travel to the netherlands, you will find that every last owner can get rid of his car cost-free.     9752.662669513422
you will also find that these cars are suitably disposed of, that new material flows have been opened up in order to recycle materials, that 85% of the weight of a car is recycled and that 92% of all cars in the netherlands are recycled.     11443.594281462152

the main thread of the discussion is about whether or not the manufacturer, the vendor, or whoever, should take the end-of-life vehicle back without any cost.     7047.533353526126

mr president, ladies and gentlemen, thank you for an interesting debate in which many important arguments have been presented.     8825.033509814886
would you be so kind as to bear with me for a few minutes.     1976.4348245277288
allow me briefly to comment on some of the assumptions and principles behind the present directive.     1941.5796192930452
allow me also to begin by commenting on and responding to a couple of the most important arguments in this debate.     13967.103095096321
first of all, i think i should repeat some of the basic facts which mr florenz and others have already mentioned.     4266.471027099639
what we are discussing here is the fact that close on ten million cars are scrapped every year in the european union, resulting in approximately as many millions of tons of waste.     5699.808878532558
this means that almost ten million tons of waste are created by these cars, and approximately seven per cent of the latter are abandoned out in the open.     7715.212341570296
moreover, they constitute environmentally hazardous substances of the worst kind.     34064.733956326185
approximately ten per cent of lead production is to be found in cars, but also cadmium, chromium, mercury and other very dangerous substances.     29963.874710787368
this is something you already know, but i would nonetheless also repeat it for the benefit of those who are listening.     2953.9123243405234
cars are one of the fastest growing sources of waste we have in europe.     7009.780273783207
we know that waste from cars exists and we know how we ought to be dealing with it, and there is no excuse for not taking action.     3226.8093826095433
the present directive has three aims.     2128.984363246663
first of all, we want to stop heavy metals from being used in the manufacture of new cars.     7973.712311532669
secondly, we want to establish the fact that the manufacturer is liable.     10989.223676015336
we can no longer take care of these matters at the end of a product' s life-cycle.     8390.55135118234
instead, we should avoid creating so much waste.     4446.787454347904
we should see to it that we recycle as much as possible of the various contents of a product.     4042.5297112153853
thirdly, we wish to achieve the recycling objectives specified in the present directive.     8976.155015082979
these are the three most important aims of the directive.     1553.2268082844928
this is, of course, because it is a waste of resources for us not to ensure that materials in cars are recycled.     5188.087822136503
it is, above all, the manufacturer' s resources we are wasting if we do not get to grips with a car' s life-cycle.     10770.560661225993
two important questions have arisen in this regard.     7850.23962543989
yes, there are in fact more than two questions, but i want to mention just two of the most important.     1828.0822961146966
first of all: who should pay?     1662.569550268196
and secondly: are we reducing the european car industry' s competitiveness by means of the present proposal?     3937.933953883225
the first question, then, is about who should pay.     1130.6952231210223
the directive states that the polluter should pay and that the cost is to be borne mainly by the manufacturer in accordance with the principles to be found in the eu' s treaty concerning the manufacturer' s liability.     16254.390563152336
it is nonetheless clear that the cost will be borne jointly by manufacturers and consumers.     8253.595140330139
we have calculated that the cost of recycling cars does not constitute more than one per cent of the price of a new car.     9264.536635859993
do we imagine, then, that the cost would disappear if we were not to adopt this directive?     2439.897755709083
no.     1401.6590927003035
clearly, we shall all have to bear the cost of serious damage to the environment, but it will be very much greater.     947.5724303129167
moreover, the cost will have to be borne in the future.     4198.513240517354
the cost will not therefore disappear, but now we are clarifying whose liability it is and how the cost is to be distributed.     3213.9810557689198
are we therefore talking about a car industry in crisis?     3975.6787049214827
is it an unprofitable industry which we are discussing and which we really have to help by not imposing more taxes upon it?     2731.33138635474
is it the case that the car industry definitely cannot afford these?     7394.162100087605
is it the case that the motives behind the proposed measures are in actual fact concerned with reducing the competitiveness of the european car industry?     2415.1421559269506
no, of course not.     1146.9069799235388
it is quite the opposite.     2924.6024499362566
do you believe that consumer demand for environmentally friendly cars and more fuel-efficient cars is going to decline in the future?     7069.702264087719
what kind of requirements do you think our children and grandchildren and future legislators are going to make of cars?     13555.383160563417
naturally, that they should be environmentally friendly, that they should be recyclable, that they should be fuel-efficient and that they should not pollute our environment.     3195.431711873877
it is, of course, only in this way that we can create a future for the car industry.     2075.56715491525
we must create forms of motive power which are not damaging to health and which are of such a kind that we can deal with the relevant waste, recycle materials and compete by means of environmentally friendly and fuel-efficient cars.     5875.193846332274
the fact that we have a european car industry which has already made great strides more than properly compensates for the fact that there are, in fact, a good many cars on european roads for which they must take responsibility.     1657.1111742194585
the cost is not insurmountable; nor is the task an impossible one.     10017.117191636024
if we develop motive power of the kind i have described, i am quite certain that market forces and commercial interests in the car industry will ensure that mechanisms develop for dealing with both the cost and the workload.     3929.1879984343313
these will consist of everything from insurance to sensible and effective scrapping and recycling systems.     13420.203512438833
the industry itself will ensure that these systems are introduced in europe.     1917.2734303526647
they will not represent an insurmountable cost for the european car industry.     3873.473177110643
they will instead help industry to be in the vanguard of eventually producing environmentally friendly and more fuel-efficient cars.     5058.319929188632
we must do this for the sake of the environment.     2591.9001958629683
the costs will not disappear.     7394.554746408759
nor will the waste disappear.     13465.948461619062
but, otherwise, we must all of us, as taxpayers or citizens, foot the bill.     11698.318134027657
what is more, the costs will be higher the longer we wait before taking action.     2186.547289737599
that is what i should like to say by way of introduction.     5565.333684182712
i also want to comment on the question as to whether there is to be any retroactive legislation.     3415.731646068758
if we assume that, on average, a car has a life of eleven years, do members, indeed does anyone, believe that we should wait for eleven years before we get to grips with this problem?     7030.4761511366905
do you think that, when we legislate about chemicals, we should say that we are ignoring those chemicals which are already on the market and that we are legislating only for what is to be produced in the future?     2976.831088827048
clearly, we must consider the problem which exists here and now and the challenge presented by the existing range of cars.     3816.020868407593
it is still not an insurmountable problem.     5708.887094128119
we can deal with it.     4736.209354602718
we already have an infrastructure.     2034.9996011487283
we have what we require in order to deal with end-of-life cars.     10844.500868750652
i naturally hope that the result of today' s vote will be a good one.     8011.989089363734
if i have not already done so, i would also thank mr karl-heinz florenz for the hard work he has put into this proposal on the committee on the environment, public health and consumer policy.     2370.4784021436035
in fact, it is in many respects pioneering work when it comes to re-use and manufacturer' s liability.     8775.164563809754
i believe it will have very positive and noticeable effects on the environment.     4671.913483109773
we cannot continue to close our eyes to these matters.     10787.441420142852
as i have said, we have both the knowledge and the resources to cope with these issues.     2740.5120496070576
after the first reading in the european parliament in 1999, the commission' s proposal ended up in an awkward political situation in the council.     6578.426817032878
however, we obtained a well-balanced common position in july during the finnish presidency.     7211.1614225651465
now, we must ensure that the present legislative process is concluded successfully.     1975.9203286207712
a total of 48 amendments have been tabled.     872.1314731344886
the commission can adopt 10 amendments in their entirety.     11339.501945782304
three amendments can be partially approved, and one can be approved in principle.     2855.083668896079
certain amendments concern improvements to the commission' s proposal which we adopted as early as at the first reading or else they reintroduce into the directive such parts of the original proposal as the council has removed.     6619.886447092642
this applies to amendment no 5, excluding section 1; 8 and 9, section 1; 10 and 12, section 3; 15, 16, 20, 22, 24 and 25.
these amendments can all be approved.     3805.191560933032
amendments nos 6 and 21 are new; the commission can approve these.     8587.37006153118
the commission can also approve amendment no 26 with certain editorial changes.     3767.835905149038
i want to emphasise that a number of the other amendments affecting the proposal' s basic components involve a considerable watering-down of the level of environmental protection aimed at by the common position.     8833.837723665069
they cannot therefore be approved.     2588.0444839823285
parliament has traditionally made a major contribution to strengthening environmental legislation in europe.     3038.573116437212
it would amaze and depress me if it were not to do so today.     455.052974697349
i am very concerned about certain amendments from parliament which bring the absolutely basic pillars of the present proposal into question.     3239.7828651407267
these concern, for example, the phasing-out of heavy metals, manufacturer' s liability and the requirement for recycling.     42386.94529979393
i want to deal with these amendments on a group-by-group basis.     5750.883337608828
amendments nos 4, 11, 12, 13, 28, 30, 32, 37, 42 and 48 concern the phasing-out of heavy metals.     41529.52461351064
amendment no 12 involves a delay of at least ten years in bringing the provision into force.     4705.605897380247
amendments nos 13, 28, 32, 37, 42 and 48 involve introducing unnecessary conditions and exceptions.     23340.41173252239
this would mean that the process of replacing dangerous substances would be implemented more slowly.     4285.147332305966
amendments nos 4, 11 and 30 mean that heavy metals have to be separated from the waste before recycling.     25996.2328264643
the commission considers that the type of phasing-out proposed in the common position is easier to apply from a technical point of view.     4422.427396881672
amendments nos 17, 18, 27, 34, 36, 38, 44 and 45 relate to the manufacturer' s liability.     289835.7282740686
the compromise reached by the council is fair but fragile.     3888.761276459005
the commission does not consider that the amendments would improve the balance without, at the same time, creating tensions.     3038.2397000480855
i regret the confusion which arose recently due to internal documents from the commission being wrongly used, so creating uncertainty about the commission' s attitude to the issue.     8338.278028039653
i want to emphasise that, as early as 1997, the commission proposed a special clause concerning manufacturer' s liability and that the commission entirely supports the common position.     8367.841729752912
this does not impose disproportionate costs upon manufacturers.     26516.500545363808
far from it.     1307.7813176881873
amendments nos 39, 40, 46 and 47 concern quantified objectives.     45335.82866316635
the high level of protection aimed at by the common position would be very much undermined if the recycling objective for the year 2006 were removed.     4085.4043630444553
these amendments would also make the objectives awkward to deal with and difficult to supervise.     6432.740060272566
allow me also - listen carefully to what i am saying now, because i have heard this reiterated in the debate - allow me to comment on the issue of vintage and "cherished" cars as mentioned in amendments nos 3, 7, and 9, section 2, and 35.
such vehicles are not covered by the definition of waste and are not therefore included under the directive.     4608.517612596452
so, whatever has been stated in this regard, vintage cars and motorcycles are not included under the directive.     8769.583516141094
we do not consider that amendments nos 2 and 14 add anything to the directive.     4719.9920864844435
amendment no 23 involves the commission' s having to adopt quality standards for reusable components.     18117.866898521683
this is not covered by the directive.     1796.1593935363499
an ad hoc directive should therefore be demanded from the european parliament and the council.     3686.626767075725
amendments nos 29, 31 and 41 water down the requirements concerning the dismantling of vehicles.     19597.553214357198
there is a danger of amendments nos 31 and 41 reducing the chances of recycling plastic, tyres and glass.     65657.22425368104
finally, the commission cannot approve amendments nos 1 and 33 for reasons to do with legal clarity; nor amendment no 19, which the commission considers to be unnecessary at this stage.     5625.738832938051
furthermore, amendment no 43 does not fall within the directive' s area of applicability.     39073.54507842971
thank you very much, commissioner.     434.5214474481301
the debate is closed.     4906.850855896859
we shall now proceed to the vote.     3010.3404121960853
vote
