- (fr) mrs mccarthy, ladies and gentlemen, i am very pleased that the urban community initiative is being continued as this aims to support the social and economic conversion of cities and neighbourhoods in crisis, all with a view to achieving sustainable urban development.     11139.218701893347
with some 80% of the population of europe living in an urban area, cities are at the forefront of economic, social and cultural change in europe.     3215.325608468923
at the same time, the social and economic problems facing european society are more pronounced in urban areas.     1355.299950503692
many european cities are divided into neighbourhoods characterised by high-added-value activities and high-income residents and other districts characterised by low incomes, high unemployment, substandard and overcrowded housing and high levels of welfare dependency.     10320.143447944727
the concentration of economic and social problems in certain urban areas calls for targeted intervention taking full account of the complexity of the problems involved.     4988.093509551372
this is why the european parliament successfully called for the urban community initiative or cip to be extended during the reform of the structural funds.     3436.9666538564147
the success of urban during the 1994-1999 programming period is indisputable.     2320.2667360965106
the results are clear in terms of the improvement of the quality of life in the target areas.     3222.113581686563
this cip has encouraged the development of best practice models in the economic, social and environmental sectors.     6642.341822067241
it has also had the distinction of strengthening the role of local authorities, the non-governmental sector and local communities and of supporting new partnership approaches to urban regeneration.     5365.710359561688
the new initiative will continue to pursue these goals by reinforcing them, while particularly promoting equal opportunities between men and women and integrating socially marginalised and disadvantaged groups.     10660.968763365687
we can therefore welcome its adoption.     13017.332303320492
we must, however, admit that in financial terms we cannot proclaim a victory!     9629.796573182131
the total appropriation allocated is not up to the challenge.     5801.134994923424
the amount for the 1994-1999 period was eur 900 million and will be eur 700 million for the 2000-2006 period.     1935.0860652119743
this represents a drop of 30%!     14053.838341957198
this reduction in funding has led to a reduction in the number of programmes in the new urban initiative.     2728.1446364680924
the chosen threshold of 50 projects seems too low.     16461.255050438245
it needs to be increased to reflect regional and local factors whilst maintaining financial awards to the member states.     4533.107206447619
in view of this concentration on a limited number of projects, the publication and dissemination of the results of the new urban community initiative will perhaps be particularly important to achieve a multiplier effect.     6724.077360274459
the mccarthy report on the urban community initiative gives us an opportunity to reflect on the appropriateness of community intervention in urban matters.     5837.562093823538
the situation of certain urban areas is alarming, with the social distress taking the form of unemployment, misery and crime.     7344.932711675877
drug dealing in particular fuels insecurity and delinquency.     42276.93237991407
faced with these social challenges, the effectiveness of the structural funds is still in doubt.     3780.3429202368316
subsidiarity should make us realise that the state, exercising its governing functions, and local authorities are the bodies most able to intervene appropriately.     12964.280931458263
the state can ensure public safety and the local authorities can help disadvantaged people.     2452.5744510001527
although we welcome the determination to create a system of exchanges between successful enterprises, the problems specific to each urban area cannot be dealt with in the same way.     4481.900307066168
the urban community initiative is helping to achieve the european union' s desire to communitise urban policy.     11463.155481592818
it would be more appropriate for the eu to concentrate its financial efforts on pre-existing european policies.     1823.044337170596
clearly some people are profiting from the opportunity to indulge in another budget bidding race which is particularly mistimed when the convergence criteria for emu are imposing a very strict slimming regime on the national budgets.     2827.6542232227534
mrs mccarthy is therefore suggesting an increase in the funds allocated to urban and the promotion of this community initiative through a costly communication campaign extolling the benefits of a federal europe.     4602.811720354553
we should remember that the effectiveness of expenditure is not measured by the volume of funding allocated to the project.     8443.35492495879
in fact, the beneficiaries of too many and too high subsidies will eventually just take these for granted.     3079.0437401950835
the aim must not be to help the people but to make them take responsibility.     1830.200341997862
finally, the report specifically highlights actions in favour of ethnic or sociological minorities.     10429.145921813302
we can only condemn a policy on minorities which is inevitably dangerous for social cohesion.     3752.871614389325
on one hand, this policy encourages the integration of immigrants where it should favour their assimilation into the culture of the host country.     5524.510925009914
this would prevent the development of ethnic ghettos which are nothing more than urban powder kegs.     9996.697201628036
on the other hand, it manipulates the principle of positive discrimination.     1871.7636976198983
this is a politically correct fancy which can be just as harmful, as shown by the us precedent.     3661.5502944319765
for all these reasons, the french delegation of the uen group could not approve the mccarthy report.     5914.128544404729
decourrière report (a5-0028/2000)
mr president, the interreg programme is particularly close to our hearts.     4093.694262127606
like many of my colleagues, i am pleased that the european parliament was able to safeguard this initiative.     6678.041264103486
as i live in the sarre-lor-lux sud region of belgium, i am well aware that the regions are where europe is being created.     1228.6704382407268
they are where we live the european experience on a daily basis and where the people make european construction come alive.     2979.8695202390772
these projects must therefore be properly taken into account and must integrate sustainable development.     2302.737005935625
why am i stressing this point?     4861.093115765042
the answer is quite simply because the projects currently being submitted are still too often damaging to the environment due to the way they are submitted.     1707.008178633524
for example, to accept new roads is to accept new pollution within the european union.     2300.930975324363
this goes totally against the policies which we are proposing to fight greenhouse gas emissions, for example.     5744.31117310342
i would also ask the commission to ensure that the environmental pillar of the eu' s policy is integrated into the projects submitted and that the goal of reducing co2 for example forms an experimental added value in the projects proposed.     6220.544382360307
i am particularly thinking in this respect of certain infrastructures which are under construction.     5596.885504328878
we know that some member states are still wavering, for example, between rail and road for routes through vulnerable areas such as the pyrenees, as in the aspe valley.     4491.964500872709
however, i am also thinking of my own region where my government is still debating whether to route the railway or to build a second motorway, the a32.     3567.3303979002258
in this respect too, i would ask the commission to be particularly attentive about ensuring that there is real cohesion between the various policies, specifically in the interreg programmes which are being submitted.     3215.998655752
mr president, i voted for the decourrière report on the community initiative interreg, mainly because of the reply commissioner barnier gave to some members who took the floor and asked for this programme to make greater reference to border regions with maritime frontiers.     5016.13496745671
i voted for it, not just because i was born in a coastal town, genoa, but above all because the frontiers of regions bordered by the sea are frontiers too.     2525.816717776451
these frontiers stretch as far as african states and the middle east: we must take greater account of the fact that it is also important to develop coastal regions in every part of europe.     6595.895542105739
as an mep from a border region, i welcome this third interreg initiative with great interest.     11017.315390326752
we can never talk enough about the difficulties experienced in the past by maritime and landlocked border regions which have been cut off economically, socially and culturally.     5675.071456051214
due to the existence of these borders, they have become peripheral areas within the countries to which they belong.     9108.8032325218
this has too often led the national authorities to neglect these regions in national policy.     3222.3177046826877
this is why the interreg community initiative was launched in 1990.
this programme was designed to encourage cross-border, transnational and interregional cooperation and to promote balanced development of the community territory in order to enhance economic and social cohesion in the union.     6358.971201238705
interreg serves principally to fund community schemes to develop smes, education and training, cultural exchanges, action to combat health problems, the protection and improvement of the environment, energy, transport and telecommunications networks.     6916.656818220522
i must stress that interregional cooperation significantly encourages the involvement of local and regional authorities in the process of european integration.     5713.878663532435
more active participation by these authorities in community initiatives should be promoted, bearing in mind that in many cases they have a very limited capacity for cooperation because of the different legal frameworks and different levels of development on either side of the border.     2482.0356946158095
in cross-border cooperation, greater emphasis must be placed on improving the conditions for the economic activity of the small and medium-sized undertakings which are the job creators.     4148.665686404815
in the same respect, given that structural unemployment is responsible for 50% of all unemployment, the interreg funds made available must be sufficient to supplement national measures promoting employment.     2982.237310799691
obstacles too often caused by double taxation and social protection are preventing, braking or hindering cross-border mobility.     3721.198815115981
i hope that the projects included within the programmes can help to provide solutions to these problems and to implement in practical terms the principle of free movement of workers which is in a bad way in my region!     2666.5491730051594
interreg resources must also contribute to the development of a european research area.     3927.604828755761
finally, and in particular, expectations in the regions about this initiative are high given that small towns not eligible for objective 2 funding are hoping to obtain compensation through interreg!     5945.729978585191
the challenges are therefore immense.     11722.556264190416
the integration of the border regions will be an essential element in the development of a future european regional development policy!     1942.0642489516038
i hope everyone, like mr decourrière, whom i congratulate, will realise this!     7264.317795597647
- (fr) the interreg community initiative is just one element of an ill-fated european regional policy.     3888.5109793645306
this apparently generous policy, officially intended to help problem regions, is a fool' s bargain for french taxpayers.     7011.670659390131
france contributes 17% of the european budget yet will receive only 8% of the regional structural funds.     1708.745177524086
between 1994 and 1999, our regions annually received an average of ff 15.4 billion but will receive only ff 14.7 billion between 2000 and 2006.
my region of nord-pas-de-calais will be particularly affected with french hainaut losing the aid paid to objective 1 regions.     4797.876936860035
this loss is unjustified in a region whose main activities have been ruined by free-trade europe.     9840.591100618063
the european regional policy also reinforces the centralism of brussels with which the regional authorities are invited to negotiate directly on the use of the structural funds.     2870.689070735408
this is a europe of regions in which the regions, which are weaker than the nation states, will easily submit to brussels.     5291.686578332147
the interreg initiative, launched in 1990 to prepare the border regions for a europe without borders and therefore without nations, sits comfortably within this philosophy.     14886.10381460057
however, mr decourrière does show some common sense by denouncing the failings of the brussels technocrats.     26412.008895828014
these failings will result in particular in the late implementation of interreg iii and therefore in financial losses for the regions in receipt of aid.     9295.431929266444
we also agree with the rapporteur about asking for more attention to be paid to small and medium-sized undertakings and, of course, about refusing to use outside service providers.     3331.580710660751
these are the procedures which caused the corruption scandal in the previous commission.     2404.777014434798
however, these isolated elements which make sense and for which we voted, in no way remedy the eurofederalist philosophy which characterises the community initiatives, particularly interreg.     8630.286403112916
this is why the national front voted against the report.     3356.9265154144045
the european parliament has delivered an opinion on the commission guidelines for the interreg community initiative concerning cross-border, transnational and interregional cooperation.     6233.412133683942
i want to defend an amendment tabled by my group on the maritime aspect of this community initiative.     4068.633384475221
this does not involve the concerns about the future of the atlantic arc but rather the need to integrate the principle of maritime borders into strand a on cross-border cooperation.     2869.9679230097236
in the commission guidelines, few maritime areas are eligible for interreg iiia.     26166.014857096212
the amendments to the decourrière report aim to make interreg more maritime-oriented.     7924.765060524344
this is an important development which must be supported as it is the future of the eu which is at stake.     1798.1050269811776
i am aware of the commission' s reluctance on this subject as it announced during the symposium on the presentation of interreg iii last november.     20460.95811775843
however, i must point out that preventing the recognition of maritime borders amounts to denying the existence of an area which has great potential for projects and innovation.     4033.9077691071034
one example of this is the 'celtic' area which encompasses the territories of brittany in france, cornwall and devon in the united kingdom and cork and waterford in ireland.     2606.146274729703
this is an area whose locally-based economy is characterised by dependence on the fishing industry and the importance of the agri-foodstuffs sector.     10482.142101749554
it also has excellent cultural and relational links, through twinnings for example.     964.3855108415831
interreg iii, strand a, would allow the regions in this area to advance a number of structural projects needed to develop small and medium-sized undertakings and to promote research and technical development through knowledge transfer.     9259.192773290979
the european commission should therefore be able to participate in the construction of port and airport infrastructure to provide links between regions.     8468.666218941404
this policy would have important economic consequences for the fishing industry in brittany in that catches could be unloaded at irish advance bases in order to then be transported to the breton agri-foodstuffs industry.     6012.935784601337
the integration of the maritime border into strand a would finally allow the western periphery to be recognised as a unique and important economic area which is confronted by the 'continentalisation' of the european union.     4103.140375071969
this would show respect for these peripheral areas which are rather concerned about enlargement to the east.     3855.953689697303
the maritime world has great potential.     4303.949782111409
we therefore must not leave it out of innovative and pilot community programmes which will allow the new geographical and economic map of europe to be defined.     2225.6133588913153
.     1000.0
(da) the european project began as a partnership between states.     5432.928396226755
this partnership has ensured peace and stability in our part of europe for over 50 years.     3619.386922375294
as a result of the economic and political success in our part of the world, of which the eu is just one proof, and in view of the ever greater proliferation of our western values, competition on the world market has grown considerably within the last few decades.     3251.843360593265
the eu should be prepared to meet that competition.     4304.254639186457
we can only do that by intensifying co-operation within the community.     4552.712509420204
it should not be about greater integration of the eu countries in the form of a federation but about using our economic potential across national borders.     2402.455312713066
it is about co-operating on joint projects and about unifying forces on all sides.     5881.545015592861
a programme such as interreg is there precisely to support regional development projects across national borders, projects which make it possible to build centres of economic growth at european level which will be able to take up the challenge from our competitors on a global scale.     5369.387853932475
i therefore welcome the continuation of the programme.     5665.921229832865
.     1000.0
(fr) mr decourrière' s report is not without merit: at the end of a clear and comprehensive presentation of the interreg initiative and its role in opening up border regions, it comes out in favour of smes in paragraph 16.
i and my colleague dominique souchet, who is well versed in this matter, have tabled five amendments highlighting the role of smes and craft trades within the framework of interreg, the importance of cooperation between undertakings and the need to involve economic and social partners in the design and implementation of the programmes and i welcome the fact that these amendments were adopted unanimously.     7923.869687871661
nonetheless, the french delegation in our group was unable to accept the fact that the commission and the federalist faction have diverted the interreg initiative from its original purpose in order to detract still further from the political role of the nations.     8211.853155022181
we have no intention of giving brussels the job of regulating regional planning in the member states, as suggested in recital of the report.     3676.1946898300857
we only ask one thing of the commission: that it confine itself to ensuring that the implementation of joint policies does not upset the balance of regional planning in our territories.     3823.3773612951773
the destructive effects of the cap and emu on the balance between regions, especially on the vitality of the more rural, the more remote and the least populated regions, is proof positive of the fact that such ambitions are not a sinecure, far from it.     2181.827534014599
we are therefore unable to approve the development of strand iiic of the community initiative encouraging interregional cooperation under the responsibility of the commission and sidelining the member states.     9723.193783169185
this infatuation with strand iiic is all the stranger in that the rapporteur himself acknowledges in his explanatory statement that 'the draft guidelines do not provide any indication of possible fields of co-operation' (p.17) and that 'the responsibilities are diffuse and vague' (p.18).     10387.01453927218
calling under these circumstances, as he does in paragraph 20, for the budget for this strand to be increased is another example of the irresponsible and ideological outbidding tactics which are customary in this house.     4628.597728041661
lastly, there is the desire expressed both in the commission document and mr decourrière' s report of using interreg for peace and reconstruction in the balkans: i do not think that the structural funds should be used to repair the damage which the americans inflicted on serbia during bombardments which were as criminal as they were ineffectual.     5324.888074045271
it is up to washington, not us, to assume responsibility for a conflict which was triggered merely to serve its interests.     3694.5340899290145
the french delegation in our group is therefore unable to support the decourrière report and will choose to abstain during the final vote.     6387.747526078977
procacci report (a5-0025/2000)
mr president, i voted for the procacci report on rural development, not only because i endorse the major policy lines of the leader programme, but also because it complements the urban programme, which i spoke about earlier.     4768.055400206615
just as elderly people in cities are very isolated, so the elderly are fast becoming the sole inhabitants of rural areas because young people are moving to the cities: they are drawn by the bright lights, and the pubs, and the countryside and rural areas are being emptied.     5485.333792626349
i therefore believe that it is very important for everyone to back this european union plan and for it to be further developed.     999.4299697283147
- (pt) although this community initiative, leader +, follows on from previous initiatives, it has some unique features.     14736.940728717385
one of these of course is the reduction by around one half in appropriations made available even though they will cover a longer period.     2528.0160676200503
nevertheless, new objectives have been included, specifically the financing of the nature network 2000, for which all rural regions will be eligible.     2928.8469436070222
in spite of the fact that this community initiative is aimed at rural development, it does not address the issues of agriculture and agricultural production in a consistent way, and this is unacceptable.     5287.398450916458
without agriculture, the rural world will disappear.     6961.000967777725
this means that any strategy for rural development must be based on agriculture' s internal potential while at the same time developing as many other activities as possible in order to prevent the desertification of the rural world.     4925.225442973751
this is why we considered it essential to improve the report by means of our proposals deliberately to include agricultural activity and farmers in the strategy for rural development and to request more appropriations in order not to threaten the survival of the programme in those rural regions which were covered before.     3425.933960695585
we also want to ensure that the new programme continues to prioritise the less-favoured regions.     4716.630570144754
our position on leader is a product of our more general assessment of the eu' s overall agricultural policy.     9878.735365598352
both leader 1 and leader 2, which were implemented in the 1990s, were ingredients of the new cap that emerged after its review in 1992 and, along with other respective measures, they constituted the so-termed second pillar of the eu' s rural development policy.     7775.214558309034
their real aim was to moderate and disguise the new cap' s disastrous consequences and the disorientation of small and medium-scale farmers, and not to develop the rural areas or retain the agricultural population therein as was hypocritically declared in their objectives.     4723.185563060647
this is proved by the fact that in the areas where those initiatives were implemented, farming incomes and employment decreased rapidly, which resulted in their depopulation.     6086.563532887574
a characteristic example is greece, which as an objective 1 country was included as a whole in the leader community initiatives, and where the average annual reduction in employment in the farming sector reached approximately 2.3%, while between 1994 and 1999 farming incomes decreased by 15.2%.     3876.282185176383
we think leader + will be even more ineffectual than leader 1 and leader 2, for the following reasons.     3921.125847943415
the real aims of leader + are the same as those of leaders 1 and 2, in other words to moderate and disguise the negative consequences of the cap to be implemented in the context of agenda 2000 and the wto.     3308.817446200266
the present cap, however, is worse than its predecessor and it also starts from a worse basis, granted that the 1992 cap review and the gatt agreement in 1995 have created serious problems and impasses for the agricultural economy.     3540.283692760897
the selection criteria and the activities that are subsidised under leader do, in the best case, moderate some partial problems of the countryside, which are of secondary importance, but in the worst case they degenerate into expenditure for the sake of public relations and pandering to conscience.     9595.26089970819
in no case, however, are they programmes for the integrated development of the selected areas and they do not create permanent jobs in the countryside because most of the activities there are not productive in nature.     3163.4491864609936
the actual appropriations available for leader + compared with leader 2 are smaller despite their increase by 15% (from eur 1 775 million for leader 2 to eur 2 020 million for leader +).     7313.055856631583
this is because the 15% increase is nominal and not real, granted that average yearly inflation in the community during those years was approximately 2%.     3341.3361128713746
leader + will last a year longer than leader 2.
leader + can cover all the regions of the eu, while leader 2 covered only the objective 1 regions and some objective 5b and 6 regions.     2396.522944987623
we must point out, however, that even if the actual appropriations available for leader + were increased, the community initiative would still be ineffectual because such programmes cannot compensate for or blunt the anti-farming nature of the cap that will be implemented in the context of 'agenda 2000' and the wto.     2715.887680172744
the real outcome of such programmes is to act as a cover-up, to lead people off the scent and to salve consciences.     2217.584873027576
we disagree with many of the report' s views.     54607.540036439466
once more, we point to the negative changes of the cap (1992 - agenda 2000).     4335.241545226377
we do not think leader + will contribute substantively towards solving the countryside' s problems, which are characterised by the decline of farming, which was and should remain the countryside' s basic social and economic branch.     5090.015665334093
for our part, we will tell the farmers about the expediency of these programmes.     6442.213659956461
we will make every effort to improve them as much as possible and prevent them from being wasted, and most important of all, we will try to foster the struggle of farmers against the disastrous cap which is disinheriting them and leading them towards economic decline and the desertion of the countryside.     4216.966405932406
unless this cap is overthrown, no programme can ensure the survival of small and medium-scale farmers and the social and economic regeneration of the countryside.     4456.619885486257
all the members in the union for a europe of nations group voted in favour of almost the whole of the procacci report on the leader + community initiative.     4182.400748327807
however, we amended the draft report adopted by the committee on agriculture and rural development in order to clarify certain points which will make this community initiative more effective.     4305.0188433868425
in particular we felt it was necessary to simplify the administrative and financial procedures which proved to be too cumbersome and slow in the leader ii initiative.     4383.8387785263185
it was also essential to ensure that better account is taken of the development priorities of local operators and that the benefit of leader + funds is not limited solely to agricultural regions with a low population density.     3783.953528381576
amendment no 10 which i tabled on behalf of my group refers to the concept of 'economic and social cohesion' rather than 'regional development' as the european union does not have any recognised competence in this area.     4189.09708974504
amendment no 11 replaces 'government or municipal officials' with 'government officials' .     333422.0745783374
although local action groups should consist of a balanced and representative selection of partners drawn from the different socio-economic sectors of the territory concerned, government officials must be involved at decision-making level, whether they come from the municipalities, departments, regions or national governments.     5323.647210520796
the rapporteur' s phrasing was therefore too limiting.     59869.94450760778
amendment no 12 responds to the expectations of the committee on regional policy, transport and tourism of this house.     15063.707243836028
it specifies the necessary coordination between leader + and community cooperation and partnership programmes such as interreg, phare, sapard or meda.     26849.25093711475
in the territory of member states of the european union, applicant countries or member states of efta, there may be interrelations between the various community initiatives.     2646.706253453225
the real priority for the european continent is to establish an operational level within a european framework and not a global framework as proposed by the rapporteur.     1715.041484713942
the creation of organisations similar to the lags may be usefully encouraged by the european union, provided that the corresponding costs are borne by the various partners.     9918.442752704257
in essence, we are, of course, in favour of this leader + community initiative.     10365.179687065014
the conference on rural development held in cork from 7 to 9 november 1996 set rural development as one of the european union' s priorities since it is essential to preserve the integrity of our agriculture and the whole rural fabric, including infrastructure, public and private services and so on.     6785.77063429007
in this context, in committee we regretted the paltry funding allotted to rural development and supported the amendments calling for an increased budget for this community initiative so that it matches the previous leader i and leader ii initiatives.     3494.645648818602
the budget allocated to leader + for a period of seven years is half that allocated to leader ii for a period of six years.     2948.944984515468
this is unacceptable given the importance of rural development and the consequences for farmers of the fall in prices and aid specified in the reform of the cap following the berlin agreements.     4111.980085826827
finally, among the priority parameters to be introduced at european level to enable local involvement, particular attention must be paid to the quality and originality of projects which have already been implemented by lags within the leader ii initiative, but which could not be concluded due to the timetable and weight of the management mechanisms specified.     6178.361568574966
(the sitting was suspended at 1.40 p.m. and resumed at 3 p.m.)
additives in feedingstuffs
the next item is the report (a5-0015/2000) by mr graefe zu baringdorf, on behalf of the committee on agriculture and rural development, on the proposal for a council directive amending directive 70/524/eec concerning additives in feedingstuffs (com(1999) 388 - c5-0134/1999 - 1999/0168(cns)).     150377.9006407902
mr president, commissioner byrne, under directive 70/524, high-technology additives are treated differently, depending on the date of authorisation.     11018.42324993827
the commission has proposed harmonising the authorisation procedure so that substances licensed before 1988 are put on an equal footing with substances authorised now.     8441.680917266558
as far as we in the committee on agriculture and rural development are concerned, so far so good.     2199.7982808005368
however, the directive also encompasses the authorisation of antibiotics, drugs, growth-promoters and genetically-modified organisms.     86226.3377765366
that makes it a question of health, not just approximating laws, which is why we in the committee on agriculture take the view that the legal base - article 37 - is not admissible here and that the commission should submit its proposal to us on the basis of article 152, that is to say with codecision by parliament.     3312.7951674332703
we asked the opinion of the committee on legal affairs and the internal market, which pointed out that, as far as approximating laws is concerned, article 37 is adequate in this case.     4725.924744237784
however, we in the committee on agriculture have not confined ourselves to approximating laws; because this proposal also encompasses genetically-modified organisms, we have tabled a further proposed amendment which affects the content and hence public health.     7927.088832141636
we negotiated with the commission for two years on the seed directive and came to a compromise regulating the release of genetically-modified organisms in the directive, taking the text on which directive 70/524 is based as a starting point.     5110.999377916461
mr byrne, we fail to understand why the commission is not approximating the text on genetically-modified organisms as part of its harmonisation work.     17724.632866511092
we have now tabled a proposed amendment on precisely this compromise with the commission on the text.     1792.4894363060464
moreover, this text has also been adopted in the forest directive on the use of forest reproductive material and we feel that this text urgently needs to be adopted in this directive as well.     4700.728497968578
the commission pointed out in committee that it plans to present a novel feed directive in the future.     6716.443118824618
however, that should not prevent us from harmonising the text in order to ensure that there is no legal uncertainty between the various directives.     3338.2688680250853
we are therefore assuming that you will accept our proposed amendment with regard to genetically-modified organisms.     10515.18193073396
directive 70/524 still contains the old text because the seed directive was submitted to parliament in 1986 and we then negotiated with the commission until 1988, while the report on this directive was dealt with in 1994 and parliament obviously did not accord genetically-modified organisms the same importance then as it did later when i was rapporteur.     3963.086589877678
we therefore assume, commissioner byrne, that you too will accept our proposed amendment of the legal base.     3131.6096780256753
if you do not, we must reserve the right to refer this report back to committee and then negotiate this sensitive point with you, exactly as we did with the seed directive.     3467.346534583282
i should point out that the text which we have now worked out is a valid compromise, both for your legal service and ours.     2225.1698180566336
in other words, we do not need to re-negotiate the text; we merely need to adopt this text in the directive.     3128.0431984869583
i can hardly wait, commissioner byrne, to hear what you have to say to our proposals.     3900.296204325993
mr president, commissioner, does regulating additives in feedingstuffs come under competition or consumer policy?     5567.670801426397
as far the commission and the legal affairs committee are concerned, it comes under competition.     1564.0059360373707
of course we have to take account of the competitiveness of the feedingstuffs industry by harmonising provisions for substances authorised before and after 1988.
we want to prevent the prices of feedingstuffs from rising and mr doyle' s proposed amendments nos 4 and 5 therefore deserve our support.     7104.320918114839
however, as mr graefe zu baringdorf has said in his report, this is first and foremost a consumer policy matter.     4130.464468193964
food safety must take absolute priority over feedingstuffs in all our discussions.     14210.370567039081
when we talk of transparency in the food chain, that applies from the hayfork to the table fork and consumer protection therefore logically starts with feedingstuffs.     3585.601422093963
the antibiotics, growth promoters and genetically-modified organisms mixed in with feedingstuffs end up in the human food chain.     33741.96897128518
the dioxin scandal brought home to us that we have to get out of this mess once and for all and regulating additives in feedingstuffs is a step in the right direction.     4825.39474807944
according to article 152 of the ec treaty, we are obliged to obviate sources of danger to human health, whereby the accent is clearly on sources.     7695.942599471992
consequently, we must cut off the supply of the original source of danger, i.e.     6500.757412712786
harmful additives in feedingstuffs.     265551.21624210663
otherwise, in my view, we are beavering away at the symptoms, but not the causes.     3194.0984872340864
people are especially wary of gmos.     4870.295864131682
we must confront increasing public awareness of gmos by regulating their use in feedingstuffs accordingly.     36104.842815759606
first, if an additive consists of or contains genetically-modified organisms, then it should only be authorised if it poses no risk to human health or the environment.     6669.816669868631
secondly, it makes sense to issue provisions for genetically-modified feedingstuff additives similar to the regulations in seed marketing legislation, as the rapporteur has said.     16738.087154677032
and thirdly, if we are to have a transparent consumer policy, we need to label genetically-modified feedingstuffs.     8590.490478699703
a clear declaration on feedingstuffs will allow double freedom of choice, both for the party using the feedingstuff and the subsequent consumer.     5649.36507815051
responsible citizens should, in my view, have a choice.     8237.145356316703
we all talk of scandalised citizens who have lost confidence in food safety.     2475.957610730758
by regulating additives in feedingstuffs consistently, we can help significantly to restore confidence.     21669.74090777482
i too, therefore, can hardly wait to hear what you have to say to our proposed amendments.     2200.722471334854
consequently, we too shall also vote to reserve our right to refer the proposal back to committee if need be.     2664.9490361946973
mr president, this proposal to amend the 1970 directive on additives in feedingstuffs is the first in a whole series of proposals on the subject of feedingstuffs.     10433.807560460546
in other words, we shall be discussing other proposals here in parliament over the next few months.     1883.7879311963209
that this subject is of paramount importance is borne out by the huge public attention which the dioxin, antibiotic, sludge and other scandals have attracted.     10525.87095560026
in other words, this is an important facet of public health protection, which is why we believe that article 152 should be taken as the legal base, not article 37, as proposed by the commission.     2176.8303043243227
the amendment which the commission has proposed, namely equal treatment for additives authorised before or after 31 december 1987, is uncontested and has our full support.     3299.756052128015
however, the committee on agriculture and rural development has voted unanimously on some important amendments to the commission proposal.     4261.677149828774
the regulation proposed by the commission conceals the danger of creating a marketing monopoly for certain additives.     12234.972298599672
only companies which obtained the original authorisation will have the right to apply and companies which obtained authorisation at a later date will be excluded.     4279.5418123359295
this sort of monopoly, which could lead, for example, to an increase in the price of feedingstuffs, should be prevented by granting provisional authorisation to all companies which had marketed a specific substance by 1 april 1998.
this would then apply until the re-assessment procedure has been completed.     5595.946526358211
the most important amendment to the commission proposal is the inclusion, as recommended by the rapporteur, of genetically-modified organisms in the directive on additives, taking the compromise negotiated between parliament and the commission on authorisation for gmos outside the scope of the release directive as the starting point.     7849.011788801653
this already gives us a basis for the authorisation of gmos.     8665.605360162775
it is therefore only logical that we make provision for genetically modified feedingstuff additives in this case, just as we have in regulations already passed in other areas, such as forest reproductive material, to mention just one example.     7748.097872693562
the labelling of genetically-modified additives is particularly important in this respect.     17620.255893298243
on the one hand, it enables farmers to make an informed decision as to whether or not they wish to use such feedingstuffs and, on the other hand, it enables consumers to avoid foodstuffs produced using gmos.     6167.158611614867
finally, our group should like once again to thank the rapporteur, who has gone to a great deal of trouble.     5702.95982152029
i think that we can all hardly wait to hear mr byrne' s reply.     4842.38194386312
mr president, the report deals mainly with formalities, in other words, the manner in which additives will be authorised.     6278.726095529642
it is less concerned with the types of additives and how they work.     4689.662519110376
i should like to take this opportunity to stress how important it is that, in future, all the directives are dealt with under article 152, since both food safety and environmental issues require a coherent policy and a comprehensive overview.     4302.288783787565
we cannot carry on as we have done up until now, that is to say dealing with each detail on its own, often only after the harm has already been done.     2581.845463151425
the rapporteur places the emphasis on the testing and labelling of gmos in feed additives.     67628.22026671628
however, i should like once again to raise the issue of antibiotics.     7137.714363712349
certainly, five of the nine antibiotics that were originally permitted in animal feed have now been banned.     10442.42568526099
it is nevertheless extremely important to ban the remaining four, both in the interests of human health and for the sake of the animals themselves.     2505.052644211345
we now know that resistance to antibiotics poses a very serious threat to people's health, particularly in the case of small children.     6228.365909616055
a number of member states are already able to show conclusively that improper practices in connection with the keeping of livestock are totally unnecessary.     19107.474307928052
several countries phased out antibiotics in animal feedingstuffs a long time ago and others are in the process of successfully doing the same.     6844.611823346916
we talk a great deal about the precautionary principle but, in the case of antibiotics, it has long since been forgotten.     9927.266096508714
however, there is another environmental principle at stake, namely the exchange principle.     12120.441302917941
i should like to say a few words about coccidiostats.     12693.49259024395
they are a zootechnical product which is not totally necessary.     1324.452585032722
there is a substitute, which is used to vaccinate chickens.     2030.9031797820678
although it is a little more expensive, it does not harm the environment.     2531.8421267216877
coccidiostats now accompany the manure that is spread on the fields.     13276.458064611044
in that way, they end up in our water supplies which, as we all know, are our most vital resource.     3202.4119709226356
mr president, commissioner, ladies and gentlemen, the report on additives in animal feedingstuffs concerns the technical and economic problem of restoring the competitive balance between different additives in feedingstuffs and between producers of these additives.     12482.152566518911
yet after the serious dioxin crisis which affected belgium and other european countries last summer we cannot just view this issue in terms of competition.     3485.61995679721
you may recall, even though this passed virtually unnoticed by the general public, that the commission' s scientific experts discovered industrial residues of dioxin-loaded chemical lime in the pulp of citrus fruit imported from brazil.     24393.636723049975
we must therefore closely examine all the elements of the chain involved in the manufacture of feedingstuffs for production animals.     8109.210752626084
incidentally, it is ironic that the problem is much less serious for the feedingstuffs of our dogs, cats and other domestic animals.     3124.350541900858
is it because of cut-throat and globalised competition that industrialists have allowed this sector to become a way of recycling all the waste from the agri-foodstuffs industry?     10964.784818674341
such a serious issue cannot be limited to a technical debate, even though it is the directive itself which limits the authorisation of antibiotics and other growth promoters.     3220.914562952012
the precautions which we are preparing to take in respect of feedingstuffs must also be applied to the food for the very farm animals which reach our plates.     2073.9851089118297
as forcefully underlined by this report, labelling is an absolute necessity so that each farmer on his holding is aware of all the ingredients in the feed which he is giving his livestock.     15629.482980803848
he must also know whether his bag of protein granules includes gmos as these components could represent a risk to public health.     5775.119185626038
in any case, the precautionary principle requires these products to be clearly traceable at all stages of their marketing.     3618.176081970254
however, before labelling we need clear rules.     13554.544367742783
straight questions must be asked about the real end-use of animal meal, for example.     6401.07196653603
although we are looking at all the problems caused by these products in ethical and health terms, it is their use which must be questioned today.     3110.555615630869
in this respect, the chapter of the white paper on food safety which deals with feedingstuffs must serve as a working basis for going beyond mere commercial questions.     9934.280359155493
the commission has on many occasions used the farm-to-table example which our colleague in the ppe group has just underlined.     4549.602925859902
this is a good idea but it must be made to be meaningful and, to do this, the problems linked to animals and those linked to consumer health must be dealt with at the same time.     1518.1734883072065
this is why it is essential to approve amendment no 2 to recital 4 which avoids the risks of the monopoly which would arise if companies which were the first to be authorised to place an additive on the market were to remain the only ones able to continue marketing it during the re-evaluation procedure.     3799.354701539495
also, amendments nos 4 and 5 to the new article 2a must be approved as these will ensure that genetically modified additives are clearly identified as such, thus allowing users to make fully informed decisions.     4364.140892265106
mr president, because of the availability of high-technology additives in the feedingstuffs industry, detailed information on their use is needed from the actors, and as we know there are a lot of them, so that we can put a stop to practices which infringe community law.     5490.843861052568
this directive on additives in feedingstuffs cannot achieve this on its own.     8685.585060809786
other directives will follow and i believe that the committee on agriculture is well placed to deal with them.     4870.257229375616
up the point at which meat, milk, bread and other products are sold over the shop counter, feedingstuffs and additives are structured, mixed, blended, cut and transported several times.     18325.569681389057
the fight by feedingstuff manufacturers to gain maximum market shares causes a lot of problems, just as it does in the foodstuffs industry.     18573.19369952901
the negative consequences are well known, the positive ones less so.     2021.8408779121003
i am firmly convinced that the most sensible way of protecting the health of european citizens in the ecological chain is through the production of feedingstuffs in the region for the region.     2746.2560858786583
however, more work is needed on this.     1207.5107566563945

.     1000.0
i should first like to thank the committee on agriculture and rural development and its rapporteur, the committee chairman, mr graefe zu baringdorf, for examining the commission's proposal.     9432.151802508062
the commission proposal is, as some of you have said, rather technical.     1233.9161635497626
nonetheless it has a relatively simple objective: to harmonise procedures governing the authorisation of feed additives.     16302.390184199527
at present there is a difference in treatment between authorisations, depending on whether applications were submitted before or after 1 january 1988.
the purpose of the commission's proposal is to harmonise procedures to ensure that no such difference exists.     5624.9716223959485
the scope of the proposed amendment is therefore very limited.     2403.263560370951
five amendments have been presented by parliament.     1860.4488938532443
i regret that the commission is not in a position to accept these amendments, despite the fact that i am fully aware of the views and commitment of parliament, the agricultural committee and, in particular, the rapporteur, mr graefe zu baringdorf, on these issues.     4224.205328144763
i shall deal with each of the individual amendments separately.     5930.355284338576
the first amendment proposes to change the legal base of the proposal by replacing article 37 with article 152.
i would simply point out that the commission proposal makes no reference to health or consumer protection.     2557.178448116057
the proposed amendment is technical and cannot be interpreted as having as its primary objective the protection of public health.     2565.9377675652036
i note that parliament's committee on legal affairs and the internal market also accepts that article 37 is the appropriate legal base.     3508.8263816736585
i remind parliament that in article 152 the primary objective is the protection of health.     4071.7905160616274
i commend to you the arguments set out in the letter from the legal affairs committee which sets out the position, in my view, with precision, clarity and commendable brevity.     4320.574901686875
amendments nos 2 and 3 go much further than the commission proposal since they aim to introduce additional provisions on genetically modified additives.     13659.53368706558
i am the first to accept that gmos are a very important issue.     3840.3338331126456
i also accept and acknowledge that a number of initiatives to update eu legislation in the field of gmos are necessary.     9334.809678835554
however, this technical amendment is not the correct instrument to introduce such initiatives.     3283.887085256286
the commission is of the view that it is premature to amend the rules currently laid down by directive 70/524 on genetically modified additives at this stage.     6776.072909273113
instead it is appropriate to await developments in relation to the common position on the amendment to directive 90/220, which is currently in second reading in parliament.     5630.131611253077
the commission envisages going much further than is currently proposed by parliament in its amendments.     2997.9145618079933
i can also assure parliament that i will ensure that the pertinent provisions of directive 90/220 are included in the proposal for a recasting of directive 70/524 that the commission has proposed in the white paper on food safety to present to parliament before july 2001.
i can also assure parliament that all observations made here today will be fully taken into account.     3322.7221281140137

the commission insists on first evaluating such applications as to their safety and efficacy before giving authorisations.     11543.755040626502

i should just like to deal with some of the specific issues that were raised during the debate.     4038.5789085515303
first of all, regarding the observations made by mr graefe zu baringdorf, i wish to assure him that we will change the procedure for authorisation of gmo additives in the additives directive 70/524, and not in the novel feed directive.     12693.006821349669
this is a directive that deals more specifically with raw materials rather than with additives.     5382.0001282985095
the text taken by parliament is from the directive which was adopted in 1998.
but the vertical legislation in directive 90/220 on how to carry out the environmental risk assessment of gmos has changed since then and is still changing.     2908.124528295606
it is before parliament for second reading.     2347.620168743835
it is my belief that we should await the final outcome of directive 90/220 and in particular, article 11.
reference was also made to the question of whether this proposal could give rise to the risk of the establishment of monopolies.     4160.653218443753
mr kindermann and mrs auroi made reference to this.     104583.42724065774
there are just a few points i should like to make on that issue.     2634.1183801385223
first of all, this matter has been in the pipeline since 1993, so it has not really been sprung on any other commodity producers.     2373.620322925124
indeed any such applicants can still apply in respect of their particular product.     4030.0118997060813
i should also stress that the commission has never approved any application for an authorisation for gmos to be included in additives.     5882.265702631082
mrs roth-behrendt made reference to the question of the appropriate legal base, whether it should be article 37 or article 152.
i again refer to what i said earlier in relation to this and assure her and the house that it would be entirely inappropriate for the commission to seek to justify any measure based on article 37 merely by excluding from the provision any reference to public health.     4470.02686543861
this is an issue that has been looked at on at least two separate occasions by the european court of justice, which has laid down the legal criteria for situations such as this where the appropriate legal base has to be properly taken into account when putting forward legislation.     3245.32643738521
the jurisprudence of the court appears to be quite clear in relation to this.     8713.003273633236
as an overall comment to the suggested amendments here, particularly in relation to the legal base issue, it would be inappropriate, in my opinion, to change the legal base to article 152 in these circumstances, as it would be very likely to fall foul of the objective criteria that have been laid down by the court.     4074.968267445069
a number of you made reference - mr nicholson in particular - to the issue surrounding labelling.     9550.997727064112
directive 70/524 foresees the issue of labelling in the genetic modification of additives.     90550.51224276735
it is already included now: "indication of the particular characteristics due to the manufacturing products".     3986.3289804107753

scrapie
the next item is the report (a5-0023/2000) by mr böge, on behalf of the committee on agriculture and rural development, on the proposal for a european parliament and council directive amending council directive 91/68/ec as regards scrapie (com(1998) 623 - c4-0026/1999 - 1998/0324(cod)).     14015.831454276298
mr president, commissioner, ladies and gentlemen, it was and always will be one of life' s mysteries, one of the unsolved riddles of the follow-up to the bse crisis, that we still have no community rules for the control of scrapie in sheep.     3585.954256424237
in this respect, the commission' s proposals to amend directive 91/68 and delete the reference to scrapie and the report by mrs roth-behrendt on the detection and control of tse infections in general, comply with parliament' s demands in the wake of the follow-up report by the bse committee of inquiry and we expressly welcome this.     11638.27795016917
i should, however, like to add that no new scientific knowledge was needed which proved that the experimental infection of sheep by feeding them bse-infected feed produced a clinical expression of the disease which is indistinguishable from the symptoms of scrapie.     18781.179422015608
no new knowledge was needed, because the theory from the beginning was that bse and scrapie must be related and this theory has increasingly taken hold.     8087.67751097706
from this point of view we are slightly behind the times.     4641.056889162965
i am therefore in complete agreement with the commission' s proposal on scrapie.     26020.931676390755
i should, however, like to add that it only makes sense if we ensure that the new tse regulation allows a reasonable symbiosis between consumer protection and practicable suggestions.     5834.815492509151
we therefore need to take consistent account of six or seven points in the coming debate on the tse proposal, and that of course includes scrapie.     5803.5042796971775
when we discuss the tse regulation, commissioner, we must also clarify that we shortly intend to discuss separate proposals under article 152 as the corresponding legal base for products excluded from this regulation, such as cosmetics, medicinal products or animal feed.     6938.802020026489
we insist on this.     17008.69889527872
as far as i am concerned, that means that fundamental core provisions in this proposed regulation must not be relegated yet again to the annex but included in the body of the proposed regulation because we here in parliament want to and will assume joint responsibility here.     2604.463408393995
it also means that, when we are fighting these diseases, the problem of destroying whole herds in the event of an outbreak of scrapie or bse and the question of defining geographical areas are items which also remain on the agenda.     4573.520200431319
although i welcome the fact that the fight against scrapie is included in the tse regulation, i feel that the commission' s proposals for fighting scrapie are still not consistent enough and i shall submit amendments on behalf of the committee for agriculture and rural development to tighten up the proposals when the roth-behrendt report is debated.     10598.763435808907
i should also like to address two other points.     3710.2033542608797
we expressly welcome the regional classification of the status of member states as regards outbreaks of scrapie or tses.     21441.495707745045
here again the commission is increasing its response to the work carried out by and the demands made by parliament.     2243.1903901400497
finally, in discussing the question of the future application of tests, we should of course bear in mind that we have still not reached the point where tests are generally applicable.     3950.8399878182972
but the tests available now are perfectly able to guarantee security in consumer health policy, albeit not for every individual animal.     5985.023110092232
but when we need to consider if a region can move from a negative epidemiological status to a better category, an epidemiological inspection of the situation in a region in the form of suitable sampling in abattoirs using such tests can provide useful pointers in the political and scientific debate as to where there is a need for preventive consumer protection and, basically, where such tests can be used to help us settle disputes on a proper basis.     3688.016214053746
i would be grateful, commissioner, if you would take these passing comments on board in the report on scrapie and in the general report on the fight against tses and bear in mind that we shall be calling you to account on these points.     3959.7376268819894
mr president, over the years, the following situation has arisen: one subject has developed into a subject of many facets.     5693.891488780914
many of us here in this house have spent years, whether we intended to or not, dealing solely with the consequences of the bse scandal right down to the last detail.     3934.127562308542
and it is still on the agenda today.     824.558529942702
it is therefore hardly surprising that i and mr böge are talking about it and that mr graefe zu baringdorf has just mentioned it in his report.     4179.272310962532
the cast is always the same.     1930.466032753876
although the cause was unfortunate, it was certainly not the worst experience of this parliament.     2048.190487158884
my task as draftsman of the opinion of the environment committee is a very simple one.     3306.836957450595
i agree with the rapporteur and am able to say that the environment committee has asked the committee on agriculture and rural development to agree to the commission proposal.     5153.9277063530335
that saves a great deal of time which i would like to use for other things and which i shall use now somewhat in anticipation, as mr böge has just done.     3827.4586682753365
i have yet to submit my report on tses to the house.     9192.776159939871
at first we were somewhat irresolute and we regret that now.     1144.966120667092
but i have come to the conclusion that it was in fact quite clever timing.     4411.622059227263
this debate does in fact give us an excellent opportunity, mr byrne, to give the commission a few things to think about.     4184.3749660000885
although we did not have a very positive exchange on mr graefe zu baringdorf' s report earlier on and will certainly have to agree to disagree, things may be different with this report.     1832.1947678799702
allow me to say from the outset that the part of the complex report which mr böge dealt with - and this form of collaboration between two committees, with one leading committee and the other giving an opinion and vice versa, worked very well and could perhaps be used as a model for the future - is relatively simple.     3438.013836959996
it will become more difficult once we see the tse proposal, mr byrne.     6255.509908858359
when we have the chance to ask direct questions, the questions which mr böge referred to will be asked.     5565.837221609074
questions will be asked - questions which i shall have to put to you and the commission representatives in committee - on how things stand with the bse status of the member states and other third countries.     2768.843971327265
how will bse status in fact be defined then?     4466.679437657298
do you envisage circumstances under which the bse status will depend on the willingness to apply a bse test?     15577.014844573432
and then there is my standing question to you mr byrne, a question you certainly already know and which will not surprise you.     1662.7954751964307
what about the bse test?     9604.701092992424
when will you make it compulsory?     1450.8880290487796
perhaps you will do so before parliament debates the tse report.     2889.9767508378036
not that i want to put it off, but it would be a gesture, as it were, if you could say: here is the proposed legislation on the bse test.     2508.358467833823
i know that i am a thorn in your side on this, but sometimes that is my job.     4200.440174142852
i say quite emphatically, even after the report by mr graefe zu baringdorf, who is completely involved in the whole idea, that animal feeding, animal rearing, animal production must be suited to the species and must also, above all, cause no harm or danger to human beings.     7322.8237265038415
cosmetic, medical and other products cannot be excluded on the grounds that they cause no harm or are not dangerous.     1952.1902753710442
that is, without doubt, too short, too sweet and too little.     740.9601205279546
you are lucky that mr böge' s report only deals with this on the sidelines; with my report you will have to justify yourself more thoroughly and you will have more trouble doing so.     5758.235112100265
finally, just one more brief comment, mr byrne.     2385.904691143295
we have a new commission.     833.8696608335896
whether or not we will agree with the work carried out by this commission and the president of the commission, only time will tell.     2054.306655009617
but there is one thing we certainly will not let you get away with and i am sure that, as a lawyer and politician you will understand this.     2512.1596674831867
important issues cannot be regulated in annexes.     11335.220446855867
they belong in the legislative text.     10506.77587457738
i might do the same if i were a commissioner, because i could simply change an annex under a comitology procedure and i would not always need to work with parliament, which takes so long and is so difficult.     2069.3784324209446
but you must understand that we cannot let you get away with this.     1640.1501051408654
in other words, whenever i get the chance, i shall take everything out of the annexes and cram it into the legislative text.     2229.0193719205768
and you would be well advised to do likewise from the outset.     3715.7723588507333
mr president, commissioner, i should like to remind you of the composition of our food chain and how important it is for this chain to provide us with safe, healthy food which is produced, as a matter of principle, without any questionable or risky interim or end products.     4885.275292371805
over recent years, these basic principles have often been neglected in animal husbandry and in epidemiological matters because of shoddy practice or greed.     3205.01088359501
the european parliament has already taken a clear stand on bse and i think that we should do likewise on all epidemics in animals.     2823.4896913413068
we should not allow food safety to be compromised because, in the final analysis, it is our health which is at stake.     3146.6434442365808
the commission' s proposal encompasses both the transportation and the placing on the market of sheep and goats and a community regulation to fight scrapie.     24200.783626229888
this is even more important given that there is no clear proof that bse and scrapie are unrelated.     6769.466984029112
animals are bought throughout europe and transported over the entire continent.     17541.705045482733
the spread of an epidemic as the result of bringing in one sick animal can have huge consequences for farmers.     5464.9926534147735
and i should point out that, for the first time in history, a case of scrapie was detected in austria about four weeks ago.     4915.389633212545
scrapie is a particularly insidious disease because the pathogen can remain active in dust for years and is then ingested in food.     6224.706958049214
the disease is always fatal.     15316.895403908387
as there is no vaccination, farmers can only protect themselves by not buying animals from countries in which there are cases of scrapie.     6826.834387490282
even if scrapie cannot be passed on to human beings, the connection with bse has still not been clarified, as i have already said.     5183.709473947295
as a farmer myself, i am most concerned that consumer health should not be put at risk.     3755.5798056234285
i am in favour of preventive measures, starting with feeding which is suitable for the species, rather than measures to combat an epidemic once it has broken out.     11609.498005222242
i therefore welcome the commission' s initiative to create a new legal base for combating scrapie.     16852.139784875006
mr president, commissioner, mr böge' s report on measures to prevent tse diseases is based on the proposals of the european parliament' s temporary bse committee.     7570.948807867352
mr böge was responsible for some invaluable work on this committee, and it is a relief to see that the job of monitoring the work of the bse committee in the committee on agriculture and rural development has fallen to him: he is the one with the experience.     3257.6464149510716
i would have liked my speech to be part of a discussion that would have also treated the second part of the commission' s proposal.     7216.86891031029
i do not think it appropriate to have to conduct in part the same discussion when mrs roth-behrendt' s report from the committee on the environment, public health and consumer policy is featured in the plenary session.     3725.5303334068108
in the previous speech mrs schierhuber said that scrapies is an insidious disease.     3122.517331711265
it is indeed.     887.6995979871776
for me as a finn, and a member who has been active in matters concerning agriculture and the health and transportation of animals, it is most important that those eu states where tses do not occur at all will in the future too be guaranteed the right to inspect as fully as is necessary the transportation of live animals.     2798.5729008889534
i wish to stress how important it is for us to remain free of disease.     5314.0376629790635
the possibility, if necessary, of having additional inspections of animals in these areas is not a question of artificial protectionism or the restriction of freedom of movement, as there really is justification for it.     5195.135510981345
the inspections must be seen as being fair and cost-effective action to promote the wellbeing of animals and prevent increased expenditure for the eu.     2690.214039114884
on behalf of the european liberal democrats, we can support the commission' s proposal on mr böge' s report, but in the forthcoming debate on mrs roth-behrendt' s report we must look again at the position of those countries where tses do not occur.     10992.797887210168
mr president, i too would like to offer my congratulations to the rapporteur.     4310.777197499933
there is no doubt that mr böge and the rapporteur for the committee on the environment are now tremendous experts in this field.     3816.954623733285
it is very good that they are continuing to monitor this issue on behalf of parliament because it is extremely important.     3194.758505284064
regardless of the many other concerns we may have, bse has given us a terrible legacy that has to be dealt with and resolved.     5543.219274105641
it is quite clear that the incidence of scrapie in sheep has been a contributing factor to the overall problem.     7705.585702221507
i welcome the legal base being set and the comprehensive way the new rules will, i hope, address this situation.     3590.4196336228547
we must ensure that nothing can enter the food chain or the animal compound feed to allow what we have experienced in the past to happen again in the future.     8553.60139483468
no-one would want to see what happened to farmers with bse happen ever again.     4888.287876265495
we have to bring in the most stringent rules and we have to make them work.     2273.0770188879897
it is extremely important both for the producer and the consumer that we restore confidence and the only way we can do it is by rigorously tackling the problem and resolving it.     2679.5330876198145
bse has certainly not gone away.     14192.311734289051
you can see it in different countries.     1765.019144445946
i do not want to point the finger at any particular country, but many countries are now facing similar problems to those we have had in the united kingdom.     4417.437430244408
it will be there for some time to come.     1271.0053805598461
we have to ensure that the whole system of traceability, of following the animal from birth until slaughter and right through the whole food chain, is part of that confidence-building mechanism.     5375.459037036966
if we are not able to achieve that then we will have tremendous trouble in the future.     3325.55891139858
finally, i have certainly believed for a long time, coming from an agricultural background myself, that if we had fed the animals the proper food and if the animal feedstuffs had been made up of the proper compounds we would never have had bse in the first place.     2443.4640016205794
it was never the responsibility of the farmer, it was the food compounders who caused this problem and we have to ensure that it never happens again.     1787.7945381611878
.     1000.0
mr president, i am pleased to have been given this opportunity to discuss a subject where there is good cooperation between parliament and the commission, namely combating tses.     5129.946643954451
i should also like to thank mr böge for his work on this issue.     4819.510140277812
considerable progress has been made on our proposal for a european parliament and council regulation under article 152 of the treaty on the prevention and control of tses.     2095.0699084608987
this proposal addresses all tse risk in all animals at all stages of the production chain.     10446.98897366865
