community policy in the field of water
the next item is the recommendation for second reading (a5-0027/2000) on the common position adopted by the council with a view to the adoption of a european parliament and council directive on establishing a framework for community action in the field of water policy (9085/3/1999 - c5-0209/1999 - 1997/0067(cod)) (rapporteur: mrs lienemann).     4472.382076604753
- (fr) mr president, commissioners, ladies and gentlemen, water will be one of the main environmental and world issues of the twenty-first century.     9617.588012882046
climate change, global resources, river quality and groundwater aquifer quality are major challenges which threaten our common future.     7425.672429139884
we will either be able to restore water quality to suit the planet' s ecosystem or we will experience a whole range of adverse events which will threaten the development of certain regions, the living conditions of the inhabitants of other regions and even, more fundamentally, the global balance.     7639.283019720663
to support this statement, i would point to an excellent report drafted by our colleague, mário soares, in the international bodies on the state of the seas and oceans.     4640.973022913403
when talking about climate change, we have rightly referred to the greenhouse effect and the state of the atmosphere.     3839.261095710065
yet we also know that the deterioration of the oceans will significantly destabilise whole areas of our planet.     6090.825382258755
europe must therefore lead by example to some extent, both because it must promote a certain development model and because it is itself facing serious problems of pollution and deterioration of its waters, including its groundwater, surface waters and seas.     4448.26370100844
furthermore, europe has signed international conventions, in particular the ospar convention in which it made certain commitments.     7941.097237344554
in this it said that in a few years' time we must have stopped dumping pollution and halted the increase of pollution and we must have reached a near zero level of toxic and dangerous substances.     3305.420922830592
despite having signed these international agreements, this directive and the specific policies in the field reveal that europe cannot achieve the objectives set or must defer these for so long that the very credibility of its signature of these international conventions is threatened.     5432.210757079123
this is why in the debate on the framework directive on water policy, the european parliament has since the first reading demanded coherence between the framework directive and the international objectives, particularly with regard to respect for ospar.     2759.7419819631255
this convergence must be effective and specific and must commit us to action.     2737.194588144717
the european union' s water policy is not starting from zero.     16305.491213955871
many directives have already been adopted.     956.6315443644311
furthermore, the commission wants to make these directives more understandable and more compatible with each other and to give them clearer objectives.     3185.8771565603065
the preparation of this framework directive is therefore prompted by a desire for rationalisation.     7940.305293147162
however, at first reading this house insisted that the current directive should not only be compatible with the commitments made, like ospar, but should allow us to reverse the course of affairs.     5530.004822554491
for despite these directives and frequent declarations about the effort which must be made in terms of water protection, when we look at the state of the environment in europe we can see that the objectives have not been achieved.     2190.469269587592
in many cases the situation has deteriorated and we therefore cannot settle for just rationalisation of the texts.     5679.888234845179
we must set objectives which are equal to the challenges facing us.     4268.328072721085
yet we do not have much time.     938.1770940151686
we could, as the commission has proposed, accept these overlong deadlines which the commission and the council have extended even further.     1341.608793374915
yet if we do, we will not only have a credibility problem with the general public, but these efforts will be postponed until tomorrow and then tomorrow they will be deferred again.     2646.7688433107387
it is clear that in this event the objectives will never be achieved.     2483.2602642276424
i am stressing this point because, when disasters are being reported daily in the news, we cannot just keep on saying for the umpteenth time that europe did not act when it should have done.     2913.5965102060386
then, in our haste, we act as if we will solve the problems which we refused to tackle properly at the appropriate time.     5275.247659026282
the current example of the danube and the pollution from rumania shows very clearly that if we fail to establish a new method of economic development, clear requirements, controls and precise measures concerning the state of our rivers, this type of incident will not only occur again but will do so increasingly over time.     2091.236202309473
it is also clear that if we do nothing, our agriculture will continue to develop in a very unbalanced manner.     2807.6809985256846
even now, the rivers in brittany are eutrophying and this is damaging tourism.     2234.7324599295894
to conclude, the issues are clear at this second reading.     5152.549674196721
do we or do we not want standards compatible with ospar, in other words to get dangerous substances down to zero?     5007.817228335748
do we want to shorten the deadlines proposed by the council for this directive?     1676.0382761477729
do we want to have a pricing policy which enables all public and private participants to clean up and save water?     3819.2479182462994
do we seriously want to respond to the hopes of our peoples?     9114.24094088814
the majority of the amendments approved by the committee on the environment, public health and consumer protection meet these objectives.     3705.4671430643784
i hope they will be supported by this house.     4985.443356788061
mr president, ladies and gentlemen, mrs lienemann has just painted a very drastic picture of how important water and air are to us, especially as the very source of life for human beings.     12393.26632564911
the problem is one of both water quality and water resources, of which there is not a plentiful supply everywhere in europe, especially in very dry regions.     1549.5415303726334
i am sorry that commissioner wallström, who is responsible in this area, cannot be here now because this is, after all, a very, very important directive on which the commission has been working for a long time, and water protection legislation, which we are debating now at second reading, affects all the citizens of the european union as well as all the people in countries wishing to join, who will have to comply with eu legislation once they accede.     2256.555542992088
we have been working on this subject for ten years and a comprehensive approach was only made possible as the result of a hearing arranged and held by the committee on the environment, public health and consumer policy in 1994.
a great deal of work has been carried out between then and this second reading by numerous members of the european parliament, the european commission and the council of ministers.     2040.2227772181284
we reduced the 243 proposed amendments in the environment committee to 77, but another 30 have been added.     1434.7891013609103
you see, there are numerous matters of concern here and they are all very different.     1551.302454965405
some want tighter legislation, some want more clarification, others want to serve national interests.     16383.246074179477
even within my group, the group of the european people' s party (christian democrats) and european democrats, there were, of course, differences of opinion.     9096.49792133598
our line is to work towards realistic objectives and practicable solutions.     17453.328073829056
in this sense, the proposed amendments tabled by me and other members on behalf of the group represent an improvement on the demands set out in the common position.     5765.443397595435
some proposed amendments which we support should strengthen the european parliament' s negotiating position in the forthcoming conciliation with the council of ministers.     7662.455657834027
we obviously reject all unrealistic demands which affect the european parliament' s credibility.     36113.647198880106
for me, that includes the zero demand, i.e.     2053.1973254513923
the demand for zero inputs up to 2020.
that would sound the death knell for every agricultural and many industrial undertakings.     6368.510330229123
i should like to stress quite particularly here that we have already made considerable progress under current national and european water protection laws, however imperfect they are and however little they are complied with in the member states.     2702.09514428607
i only need to remind you that we again have salmon in the rhine, which would have been unthinkable 20 years ago and that salmon are due to be released this spring in the elbe, which was particularly polluted.     3267.6388203870824
that does not mean that we do not still have huge efforts to make in order to improve water protection yet further and maintain the quality of water and ground water and this will doubtless cost a great deal of money.     3119.1396627903564
farmers have voiced a number of concerns over the past few days.     11033.216825440823
you can only farm if you have an adequate supply of clean water.     2983.7231558119843
the line followed by our group for the negotiations with the council of ministers will allow an acceptable solution to be found for farmers too.     2577.4315691758848
i am horrified that both members of parliament and the public have been misled by false arguments in this difficult and sometimes highly technical matter.     2354.8945167270463
i therefore extend my special thanks to my colleagues in the group who helped us find compromises.     12494.1488555834
i also extend my special thanks to our rapporteur, mrs lienemann, for all her work and her cooperative attitude, even if we have agreed to differ on certain points.     8377.936419463229
and i also thank the representatives of the european commission, who stood by with advice and expert help at all times.     3325.884394706217
if the representatives of the council of ministers take an equally constructive approach, then i have no doubt that together we shall find an even better solution to the problem of water protection in europe under the conciliation procedure.     2998.017103490782
mr president, i would firstly like to congratulate mrs lienemann on the excellent work which she has presented and on her efforts in terms of overcoming the differences between us with regard to water.     5952.695799560619
the framework directive on water is a necessary initiative.     2316.3437982749924
nevertheless, given that the idea of solidarity is central to the process of european construction, this directive must take into account the fact that water resources should be seen as a factor in social cohesion.     2973.2265894034867
nobody doubts that water is a resource of interest to all of us.     3024.9358222687742
however, in spain and the other southern countries, which have an unusual climate, the management of water resources require political action in order to rationalise their use and other cooperative measures.     4036.323263863705
therefore, we believe that this framework directive should advocate a use of water which allows us to overcome territorial imbalances and, to this end, i ask that you support amendment no 95, which has been presented by our group.     3321.051426799252
we wish to ensure that article 1 of the directive promotes the sustainable, efficient, fair and considerate use of water.     3676.789447464356
i am now going to briefly discuss the main issues on which the spanish socialist delegation differs somewhat from certain positions which have been expressed here.     3559.595107693316
one example is water transfers.     10588.074243675965
we would prefer the future framework directive not to subject the implementation of transfers to community supervision.     4595.167464436897
we consider that, given the hydrological characteristics of spain, where transfers of a structural nature are carried out, the authorities of the country should make decisions with regard to its own resources, and these decisions should, of course, always be taken in accordance with the quest for cohesion and the rational use of water.     6666.894996687293
with regard to one of the most controversial points of our debate, the application of tariffs, the spanish socialists have been arguing, in accordance with the principle that the polluter should pay, that the directive should propose policies on accessible prices for all levels of consumption.     5844.857016780956
in spain, a policy of full recuperation of water costs would clearly not have the same effect as in the countries of the centre and north of europe.     2252.388157443205
the spanish would see an increase in the price of water for its different uses, owing to the scarcity of water resources and the internalisation of the environmental costs which will have to be met within the framework of the provisions of the directive.     2872.947106762856
we have therefore always argued for a progressive system of recuperation of costs.     9151.102157594736
this system should take good account of social, environmental and economic effects, and its implementation should be modulated in accordance with differing geographical and climatic conditions.     5101.838989922357
we want to build a europe with a sustainable and healthy environment.     5896.704121080312
however, an environmental europe cannot, in any circumstances, be built at different speeds, but must be built from a point of view of solidarity.     7185.911492089543
mr president, the ecological disaster in the danube reminds us of how closely linked all the countries of europe are and how important water is to us all.     2648.6831665523905
discharges from chemical factories in my own constituency in the north-west of england will end up on the beaches of mainland europe.     10839.098073930028
this brings home the connections which bind us together.     33935.61612874278
this framework directive is intended to paint the broad brushstrokes of the policy for the next decades and there has been much panic over the details.     5643.073723855879
it is important for us to remember that this is a framework.     1798.1113249246011
these brushstrokes are indeed very broad.     11042.532537408473
it is easy for individual countries, for individual sectors of industry, to escape the effects which have been painted to us in recent days.     4120.777007393462
there are many opportunities to hide.     4591.971760210728
the reality is that the details of this legislation will be set out in daughter directives in months and years to come.     2028.0614498633179
that is the time to debate some of these problems of detail.     1982.8977667066836
the broad principles are surely ones we should be able to accept - the broad principles that we wish to reduce hazardous waste leaking into the groundwater, the broad principles that we should be aiming to ensure that chemicals, which we all need in society in all circumstances, should not be finding their way into our water system.     6555.975349073485
there is a chemical factory in the north-west which wrote to me to say that we must vote against this legislation.     3048.942628520643
i would have to ask them what chemicals they are putting into the water supply at the moment and why they are not broadcasting to all the residents in that area exactly what they are doing.     1799.9960102957723
this is something that they prefer to avoid doing.     4168.770977209782
the principle also concerns water charging.     1620.1731086416862
the basic understanding which many of us now share is that we need to introduce environmental taxation to encourage conservation, to use the carrot-and-stick approach to make best use of our assets so that we do not add to the pollution or loss of a valuable resource like water.     2651.123126982877
i want to refer to the political position here.     5425.990590952975
the common position has weakened the stand taken by parliament at first reading.     3895.865672503671
some of the ngos would say that the directive as now framed is worse than useless.     2145.985683312758
it is a retrograde step.     4297.037585814295
this is an opportunity for us to improve the situation.     3016.978612799467
we need to get ourselves into a position of conciliation.     8657.481594009289
we have seen vote after vote lost.     5505.947142574069
measure after measure has come out of the environment committee.     3155.9206577986574
we have failed to achieve a qualified majority.     5749.699056122383
our attempt to improve the environment of europe has failed.     7013.791942876092
we need to get into that conciliation position.     8911.664527756673
we need, at the end of the day, to ensure that we end up with a sensible, balanced policy of real improvements at a reasonable cost.     2878.4670807474404
mr president, ladies and gentlemen, as long as nearly thirty years ago, the european commission made attempts to draw up a european water policy.     8824.464409502178
a hundred and twenty-nine chemical substances had to be regulated.     9098.085432298574
eventually, standards were drawn up for only a dozen or so substances.     3309.5477483354716
the unanimity principle was at the root of this debacle.     27782.997078247736
some seven years ago, it still looked like the european water policy was going to be sacrificed on the altar of subsidiarity.     3605.8403008048085
the conservative government leaders messrs major and kohl decided in a cosy fireside chat at the edinburgh summit that it was totally unnecessary for the spanish to receive drinking water supplies which were as good as those of the germans or english.     1953.8719639121966
this sentiment is partly reflected in the common position which came about under british socialist presidency.     3550.024174946964
that position is like holey cheese, or to keep it the sphere of water, as leaky as a sieve.     2103.5012963104173
it explains the ground swell of amendments which were tabled by the committee on the environment: nearly one hundred amendments for the second reading.     4702.434244895874
this is unusual, but most of these amendments are desperately needed to plug the holes.     8276.940072596553
the dangerous chemical substances constitute a big leak.     68984.0894086651
the chemical industry and, unfortunately, the european commission and the council of ministers too want to draft a separate risk analysis for each dangerous substance.     2039.6729811671737
this is very time-consuming, and, more importantly, there is no acceptable level of pollution for dangerous chemical substances.     5341.323351034941
only the background level of the ocean is acceptable.     5399.6360770373785
this is what is meant by the term "close-to-zero" .     4130.169951080051
the commission already has a list of 32 priority substances, many of which have a hormone-deregulating effect.     7022.667779391796
these are so-called endocrine disrupters and cause, even in extremely minimal quantities, changes in the gender of animals and even humans, as is more and more being ascertained by scientists.     2812.3348371187462
this is why it is so important that the european parliament declares itself openly in favour of the ospar objective of close-to-zero by the year 2020.
i would like to mention two substances from the commission' s list: mercury and tributyl, abbreviated to tbt.     5411.892606497322
on the bottom of the wadden sea, the values of mercury and tbt are higher than the ospar value, to wit, ten and one thousand times, respectively.     12643.320969948465
the chemical industry and society as a whole have to learn to replace these dangerous chemical substances by harmless substitutes, and if this is impossible, to apply them in closed systems.     3667.715332176057
for the substance tbt, this means that it can no longer be used as an anti-fouling substance.     5356.284854003726
a sound alternative would be to clean the ship' s sides mechanically.     11303.366186539248
i would like to finish off by expressing the hope that the european parliament will pick up once again its green image and will argue in favour of the close-to-zero option for dangerous chemical and hormone-deregulating substances by the year 2020.
mr president, the european parliament has a crucial role to play in the development of the water directive.     5641.528314475231
the council's common position is totally inadequate in a number of areas.     5605.087031573262
therefore, our task is to make the requirements in the directive tighter and more concrete.     2063.012916354126
as members of the confederal group of the european united left/nordic green left, we regarded certain principles as particularly important when we adopted a position on the different proposals.     4567.883284484511
firstly, we think that the time-frame for implementing the proposed measures should be shorter than that proposed by the council.     2357.901542022617
we therefore support the proposal to reduce the time allotted for implementing different parts of the directive.     3431.1007828257952
secondly, i should like the phasing out of noxious materials to be carried out consistently.     5751.325264296427
there should be no initial delay to allow new evaluation criteria to be developed before the measures are implemented.     4426.604378772914
the legislation should also have written into it the fact that the international conventions, such as the ospar convention must be respected.     4344.461662959785
thirdly, we should like a clear statement regarding the pricing policy.     6242.787986184541
this means that the basic principle must be that the price should represent the real cost of the water used.     1878.575370522531
nowadays, taxpayers often have to subsidise industry and farming.     7771.275603524093
the obvious point of departure for legislation should be that polluters or consumers pay, although exemptions would be permitted in extreme cases.     6983.304366646794
fourthly, we should like exemptions from the rules on water status to be kept to a minimum and clearly defined.     4168.3825416626805
fifthly, the protection of groundwater status and measures against persistent pollution of the groundwater should be transparent and clear.     11174.120788656357
against that background, our group is able to vote in favour of most of the amendments tabled by mrs lienemann, the rapporteur, as adopted in committee.     7920.755629732691
we should like to see the scope of a couple of points extended.     34911.010723757565
we will therefore vote for the greens' amendments nos 102, 103 and 104, which we consider to be improvements on the present position.     5596.771623065334
as regards pricing policy, there are sometimes extreme cases which may require derogations from the principles laid down in the report.     10929.798295845667
my own view is that the options for allowing derogations had already been given proper attention in amendment no 43, tabled by the committee and been even more clearly set out in the greens' amendment no 105.
i cannot see any need for other derogations besides those specified in the two amendments just mentioned.     2761.132402324664

on the whole, i welcome the committee's proposals set out in mrs lienemann's report and think they could provide a good basis for a hard-won compromise.     4142.62012201328
mr president, every satellite photo proves that we do indeed live on the blue planet, yet the abundance of water is an illusion.     4677.760776176569
the recent disaster on the danube serves as a reminder that our rivers are the lifelines of our planet and that pollution does not respect borders.     3841.6794121699177
water is a renewable but limited natural resource.     3667.0583775056675
it becomes scarcer when it is mismanaged and when the geographical and climatic conditions are unfavourable.     1496.9223569861783
in the eu, this is a crucial issue in the mediterranean region and also in other european countries where we are witnessing the gradual drying of wet areas.     1953.6807499047497
the increase in vulnerable areas, both wet and dry, inequalities between countries, inequalities between different regions within countries and unusual climatic, economic, geographical and geological events are all the evidence we need.     8952.81506499436
however, these must not lead to competition distortions within the community.     2265.2667986747615
we have therefore tabled amendments to highlight the importance of the special relationship between agriculture and water, given that water is an essential factor in the occupation and development of the land.     3053.2685819790963

last october in turin, the countries in the euro-mediterranean partnership bluntly demanded a marshall plan for water for the south coast of the mediterranean.     3037.920952905722

we need this framework directive as an essential element of a sustainable development policy which must make the various uses of water compatible with each other.     3633.209181745336
yet we also need to integrate the protection and conservation of biodiversity into this new management of water.     3380.3701257679795
the water issue will definitely not be settled today.     2680.2591680444134
in fact, the problems of enlargement and climate change will offer new prospects.     5033.61472018491
mr president, it is very likely that, during this century, good-quality water will be of even more strategic importance than oil.     2525.659367334363
this is enough reason to thank mrs lienemann for the efforts she has made to obtain sound surface water.     8920.66600084831
it seems rather complicated to attain a proper european legislative framework.     2504.191241993201
with the framework directive on water policy, we are hoping to end the fragmentation of the eu' s water legislation.     15312.89016633673
but that has not dismissed us from the task of drawing up an extensive and complicated directive which will require great care in its implementation from the executive bodies.     2539.8198294666477
more specifically, the possibilities for adopting a specific policy by member states and water boards, which have been set up thanks to the catchment basin approach, need to be maximised.     2344.9778133118625
the key goals remain the prevention from further pollution of ground and surface water, the protection of eco systems, the promotion of the sustainable use of water, the control of floods and droughts and the termination of discharging dangerous substances into surface water.     5928.075789594406
as far as the discharge of these dangerous substances is concerned, i am of the opinion that the councils' objectives lack both commitment and ambition.     4318.63387938831

mr president, we need to get used to the idea in the 21st century that we have to show water a completely new respect.     2318.8017701323042

mr president, it must be some six years ago now that mr karl-heinz florenz, assisted by mrs ursula schleicher, put in a request and managed to overhaul the water policy in its entirety.     6054.023083006661

the decision-making process concerning this framework directive on water has reached a crucial stage.     3594.67001693867
the topic has been brought under the codecision procedure and as a result, we have been able to draft important, more stringent legislation as early on as at first reading.     4550.118819663994
in fact, the common position which was issued at the end of last year was already a major improvement on the original proposal.     2270.144621006069

mr president, i want first of all to say thank you to mrs lienemann for her report.     3546.315406966545

we cannot delay environmental work by going into inordinate detail rather than working on reducing total discharges of hazardous substances into the environment.     13784.017053808346

mr president, it is difficult to summarise the areas covered by such a large and complex directive.     3509.1517597932016
the treaty of amsterdam reiterates the priority criteria for prevention, for the implementation of the 'polluter pays' principle and for sustainable development through an environmental impact assessment.     6148.446015444302
clearly, the importance of recognising the economic value of ecological factors in setting prices and making operators accountable through the adoption of incentives for non-polluting systems, seems unquestionable in the light of events that characterise the relationship between economic development and the protection of our environmental heritage, particularly our water resources.     5034.539578079762
this situation is loudly echoed in many sectors of the economy, and especially in the agricultural sector.     1962.3027018999605
the concept of good agricultural practice has been introduced in amongst the complexity of the current relationships between agriculture, environment and water resources, and between positive and negative effects, and amongst the wide range of local realities and production systems and so on.     5159.714671198364
this concept indicates which agricultural production method to use in order to satisfy the community expectation of an environmental protection of water resources that surpasses basic standards, along with the subsequent costs and reductions in income.     6763.231382667813
the obligation to develop and consolidate a strategy for integration derives from this notion, with the aim of keeping the management of water resources at the heart of the sustainable economic production model in the light of current conditions.     5271.8202493345
we must not, with this in mind, pursue a strategy of separating the objective of preventing a deterioration in the state of surface water and groundwater from the objectives of protecting, improving and restoring their quality, as this would create a superfluous hierarchy of priorities detrimental to the overall functioning of interventions and specific measures that target a combined approach and the use of the best available techniques.     4370.472466327324
as regards eliminating the pollution generated by dangerous substances in the aquatic environment, an optimum approach should provide a regulation at both national and community levels, which enables clearer identification of the various classes of bodies of water polluted as a result of human production activities.     6571.820082229494
finally, we need to set up a system which prepares an objective list of potentially dangerous substances, using the increased information available on their chemical, physical and biological properties, in such a way as to create an integrated model for intervention at a range of strategic levels in order to protect our water heritage, which is fundamental to us all.     4903.615993157059
mr president, commissioner, ladies and gentlemen, if we are to take the principle of sustainability enshrined in the treaty of amsterdam seriously, then our long-term objective must be to achieve zero inputs in our water because we need to safeguard water so that future generations do not pay the price for our water consumption.     6286.002546035902
we can achieve this long-term objective by developing measures with demanding standards.     4044.5109408527223
in this respect, i fully support the amendments proposed by mrs lienemann to develop a reasonable approach using quality standards so that we can eventually say that, when people use water, they are not consuming it but maintaining present stocks.     3364.674182933537
this directive contains a number of very positive elements which are worth highlighting.     5670.30407526119
first there is wide information for and involvement by the public, a rarity in a european directive.     922.1498720651712
secondly, there is compulsory cooperation.     2600.755273194099
i am glad a holistic approach to water is being taken here and that authorities in the individual national states and beyond the borders of the national states are at last obliged to work together and ensure that water is of a high quality.     2056.410400943018
thirdly, i should like to address the question of the timetable.     9339.109295324775
i am firmly convinced that we need a strict, tight timetable in order to work towards our long-term objective.     11098.764201946764
compare it with what happens in december.     3954.232218181625
we all know in december that christmas eve falls on 24th december and we start buying presents.     15805.760698866936
but what would we do if we knew in december that christmas was in thirty years' time.     4085.457529448163
we all know what we would do, which is why we need a tight timetable for our water policy.     2767.139177135388
i emphatically support the rapporteur' s proposals and think that we need a tight timetable for all areas of the directive, from the list of measures to the question of employment measures.     6206.974222177624
mr president, the purpose of the present objective ought to be to ensure that the member states work together to protect groundwater, drinking water and surface water and that the level of protection corresponds to existing eu environmental legislation.     3753.2637758077167
may i take this opportunity to remind you that previous resolutions concerning the nitrates directive have still not been implemented in all member states, in spite of the fact that there is a common eu resolution.     5623.936151168421
the present proposal tightens up the protection of the aquatic environment in a number of ways which exceed our current environmental objectives.     4995.026088804781
it is proposed that a target should be achieved for concentrations in the aquatic environment which is close to the background values for naturally occurring substances and close to zero for humanly produced synthetic substances.     6380.552743740186
this cannot be done, and it also, in any case, goes against nature.     2073.87088031909
adopting this report would have serious consequences for agriculture in the eu if concentrations of, for example, phosphorus and nitrogen could not exceed the background values for these substances in the aquatic environment and if a limit of zero were to be established for pesticides in the aquatic environment.     2686.7001334525403
european agriculture would not, for example, be able to grow bread grain with a high enough protein content for it to be used for making bread.     5865.250644404734
as a result, agricultural production would move to other countries outside the eu, with consequent high socio-economic and employment costs.     4819.796414444341
i cannot vote in favour of the sections in the water framework directive which deal with these states of affairs.     5437.729608577288
mr president, ladies and gentlemen, water is a precious raw material.     111602.68411949428
millions of people do not even have clean water - a absolute necessity for survival - at their disposal.     22894.895626672635
it should, therefore, not surprise us that water is the cause of wars.     3898.5675134147436
the capitalist world too sends out its pawns in order to gain maximum control over water reservoirs.     26778.885676803537
general interest or solidarity often do not come into the equation.     4833.451024564632
europe is therefore facing an arduous task; that of combining a community water policy which is fragmented in terms of objectives and means into more coherent framework legislation.     5275.869129936412
i have to admit that in flanders, where i come from, we still have a long way to go too.     1503.4956807963933
we were recently rapped on the knuckles by the commission regarding this matter.     1508.9219315839703
anyone with any sense would like to see this framework directive used as a big stick for those pursuing policies who are now failing in some way.     6874.87838077166
the common position is in this respect a farce, a document with no strings attached and a true affront in the light of the ospar agreements.     8242.018534126364
the period of implementation can even be extended up to 34 years.     3567.579712619939
by then, my children will be older than i am now.     4811.228262747572
you have to admit: this is a joke.     1368.7709931543827
tomorrow, we might opt in favour of a weak and permissive framework which ruins the future of our children.     3346.623134599268
either we opt for the amendments of my group, of the rapporteur or the committee on the environment.     2912.4839468874866
i would like to finish off with this observation.     14995.9299134561
in the past couple of months, we have witnessed a great deal of lobbying in belgium.     42242.95048779615
what was remarkable about this dossier was the enormous pressure from the council of ministers.     8227.920288126086
in our own country, this pressure was exerted by both flemish and wallonian environment administrations.     3287.3751764904837
they have opted for the weak common position in favour of our parliament' s more drastic positioning.     11352.406365671322
i really do wonder whether this is an approach of which the green environment ministers in my country are aware.     2614.6419256396644
ladies and gentlemen, fine words are no longer sufficient.     56849.44582617427
we need to change tack completely.     7259.344676356646
during the vote tomorrow, we can make it clear that europe is drafting a responsible and future-orientated water policy.     3043.9587673698934
this can only benefit our credibility.     7209.160959056692
the debate is suspended and will resume at 9 p.m.
the dialogue on europe - institutional reform
the next item is the commission communication - the dialogue on europe - institutional reform: the issues at stake.     48201.93839180399
mr president, ladies and gentlemen, the commission is discussing this issue right now but it will be very surprising if its work is completed today.     7630.565194879425
i am basically here to speak to you, as indicated by your agenda, on a new initiative which the commission is proposing as just one of its responses to the major challenge of institutional reform.     3290.0459881766487
this reform must involve all the actors in european construction, with yourselves as meps at the forefront but also myself as a commissioner, the council ministers and national mps.     6615.5592612436485
it must also involve the officials who work in our various institutions and who are committed to and motivated by this european construction.     2178.770146627285
this morning, in the lengthy debate which i attended with mr prodi, many of you mentioned the lack of democracy and remoteness of the european institutions.     5920.395198862649
yet it is not just a question of who does what, as one of you said very clearly and forcefully.     946.0009014216691
ensuring that our citizens understand who does what in our various institutions is the very least we can do.     2444.7517864034694
we must also now explain what we are doing together and what we want to do together in the future, particularly with the countries which will be joining us.     1758.3914272693476
we too are concerned about this lack of democracy, and the commission has therefore decided to launch the 'dialogue on europe' in order to play its part in this direct contact with the people.     6984.049332730299
this initiative will be launched on the day after the opening of the intergovernmental conference.     5962.291641126096
during the igc i will have the honour of representing the commission together with mr prodi and of working closely with your two representatives, mr brok and mr tsatsos.     8235.054929101374
all the commissioners have undertaken to use some of their time when they go to any country or region, and not just their own, which in my case happens three or four times a month, to engage in direct dialogue with the public, and not just with the élite or those in power whom we meet as a matter of course.     1842.784323603601
we also plan to establish direct contacts in universities, schools and factories, to meet the people, answer their questions and listen to them.     10692.246342850205
in this way we will play our part, as will you, in making the necessary effort and meeting the urgent obligation to reduce the democratic deficit and the remoteness of our citizens from what we do.     3798.217429283676
we hope to conduct this initiative in cooperation with the member states and in conjunction with the european parliament.     6830.057527675812
we will produce a regular summary of this initiative so that public opinion can be measured and we can adjust or reorient the work.     4988.885697635393
we also hope to conduct this initiative in consultation with the national parliaments, local elected representatives, non-governmental organisations, socio-professional organisations and the media.     4440.928889298004
opinion multipliers, political groups and parties, meps and national mps, elected representatives, as i have just said, local authorities or national parliaments, the committee of the regions, the economic and social committee, organisations stemming from civil society and university and education circles will all be involved.     3098.632493462631
need i remind you that the european parliament itself took the initiative on 1 february?     2161.9968526920215

in terms of social protection, i can confirm that we have made proposals about qualified majority or unanimous voting but these are regardless of ideology.     6683.658468563467
in your country, mr bonde, like everywhere else, i believe people are keen for the internal market to work properly under the fairest conditions of competition and movement of goods and people.     5343.58569522543
this will therefore be our rule.     1605.4597701941243
we are proposing that decisions on certain policies or measures involving taxation or social protection and social security should be taken by qualified majority voting when there is a direct link with the proper operation of the internal market.     3152.869969860506
our proposal is not general or systematic.     2186.597197271437
i am well aware of the sensitivity of these taxation and social protection issues.     5098.967907297553
i do not believe that all the national systems of social protection should be made identical.     1827.0691274307137
this has never been the case.     694.1652633392159
we are simply trying to ensure, in a europe with 30 or 27 member states, the best conditions for the operation of the internal market without one country being able to block the other 26 or 27.
mr maccormick, i agree that this dialogue must reach beyond the national capitals.     6795.525256552378
it must get as close as possible to the people, where they live and where they have their roots.     1843.294684769973
if i said anything different i would be contradicting myself as commissioner for regional policy which is one of the most specific and most visible policies for increasing employment and improving the quality of life of people in their regions.     1314.2085124382586
you will often hear me say that this policy does not just have one main objective of achieving cohesion and solidarity between the regions but that it is intended in addition to help people, where they live, to preserve their roots, traditions, soul and identity.     3271.739181675991
we will therefore conduct this dialogue with the towns and regions.     2953.6086696944085
mr berthu, the helsinki mandate is clear and we are working within this.     10983.124200116383
it specifies that we must deal firstly and as a priority with the three basic issues which were left out in amsterdam.     3697.914952339169
it also adds related institutional questions and issues which may be included in the negotiations depending on the portuguese and french presidencies.     5621.487914708402
the commission is acting correctly if, on a particular point, it believes it must add to its opinion which covers many institutional questions.     5663.697058509776
i am not yet sure that we will do this on article 7.
however, if we do, this will not be solely in response or as a reaction to an economic situation which is sufficiently serious that the fourteen governments of the union must mobilise together to give their response.     1906.0706227127687
we will also do this with regard for the future in general.     1790.9405415967822
we will use all the means available to permanently strengthen the community of values which we have been building together since the founding of the union in 1957, and even before that, on the rubble of the second world war.     1756.0225984074589
everything which can be done will be useful.     2677.3772854350946
i am sure that you and i, mr berthu, can at least agree on this.     7206.999716269537
thank you, commissioner.     696.3226540346473
you have answered the questions precisely and also opened the dialogue for europe that you referred to.     3680.3900272104115
you have set a very good example of time-keeping today.     1533.1638172589885
that concludes the debate.     8638.001648140955

question no 36 by (h-0025/00):
subject: high infant mortality in kosovo according to the latest un data received from the world health organisation, kosovo has the highest rate of infant mortality in europe; almost 50% of babies born prematurely die, while the war has produced a steep rise in miscarriages and children who manage to go to term are smaller than the norm.     16340.287790077871
in the context of the humanitarian aid provided by the union and the efforts of its envoy bernard kouchner, will the commission say what measures have been taken to safeguard the women of kosovo's right to motherhood and the health of pregnant women, women in childbirth and babies?     4723.03425975465
mr president, the commission is conscious of the very difficult situation concerning health and health facilities in kosovo, not just for pregnant women but across the board.     3707.050531245707
this results both from the recent conflict and from the years of neglect and lack of maintenance that preceded it.     6381.4458715654755
the statistics quoted on child mortality speak for themselves and are wholly unacceptable.     22149.100415921643
however, the situation is hardly better for other parts of the population.     2445.7067538559177
the commission' s response has been as follows: firstly, the health sector has been a significant beneficiary of echo funding.     12540.990439708652
echo is currently focusing on the provision of medical supplies and emergency health care, on support to institutions as well as vaccination.     4807.948901644844
however, efforts are increasingly oriented towards the establishment of a self-sufficient healthcare system in the province.     11011.142999378406
unmik has already taken a significant role in this field.     4957.309185897358
secondly, under the reconstruction programme, urgent intervention at the hospital in mitrovica has already been initiated with a eur 1 million rehabilitation programme.     8074.986440083354
progress is slow due to the tense relations between the ethnic groups of this divided city.     10982.907218590322
however, the commission is maintaining its efforts with the support of unmik for this project.     4607.161471709461
we hope that one day it may become a symbol promoting the benefits of ethnic reconciliation.     7628.272401882713
under the european community' s damage assessment, estimated costs of the reconstruction of healthcare facilities and provision of equipment to dispensaries and health houses amount to eur 4 million.     18143.604958363616
the commission is now turning to the programming of 2000 funds.     2187.9226054331157
we expect to dedicate a considerable sum to improving the healthcare system.     36647.61791872206
the emphasis will be on long-term reforms covering health financing as well as training and procurement.     6692.792286482553
work is already under way with unmik to determine an appropriate contribution from the commission to this enterprise.     5973.135446269239
commissioner, this morning president prodi, among others, stressed that the balkans are testing our ability to act effectively, the very credibility of the european union is on trial, and the gap between words and deeds must finally be closed.     7032.146813038669
commissioner, do you really think that the facts we have about high infant mortality in kosovo - the highest in europe - and more generally the picture of human misery and destruction that prevails in kosovo, are not already damaging our credibility and casting doubt on our ability to live up to our promises?     6197.770161354062
besides, commissioner, is so-called humanitarian military intervention justified by the present picture of human destruction in kosovo?     6024.493228694485
do you think that so much inaction is excusable when, on the continent of europe itself, the right to life is at risk?     1480.357346068017
i do not think that our reconstruction agency or the task force before it, which have been working incredibly hard in kosovo, would regard it as an adequate description of their labours to suggest that they had been standing idly by.     3152.9944214071033
i am sure the honourable member did not mean that.     3708.6915720801185
of course, she is entirely correct in saying that the union' s credibility is on the line with what happens, not just in kosovo but in the balkans as a whole.     5793.717018641092
i am very concerned that the assistance we provide should arrive expeditiously and in a way which can maximise our assistance.     7160.830913327034
i would just make two points about the situation in kosovo which we are addressing as enthusiastically as we can along who lines.     3092.863195478114
first of all, i am sure that the honourable member recognises that over the period of the 1990s the government in belgrade underfunded the health service in kosovo, and many albanians found that they were not really receiving any health care at all.     2368.5963775154055
as a result of that, a parallel health care system was put in place through the mother teresa society.     3508.172121046443
so what we are dealing with is not just the consequences of conflict but the consequences of years of neglect and chronic under-investment.     5455.739800393466
secondly, and i am sure that the honourable member recognises this as well, some of the tragic stories to come out of kosovo in recent weeks have not been about child mortalities during carriage of a baby, but child mortality after a healthy baby has been born.     7441.787466404221
those were cases in which women who during the hostilities, or before the hostilities, tragically killed their own children.     5908.064535807268
we are dealing with a horrific story in kosovo.     2827.231103167529
we have to work as well as we can in the health care field and in others to restore something approaching civilised standards and civilised behaviour, but it is not going to be easy.     4449.931738978884
commissioner, i would like to thank you for your response to the member' s first question and the supplementary question.     3390.6802483892266

are you aware of this?     2944.1767361118436

this is my first additional question.     2434.935373677397

what does the commission intend to do in order to ensure that albanian kosovars too can get to hospital?     11729.29056701843
on the first question, i am going myself to kosovo at the beginning of next month for another visit and i will certainly look into the allegation made by the honourable member about the echo food package.     6819.358663873156
i have not heard that suggestion before, but it is an important point and i will certainly investigate it when i am there.     2748.8960971507177
secondly, i do understand exactly what the honourable member is saying about mitrovica.     4899.836762052058
i was in mitrovica a couple of months ago, i saw the situation there for myself.     21607.249343431955
i had discussions with municipal leaders from both communities, including the leader of the albanian side who had himself in the past been a doctor and was generally recognised for the medical work he had done during the hostilities and subsequently.     3171.4172983909807
so i know the very serious problems that exist in that hospital and i can assure the honourable member that we will be doing all that we can to see that adequate health facilities are available for everybody in kosovo, whatever ethnic group they come from.     3304.331260274182

question no 39 by (h-0036/00):
subject: drafting of charter of fundamental rights the general public welcomes the fact that a charter of fundamental rights is to be drawn up.     10482.783894359623
it is also to be hoped that it will meet the challenges of europe in the 21st century.     3439.3172008791057
what are the commission's views on the substance of the charter?     4618.221636098261
what type of rights will the charter set out (economic, social and cultural)?     1949.8370380201702
which citizens will it cover (those of the european union or the citizens of all european countries with a view to enlargement?     1730.3791015829947
will it cover immigrants etc.?)     6844.238326814423
will it set out established social rights in the european union or will it set a broader agenda?     2056.550374783287
what means will the charter use to ensure that it clearly guarantees equality between the sexes?     5051.501987375632
what are the commission's views on incorporating the charter into the treaty on european union?     3393.391261632309
like the honourable member, the commission welcomes the decision taken by the heads of state and government to start the process of drawing up a european charter of fundamental rights.     3705.5623695944896
in the conclusions of the cologne european council the heads of state and government even established the broad outlines of the charter' s content.     3914.53477504173
according to these conclusions, the charter must contain three categories of rights.     17574.593417368673
the first category is rights of freedom and equality and procedural rights, as guaranteed by the european convention for the protection of human rights and fundamental freedoms.     2988.1399999087985
the second is the rights reserved for citizens of the european union.     2782.6133416619273
the third is economic and social rights, as contained in the european social charter and the community charter of the fundamental social rights of workers, insofar as they do not merely establish objectives for action by the union.     2024.2608678753986
the commission considers that the convention responsible for drawing up the charter of fundamental rights must respect the mandate given by the cologne european council.     4492.729632915858
having said that, this does not mean that the convention should be limited to a codifying role of simply compiling the existing rights from various sources.     2642.6263365651535
as i have said before in this house, the commission is convinced that the convention must be able, if necessary, to adapt and develop these rights in view of current circumstances, particularly technological and social changes.     4233.954263499167
however, given the mandate and the final objective of the text to be submitted to the heads of state and government, this power must be exercised with caution and on a case-by-case basis.     4598.128978628185
the rights conferred by this charter will have at least two types of beneficiary.     11654.643803523182
some rights will apply to anyone present in the eu territory while others will benefit only citizens of the european union.     3236.1072507577687
it may also be envisaged that some economic and social rights could be guaranteed only for citizens of the european union and third-country nationals meeting certain conditions.     1964.9436271792376
it is not possible at this initial stage of the process of drawing up the charter to prejudge its content in more detail.     3726.719198838242
however, i can confirm that the principle of equal opportunity and equal treatment between men and women will certainly be included among the rights guaranteed by the future european charter of fundamental rights.     4595.451901842361
this is because this principle already figures among the rights guaranteed by the ec treaty and by secondary legislation and because it undoubtedly constitutes a common constitutional tradition in the member states.     3573.064051354041
as i have also said before to this house, the commission is favourable to inserting the charter in the eu treaty.     1645.8901685126652
however, we are fully aware that the final decision on this matter will fall to the heads of state and government.     2968.4903526686967
the difficult part, which will fall to the convention, is therefore to draw up an ambitious and politically significant text which can be integrated as such into the treaties.     2928.0194825387475
in its opinion on the holding of an intergovernmental conference to amend the treaties, the commission noted that the european council must decide during the year 2000 on the inclusion of certain items on the igc agenda, in particular the european charter of fundamental rights.     6299.5423109594485
commissioner, it is quite significant that at the beginning of this new century people in europe, men and women, are being called upon to redefine their rights and obligations.     3374.539888318083
we hope that this important cologne council decision will become a reality.     2353.3874395271146
globalisation on the one hand and enlargement on the other, make it urgently necessary to define those rights.     2707.6889084570703
seattle is a real source of concern in that context and we hope that the european council in nice will not be a wasted meeting.     2781.355358572321
but if we are to secure the public' s participation in this new planning, we would like to learn what plan the european bodies and specifically the commission are proposing as the social and democratic model of europe in the twenty-first century.     2539.3368150095152
i listened to your general guidelines and i would like to ask you what is the position of children, as entities in their own right, in the commission' s plans for the new model of society in the twenty-first century.     5795.828455400993
mr president, mrs kratsa-tsagaropoulou, in my opinion, and as mr prodi said this morning, the drawing up of the charter is an exercise with great political significance.     6003.018806897873
this is because it shows that the union places the fundamental rights of citizens at the top of the political concerns for the future of our common project.     2986.1424679939723
in this context, the criteria by which these rights are to be chosen must be clearly indicated.     3202.050794997155
the essential criterion is the rights of citizens with regard to the european institutions, as contained in the european project enshrined in the treaties of the european union.     2835.1396055029104
i understand your concern about the rights of children.     5792.679507678078
many elements of our work, and particularly of my work as commissioner for justice and home affairs, involve protecting the rights of children.     2920.693718375081
this is an area in which the principle of subsidiarity applies fully.     3547.7002412490247
each member state is principally responsible for defining its responsibilities towards children.     23730.10698384124
as for the eu' s responsibility, the rights of children must be recognised in the charter according to the areas in which the union can give added value to the promotion of the social and economic situation and even the citizenship, in its broadest sense, of children.     6135.326040959987
i hope we can meet the challenge which the honourable member has posed for the commission and the whole convention.     3002.767603588274
mr president, i have had the privilege of hearing the commissioner a number of times on this issue and i very much agree with his approach.     3036.0615673491216
however, i should like to ask a slightly different question.     5948.269324227901
we have just heard commissioner barnier say that he is anxious to communicate better with the people and to aim the information policy of the commission towards the major issues that the community is dealing with this year.     2694.0239534068082
would the commissioner argue for some of the information budget of the commission to be put towards informing and consulting the citizens on this issue of a citizens charter?     4365.031295298507
it is very important that the people feel engaged in this process.     1895.0117778321332
i know that the convention itself will do much in this area but it does not have the resources that the commission has.     1371.5240614942734
will the commission look at using its information budget to ensure people are engaged in this debate?     3199.3702500105014
it is the policy of the commission to promote an open and wide debate on the charter of fundamental rights, not only with non-governmental organisations but also with the citizens in a project as far-reaching as the charter is.     2176.8319032291542
i can assure you that the commission will do its best to promote the debate on the future charter of fundamental rights.     4657.11390039604
we can achieve the best of both worlds by having a clear text, a text that can be easily read by the citizens, and at the same time a text that can be used as a legal instrument.     2758.567934194197
there is no contradiction between the two.     3559.253041796194
the challenge we have is to bring them together in the final version of the charter.     2764.0559843054643
i am very glad to know that i can count on your support here.     7392.8900429537125
question no 40 by (h-0095/00):
subject: articles 6.1, 7.1 and 7.2 of the treaty on european union article 6.1 of the treaty states that the union is based on principles of liberty, democracy, respect for human rights and fundamental freedoms.     5069.955789831993
it is clear from statements over the past years that jörg haider and the freedom party do not respect human rights and fundamental freedoms with respect to third country nationals and minority groups living in austria.     5548.096922480834

, and urge the council acting by qualified majority to suspend certain rights deriving from the application of this treaty to austria including the voting rights of the representative of austria in the council?     5738.6106657359505
mr president, ladies and gentlemen, i would like to begin my answer by reminding you of the position adopted by the commission with regard to the formation of the new austrian government.     14069.671459812796
this was reiterated this morning in president romano prodi' s speech on that government which includes ministers from jörg haider' s freedom party.     69580.95562842341
i am referring ,of course, to the statement made by the commission on 1 february 2000, the basic elements of which i would like to restate.     5247.270937961048
the commission confirmed then, as it does today, that it shares the concerns underlying the portuguese presidency' s statement of 1 february 2000.
these concerns are legitimate and there are good reasons for them.     4547.875357082283
there is no need for me to remind you of jörg haider' s political bias and of his many xenophobic and racist public statements, which are also anti-european.     5507.335573027725
the fact that an openly extremist, racist and xenophobic party can reach power in a member state of the european union will inevitably be a source of concern to the other states that are taking part in the european project.     3177.4250340164804
the european commission is certainly not indifferent to the matter either.     3100.383548244347
secondly, the commission reiterated and reiterates again its commitment to continue fulfilling its duty as guardian of the treaties, specifically with regard to articles 6 and 7 of the treaty on european union.     8500.391485127111
one of the most important innovations provided by the treaty of amsterdam was to clarify the basic principles which represent the shared heritage of the member states.     8098.349824329797
another innovation was the establishment of a mechanism which would monitor whether these principles are being fulfilled, and which would react if any of them were violated.     5381.130769088904
the commission thereby gave a practical demonstration of its commitment to one of the essential pillars: that of the rule of law.     3776.0978038641792
this principle means that the commission must act within the limits laid down by the treaties and it is in this context that the statement of 1 february was made.     2131.2765549818378
the commission is not a state.     812.0416976371682
it cannot and must not act as if it were one.     1154.5431046367837
in order to give a precise answer to your question, mr ford, i must remind you that in order for the mechanism established by article 7.1 of the treaty on european union to be applied, there must be proof of the existence and i quote, of a "serious and persistent breach by a member state of the principles mentioned in article 6."     7107.259332204004
i repeat, a "serious and persistent breach" is the condition necessary for the commission to be able to propose to the council that measures be adopted against a member state.     4140.39095639903
i think that it is obvious that conditions have not been met which would apply to the current situation in austria.     1866.500834909175
as a matter of fact i think that i am right in saying that we all hope that these conditions of a serious and persistent breach of human rights and of democracy are never fulfilled, either in austria or in any other member state.     3108.0030857243396
i would like to assure you of my personal commitment and of that of the commission, as stated once again by president prodi this morning, to doing everything to ensure that we do not have to apply the mechanism stated in article 7.
i would also like to assure you though that we will not hesitate to apply it should it prove necessary.     4226.733761724949
the values at stake are too central, too fundamental for us to accept dithering or compromises.     5003.508491078318
individuals and governments should be judged by what they do rather than by what they say.     5774.257434801691
in austria' s case, the statements are contradictory to say the least.     35097.50403524315
on the one hand, any of us could pick up on a whole cluster of inflammatory remarks by the leader of the freedom party.     5485.919924987897
on the other hand, the new austrian government' s programme reaffirms its commitment to defend democracy and human rights.     13038.414039689671
we will soon have our first opportunity, and an excellent opportunity it will be, to see which one of these two faces of the freedom party is the real one.     1590.8916218302563
i am referring to the position the austrian government will adopt at the council with regard to the commission' s proposals for the establishment of an action programme to combat discrimination and for two directives.     7962.490126666677
one applies to the principle of treating people equally, without differentiating on the grounds of race or ethnic origin.     16617.580000423102
the other proposal will provide protection at work against discrimination on the grounds of ethnic or racial origin, religion, handicap or sexual persuasion.     21712.494254892397
these are significant texts, adopted in accordance with article 13 of the treaty, which gave the community jurisdiction to fight discrimination on the grounds of race, ethnic origin, sex etc.     14507.335848442703
lastly, i would like to assure you, mr ford, that the commission will remain vigilant and will not fail to act in accordance with its responsibilities, should it prove necessary.     20042.971783570447

questions to mrs wallström
question no 41 by (h-0021/00):
subject: surplus electrical and electronic equipment each year the european union has to deal with a growing volume of surplus electrical and electronic equipment (6 million tonnes' worth in 1998), mainly on account of the speed with which such equipment becomes obsolescent.     15128.553569863841
the environmental problems stemming from the incineration and the dumping of such waste are mainly attributable to the presence of hazardous substances (lead, cadmium, mercury, hexavalent chrome, pvc and halogenated flame retarders).     39155.33116786222
a preliminary draft directive intended to regulate the management of such waste has been through three successive versions, the most recent of which dates from july 1999.
can the commission account for the lengthy gestation of this draft, which should in theory have emerged in its final form in 1998?     4890.327145448717
is it true that the us government is disputing most of the provisions of the current draft and is even threatening to appeal to the world trade organisation (for infringement of article xi of the gatt and article 2(2) of the agreement on technical obstacles to trade) if it is adopted?     4231.656567734537
the commission agrees with meps that the current method of dumping electrical and electronic waste is causing major environmental problems.     8268.956514495307
consequently, the commission decided to prepare a proposal on the subject and, since 1997, has been involved in discussions with the parties concerned.     2423.6297881184814
the outcome of the discussions, as well as of the in-depth investigations which are being carried out in this area, are now being examined by the commission.     2311.1040392395116
some parts of the proposal have been criticised, among them the phasing out of some heavy metals and brominated flame retardants, as well as the application of the principle of making manufacturers responsible for the disposal of electrical and electronic waste.     13426.75826352683
the united states' delegation to the european union is concerned about certain points in the last consultation paper, inter alia the phasing out of the materials already mentioned, as well as certain questions arising from the principle of manufacturers' responsibility for the disposal of electrical and electronic waste.     8624.72519011639
the main argument put forward by the united states in this context is that the present provisions are, they say, incompatible with international trade law.     4782.9546642337955
the commission is currently carefully reviewing the united states' arguments.     9352.44461258648
i should just like to add that my intention is that the commission should be able to present a proposal on the dumping of electrical and electronic waste before easter.     6711.763541050702
my question specifically concerned the reaction of the us government to the text of the preliminary draft.     2092.0824645271086
there have in fact been three preliminary draft texts with the last one, to my knowledge, dating from july 1999.
is this the text which you will be submitting, or an amendment to the original version?     3302.1287608660396
version 3 is already much weaker than version 2 following the comments of us industry as relayed by the us government.     9918.129448667296
i note in particular that when you talk about the international law with which the text would not be compatible, you are referring in fact to the rules of the world trade organisation.     2736.6045427823346
the arguments of the us government concern the fact that a future ban on the presence of dangerous substances in electrical and electronic waste would be contrary to the wto rules.     8941.287477202508
this worries me because this means that, when adopting texts, we are going to be continually threatened by attack from the world trade organisation.     5103.7485400204705
this will fundamentally weaken european environmental legislation and particularly this proposal.     2983.091445747489
thank you for the follow-up question.     2247.9894032312272
it is important for me to have a chance to answer it.     2683.872315972193
it is clear that in a process such as this, where we are discussing the disposal of a considerable stream of both electrical and electronic waste in europe, there is an on-going dialogue between the different parties concerned.     4936.07099839043
the dialogue has been fruitful and has led to our preparing a proposal.     2668.623406291226
parts of it have been strengthened, but other parts may well contain too many compromises.     9855.91172901777
we are still in the process of preparing a text, and the dialogue with the relevant parties will continue up until the last moment.     2300.75549879854
i do not think that the intervention of the united states should push us in a direction that would lead to our taking insufficient account of environmental considerations.     4546.293212466322
i strongly oppose using the wto and trade rules as a pretext for preventing the eu from introducing radical environmental standards.     4896.150778980434
i start from the assumption that we should in fact introduce these.     8344.793858474268
however, i do want to listen to all the points of view.     9085.947392213933
for example, i recently met representatives of the electronics industry who put forward suggestions - including practical ones - for improving our own proposal.     2275.8774918727568
however, i totally reject any suggestion that i would allow the united states to determine the shape of our directive.     8347.365193847572
in fact, i believe that we should be showing the way, an intention that will also characterise the final proposal.     4053.0077001496375
question no 42 by (h-0026/00):
subject: gender and environment practically all the contributors involved in the rio and kyoto processes - individual organisations, civic action groups, governments, the world bank, the un and relief organisations - believed that women should take a greater part in decision-making on the environment.     6751.27763670772

through the current programme, which covers the period 1997 to 2000, we are endeavouring to encourage a workplace culture in which the value of both men and women is integrated and where gender-specific needs are taken into consideration.     3821.944167698526
one of the programme's aims is to develop and supervise methods, strategies and measures for promoting gender equality in the decision-making process and in positions of responsibility.     3655.3874173665954

subject: the boyne estuary and the drogheda port company the boyne estuary is designated a special protection area (spa) under the wild birds directive and is under consideration for designation as a special area of conservation under the habitats directive, as it is an area of international importance.     14098.60474573821

the commission is aware of the construction of the harbour, but we know nothing about the warehouse or the rest of the infrastructure mentioned by the honourable member.     5207.685743278564
the harbour construction project is receiving support from the structural funds and chiefly involves dredging the river boyne estuary and depositing the mud on the mudflats at stegrennan.     8840.234928776666
the boyne estuary is designated a special protection area under the wild birds directive.     19235.849652867768
that means that any development that takes place in or near the boyne estuary and that is likely to affect the special protection area must take account of the protection criteria for the area laid down in the community' s habitats directive.     7805.9416615941045

an in-depth environmental impact assessment had been carried out.     2719.980629954699

with regard to your specific concern, i can say that, as in the previous period, the commission does not intend to directly finance through the structural funds research and technical development in the area of nuclear fusion or fission.     5284.03693658966
however, it is still the responsibility of the member states' management authorities to select the projects to be implemented within these csfs.     9024.215579364216
consequently, even though this is not a community priority, a member state can decide to finance research and development projects in the civil nuclear technology area, provided that these projects contribute to regional development.     2504.503642028156
the commission does not have to be systematically informed of these.     2648.4632091364074
on your second point of renewable energy which, as you know, is of great interest to me, the commission' s approach is given in the document on the structural funds and their coordination with the cohesion funds.     3795.278346693449
these funds specify that investments in the renewable energy sector must be encouraged where they favour the development of local resources, where they contribute to reducing dependency on energy imports and where they create jobs at local level.     7435.807761872728
i was able to verify this, for example, during a visit i made to the azores in portugal a few days ago with regard to a natural source of renewable energy.     10010.321758196274
the structural funds contribution which has enabled greater penetration of the renewable energy market was also underlined in the campaign to promote renewable energy sources launched by the transport and energy department.     5192.28404701648
finally, on large-scale energy transmission networks, the guidelines also provide for financial participation from the funds to develop these networks where this helps to reduce dependency on an external supplier or to combat the effects of isolation.     7598.254701570155
this is particularly valid for the region of the azores which i have just mentioned and also for all most remote regions.     1523.432885345233
it is also valid for certain regions handicapped by their isolation due to mountains, for example.     9114.257631360364
commissioner, thank you for your answer.     2531.5072336731705
