this project amounts to several billion ecus and areas eligible for structural funds, such as those listed in objective 1, might welcome this type of installation.     12713.032393227315
we would like to know whether the commission would be likely to finance this type of project in terms of the installation of this research site rather than directly in the specific 'nuclear research' context.     3696.6860505834843
you did not fully answer the last part of my question on large-scale energy transmission networks.     2993.896889316713
could you please give an answer on this issue?     6306.992222631579
you are concerned about whether the structural funds will be used to finance the installation of nuclear plants anywhere.     5777.229787397915
quite simply there is no question of this.     1787.44749862773
i said that the selection of projects remains the responsibility of the member states pursuant to the structural funds regulation.     4534.413766032998
as a result, the commission services are not systematically informed of all projects selected.     5550.520518994421
however, you are right to question me as this is your task, as mine is to answer you.     2550.300401942118
as has happened in the past, the member states are required to provide answers when the commission questions them or asks for clarification.     5802.421396417006
i will therefore step beyond the confidence usually shared between member states and the commission and i will find out exactly what is happening on this specific point.     3550.8063329454103
the member state or states concerned will then be required to answer me and i will send you this answer as soon as possible.     3868.450342006283
i thought i had answered your question on large-scale energy transmission networks.     8122.785933186164
the energy situation varies widely from region to region in the european union.     3086.9605453851696
assistance from the structural funds could be justified in certain cases and for certain regions, particularly in the case where the connection to basic energy networks is still underdeveloped.     3270.8880073010637

question no 46 by (h-0052/00):
subject: structural funds and additionality in the light of the commission's recent helpful response to my priority written question about structural funds and additionality, i wish to ask whether the commission has any plans to seek amendment of the rules concerning additionality.     9969.689432383064
in particular, would the commission look favourably on a change in the rules to ensure that additionality operates not only at the level of member states but also within member states, with regard to budgetary provisions between central government and internally autonomous regions or countries?     5799.781217014916
mr maccormick, the commission has no plans to amend the rules concerning additionality which were stipulated, for the 2000-2006 period, in article 11 of the general regulation on the funds.     5798.895226421221
as in the past, these rules state that the additionality principle applies to the relationship between the structural funds and all development expenditure of the member state.     3034.4649260202896
in this respect, it must be stressed that it is the expenditure financed by the structural funds which must be additional.     2585.276657318339
the cofinancing provided by the member state does not need to be additional to its existing expenditure.     4551.867988992871
provided that the member state does not reduce all its own expenditure, the structural funds are added to the national expenditure and the principle of additionality is therefore respected.     2217.315034990566
as for the budgetary provisions within the member states, between central government and internal autonomous regions or countries, these are determined according to exclusively national considerations and are therefore not affected by additionality within the meaning defined in the rules.     4908.951722226233
please excuse this very legal answer.     5353.792360723735
however, after verification it is precisely in this legal manner and specifically according to the post-berlin structural funds regulation that we must define and read the additionality principle.     6768.378645627279
- i am grateful for a clear answer though naturally a little disappointed by its content.     12886.001448326653
we are left with article 11(1) which says that the appropriations of the funds may not replace public or other equivalent structural expenditure by the member states.     4196.859062382197
very well: that is to be the unchanging rule.     600.9235769633281
does section 11(1) conceivably authorise the following practice.     61714.48801909634
when an autonomous region's or country's funding from european structural funds increases, the state makes a concomitant reduction in central funding to that region, so that the total funding package available matches a formula set nationally without regard to the quantum of structural funds granted by the union.     7909.940600612543
is that really authorised?     1113.5176873273213
unfortunately i do not have time to go into depth on this issue.     2607.892269277021
if mr maccormick would be satisfied with my partial agreement with his reasoning, i will give him a full answer in writing by restating the legal bases of both article 11 in general and article 11(1) in particular.     8476.318221544738
i am aware of the member' s competence on these matters.     23419.687295526608
i also know the particular problems in your constituency in scotland where disputes and discussions are occurring on these subjects.     7471.919053427784
however, on a general level, i would say that in all cases it is the structural funds expenditure which must be additional.     1663.0809483991675
this is how, provided that the member state does not reduce all its own expenditure, the structural funds can be added to the national expenditure and we can consider that the additionality principle is respected.     2291.981239764711
i will go further in the written answer which i have promised in order to set things out more objectively and precisely.     5475.175106831491
- additionality is a major issue too in wales, which i represent.     5556.792218426435
just last week it was one of the issues that brought about a vote of no-confidence and the resignation of the first secretary of the national assembly of wales.     2904.8540887597724
as recently as 1991 and 1992 the commission won a battle with the uk over additionality, at that time linked to the rechar funding.     3975.9864068875554
this led to an agreement being signed whereby the uk government agreed to introduce procedures to ensure that eu spending reached the areas for which is was intended and was actually additional.     4314.336368591002
and yet we still have these problems in wales and in scotland.     1828.6720034810548
could the commission look at this particular situation in relation to the agreement that was signed with the uk government?     6792.401218210579
i take this to mean that the debate of which i was aware in scotland is also occurring in wales.     3573.1628551235303
i will verify this point and, if you wish, i will give you a written answer at the same time as the one i have promised to mr maccormick.     4547.279772031906

question no 54 by (h-0117/00):
subject: alcohol monopoly and the internal market what action does the commission intend to take in order to prevent the extension of the state alcohol monopoly apparently being planned by sweden, and the associated import restrictions, and to ensure that the rules of the single market are applied?     7545.766363169139
i would like to answer these two questions as follows.     7010.681371145883
when sweden joined the european union, it was allowed to retain quantitative restrictions on alcoholic beverages which travellers can bring into the country from other member states.     14275.12068050847
this derogation from the principle of the free movement of goods lapses on 30 june of this year.     16585.979760484643
sweden now wants to extend this measure by another five years, as this would be necessary to protect public health.     4481.015992293801
my position on this is clear.     3968.4093084521674
sweden has now had sufficient time - since its accession to the union - to adapt its policies to a situation where such import restrictions do not exist.     4077.863385232975
i cannot see a reason, therefore, why a further extension for this measure should be proposed.     4425.402058123063
european citizens have the right to buy goods for their own use, including taxes, in any member sate and to take them to another member sate without having to subject these goods to controls and without possibly having to pay another set of taxes on them.     5123.8545616206275
that is a basic principle governing the internal market and derogations from this principle must be exceptions and must be limited in time.     4519.595724477191
we want to guarantee that swedish citizens too can enjoy the benefits of the internal market, just like other citizens of the european union.     3472.512876411862
this certainly does not mean that i do not share the concern in sweden surrounding the possible health problems which can be caused by alcohol abuse.     4352.317325857344
a recent study, carried out by professor lindgren of the university of lund, however, has shown that abolishing the restrictions would not lead to a higher alcohol consumption in sweden.     17166.454845133303
i have already had a chance to discuss my position with mr ringholm, the swedish finance minister on two occasions.     11256.33795650137
i also discussed this matter with the swedish parliamentary finance committee last week.     6056.194149910461
it is now up to the swedish government to take the necessary measures.     1674.273624767307

the commission has no means of influence here.     3068.9373658513314
in the case of sweden, the instrument of excise taxes is indeed used to reduce the consumption of alcohol.     14471.167416644119
although this leads to a disparity in excise taxes between sweden and other member states of the union - and that by itself increases cross-border smuggling of alcoholic products - it is a legitimate instrument to decrease the consumption of alcohol.     13230.029223659507
there is ,of course, elasticity of demand, in this case a price elasticity of demand.     17122.841099254776
i am not sure exactly what it is, but it is not zero, therefore it must have some effect.     1661.4183894453834
my question would have been almost exactly the one raised by mr purvis, but i would like to press the point further.     4723.873314138259
granted, as you say, that excise duties lie within the prerogative of member states, nevertheless the use of that prerogative in a way which is unfairly discriminatory against producers in one part of the community versus others might still conflict with the single market principle.     8376.30559839022
for example, if we take what mr purvis and i would be thinking of, the malt whisky producing parts of the highlands - a very peripheral part of europe, very hard-pressed, a whole farming community as well as a distilling community dependent upon it - a general practice of taxing the alcohol in scotch whisky, dutch gin or danish aquavit more than the alcohol in beer or the alcohol in wine, seems to me to be discriminatory and a discriminatory use of something which is admittedly within the prerogative of the member states.     9810.375375345027
may i assure mr maccormick and other members of this parliament that the present situation where excise taxes differ between member states is indeed something which is not conducive to the proper functioning of the internal market.     8424.532520268796
we need not restrict ourselves to alcoholic products.     12728.060291579259
if one looks at petrol, for example, one sees that excise taxes in germany are different from those of holland, and consequently dutch motorists cross the border and fill up their tanks in germany.     10286.181768055158
that is indeed distortion of the internal market.     3698.8007496554515
if i had my way, i would wave a magic wand and equalise excise taxes across europe.     19783.456915953582
as a result, smuggling would no longer take place except if products were intrinsically more expensive in one member state than another.     8867.16381058793
but i do not have a magic wand and i do not have my way.     4977.9206319547775
this is an area of unanimity, as mr maccormick knows, and unless all member states agree on the equalisation of excise taxes, it will not happen.     20279.685517798775
once again, towards the end of this year, the commission will forward a report on the state of affairs on excise taxes, and i am obviously available for any discussion with parliament on that report.     2853.9300037598628
thank you very much, commissioner.     434.5214474481301
today we will take note of what you say and have a glass or two of alsace wine, although we do not know how much tax is put on it, but i suppose it is a lot.     5131.480079138578
since the time allotted to questions to the commission has elapsed, questions 55 to 114 will be replied to in writing.     11292.44917998921
that concludes questions to the commission.     4373.656654040384
(the sitting was suspended at 7.30 p.m. and resumed at 9.00 p.m.)
community policy in the field of water (continuation)
the next item is the continuation of the debate on the recommendation for second reading of the community action in the field of water policy.     65808.40520406605
mr president, the situation regarding fresh water in europe is not as serious as in other parts of the world but, in general terms, it is a fact that the demand for water has continued to grow and its quality has continued to decrease.     2557.835605654612
to this we must add the water pollution problems which afflict the potential new member states in the east.     1625.8881529833714
in fact, a reservoir containing cyanide has just burst its banks in romania, threatening aquifers which supply the population of yugoslavia.     16987.462807629425
i understand that mrs wallström, whose presence here tonight i appreciate, is going to visit the area.     23435.632273227977
i believe that no country, no government and, of course, no member of this parliament can fail to recognise the need for this directive in order to end the current fragmentation of water policy and facilitate the implementation of a programme of specific measures for each river basin.     5864.076627502247
the development of this directive has been difficult and complex, involving diverse interests and divided opinions.     6187.061215473212
i have no doubt that the rapporteur for this legislature, mrs lienemann, has made great efforts and shown great dedication in reconciling positions, and has succeeded to a large extent.     8884.792869980756
however, it is almost impossible to understand and correctly assess all the different situations and expectations.     4709.986172030906
i come from a country whose southern half is on the mediterranean and the availability of water historically depends on the whims of an unpredictable climate and a difficult terrain.     2141.548394519508
the mediterranean region has had to fight for its development by trying to overcome this adversity, century after century, year after year, day after day, and is still doing so today.     3500.1326248503156
therefore, it seems essential to us that the directive complies, in practice, with the obligation laid down in article 164 of the treaty on the need for community legislation to take account of the diversity of its regions.     3350.862545495107
in accordance with this principle, the spanish delegation of the european people' s party will oppose amendments nos 4 (recital 21), 13 and 49 (article 11(d)), since they imply a serious limitation in an area of management exclusive to the member states, that is, the regulation of water resources.     12491.150510494203
it would be very difficult for the community to determine the conditions for the regulation of water in the different regions, given the different internal balances which are always in play and which require profound knowledge of the different areas and the relevant interests.     1969.7327025790776
we also reject the amendments which aim to incorporate the integral cost of water for 2010.
we must clearly set water prices which promote its efficient use but which, at the same time, safeguard the competitivity of the producing industries in the less-favoured regions and do not hinder their legitimate development.     4679.226359982294
i would lastly like to refer to the amendments on dangerous substances which establish that we must achieve a zero, or close to zero, degree of pollution.     4602.7169489468015
in no field of human activity have we ever discovered a zero degree of pollution.     6901.708583382038
we will be doing a general disservice to the objectives of this important directive if we are not able to provide it with the necessary flexibility and adaptability to guarantee compliance with its provisions.     4832.039042204745
i hope that, once again, this house will vote with a sense of reality and respect for the principle of subsidiarity, seeking a suitable balance between the environmental objectives and economic and social considerations, the three essential elements in achieving the sustainable development which we all wish to see so much.     2550.5720123799033
mr president, commissioner, this is a very important directive.     668.8904867258707
its aim is to improve the quality of water and the reliability of the water supply and sewage systems.     6200.633292836972
the public must have a right to clean water.     1515.4648646738988
it is important not only for the environment but also for public health.     634.2517644140408
i am not speaking so much for myself, as i happen to be one of those few lucky people in europe who can drink water directly from their own lake at home.     5849.164591371021
we must make sure that we get started quickly with the programme to improve water quality.     3587.5091897113743
action must get under way immediately.     6928.006496398156
we cannot afford to waste time.     11637.68815887219
it is important that the timetable is an ambitious one.     2306.06923154141
in my opinion, the timetable is not too ambitious if we believe that by 2020 we will no longer allow impurities in water and we aim at zero level quantities of impurities and toxic matter by then.     7968.477525789233
it is a question of doing what is technically possible; obviously it is not possible to do anymore, but we must be sufficiently ambitious.     3238.3081211707495
i would like to comment on one issue, which was spoken about earlier.     5824.255975585888
i believe the committee on the environment, public health and consumer policy has restricted too tightly the debate on the transfer of water.     6190.380466090474
this is not merely a problem for spain; it is one for the nordic countries, and i sincerely hope that we can rely on national solutions in these matters, when they are better for the environment and the economy than what is proposed in the directive.     1680.2112988032586
mr president, madam commissioner, congratulations, mrs lienemann on an excellent piece of work.     20201.25823306248
water and air surround us everywhere.     21336.682924185647
we share them with everyone on this earth.     8908.099841036354
water is a prerequisite for human life.     8757.649675660217
and there are more and more of us in the world.     696.9422564598948
mrs schleicher intimated earlier today that clean water was an unrealistic goal.     18220.547559264494
nothing could be further from the truth!     4119.5011521738215
it is unrealistic to go on causing the quality of our water to deteriorate and so, too, the conditions for life.     2658.1336469264584
it is especially unrealistic in regard to agriculture which is most dependent upon a clean natural environment and unpolluted resources.     6067.911874866758
i would therefore appeal to members of parliament to appreciate that tough demands for a clean environment are a sign of long-term realism of the highest order.     7408.253545279478
mr president, we - myself included - support the report by mrs lienemann, the targets it sets and the directive under discussion.     4799.028631459366
however, we should look a little more closely at some points.     1823.2862299181998
for example, a major problem in my country is the diversion of rivers and the consumption of water from lakes, which people try to justify by pseudo-developmental arguments, and which, of course, are a great risk to ground waters, water tables and surface waters.     11092.499053910631
i therefore think that regardless of whether we finally conclude who should decide whether such diversions take place, the specifications common to us all must be very stringent.     3536.290359810029
but the key issue is, in my opinion, planning how to restore water tables, the natural flow of rivers and the natural content of lakes, which must be carried out within the ten year period we have in mind.     5756.021956624414
i think such projects ought to be eligible for funding, because, at the end of the day, that restoration may well prove to be a first-rate development policy.     4506.57993977961
mr president, here we have what is basically a good measure.     1008.1917700885853
it sets out achievable standards based on subsidiarity principles and the water basin management.     8299.453564645119
its aim of good-quality water for drinking, for wildlife, the environment and economic purposes is right.     3124.0474584437143
the prevention of pollution and water deterioration has to be right as well as the ability to manage water in times of drought and flood.     6378.9554490277915
we have three outstanding problems.     2836.0315171568805
the first is the transfer of water between basin areas.     7278.623839293474
that is a matter which affects my colleagues from spain, ireland and the united kingdom.     3554.7538496639313
amendments nos 4.     3802533.784377513
49 and 87 would not be acceptable because they would restrict the ability of a country to move water from where it is in supply to where it is needed, be that arid areas or urban areas.     3526.887193065035
secondly, we have to have realistic targets, but targets nevertheless.     3156.7603103086963
looking at amendment no 7, for example, which calls for the complete elimination of naturally occurring substances, you can see that some of the targets set are unrealistic.     6903.860041638211
also, some of the targets which are set at close to zero - in english, that is quite a meaningless term.     2369.2787713891616
we need to look very carefully at those.     2560.6961480731356
the concept of continuing reduction as in amendment no 58 is much better.     2721.929242122379
thirdly, i raise the problem faced by scotch whisky.     140901.76421539477
scotch whisky - especially malt whisky, which is the best - requires water extraction which is used in some quantity and then returned to the water stream.     15470.160502459506
some of it ends up in the bottle and is drunk.     11990.122609306844
we must make sure that amendments nos 49 and 87 leave the deletion exemption so that whisky can continue to be drunk in "high quality", which means it must use good, scottish peat water.     18151.96258193234
that is my third request - that we look carefully at these measures before passing this measure as a whole.     3234.881143827498
mr president, first of all, we want to denounce the fathomless hypocrisy of those who claim to be concerned about the environment and water, but whose criminal activities such as the attacks against yugoslavia, aside from leaving thousands dead and wounded, have also brought about huge ecological disasters for water resources too, making them not only unusable but even extremely hazardous.     3869.7580426707677
turning to the report, we believe that implementing the principle that the cost of water services should be recoverable must never result in additional taxation of the economically weakest strata, or even in the annihilation of small and medium-scale farmers because of the prohibitive cost of water for irrigation.     3938.2881607759623
we also wish to point out that especially in areas with acute drought problems such as my own country, and especially on the islands, funding must be provided for infrastructure for the saving of water, by increasing not its cost but its quantity.     3491.547072004019
this should mainly be carried out by collecting the rainwater that now goes to waste, running off into the sea and eroding the soil, with all the problems that creates.     2771.4948069810066
in that spirit, we support amendment no 107 by mr marset campos.     10268.432716074081
river waters must also be used more effectively so as to preserve life in areas that suffer chronic drought.     7265.962949491988
finally, as regards the council' s allegation, which the commission echoes, that there is no need for many specific references and explanations because they are covered by the examples given in the directive or contained elsewhere therein, this is chicanery.     9026.387001843374
its purpose is to preserve a semi-transparent field of action, which during the directive' s first implementation period will allow critical choices to be finalised to the benefit of big business.     16290.597506319335
mr president, first and foremost, i want to say that protection of the aquatic environment, both surface water and groundwater, is probably one of our most important duties of all.     4381.889858712191
that is not only because it is about guaranteeing sufficient water resources but also because it is, to a large degree, about safeguarding water resources against pollution so that we can also have clean drinking water in the future.     1685.6324095965902
we are all entitled to clean drinking water.     6493.080527565228
i should like to express my great appreciation of mrs lienemann' s work on this matter.     37857.92890619039
it has been a huge task, and i should like to express my support for all of mrs lienemann' s amendments.     8833.456234633435
these are all improvements to the common position.     1687.4517378572837
moreover, i shall only highlight the most important ones.     4827.136852685003
first and foremost, i think it is important that we should establish some clear objectives concerning the condition we should have achieved for our water within a period of no more than 10 years.     2999.230083384083
i also think it is important that we should put pressure on the member states to devise the necessary action programmes more quickly than is proposed in the common position.     2943.5055657979447
and, finally, i want to say that i think that the tighter arrangements which are being introduced in regard to payment schemes and pricing are quite proper if we, as consumers, are to be given an incentive to use water resources efficiently and, at the same time, obtain arrangements which can promote the achievement of those environmental goals about which, i believe, there is broad agreement.     4471.651067700937
in this regard, too, i think that the 2010 deadline is appropriate.     2775.996414528083
finally, i want to say that i attach great importance to making a start on phasing out discharges, emissions and leaks of hazardous materials and to the fact that we can do this gradually but, at the same time, be set a target, namely the year 2020 by which time we should hopefully have reduced such emissions to zero.     5897.021192079271
if this cannot be adopted as the objective, then i am naturally prepared to support the proposal to the effect that we should have achieved levels very close to zero by the year 2020.
i think that mrs lienemann' s proposal is a good basis for further negotiations with the council.     3805.6315680548173
mr president, i would like to thank the rapporteur for some excellent work in drafting this important directive.     3169.4569706165607
the second reading of the framework directive on the issue of water is most timely; the shocking news of the environmental catastrophe in romania must feature in today' s discussions and also more generally when we ponder the environmental dimension of union enlargement.     4562.405488195625
first of all we have to find and call to account those who are guilty of the act.     2276.4243799888045
the cyanide and heavy metals in the rivers are gruesome examples of how environmental negligence may ruin the waterways for decades to come.     27223.977006167952
the event proves that in some countries applying for eu membership the environmental norms and environmental thinking are still light years behind the eu.     4757.519859003525
the union ought to reassess how aid for environmental projects can be allocated in a better way in support of a sustainable policy on water.     2274.0505673207786
with regard to the proposal for the directive it is worrying that the amendments tabled by the committee on the environment, public health and consumer policy do not take sufficient account of the importance of unpolluted surface waters to increase the occurrence of clean, natural groundwater.     5463.219410639776
in finland surface waters are very clean.     3266.9771942572515
the production of so-called artificial groundwater is an ecological means of filtering a clean supply of fresh surface water through to the groundwater reserves.     9012.429852456886
the process does not require a chemical sewage plant.     16915.301129077823
the directive must not be allowed to endanger this work.     2915.9859850600524
deviating from the council' s common position, the committee on the environment, public health and consumer policy is also attempting to restrict further the facilities for the transfer of water.     9294.876871966038
this would cause problems for those countries where reserves of water are unevenly distributed.     10648.573029992502
the ecologically sustainable transfer of water must not be restricted by union acts.     11959.687014426228
the work should, of course, be regulated and subject to license in the future too, but the same rules cannot apply to, for example, finland and greece, which suffers from drought.     2900.3278618369677
mr president, commissioner, water is a precious natural asset, a vital necessity, and it is our duty to protect it.     6562.64299181716
i think it essential to have a framework directive on water policy in the european union, which will be the basic legal instrument for improving the quality and proper management of water resources, and i congratulate the rapporteur on her attempt to address this thorny issue and take due account of all the points of view.     3272.9890975419094
i must say, however, that, there are discrepancies between the countries in the north and south concerning water, and that is why the matter cannot be dealt with in the same way.     2361.799126320466
there are parts of europe with an inherent shortage of water, and that should be taken into account by this directive.     3147.1532711950654
in recent years, climate change has compelled mediterranean countries to face serious problems of flooding or lack of rain.     15443.685650615807
we agree with the common position but we also accept several of the amendments tabled, whose aim is to improve it.     3136.960745047608
however, we cannot accept amendments such as those that refer to the issue of costing water, granted that in my country a high proportion of the users are farmers.     4668.804378660616
in addition, amendments which ban the transfer of water from one hydrological basin to another would condemn many areas in my country to water shortage.     10906.878678989688
the issue of eliminating hazardous substances ought, i think, to be made more realistic so that we can achieve it.     5051.475235521848
as for the time scale demanded for the directive' s implementation, it must be borne in mind that there are countries where creating the infrastructure for this directive' s implementation will take time and considerable resources.     5122.776871359944
in spite of these remarks however, i believe our common objective of sustainable use of water resources can be maintained.     9713.622192102208
mr president, commissioner, the measures contained in this directive range from drafting water management plans, comprehensive information and hearing rights, and close cooperation between the member states, to combating water pollution caused by individual pollutants.     4157.412422706399
and considering how long this chamber has been trying to introduce a framework directive of this kind, i am pleased to be present here today, and i would like to congratulate mrs lienemann on being in the happy position of presenting this report to us.     5489.342384051513
however, it is also extremely important to me to point something out that many people actually take for granted.     1125.4774944614915
i would still like to highlight this point though: this directive does not constitute any kind of legal basis for diverting water from a member state' s territory against its will.     5647.425446528583
and by way of reaffirming this once again, i have introduced an amendment - gratifyingly with the support of a large number of members - and i would be very pleased if parliament would endorse my proposed amendment tomorrow.     2943.8618156775983
it is all about making it clear that water - and this is not the first time this has been mentioned today - is no ordinary commodity but a resource which belongs to the individual eu member states and which must be protected and treated with the respect due to it.     1846.0889019516846
i am aware, and the previous speaker highlighted this, that there are states in the european union that have problems with water.     2545.081251401216
i believe that every one of us, in every country, could improve the way in which we manage our water and could make use of it in a cleaner way.     3683.3905774595823
this applies to every country.     5622.818392968847
but only when each country has done its utmost to conserve its water resources, when literally everything has been done that could have been done in our own countries, only then, in my view, should it be necessary to start thinking in terms of harmonising water usage.     3683.9511115358387
mr president, commissioner, we should be pleased that this directive has already reached second reading.     2036.3483646937157
however, i must say that i am not over-pleased because i believe that this directive is not of great quality, it is going to be very difficult to implement and has many shortcomings.     2192.052777861806
firstly, i believe that this directive is excessively tough, simplistic and reductionist.     8066.102346393918
in many cases other existing directives on water have not been complied with and governments and the commission have often not imposed discipline as they should have done, although i know that this is not pleasant.     2235.1306994174
it is not a question of having much tougher regulations, because in many cases the problems concern management.     2345.499425940414
it is not a question of exercising the relevant competences and not referring the big problems of the water systems to the legislative, the complications of which we are all well aware of.     1681.2056401893515
this is a reductionist directive.     14089.347672871469
we cannot lump countries with navigable rivers together with countries which lack water, where great cracks open up in the ground through a lack of water and which see temperatures of 55ºc or 60ºc in the sun.     3275.3157180218723
i say this because the ball does not need to be in our court.     3955.170450474586
this issue is in the hands of the governments and the commission.     2054.549580052847
in the same way, this damage and these tragedies which are happening, as in romania, are not the problem of the legislative, but of the executive and of the governments.     1522.0355171487536

that is how the common position has been worded.     2490.0438790254684
the same can be said with regard to transfers.     5353.699803204582
transfers are an example of the distribution of wealth, of solidarity between peoples and territories, because unfortunately the earth is not perfect and some areas have more and some have less.     4013.9401546546387
lastly, i am highly critical of the fact that the directive is not accompanied by a study of its impact.     4157.551449460785
it is very easy for us to demand that others assess its impact but, on environmental questions, we should demand it of ourselves and consider how many people, how many farmers, could be ruined by having to pay the costs.     3423.8858326729287
some kind of study has been done which has not even been read, and i believe we should carry out impact studies by way of good political practice, rather than just laying down doctrine.     7445.259563336521
mr president, through the consolidation of existing directives this framework directive establishes the basic principle of a sustainable water policy in the european union.     3931.288150872635
it is a framework for the protection and management of our water - surface, ground, transitional and coastal - through the establishment of river basin districts at national and, in our case in ireland, cross-border level.     4239.8393476916135
ireland is very supportive of the common position and many of the european parliament amendments.     1966.389125838529
a 25-year roll-out for legislation, for example, is hardly acceptable.     5443.23147828181
we have no difficulty with the "polluter pays" principle in relation to the recovery of costs on water usage at industrial, commercial and agricultural level.     10046.513508921389
however, i feel very strongly that as water is life and access to clean drinking water is a basic human right, this directive must allow for a basic domestic water provision free of charge, a quota per household for drinking, cooking and sanitation.     3537.364673557907
we must of course pay for wastage and luxury use at domestic level and the recovery of costs can be balanced accordingly.     7607.798945103467
the introduction of water meters is viewed with great suspicion in ireland where we have had no domestic water charges and hence no meters since 1977.
meters could be viewed as a very important tool in water management by the new river basin districts, to help the elimination of water wastage and for the proper planning of future requirements.     10719.971268855748
the whole purpose of this directive is to promote sustainable use of water in the eu.     3329.1182319209374
water is a scarce and precious natural resource.     10881.386516507977
ideally, i would like amendment no 45 to fall tomorrow so that the common position on article 9 would hold on water charges.     4313.410633310062
that would allow for subsidiarity on a cost policy for water charges, with each member states drawing up its own scheme, so long as the principles of this directive on incentives for sustainable and efficient use of water are fully protected.     3694.033379714994
if amendment no 45 does not fall, i will have to move an oral amendment tomorrow to ensure that member states may grant exemptions to the provisions of this article in order to allow a basic level of water use for domestic purposes before contributions to the recovery of cost of water services.     5448.814206797288
all legislation must be enforceable and be based on the general principle of political acceptance.     3479.4862799161224
i believe that access to clean drinking water without charge is a basic human right in the developed as well as the developing world.     3326.8219425173593
mr president, commissioner, at this point in the debate i am going to insist on certain aspects which, from an agronomic point of view, and since i come from a southern country, i consider to be absolutely essential to this directive.     3053.935515035202
these include, for example, costs and transfers.     6635.271413143933
with regard to costs, i would like clarification of what costs we are talking about.     8328.815997143292
are we talking about the distribution and sourcing of water?     2089.414900567709
are we talking about the quality of the distribution networks?     8288.88197031612
are we talking about the nature of the works required to provide water services?     3796.5532565528733
or are we talking about large-scale hydraulic works and reservoirs?     4285.395521110471
all these costs added together would be horrendous and would have repercussions for the agricultural sector.     4957.7381764330285
transfers are defined as the transfer of surface water between different territorial areas.     9894.758831494291
is the commissioner aware that our country has one of the oldest systems of transfers in europe?     1694.8254494762923
they already existed in ancient times: they had them in roman times, although on a smaller scale.     8537.770361210598
one of the most significant transfers - the tajo-segura transfer - which has been in use for many years, almost 30 years, has an allowance of 658 hm3 per year.     5289.324061264836
the largest volume transferred in one year has been 453 and the average volume over twenty years has been over 200.
we cannot be accused of bad management.     6775.776352036581
is the commissioner aware that, of this 263 hm3, we used 25 for irrigation on the left bank of the levante, 30 for water supplies in the area of the júcar, 7 for supplies in the southern area of the júcar and the rest in the segura, equally divided between supplies and irrigation?     9020.673153104712
we are not doing a bad job of management.     1720.797537912589
we cannot be prohibited from carrying out transfers when our economy and the development of our regions depend on it.     4878.492664710124
i will not tackle the issue of quality and the best available technologies, because i believe that we will return to a discussion of this directive.     2569.8872372093933
take good note: in this diverse europe, which we are building in a spirit of mutual cooperation, we have to find diverse solutions, not single solutions.     4865.151111401764
i therefore appeal to the common sense of the commission, the council and this house so that, when voting, they do not impose uniformity on the different countries of europe in areas where subsidiarity is required.     3552.7009367942724
commissioner, andalusia is not lapland.     38812.64236558054
.     1000.0
(sv) mr president, ladies and gentlemen, i want, of course, to begin by thanking the committee on the environment, public health and consumer policy and, above all, the rapporteur, mrs lienemann for the ambitious and constructive work she has put into the present framework water directive.     8807.737019691602
water is one of our most important natural resources, as many speakers have already said.     1115.3738160243818
an ambitious and well-balanced european policy in the field of water is an important component of guaranteed sustainable development for europe.     3921.431191028549
water is also one of the issues to which i shall be devoting particular attention during my term of office.     3045.812847881193
i am pleased about the spirit of agreement which permeates the amendments that have been submitted to parliament.     5178.5964935397
many of them are based upon the very constructive, informal discussions which have taken place between parliament and the council in connection with the first reading.     3303.153598503891
most of parliament' s proposed amendments help improve the text and make it more ambitious.     9357.06719468561
the commission can approve 72 of the 108 amendments, either in their entirety, partially or in principle.     6936.3705005469255
many of the amendments which parliament has submitted show that reconciliation will be necessary.     1989.538570285668
with a view to these forthcoming negotiations, i would therefore urge parliament to vote in favour of an ambitious position.     10593.201648805485
firstly, i should like to mention some of my most important arguments.     5852.336933032866
we now have the opportunity to put into practice those measures to which we agreed under the ospar convention.     4154.652709570015
rather than make these objectives legally binding, we must consider their political nature.     3538.900624939931
this is also reflected in many of parliament' s amendments.     12589.11271183171
where water charges are concerned, the present directive will constitute europe' s water policy for the next three decades.     7336.057080185739
it is our duty to ensure from the very start that the present directive provides the right incentives and the proper driving force for a pricing system and an efficient structure of charges which will improve our environment in a cost-effective way.     3152.8039116140603
it sounds good, saying that access to water should be a human right and ought to be free of charge.     3535.8272899439517
but water is not free of charge, any more than houses or food are free of charge.     3722.247479062609
it is only by means of efficient instruments of control that we can arrive at a situation in which water is better managed and more carefully husbanded as a resource.     2852.005556692588
i also want to add that, when it comes to groundwater and to water which has been heavily modified by human activities, i support a tighter timetable and a clarification of goals and of criteria for allowing exceptions to the rules.     3986.750269000093
i also support expressly mentioning radioactive substances.     26791.462139900435
in the course of the debate, many speakers have pointed out how important it is to consult the general public and keep them informed.     4603.844855658506
i just want to emphasise this further.     4158.224489226724
it is absolutely crucial that we involve the general public by keeping them well informed and by proceeding to consult them.     4651.674193464138
this is also expressed in recital 14.
allow me also to say to mr bowe that, in common with my scottish colleague, he need have no pangs of conscience about continuing to enjoy a glass of scotch whisky now and again.     5490.390273604057
we have not considered it necessary for the commission to regulate the very limited abstraction of water for whisky production.     2890.8240741790682
allow me now to comment on some of the areas in a little more detail.     2242.8779475806896
limiting discharges of hazardous substances into our waters must be one of the most important objectives.     11410.698012712532
i am glad to see that parliament' s amendments have focused upon this.     22463.682857460655
the commission fully supports the proposal to introduce the commitments under the ospar convention into the text.     7420.242516566647
we must, however, keep to their precise scope and content.     3671.9290212647843
as i said, their purpose and the timetable for implementing them are political in character.     3724.045982916592
it would not be in keeping with this to introduce a legally binding timetable.     2289.275723772456
it must nonetheless be guaranteed that the member states and the community are compelled to take measures to achieve the relevant goals and comply with the timetables.     6601.172939505245
i therefore welcome amendments nos 6, 10, 14, 24, 58, 60 and 88 .     12032.051460265657
the commission cannot, on the other hand, approve amendment no 19, in which hazardous substances are defined differently than in ospar.     10544.434888196929
the commission' s proposal introduces a clear procedure for selecting substances, and so no definition is needed.     28065.11655167926
even if i too agree with the contents of amendments nos 79 and 106, these go beyond the commitments under ospar, and i cannot therefore approve these amendments.     8679.975842630709
the commission can also, in principle, approve amendment no 60, according to which a timetable is required for implementing the commission' s proposal to limit discharges of prioritised substances.     14918.817619520489
amendment no 59 introduces requirements to the effect that the list of prioritised substances should continually be reviewed.     18156.89989493583
this would involve legal uncertainty as to the status of the list and cannot, therefore, be approved.     2316.9483952544288
the desire expressed in amendments nos 60 and 93 for a list of goals and a list of substances for which no adequate data is available cannot be approved.     5617.412842972369
lists of this kind would not have clear legal status and do not fit in with the procedures for adopting the list of prioritised substances.     4391.438388772535
i would point out in this regard that the ospar convention does not in any way alter existing community legislation concerning nitrates from the agricultural sector.     8900.990519929064
the nitrates directive will not therefore be influenced by the present framework directive.     2344.9238805838036
i appreciate the fact that parliament has proved to be flexible over the very sensitive and difficult question of imposing charges for water services.     4526.432346011564
i can wholeheartedly support the demand that the price of water should be set at a level which creates incentives to use water resources in a sustainable way.     3024.661940065272
i also support the demand for a suitable contribution from each sector to cover own costs.     3637.8863508692443
i can therefore partly, and in principle, approve amendments nos 43-46, together with amendment no 85.
i believe that we need legally binding requirements stating that the financial costs of water services are to be covered by each economic sector.     4056.6475042496636
this would constitute a clear goal against which progress could be measured.     9787.296341448497
i therefore support the overall thrust of amendment no 105, which is in line with the commission' s original proposal.     11461.31665605524
i also support a gradual development in the direction of covering environmental costs and the costs of resources.     1367.473117028137
we are aware of the fact that the price of water and water services is a complicated issue and that account must be taken not only of environmental goals but also of social and economic goals.     1611.9361026416695
this must not, however, be an excuse for subsidising inefficient economic sectors which cause pollution.     1769.2408408617123
we intend shortly to issue a communication from the commission on precisely this question.     5313.657147261306
i agree with parliament that the common position' s sixteen-year timeframe for implementing these measures must be considerably reduced.     2875.5570645101807
the same also applies to the possibility which exists of extending the period of implementation by a further eighteen years.     3738.9810234354804
amendment no 28, cancelling the third extension period, is an important step in the right direction.     9740.35250159385
this proposal can therefore be approved.     1934.808859436541
a period of ten years, as proposed in amendments nos 24 and 26, among others, is, on the other hand, too short.     3203.8445991258404
i believe that consideration ought to be given to wording the overall definition of the requirements under the proposal in such a way that it includes a strict and explicit condition to the effect that causing deterioration of the environment must be avoided.     3465.1539965865795
the proposal must also include more stringent criteria for establishing lesser environmental objectives and for extending deadlines in connection with heavily modified or artificial bodies of water.     11010.01510450464
this applies to amendments nos 6, 24, 25, 27, 30, 31, 32, 33, 34, 78, 80, 102 and 104.
these amendments may in large part be approved and will be supported until such time as an overall solution can be found.     4269.267789879883
to comply with the demands in amendments nos 27 and 29, to the effect that the commission should approve every extension, would be going too far, and these cannot be approved.     4566.890413554976
another important area is protection of the groundwater.     5923.911653433931
we need both a starting point and a final goal if we are to reverse the upward trend in pollution.     3729.344469525604
we also need stricter criteria for interpreting the results of any monitoring.     10843.300209796833
the most important parts of parliament' s amendments nos 25 and 73 can therefore be approved in principle.     15597.201509690345
the new definition in amendment no 72 is, however, too strict and cannot be applied for this purpose.     2305.120677465504
i would propose that amendment no 92 should be incorporated into a broader solution.     8609.180010542508
amendment no 71 does not take account of seasonal variations and annual changes in the level of the groundwater.     8333.389709214029
the proposal cannot therefore be approved.     2314.666147532622
the commission considers it unrealistic and unnecessary to lay down standards guaranteeing that the least intensive water purification is adequate to ensure that surface water is of drinking water quality.     5455.549147766457
instead, we prefer an addition to those measures which are required in accordance with article 11 to promote activities in this area.     14846.17116577404
the good status which is required under this proposal ought to guarantee that the demand for surface water of good quality is fulfilled.     4016.714865732112
this part of amendment no 41 cannot, therefore, be approved.     3152.319135993233
the commission appreciates parliament' s efforts to guarantee that the so-called combined approach is given a central role.     23155.48026664336
this approach and its scope, which the commission can in principle approve, are defined by amendments nos 22 and 47.
in order to achieve the greatest possible legal clarity and proportionality, we wish, however, to reformulate amendment no 47 and to add a de minimis provision.     5924.332795156726
the transfer of water is already covered by controls, but explicitly mentioning it makes the text clearer.     5132.480671546751
these parts of amendments nos 49 and 87 can, therefore, be approved.     8595.513673728334
it is nonetheless unnecessary to make it a condition that all the measures for controlling demand should be adopted.     1855.3277204081642
the proposal already specifies that water must be abstracted in accordance with the ecological needs in the river basin district concerned.     8727.532390505654
these parts of amendments nos 49 and 87 cannot, therefore, be approved.     9470.897066821333
the commission approves amendment no 76 to the effect that radioactive substances are explicitly to be included in the proposal.     6436.260568792021
to summarise, the commission is able to approve 72 amendments, either in their entirety, partially or in principle.     18609.93679941708
the following amendments cannot be approved: 1, 4, 9, 11, 13, 15, 19, 23, 39, 40, 51, 59, 61, 64, 66, 70, 71, 71, 72, 74, 77, 79, 81, 83, 87, 89, 90, 91, 95, 97, 98, 100, 101, 103, 106 and 107.

important progress has been made, and the constructive tone of the discussions creates a good working climate for future discussions.     3767.1486665189605

financial instrument for the environment (life)
the next item is the recommendation for second reading (a4-0014/2000), on behalf of the committee on the environment, public health and consumer policy on the common position adopted by the council with a view to adopting a european parliament and council regulation on a financial instrument for the environment (life) (rapporteur: mrs lienemann).     10185.079526891257
rapporteur.     26883.579555531494
(fr) mr president, we have already had the opportunity, at the first reading, to examine this report and i was given to believe in committee that there was a broad consensus within this house.     4092.8652270503585
life is the only budget line for direct assistance for the environment in the community budget, and one cannot help but note that it does not live up to our ambitions since the amounts allocated to it have been stagnating for years.     1434.456786045679
despite the enlargement which has already taken place, when we renewed the first life programme to move on to the second, despite enlargement, the level of appropriations remained the same.     3196.0103182592393
and then we see that, increasingly, the regions, the various operators, businesses, associations, are submitting life projects, both for life-nature and life-environment, projects that are of excellent quality, which following technical assessment by all the competent committees are unanimously found to be worthy of european union support, but we still cannot fund them, for lack of budget appropriations.     3192.8987416838354
it must also be mentioned that this budget experiences a rate of consumption of appropriations which is quite exceptional within the community budget.     2533.0714325069944
patently we have here an instrument that is appropriate for the policies which we wish to implement.     2690.5522772632817
i would stress the life-nature strand in particular.     12277.078421766046
we are well aware that, in the european union, a whole raft of directives such as the directive on habitats and the directive on migratory birds encounter problems in implementation.     6245.748551102497
with life-nature we should be able to support policies which demonstrate the feasibility, the validity of the objectives of these directives, and their capacity to be implemented.     7681.609794537479
and, for want of appropriations, we lose this legitimacy and allow the idea that these european directives of ours are not reasonable and are not applicable to develop.     3980.2210302046606
and so the debates taking place today focus on two major issues: firstly, comitology, an ongoing debate within this house, but parliament wished to implement policies settled rather by management committees or advisory committees which give the commission some flexibility and which do not give the council too much scope to obstruct and have their intergovernmental philosophy prevail over the community philosophy which we are here to stand up for within the european parliament.     4923.347687512877
but we have these comitology debates with regard to most of the european union' s financial programmes and instruments.     6890.957534638209
i would say that in my opinion - and the experiences we have had in the past tend to show this - that reconciling opinions on comitology is rather easier than reconciling opinions on the budget.     3425.429926468116
let me point out that parliament is asking for 850 million ecus in appropriations, not a vast sum in the community budget, and that the current proposals are for 613 million ecus.     4923.228871937959
i feel, at any rate, that for the ultimate application of life, at least, there would be some way for the union to make a significant budgetary gesture in favour of the environment.     3168.317247139256
let me conclude with the matter of our institutions' way of working.     11740.80460548701
this is the essence of how i understand the situation: we are being told that it is essential for us to reach a conclusion quickly, that, basically, the members of parliament should drop their claims, particularly claims on the budget, for if we do not soon, in line with the codecision procedure, get around to establishing the framework of the regulations for life, then its implementation for the year 2000 is going to suffer a delay, and in the meantime the ngos and the operators at grass roots level are waiting for our appropriations.     3503.856785779435
i would, however, like to point out that the council has plenty of time to formulate its ideas and that it is submitting its proposals to us only a few months before the fateful date.     4201.979022395139
as for us, we our bound by the terms of the texts: a maximum of four months.     4133.599892183803
we meet our deadlines and afterwards we are told that we should hold on and that in order to be reasonable we must go along with the position of the others, because in the codecision procedure there is just not enough time.     3152.289592468262
i think parliament often protests about this method, not to point the finger at any specific party within the council, and i certainly would not want the portuguese presidency to feel responsible for this state of affairs, since, the truth is, it is something of a tradition, which the council has kept going for a long time.     3140.610101615119
but i should like to stress the fact that, on this issue of the environment, the budget issue is a central one and parliament cannot yield to the blackmail which requires it to be the only reasonable party in the deal.     4112.0315604883635
i therefore hope, firstly, that our fellow members will support us in the conciliation - but votes on this area are always very much in agreement - but also that the council and the commission will manage to listen to us and that we shall each take a step towards the other.     1873.5987625733417
mr president, it is true that we are in open disagreement on some of the choices made by the council and the commission, and i will refer to this later on.     3019.3375607453886
it is equally true, however, that we should begin by saying that this regulation is better than those covering life i and life ii.     2458.1859504407116
it is better firstly because the framework proposal made life i more rigorous, more transparent and more rational.     1453.0569788374316
secondly, it is better because the common position includes some fundamental recommendations, which were advocated by the european parliament at first reading.     2742.8715107572034
specifically, these are: to promote employment as a factor to be taken into consideration when selecting candidate projects, and to reduce the environmental impact of products at every stage from production to recycling and elimination.     3702.4949863800966
this is one of the aims that life-environment must achieve.     2678.1003406726504
thirdly, the fact that the common position has for the first time introduced upgrading and planning for coastal areas as one of life-environment' s priorities is an extremely positive step.     3627.3515550543298
in spite of all its virtues though, life iii' s regulation fails in what is obviously the most important aspect of determining a financial instrument' s impact, which is its budget.     5195.139857156049
by persisting with their proposal of eur 613 million as a reference amount for 2000 - 2004, and by refusing to accept the european parliament' s proposal of eur 850 million, the commission and the council are taking a decision which we do not feel is based on the same criteria of rationality and justice that they introduced, and rightly so, for other measures in life iii.     3043.5151837861927
we want the commission and the council to know that we will not give up trying to provide life with the budget it deserves and which best guarantees the environmental results that we are seeking to achieve with this kind of instrument.     2748.504250411704
we have four very straightforward arguments which we feel justify our choice.     14174.676161210366
firstly, life is the only direct financial instrument which is designed to promote environmental policy within the european union.     2775.0412731772335
there is no other such policy.     1583.8201656325764
secondly, life is an instrument that has already produced good results, and has enabled innovative methods and techniques to be developed and has an excellent rate of implementation.     4833.07340468546
thirdly, the life budget has been reduced in real terms has not kept up with the enormous dynamism and creativity of demand.     5929.304117802603
fourthly, as parliament ascribes so much value to life, it has allocated ever-greater appropriations to this project in its annual budget.     3559.3489270071145
this means that approving the proposal put forward by the council and by the commission would represent an unacceptable reversal of this trend.     4302.502409108057
our concern does not centre purely on the budgetary issue, however.     4544.893174211265
climate change and water policy are subjects that are of great concern to the european public and which have deserved considerable attention from this house.     2337.6026222508544
we therefore feel it is crucial that as amendments nos 5 and 6 seek to establish as objectives which life-environment should achieve a plan for the sustainable management of ground water and surface water, as well as for reducing greenhouse gases, they should be adopted.     5028.6123491114795
lastly, i would like to congratulate mrs lienemann on her report and to take the opportunity to say to her that we will be voting in favour of her amendments on comitology even though, as was made clear in the committee on the environment, we would prefer life to have been monitored by an advisory committee, we do want to contribute to allowing the solution proposed by the council to be accepted as quite frankly, it was worse.     4109.692633401463
mr president, ladies and gentlemen, life is the european union' s most important instrument and indeed its only financial instrument dedicated exclusively to the environment.     15864.736299999136
that explains why this instrument must live up to our ambitions, ambitions for which, of course, there is a price.     3458.366952660566
obviously, the basic item for discussion with the council will be the allocation for life iii.     5106.768171995072
the common position stipulates a total appropriation of eur 613 million for the period 2000-2004. this is not enough.     2995.6053230390976
the group of the european liberal, democrat and reform party is in favour of increasing this amount to eur 850 million.     11141.074672461984
we therefore support mrs lienemann' s amendment which observes that the allocation for life was not increased when the union was last enlarged, without even mentioning the next batch of new arrivals.     4887.994972445999
the amount that we are asking for is no great mountain of useless expenditure.     5603.458786279215
quite the opposite, it is still fairly modest in relation to the requirements to be funded.     9420.396531834793
a drop in the ocean, i might say, in comparison with europe' s structural policies.     41727.69438830251
the environment is still the poor relation in the european budget, even though it is primarily an investment.     2633.197348214145
an investment in natural resources, of course, but also in employment, since it has been demonstrated that an active environmental policy generated new jobs, as shown particularly by the great success of life, a success with ngos, businesses, local authorities and third countries.     2332.170287798402
more than one third of the 8,500 requests received meet the required conditions and it has been possible to cofinance approximately 1,300 projects between 1992 and 1998.
a success which parliament wishes and intends to see perpetuated, and this is why we also support amendment 14 outlining the fourth phase of the life project.     2392.5751160723203
in matters of the environment, investment and ambition come in a variety of forms, and indeed must come in a variety of forms in the long term.     6558.975570737144
this is the price of the future of generations to come.     2660.570588421262
mr president, commissioner, i am probably going to repeat things my fellow members have said but i believe that, as far as the council is concerned, the thing is to repeat over and over what we think of life.     2087.64784738266
i shall therefore reiterate that, as the rapporteur has already said quite clearly, life is the only specific financial instrument for the implementation of the european union' s policy on the environment.     2870.6460990950886
this instrument is absolutely essential since it provides the impetus for truly innovative actions in favour of nature and the environment in europe, which are adopted and expanded upon by the member states.     3521.3631249662644
this financial instrument is, furthermore, deliberately democratic since it is accessible to both governmental and non-governmental organisations.     4971.406914173891
that is why, ladies and gentlemen, i would urge you not to vote in favour of the amendments which have no appreciation of the spirit of life.     9600.736713457374
through the associations, then, life is a facility that can be used by the european citizen directly, and for the benefit of actions in the interest of the community.     2112.811143818971
several million ngo members - millions, i say - recognise in life the union' s determination to build up an ambitious policy in favour of our natural heritage.     22111.995029176727
life is, moreover, the fundamental instrument of the natura 2000 network in europe into which the member states have put a lot, pursuant to community directives on the habitats of rare and endangered fauna and flora and on migratory birds, and which makes it possible to set up the protection of sites of high biological value in our states.     6038.874683220245
i should like to say that those who run down this policy in their own states bear the responsibility for obstructing european nature conservation policies.     3452.6823176351922
this is a pity because everyone within this chamber knows that life is a financial instrument which is not sufficient to carry out the projects submitted by member states, a good many of which are turned down due to lack of funds.     5123.084108494483
let us not forget that, to date, life amounts to only eur 100 million per annum, i.e.     4542.90613641844
scarcely 0.1% of the budget of the european union.     1612.452319758872
for the record, the budget for agriculture, which has a real impact on the environment, amounts, as we all know, to eur 37 billion.     2284.7353278662763
against this background, my group thinks it essential to increase the allocation to life and unreservedly supports mrs lienemann' s proposal to increase the budget to eur 850 million, since we know, and i think it bears saying, how high the expectations of our fellow citizens on environmental matters are and how excessively cautious and unacceptable the council' s compromise regarding the budget for life is, especially as the life programmes show exceptional implementation rates and have demonstrated the extent to which they made it possible to bring about new environmental practice.     6949.0125020429805
the first sustainable development projects are unquestionably those.     3091.356803338281
mr president, the european union environmental programme, to which a considerable amount (eur 613 million) has been allocated, gives rise to many questions regarding the proper use of public funds.     2958.5578023419657
a financial instrument of this order ought to be transparent and effective, and this is not the case for the following reasons: poorly defined selection criteria, dubious uncoordinated operations, assessments not made public and the lack of any assessment policy.     5949.785042275325
we are therefore proposing to amend the regulations as follows.     6403.583629869945
firstly, to reserve the acquisitions made thanks to life funds exclusively to public bodies, subsequently, following invitation to tender, to entrust the management of these sites to authorised nature conservation associations.     6886.185025651709
it is a question of the continuity of the ownership of the acquisitions, since private law associations may disappear or resell acquisitions.     7674.307504230764
it is shocking in philosophical terms and dubious in legal terms for 100% public money to be used to build up a heritage of private land ownership, even under cover of environmental protection.     5215.7512054932395
secondly, to define the criteria for the eligibility and allocation of life funds, in order to rule our any suspicions of clientelism or ostracism.     8359.105501164102
thirdly, to make prior coordination with the users and communities concerned a prerequisite.     9101.847620155135
for example, the life-nature project in france, at grand-lieu lake, planned without any coordination, led to a real ecological disaster, the de-silting operation which was carried out without regard for french law on the subject caused significant silting of the acheneau, a water course located downstream, destroying spawning beds, filling wetlands, and who knows what else.     6295.465270395768
the silt was in fact shifted 3 kilometres, at a cost of ff 6 million, the cure being worse than the disease, and a consequence that was foreseeable and actually predicted by the parties involved with the site.     4735.347772872906
result: a court action and repair costs greater than the cost of the programme itself.     5475.236612114073
this is the sort of thing that we want to avoid.     3602.295340112219
four, to include representatives of parliament in the monitoring committee, at least one member per political group.     2354.6452765445956
what could be fairer than for members of parliament to monitor the use of the appropriations they voted for?     3868.46678803576
fifthly, to publish an annual scientific, technical and financial assessment of life operations carried out.     9322.489008878514
six, to carry out proper evaluation of the programmes on a regular basis.     5654.937561741869
seven, to make it possible for these funds to be used in the event of an environmental emergency: for example, in reforestation following storm damage, or restoration of the natural environment following an oil slick, etc.     3631.9166856986258
here, mrs lienemann, mr president, we are putting forward here a practical proposal, a common sense proposal, easy to implement, likely to limit local conflicts and to vindicate the proper use of public monies, before increasing the life budget line by as much as 40%!     4757.239732211759
mr president, commissioner, i would firstly like to congratulate mrs lienemann on the report she has carried out, and also the commission - why not?     2279.7196233935656
- because this report, this position, generally improves on the texts in force.     4729.048752704156
furthermore, it contains more acceptable systematics and is clearer and more transparent.     1458.3378780244016
i would also like to congratulate the services of the commission, since the evaluation, control and monitoring of the life programme provide a reasonable guarantee with regard to the selection and execution of projects.     4065.389102335232
however, i would like to insist on certain points.     13976.474211950685
firstly, its continuity, which, in principle, i am not questioning.     8701.69975616815
secondly, the financial contributions.     5038.305679498378
sadly, only 7.2% of the projects requested receive funds.     4654.8659809099245
of the eur 1 919 million requested, only eur 784 million have been granted.     2558.0592125846943
now the financial statement shows only eur 613 million to cover more countries and competences.     3035.111000517703
parliament has proposed eur 850 million, but the document repeats the initial proposal of eur 613 million.     4127.745683352571
commissioner, our environmental commitments are increasing in all areas, while the only financial instrument exclusively dedicated to the environment is decreasing.     2680.9490989667574
is this not a contradiction?     1994.7772771695998
let us not demand from others what we are not prepared to give ourselves?     4081.2401924322626
why do we all go on about the environment and are then not prepared to pay for it amongst all of us, but rather expect the usual people, the ones who live there all year, to pay for it and maintain it?     1489.0225425083315
commissioner, we must be consistent.     2066.007948416041
the cost of maintaining the environment, which we all enjoy, must be met by all of us, and the life programme is the only financial instrument aimed exclusively at the environment and whose projects have generally been effective.     1931.0748865290643
mr president, life has been the principal instrument available to the community since 1992 for supporting and developing environmental policy, both within the community and in the surrounding third countries.     1199.9881188817044
on the basis of the experience gained over the past eight years, an attempt is being made to increase the effectiveness of action on behalf of the environment in the third development phase of that financial instrument.     6327.342914906566
the funding of activities for the implementation, adaptation and development of community policy in the environment sector and the incorporation of the environment in other policies, particularly ones that contribute towards sustainable development, are central elements of the regulation we are to vote upon.     3232.4527959265943
an important element of life-nature, which mainly concerns the realisation of the natura 2000 network, is the introduction of a multinational approach to formulating plans for international projects and activities, so as to avoid the fragmentation of biotopes and to offer better protection of biodiversity.     6590.103364644837
in the second, life-environment strand, which is more concerned with projects involving small and medium-sized enterprises and local government agencies, the incorporation of the environment in other policies is expressed very clearly indeed.     3439.828729763326
as regards the financing package for the implementation of life over the next five years, i wholeheartedly support the increase from eur 613 million to eur 850 million proposed by the rapporteur, mrs lienemann.     5834.153672410056
unless the necessary means are secured to pursue environmental protection, there is no point on the one hand in continuing to raise the environmental awareness achieved in recent years, and on the other hand in incorporating the environmental dimension in other policies.     2725.309275468105
besides, we cannot remain indifferent towards the increased challenges in areas around the union or towards addressing very acute environmental protection problems in the applicant countries.     11693.548117559472
what has happened over the past few days with the pollution of the danube and its tributary has been instructive.     5652.258264906288
consequently, i consider that support for environmental action under the life financial instrument will help to further community policy in the environmental sector and can contribute substantially to fulfilling the community' s mission of achieving a high level of environmental protection and improvement.     4111.345751346838
mr president, i should like to begin by congratulating mrs lienemann on her sensitive approach towards the environment, ably demonstrated in the two reports we have discussed this evening.     12870.791874737728
two minutes is certainly not long enough to deal with such an important topic.     3117.902114686548
so i will simply appeal to the commissioner to undertake full responsibility for such an important issue.     2820.213854321191
