for five years, i have been the representative of the committee on the environment in a regional organisation, which assuredly does not have parliament' s broad perspective.     5077.7900644152305
the environment is in a parlous state.     650.2094764440259
like a number of previous speakers, i, too, wish to consider the economic problem and the problem of finding funding for projects, which, in my view, should be incorporated into the pubic domain.     5536.90653662869
as regards funding, eur 613 million is not enough.     4166.585767184065
even eur 850 million is not enough.     1384.2059673451593
at the end of august 1999, i took part in a meeting of the committee of the environment in which it was said that eur 2 million, or itl 4 000 billion, should go towards eliminating or, at any rate, reducing smoking.     4044.1076260703917
however, the environment constitutes a far bigger and more serious problem than smoking.     4404.416841615675
eur 850 million - assuming that such a figure is actually agreed - believe me, is not enough, in practical terms, to allow any type of project to be carried out.     4393.145981425501
in many areas the parks which are a fundamental element in the life programme have been established.     2547.2988860015203
but creating parks with very limited financial resources, without any system of signs is inefficient and ineffectual.     6445.531853937996
i would therefore ask parliament and the commission to give the matter their full attention.     2338.35238534099
mr president, ladies and gentlemen, ever since the beginning of 1999, discussions have taken place between the institutions on the subject of the proposed regulation for a third stage for the instrument for the environment known as life.     4576.771952190217
i would again thank the committee on the environment, public health and consumer policy and mrs lienemann for the good and constructive co-operation we have had on this matter.     4151.241815884373
as a result of this, i can confirm that the commission wholly or in principle approves nine of the fourteen amendments which have been adopted by the committee on the environment, public health and consumer policy.     2897.316888026738
the commission welcomes, above all, amendments nos 1, 2, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 and 13, which apply to the committee procedures.     8236.634061475188
in these cases, parliament has fully taken account of the objections which the commission had to the common position.     2100.1511288856186
the commission can, in principle, also approve amendment no 4 which ought, however, to be introduced into another place in the text.     3478.9027822195735
with regard to amendments nos 5 and 6, i want to emphasise that projects for the sustainable exploitation of groundwater and surface water, together with projects for reducing forms of air pollution which contribute to the greenhouse effect, are to a large degree covered by life.     8088.126034142172
the commission nonetheless considers that, if this is stated separately, then that is to go against the original decision to have the proposal focus on just a few priority areas.     2124.5723736710006
there is also a risk of overlap with other community programmes such as the fifth framework programme for research, technical development and demonstration.     2606.5540978660983
the present programme' s key measures - water quality, tomorrow' s city and innovative products and processes - to a certain degree cover the same questions.     12559.682302884257
where the question of the budget is concerned, i am pleased about the confidence shown by the committee on the environment, public health and consumer policy.     3053.1049815297374
amendment no 12 requires a larger budget than that stated in the common position.     3735.454943015227
i am also convinced that life has great potential.     4517.782710084758
this instrument could finance good projects with a higher amount of money than that quoted in the proposed budget.     5091.190704991252
the amount quoted in amendment no 12 does not, however, tally with the budget set on the basis of agenda 2000.
allow me also to add that, if the budget is increased, greater demands are obviously going to be made upon the directorate-general for the environment if an increased number of projects are going to be dealt with.     3225.7812743985614
account will also have to be taken of other programmes where decisions are to be made within the framework of the joint decision-making procedure.     1228.6976106924465
at this stage, the commission is keeping to the amount which has been set in the common position.     1831.6666014696036
with regard to amendment no 3, which we did not approve at first reading, i want to emphasise that each programme has its own objectives, conditions under which it may be applied for and costs which make it eligible for aid.     4153.456707348228
it is therefore meaningless, and often impossible, directly to transfer projects from life to other instruments.     4269.153626172015
if other sources of finance were to be investigated for each project, more resources would, moreover, be required than are available in the present situation.     2499.9962188576797
now that the commission has to consider carefully what resources are required for each activity, this is an example of the kind of activity we ought to avoid: one that is inessential but demanding in terms of resources.     2113.254143345193
nor can the commission approve amendment no 14, which is contrary to the commission' s right of initiative.     12560.973428339097
with regard to the discussion concerning the deadline, i want to say that the final date for beginning to implement life - 31 march 2000, as set in the common position - must now be moved forward.     5124.758410181254
the date will be fixed as soon as the present regulation has been adopted.     2507.2129735474327
finally, i want to repeat that i am convinced that life will be an effective instrument in support of development and the implementation of the community' s environmental policy.     7041.679885090638
parliament has contributed greatly to improving this instrument.     7030.116645710711
i am convinced that you share my wish that it should be possible to adopt the regulation as soon as possible, especially in view of the fact that there are great expectations in the member states and in other countries, especially among the applicant states which are now being given the opportunity to participate in life.     3919.203718602028
thank you, mrs wallström.     71419.43050994186
the debate is closed.     4906.850855896859
the vote will take place tomorrow at 11.30 a.m.
modernising social protection
the next item is the report (a5-0033/2000), by mr andersson on behalf of the committee on employment and social affairs, on the communication from the commission on a concerted strategy for modernising social protection [com(1999) 347 - c5-0253/1999 - 1999/2182(cos)].     24567.69676717232
mr president, the issue of social security systems is an important one.     1410.8639624624182
this is a natural consequence of cooperation within the eu.     3170.810730767399
the basis is monetary cooperation, which has since been followed up by macro-economic cooperation and cooperation on employment policy.     2489.5973519280524
it is now a natural consequence to move on to social security systems.     2303.406767258723
these areas are mutually dependent upon one another.     7720.920262543006
economic stability is a reason for growth and can provide high employment.     2099.4815489180223
it also provides the opportunity to develop welfare provision.     6423.040664366338
but the reverse is also the case: a well-developed policy on social security can contribute to high employment and to increased growth.     1804.1166591860626
in other words, social security is a factor of production.     1838.382866477486
social security systems differ from one eu country to another.     1689.2415360809266
this is largely due to historical factors.     4305.93851407701
we are now faced with a number of common challenges, however; demographic development, for example, which has to do not only with the increase in the number of elderly people but also with the lower birth rate.     4115.023877265569
the labour market is also changing.     2714.2214534553777
women are venturing into the labour market in ever greater numbers.     8663.37012866283
another challenge is presented by technical development.     5315.373144590677
we must meet these challenges together.     7974.914466912664
the commission' s communication recommends that experience should be more widely shared, a high-level group formed and benchmarking introduced.     18268.432558417913
what is more, the report on social security should be published annually and examined in conjunction with the report on employment.     3896.9923825162587
this is good, but it is not enough.     848.2369779340298
we propose a procedure like the luxembourg process.     6422.4094186509565
clear guidelines and indicators are required.     3092.24614043288
at the same time, the member states are to formulate the relevant methods in national action plans.     3335.14516426212
we are creating a common model, but retaining the principle of subsidiarity.     3219.194549244369
this social convergence is a process which will continue for a long period.     2221.9925304029484
parliament must therefore be involved in the work - not by being part of the high-level group, but by enabling parliamentary representatives to track the work of the latter and come up with proposals as to how the process is to be carried on.     1995.4448614733353
like the work on employment, the commission' s proposal is founded upon four pillars.     24871.28916946213
these are: to make the systems employment-friendly; to make the pensions systems sustainable in the long term; to promote social integration; and to guarantee sustainable, high-quality health and medical care.     2169.128872319209
all this is important.     1143.824527609927
in our report, we have highlighted the importance of combating poverty within the eu and of finding indicators as soon as possible so that we can make progress in this area.     3116.903376832511
equality has no pillar of its own.     3539.7693211775027
there, it is mainstreaming which applies.     11234.682786516067
all four pillars should reflect aspects of equality.     14446.722952205868
this is something we have borne in mind.     5076.686645290876
we are submitting a wide range of proposals to the effect, for example, that there should be a transition to individualised social security systems; that parental leave should involve entitlement to benefits under the social security systems; that there should be better opportunities to combine family and working life; and that the basic old-age pension should guarantee a decent standard of living.     3025.248658666219
these proposals favour women especially.     14468.770991901205
parallel to this european strategy of social convergence, a concrete plan for legislative work in the social sphere is required, together with proposals for framework agreements in the context of the dialogue between employers and employees, accompanied by a concrete timetable.     4019.2177914146173
we expect the commission to present this as part of its proposal for a social action programme.     1946.86053256625
social convergence is also needed before the enlargement of the union, partly in order to strengthen the social model and partly to combat the risk of social dumping.     3894.4156609690394
the commission ought to consider devising a special action programme concerning social policy in connection with enlargement.     6302.226868126957
we have had a constructive discussion in the committee.     1278.5018090356923
we had a lot of amendments, but succeeded in agreeing upon a report.     7092.535089944805
now, the amendments which are to be voted on at the plenary sitting have been reduced in number to only fifteen.     2609.3943459524885
this indicates that we have a broad consensus.     4764.21389713937
a number of these amendments have come back from the committee reading, and these i shall reject.     3773.6488901006205
i shall also reject those amendments which have been tabled by the technical group of independent members, because i do not think that these add anything to the report.     1684.7842676284556
on the other hand, my group will applaud amendment no 14 from the group of the european liberal, democrat and reform party.     10145.136487136457
i think this proposal is a good one.     1617.3913724141023
certainly, it resembles amendment no 1, but amendment no 14 is better.     4477.3614245515555
finally, i want to say that the eu is not only concerned with cooperation on trade.     1848.7307010579243
the eu is also a union for employment and social justice.     858.3654567108293
in creating a citizens' europe, social policy has an important role to play.     2523.4627245424713
by means of this report, parliament wishes to make its contribution to reinforcing the social dimension of european society.     5555.138101850155
we hope that the council and the commission will take this contribution seriously and include it in future social policy.     1567.0833063555401
the lisbon summit will provide an excellent opportunity to present a strategy of this kind.     4158.645577627745
mr president, i would like to congratulate the rapporteur, mr andersson, very warmly on his report on modernising social protection which is now under discussion.     5000.303013183603
in the capacity of coordinator of the european people' s party in the committee on employment and social affairs, i am very pleased with the compromise which was reached and which mr andersson put so much effort into.     5556.644894059022
i am also delighted that he based the report on a report which was written by myself as rapporteur under the previous parliament and has partly followed the gist of this old report.     2465.338526187617
the goal of the commission' s notification of june 1999, on which the andersson report is based, is to reinforce cooperation between the member states and the eu in terms of social protection.     8605.902942806986
key sub-goals are: promoting employment and ensuring that work secures a fixed income; secondly, safeguarding old-age pensions and making these affordable; thirdly, promoting social integration and fourthly, guaranteeing affordable, first-rate health protection.     6802.418431434117
gender-related aspects must feature prominently in all these goals.     15230.25276803451
i believe that congratulations are also in order for the commission with regard to this notification, for these four points are, in fact, to a very large number of eu citizens extremely important - if not the most important points which merit further thought.     3243.8362333421765
the commission' s communication is an important strategic document for bringing about european social convergence.     11414.900461951143
macro-economic policy, employment policy and social policy all affect each other and this is why they should be brought more closely together.     2024.9267673226848
the commission has recognised this interrelatedness.     1312.7352492076543
it is our intention to bring about an integrated social strategy which is, of course, underpinned by a sound macro-economic strategy and a sound employment strategy.     4791.659830357302
these aspects are very much interconnected.     2372.448530195305
it is, therefore, surprising that this topic has, to date, not been incorporated in a strategy and this is why the commission' s notification constitutes a break-through in a way.     7027.780033541722
it brings me great joy to witness that the council now seems to accept this break-through and also wants to bring it to the fore in lisbon.     2881.177359428107
we should, of course, not get overly excited about it.     3253.743402825683
it marks the beginning, rather than the end, of a process.     7324.3927706944705
some people have wondered whether our demands are too low or whether there should be more dialogue.     4961.723273627834
but it is extremely significant for the process to be set in motion.     1552.89620172145
the issues involved are extremely important but the systems in the european union are quite different from one another.     1296.7677571495649
the challenges are different.     7002.421830340896
the levels of employment are different and this is why it is very important to set the ball rolling gently but to make steady progress nonetheless.     3420.2732573781773
in this way, we will be able to meet a number of huge challenges.     15770.822772173626
the majority within my group is of the opinion that something like this needs to happen.     3612.139401366296
the funny thing about the andersson report is that it does not get bogged down with too many details of how everything should take shape; from this perspective it is not a pie-in-the-sky report.     6863.701649175573
it is a report which tries to give direction.     4767.38106633166
there are, however, one or two aspects about which i have doubts as to whether or not we are being too specific.     1335.4659582074307
i think that a number of meps will make reference to this.     6162.941182199321
but that does not take away from the fact that the report' s importance mainly lies in the fact that we are developing a strategy and that there is a reasonable chance that it can make a significant contribution in lisbon.     4912.38906180684
i believe that if it were to be adopted in lisbon in this way, lisbon will turn out to be a success.     2907.39454806268
if it is not adopted, lisbon will not add much to what has already been said in the past.     1227.8555739020248
as far as this is concerned, the report, albeit a component, may well be the key component of lisbon, because it is really innovative, while many other things are merely a rumination of what has been said before.     2104.1579560162636
all in all, i am indebted to the rapporteur and the committee for this report.     2927.5973549463865
i hope that europe, in terms of social protection, will make a step forward, taking into account subsidiarity, for without subsidiarity, this would not be possible.     3200.406668461615
commissioner, ladies and gentlemen, like my fellow mep, bartho pronk, please allow me to thank the commission for this communication and, in particular, to congratulate our rapporteur for the work he has put into this report.     5602.186699230084
commissioner, the fact that this report is no longer controversial within parliament does not mean that it has lost any of its crucial importance.     3084.1720486112363
there have been times within this parliament when such discussions on social security were actually quite awkward.     2674.1118545371814
it is also a timely discussion - as mr pronk said just a moment ago - at a time when preparations are in full swing for the lisbon summit, which intends to find out how we can put economic growth, employment and social cohesion on the agenda in europe via the information society.     4153.018778728863
within my group, it speaks for itself that social protection forms an important part of this social cohesion and should thus also be added to the agenda in lisbon.     3140.539690258533
my group is pleased that the social affairs council gave the go-ahead for a better cooperation strategy as early on as the end of last year and that, in fact, a group of high-ranking officials has already been commissioned to submit to the council an initial report by june 2000.
however, we, the parliament and, in particular, the group of the social democrats, want lisbon to go one step further now.     3223.8770378183694
it is of great importance that, in our fellow mep' s report, this european parliament advocates a true european strategy of social convergence, based on what provisions have been made for employment via the so-called luxembourg strategy, that is to say it should encompass common goals, guidelines, national action plans and performance evaluations.     2674.211334945271
we believe that this course of action is ambitious, yet achievable.     6460.931386088255
needless to say, it is clear that harmonisation of social security is not viable at european level.     6929.653995702529
the financial and organisational systems vary too greatly for this, and this is also an important reason why social protection has only just been put on the european agenda, and why, in fact, scenarios such as danny peters' thirteenth member state or the european social snake have scarcely got off the ground.     1848.8476923839494
but this new course of action is very exciting.     1975.0529537793936
the employment strategy has proved that it can function well and social convergence is also necessary, for our social security systems are facing the same challenges, and in an emu era, social security systems would otherwise threaten to fall prey to social competition.     3437.446959501673
but let me make one thing clear on behalf of my group: social convergence strategies should not be an alternative to social legislation.     5264.045993566589
they can merely serve as an extremely useful complement to what we are developing and expecting from the commission in terms of social legislation.     3607.3567560885717
i think that it also a good thing if, moreover, the european parliament tried to convince the council to substantiate the common strategy as early on as in lisbon and to launch an annual procedure to reduce poverty levels no later than at the beginning of 2001.
i am pleased about the fact that the european parliament supports my group' s idea and we hope, commissioner, that we can also count on your support.     3209.3438396478314
finally, we urge the european parliament, both sides of industry and social ngos to be closely involved in this process.     2231.88521402428
the success of this strategy hinges on a broad and democratic base and we hope that we can count on your support.     6336.644240133727
mr president, the four chief objectives which the eu countries' governments endorsed in november of last year and which were mentioned earlier here today were as follows: that care should be taken to ensure that it is worthwhile working and guaranteeing oneself a secure income; that pensions should be guaranteed and pension arrangements made sustainable; that social integration should be promoted; and that sustainable, high-quality health care should be guaranteed.     3731.7160883929837
these are four goals which have been worded in such broad terms that everyone must be able to support them.     5683.565407901192
having a group of high-ranking officials as the focal point for the pooling of experience and for the coordination and evaluation of the ways in which social and labour market policies are being developed is also something we can all benefit from.     3586.3409415835263
the commission' s communication, which we are discussing today and which was put by for a decision by the council in november, is a well-balanced report on how we can obtain a more professional debate and more knowledge of the challenges faced by the member states in the field of social policy over the next few years when we have ageing populations and are experiencing the effects of the enlargement of the union.     3461.1672125609093
we now already have major social problems and major social costs to find from the public budgets, which is a problem for many member states.     1371.1154516754393
if reforms are not got under way, we are in danger of eroding the economic basis of social policy, and a strong and competitive economy is now undoubtedly the most secure basis for effective social security.     3179.1838448772314
it is therefore only reasonable to put these subjects on the agenda.     2878.0165784091387
in the debate about coordination of social policy in the eu, we often use the expression social convergence.     2385.972542957234
as liberals, we can support this concept when it means that we in the eu are to establish broad common objectives with which the individual countries then seek to comply by means of their own national social policies.     3254.7824473526207
it is not, therefore, about harmonisation but about common objectives.     1476.022131451622
it is, of course, a fact that the eu countries have different ways of organising social policy.     2382.718940383423
we have different traditions and there are differences in culture and, in spite of there being many points of similarity, there are also major differences between one country and another in the nature and extent of the social problems concerned.     2367.364434696728
it is therefore important to emphasise that social policy is a national concern.     1322.8007810754068
national policy is, of course, subject to a number of common parameters.     5155.2707062954
that is the case with minimum social entitlements, which are established by treaty.     4493.962047108302
it is true in regard to guaranteeing the free movement of labour without loss of social entitlements.     17937.59729620133
it applies to the fact of our having to coordinate our economies; and it applies in the case of economic cooperation.     4418.856374614225
so coordination and cooperation are facts of life.     2173.6762245707646
but convergence should be about goals, not means.     3348.4396112245136
i should also like to thank the rapporteur, jan andersson, for his report and for his very constructive contribution to the attempt to reconcile different positions and to secure support for his report.     6440.963595738662
i can support the report, but there are a few points which are not completely to my taste.     1967.4762141770861
i do not agree with the requirement that the commission should intervene in regard to what is referred to as unfair competition between social and tax systems, if interference of this kind is to be used to stem the tide of new thinking and to restrict development and the process of making our social systems more efficient, or else to prevent a reduction in the unduly high tax burden in many eu countries.     3409.074288052287
i am sceptical about the detailed regulations that might result from the commission' s having to deal with the scope and quality of child care and care of the elderly, and i am sceptical about the value of a common poverty threshold.     8972.298965497688
dialogue and the coordination of social policy are to be commended, but we should not lump all nations together as if they were the same.     1416.3661341215861
there should be room for a multiplicity of solutions.     15690.700730609506
mr president, commissioner, with regard to the strategy which has been set out here on modernising social security, i would like to point out some positive things as well as raise some questions that have come up for me.     2975.7654521598797
i feel positive about the movement which is very gradually taking place in the dossier.     4233.969110665161
this seems to be a good thing and useful.     1540.5176561433796
i also feel positive about the essence of the communication, namely the strategic approach.     6697.238208951135
we therefore hope that the four objectives which are mentioned in it can reasonably be achieved.     1660.7561523683537
we would also underline the importance of the way in which the strategy is handled.     5555.294489583934
on the one hand, it would be useful to have guidelines and a process styled on luxembourg, but we also think it is important, as is already mentioned by mrs van lancker, that guidelines are drafted to accommodate less typical work scenarios, such as sole traders, etc.     7806.414400652168
we nonetheless foresee two problems for which solutions will need to be found.     5824.155395999572
the first problem is that concerning the downward adjustment of social security, which, we hope, will be replaced by high-level convergence.     5294.525756769797
i can illustrate this with examples from a study which was carried out recently by the dutch trade union movement into social security reforms within the european union.     5203.057776442848
it appears from this study that adjustments are rife but most of these adjustments, in fact, the majority of them, appear to be downward adjustments, the remainder being improvements.     14754.570888177748
the lion' s share of these adjustments pertains to pension plans: eleven restrictions and two improvements.     38467.074879922366
in the case of unemployment benefits, we came across six restrictions and five improvements.     6948.946266510645
the reasons for adjustments are, for example, to improve employment, reduce social security expenditure, but are also due to policy competition and emu criteria.     3484.4911437276733
policy competition mainly occurs in the northern countries.     6008.585824353885
the highest level of stability and even improvement of social security can be found in the southern countries.     2966.959515475896
all in all, both sides of industry only have a limited role to play in the process as a whole.     1592.4518936779364
this is nothing to write home about and means that the convergence strategy must focus on the best approaches.     6993.648734106417
to use a figure of speech, cappuccino is not always good for us.     1752.6516673271442
mr president, i would, in turn, like to congratulate both the commission, for making another step in the convergence process with regard to social security, and the rapporteur for the sound report he has compiled.     2651.0879295231916
the european union' s policy encompasses social policy in addition to economic and employment policy.     17126.84926614721
so far, social policy has remained limited in terms of set-up and resources.     1683.3119286358703
but under the influence of the free movement of employees, of the internal market and of the euro, the labour markets are actually beginning to become integrated.     5060.457724615802
this has undeniable repercussions on social protection which is tied in with labour to a large extent.     7157.839715347813
cross-border workers, employees on secondment and migrating employees, as well as job-seekers, students on placement and other students, experience hindrances in exercising the right of free movement.     20229.28528925204
this is due to a lack of coordination and a lack of convergence in terms of social protection.     3429.6228124472855
in fact, the same phenomenon occurs in tax law.     9079.139520762816
in order to combat distortion of competition and prevent dumping, the commission has, in my opinion, developed a well-balanced approach which steers a midcourse between harmonisation and non-intervention.     4145.861541717681
on the one hand, binding and enforceable rules apply to equal pay or additional old-age pensions and on the other hand, programmes are used to offer incentives with regard to non-discrimination or social exclusion.     3622.99129436946
based on a number of recommendations, reports and communications, the commission has made another step in the convergence process by setting out objectives and by setting up a working party of high-ranking officials.     6141.043556847233
both proposals have already been accepted by the council and it is thus a matter of endorsing this approach in order to be able to make an assessment in time, with knowledge of all facts and/or in order to establish to what extent and in what way social protection should be reorganised in the european union.     3108.0938598739517
indeed, expansion, globalisation, a sharp increase in the ageing population, individualisation and changes in family situations render such reorganisation inevitable.     32278.8510775021
denying that such shifts take place is tantamount to surrendering social protection.     17794.22932432979
with regard to eurosceptics and nationalists, i would say that social protection within the boundaries of a member state is not social and offers no protection.     6995.683980866271
indeed, if social protection were to be phased out as a result of competition, this would also harm social protection as a production factor.     3628.855669605878
and less social protection means less purchasing power, fewer healthy employees and fewer happy employees.     10026.06724181951
in respect of the super-europeans, i would say that european social protection is neither attainable nor desirable.     3607.6570723907303
the discrepancies between the member states are too immense in terms of provisions, rightful claimants, revenue or benefits, etc.     13495.31455952303
it is thus impossible to bring all states into line.     7901.932004359816
the path which the commission is walking is, in fact, obvious.     7911.886679660044
it has the advantage of clarity, and it can collect data and exchange experiences in order to agree on objectives and finely tune solid, comparative indicators and put forward recommendations.     5848.901034170796
i endorse this view.     17739.074876214883
i will give my support to the commission and i hope that lisbon will take us a step forward in this matter.     2855.9534615223206
mr president, mr andersson' s report contains a lot that is of interest.     11405.775864308089
unfortunately, what is most interesting is that the report, which purports to be concerned with a combined strategy for modernising social policy, instead proposes an extension of traditional social policy and more supranationalism in the social sphere.     3377.086131359425
social policy needs to be renewed in order to reduce unemployment in the member states.     5517.04217151568
even though the socialists, now that there are so many social democratic governments, talk much less about unemployment than they used to, the unemployment in question is still high, and this in spite of the trade boom.     4176.470228386084
something must be done so that unemployment does not turn the so-called european model into a parody.     1919.1962254987739
social policy must facilitate employment, creating incentives for employers and employees to, respectively, provide and accept jobs.     7969.5235850905765
mr andersson' s report demands social convergence, that is to say that systems should be increasingly standardised within and throughout the eu.     4619.210868153215
the eu should apparently adopt what are called real convergence criteria, which would be binding and of real effect.     3582.6054405816312
mr andersson also wants to see efficient and ambitious tax coordination, that is to say supranationalism in the fiscal sphere.     17652.00003813353
the eu should also, on the basis of a uniform definition of poverty thresholds, table recommendations concerning what is called the minimum acceptable subsistence level in the member states.     8744.112695913276
the eu should devise guidelines governing the quality of the job opportunities which are to be created, whatever that may mean.     6372.123273623855
it is certainly not going to be possible to reduce unemployment with all these additional regulations and forms of interference.     3068.420740884856
those structural problems which make unemployment higher in europe than in the united states will become even greater.     2816.427479975018
there is no need at all for supranationalism in this area.     5939.561634487942
the member states can devise their own systems in the social sphere, each within the framework of its economic resources and political preferences.     5627.406856078499
it is most especially important not to create unnecessary problems before the enlargement of the eu.     2961.8845065967976
mr andersson thinks that the less wealthy eastern countries should be reminded that social convergence is to apply to them too.     6584.197708475189
it is obvious, however, that these countries, impoverished within a communist economic system, cannot cope with a social policy like, for example, sweden' s.
talk of minimum regulations and poverty thresholds is meaningless to the present member states and unreasonable as far as less wealthy applicant states are concerned.     5839.1635495669025
mr president, as others have done, i should also like to congratulate mr andersson for the report he has produced.     5205.86132884123
he has done a great deal of work on behalf of parliament in producing a wide-ranging consensus on what is, by any definition, a thorny issue, not just in this parliament but in virtually every member state.     6382.493084656756
i also welcome the commission's communication.     2736.38656531142
it was slow in coming but well worth waiting for.     2830.642046411913
it could be a landmark in the development of a social europe, providing we take it seriously and do not allow individual member states to baulk at the steps that must be taken to implement it.     1512.590684479007
we can, and must, take the unique european social model we have and reform it to fit the twenty-first century, a century which is globalising fast.     3583.373227849129
i am afraid the proposals that are being put forward by mr herman schmid will not deal with that globalising world.     6637.764667725111
to be successful we have to ensure that our economic policy, our employment policy and our social protection policy in its broadest sense, are integrated and that we deal with them as a whole.     2088.465942242907
we have to ensure that our ecofin ministers and our employment ministers acknowledge that social affairs ministers have a contribution to make to the establishment of a society in europe which is creative, has initiative and which will provide a high quality of life for all of our citizens.     2969.478089065146
this is not an option for us any more.     2181.7772066276375
it is an imperative.     16044.943916156117
european citizens will not settle for any less than a quality of life which befits them as human beings.     5513.232805565266
we do not want the us model - a model which has spectacularly failed to create the employment that is necessary to provide a decent quality of life.     2682.277461596962
what it has done is increase dramatically the number of working poor in the united states.     5186.847469921511
that is not what we want to do in europe.     1305.499521971309
we have the traditions of solidarity in europe and we have the experience of a better way of doing things.     2359.450299662094
but we have to grasp the tools to do that.     5779.17255927631
i therefore urge that we not only implement the high-level group as proposed by the commission but also go further and have a luxembourg-type process so that we have an integrated approach to this issue of social protection.     1950.0379233224444
mr president, commissioner, this is one of the social areas to which the european union ought to pay much more attention.     938.8802046839562
macroeconomic policies, the stability pact and the criteria for nominal convergence have put a brake on modernising social protection which would improve the quality of people' s lives, specifically by means of a significant increase in pensions and pension schemes, particularly of the minimum pensions, and through fighting social exclusion.     9818.390122712168
this situation is particularly serious in countries such as portugal where the minimum pensions and pension schemes are barely more than half of the minimum national wage which in turn, is the lowest in the whole of europe.     2863.680051924797
therefore, while the commission does not accept the need to amend its macroeconomic policies to create more jobs of quality, with employee rights, which is a fundamental basis for maintaining and modernising a solid, public social protection system, we harbour serious doubts as to its position.     4835.316195032654
although the andersson report has gathered some positive proposals, including some that we put forward ourselves, we are still critical of some of the bases for the commission' s proposal and we still have concerns about the strategy for modernising social protection.     5253.853740387925
mr president, in my previous life i ran two manufacturing businesses, one in the united kingdom and one in the netherlands.     7083.6790965849395
as the uk business grew, we invested in more people, as the dutch business grew we invested in more machinery.     7971.793676982655
the simple reason for this was that social costs in the netherlands were just too high in comparison.     3394.627664722773
i am in favour of social protection, i am in favour of the original commission document, but i do not want to see people priced out of jobs because social protection costs become unrealistically high.     4422.264002552489
that is why in committee i proposed some form of business impact assessment, so that costs and risks to jobs could be taken into account, and the epp-de group supported this amendment.     2961.280379830732
but i must record that other groups, led by the pse group, guided by my countryman, stephen hughes, voted this out.     4316.993885596631
i accept that mr andersson's report is very well-intentioned, and i respect him for the work he has done, but in my view the way the report defines and promotes social convergence would drive social costs higher throughout the eu.     2780.378164434813
this would put more jobs at risk and also put at risk the credibility of this house by showing how remote we are from the real world.     3389.9016123164947
i therefore urge this house to reject the full report and let the original commission proposal, which was very well-balanced, stand unamended.     2926.1101097677033
mr president, we are unable to vote for this report.     8284.720699051599
the european union should actually place social issues at the heart of the building of europe.     2978.659308492608
social rights should determine economic choices, and not the other way around.     2610.300666066078
this is why, when it comes to social protection, we are opposed to schemes copied from the luxembourg process which run counter to this rationale: broad economic guidelines, then convergence criteria and, finally, social issues, dealt with as the last resort.     5507.735279438433
the european union should proclaim one general principle, to wit, that the social rights acquired in member states may not be restricted.     7535.764928839432
any community intervention should aim to complement and to harmonise existing guarantees according to the highest common denominator.     8276.362297290121
in order to strengthen the funding of social security systems, we need a policy of general wage increase, the establishment of a minimum legal wage in each member state.     5482.3160503280305
in order to fight poverty, entitlement to an income and to employment are inalienable rights.     5115.089639344934
finally, in order to avoid health being treated in any way as a commodity, the european union should declare itself clearly opposed to pension funds and to a situation where private insurance companies have access to the health system.     4010.9386886701054
i sincerely hope, in conclusion, that social europe cannot be satisfied with mere words and pious hopes.     13884.799949642127
for millions of people the situation is too tragic for that.     2115.3347848949893
mr president, commissioner, the aim of developing a coordinated strategy for modernising social protection in the european union is certainly an admirable one.     2448.3362771123425
just as the launching of a european convergence strategy on employment and labour market policies at the luxembourg summit was commendable.     7235.213404295453
the labour market and social protection are, in fact, two sides of the same coin, that require harmonised policies and rules.     3896.313534077317
if the labour market functions by means of certain levels of employment, flexibility and salary discipline, then it is impossible for the welfare state and social protection not to function, and vice versa.     5683.095296407447
until recently, the european countries fixed, it could be said in an autarkic way, the rules governing the functioning and financing of the two markets.     3055.4990507989087
budgetary and exchange rate policies were responsible for maintaining internal and external stability.     2082.2265388143614
with the maastricht treaty and the single currency, budgetary discipline became tighter and recourse to devaluation ceased as a means of endeavouring to restore marginal competitiveness.     12077.607108862228
the structural and economic differences of the various countries cannot, therefore, be rebalanced through budget deficits and trade.     1623.845635504452
that being the case, in the absence of complete labour mobility inside the union, and without a redistributive fiscal policy at federal level, it is left to the labour markets and welfare systems to provide what degree of flexibility there is to remedy both structural and economic disequilibria, the so-called exogenous asymmetric shocks.     4323.590699548521
and therein lies the paradox.     26882.40412979424
political and monetary convergence in europe unavoidably entails convergence of the respective rules of the game in the labour market and welfare systems.     7209.832053119835
but, at the same time, the single currency and the absence of labour mobility, as well as of a redistributive fiscal policy, force us into a situation of non-convergence in terms of salaries and welfare, in order to compensate for the differences in marginal productivity at national and regional levels.     6745.262000210373
hypocrisy and fine-sounding words such as convergence, benchmarking, modernisation and concerted action are not going to extricate us from those contradictions.     10939.00683049273
either the european union becomes a genuine federal state with a genuine redistribution of resources, genuine free movement of workers and therefore - but only at that point - common labour and welfare regimes, or it would be better not to deceive and be deceived by hopeless paths to convergence which sound impressive but which, in reality, only cause harm, above all to the most vulnerable.     3475.0256395844003
that is something we would do well to recognise.     1532.1628136234879
mr president, unlike several of those who have spoken in today' s debate, i am inclined to endorse the andersson report, one of the reasons for this being that i can see clear differences between the report and the commission' s communications.     12957.46843671045
in particular, i am referring to the fact that this report - and i would like to congratulate the rapporteur on this - defends the european social model' s approach, which is one based on solidarity.     5693.450009998507
i would like to highlight the clear positions adopted against social dumping, together with the right to a basic level of social security, the goal of social convergence, and, in particular, the quite specific points that have been made about equal opportunities for women being the primary objectives of this social policy.     2779.010324029969
i can certainly see that it has its shortcomings in failing to undertake a sufficiently rigorous analysis of macroeconomic policy within the european union, and it is this policy which stands in the way of the aforementioned solidarity-based revival of the european social state, model.     4605.32340933036
all in all, though, i deem this report to be worthwhile.     5198.170333724122
mr president, i have taken the floor in order to reaffirm the need for the modernisation of social protection, which is much more then simply one more step in the construction of europe.     3587.6262225980317
this message is contained in the excellent report by mr andersson.     6530.347560555677
europe, the european union, although it is very strong in economic and political terms, will remain shaky, weak and limited, perhaps inadequate, if we are not able to strengthen it from a social point of view.     2173.781269953551
i believe that the latter means the possibility of employment for those who require it, but also decisive action in support of the socially disadvantaged.     3132.4360792364155
bearing in mind that everybody has the right to social protection and a minimum guaranteed social income, i believe that we must not forget about those people who were not born in the european union.     2148.53374098868
let us not forget that, in 1999, 717,000 people came here to compensate for our low birth rate.     6502.316356252908
in social terms, it is therefore absolutely essential that we have an immigration policy with a social conscience, as president gutierres recently stated in spain.     1502.1065052122797
we must be very aware of the situation of those people whose prospects of finding work are threatened or reduced by economic changes, mergers, take-overs, new technologies etc.     6517.937132586104
what is required here is a training policy to qualify and prepare people for new jobs, for those who already have a job and for those who need a second opportunity, because sometimes, as the title of the film states, the postman always rings twice.     3365.5164266011684
in my humble opinion, it is the responsibility of all european, national and regional governments to ensure that he rings on more than one occasion.     4027.7918382651
it seems to me, commissioner, that the commission has the responsibility to promote and coordinate these opportunities.     2572.2194633835743
mr president, i should like to congratulate mr andersson on his really excellent work, which is the reason why there is broader agreement, and i also thank the members of parliament involved for their contribution.     5636.860227886911
i would like to make a number of comments about what has been said.     4447.265041023476
first, i must stress that the debate about social protection issues began several years ago among the european bodies and since 1995 there has been the first statement entitled: "the future of social protection" and there have been statements from the european parliament, those of mrs weiler and mr pronk.     2470.958297581527
today, we have the statement by mr andersson.     4595.008103364511
i believe we have made considerable progress since we are now talking about the modernisation of social protection.     3883.682975315462
the second point is that we must bear in mind the legal basis by virtue of which the european commission can operate.     2907.4863300392462
you know very well that the treaty provides no legal basis for policies at european level, but there is the agreement expressed at the last council, there is agreement that work should begin on coordination and the exchange of experience, as well as the exchange of successful programmes between the member states.     2591.408338785129
the third observation is that as regards the four issues on which the specific statement focuses, which relate to paid work, the sustainability of pension systems, social convergence and lastly quality in health care, there is a horizontal approach to the issue of women because in each of these categories - as the speakers said - there are special problems relating to women and those problems should in many cases be addressed by separate policies.     3835.3027775944583
the next point concerns the specific action we have undertaken.     3108.0292342741604
as you know, a high-level group has already been set up by the portuguese presidency, which will present its first report to the summit conference in june.     1872.5522947376644
the portuguese presidency has focused on two things: sustainable pension systems and social accession.     4597.642153636524
i believe that the proposal for a corresponding group at parliamentary level is very positive and can contribute towards our progress to the next step after approval of the said high-level group.     2341.843073550293
concerning some remarks about the role of social protection systems, the cost of social protection and the part played by those systems in the competitiveness of the member states, i should like to say that in europe policies strive to combine competitiveness and social cohesion.     4504.63899382548
of course, we must take due note of the issue of competitiveness, but social protection too must be planned in a way that makes it a factor of economic growth and employment.     2162.250908057104
i think the statement' s content makes this clear.     2505.6285881899457
ladies and gentlemen, the aim is that in the next year, and after a unanimous decision by the council, we should progress to a first level of coordination, exchange of views, listing of social problems and creation of a data base for the better coordination of policies.     4437.525987824044
as many of the speakers have said, i believe a first important step has been taken.     1639.0237463630708
already at the intergovernmental conference, social policy issues are being addressed more broadly and i believe we are in a new phase, in which social policy will, to a large extent, become a european policy.     1691.1469067891974
thank you, mrs diamantopoulou.     22242.003404555715
the debate is closed.     4906.850855896859
the vote will take place tomorrow at 11.30 a.m.
wipo treaty
the next item is the recommendation (a5-0008/2000) by mrs cederschiöld, on behalf of the committee on legal affairs and the internal market, on the proposal for a council decision on the approval, on behalf of the european community, of the wipo copyright treaty and the wipo performances and phonograms treaty [com(1998) 249 - c5-0222/1999 - 1998/0141 (avc)].     39044.96722760772
mr president, we are now taking a stance on an important issue, an international agreement, which may influence the prospects for prosperity of many member states.     4637.256530349021
in my own country, sweden, one of our biggest export industries is affected, namely the music and sound recording industry.     6774.249548933766
it is about artists' entitlement to copyright or to protection for their artistic products.     6356.309592599112
it is about the wct (world copyright treaty) and the wppt (world performance and phonograms treaty).     4901.536005874692
without protection under intellectual property law, creativity is inhibited and financial incentives obstructed.     31128.28344996954
forgeries are big business.     1920.6607776845467
it is estimated that, in europe alone, income of eur 4.5 billion is lost because of piracy.     3112.554039223978
the problem affects a lot of jobs in the entertainment industry.     4597.396884603677
this is an historic agreement because the eu is the contracting party.     2612.067165147066
this is the case, in spite of the fact that it is not the eu, but only the member states, which belong to the organisation.     4268.212409132795
there exists a special clause which requires parliament' s assent in two cases.     20747.368903693125
with regard to the present agreement, both these conditions are fulfilled.     5300.9692774025925
a new institution is being created, an assembly which, in the future, will monitor the agreement and its consequences.     3763.081585176394
the agreement also concerns questions dealt with in accordance with the co-decision-making procedure.     9088.663359206119
parliament must therefore give its assent, because the agreement concerns its sphere of competence.     16138.150091341231
the future will show whether it is right, by means of this new parliamentary assembly, to reduce the commission' s powers regarding these issues.     9402.491660907841
parliament cannot, in any case, at present do anything about the matter.     2903.534871091037
the agreement complements the berne convention in regard to literary works.     79533.68912772162
it creates international legal protection in connection with distribution, sale, rental, public reproduction and the making available of works.     22927.51317045445
it also creates protection for software and databases.     2350.302667983432
there are problems regarding the term "author" .     20097.29919505194
for example, it has not been defined.     873.4389959132959
another issue, which is somewhat problematic, is that of intermediate storage.     12212.063193930404
this too has not been solved.     2997.2618967984326
it is dealt with by means of a general clause which does not provide any definition.     3301.4499268065692
if no definitions are provided in questions such as this, it can lead to their being dealt with differently around the world, with no uniformity of approach.     6054.079160033936
that would not be a good thing.     767.9983625606109
the question of arbitration procedures is still to be resolved, too.     1240.8402757624729
parliament cannot change these details.     8786.398105774104
we can only say yes or no to the agreement as it stands.     1634.1845417923944
yes or no to an agreement which contains deficiencies.     4587.341816255574
both myself, as rapporteur, and the committee think we ought to say yes.     2667.0194814612205
we have here an agreement which, in spite of its defects, constitutes a significant step forward for the issue of copyright.     9706.065308263489
we have of course discussed at length whether parliament and whether the eu should have powers in regard to patents under intellectual property law.     9710.078496021417
i think they should have.     3498.2469744323225
however, there is still no community legislation in the areas of patent law and protection of designs, and there needs to be.     1679.4999586765623
a european patent is in the offing, and that is a good thing.     1354.592790708827
now, the union and the member states are combining to take the first steps in the area of intellectual property law.     3641.443754113502
it is good for us that we have the power to do this, in spite of the fact that we are not completely satisfied.     3081.3447027121847
in that way, we can act as a model for the applicant states.     2732.856249580429
we have a direct financial interest in these issues being settled before enlargement.     4069.4242973539767
i want to thank the commission for its efforts in this area and for the cooperation with the commission' s officials, which has been quite excellent.     3853.3855651195863
finally, i also want to thank this chamber for being so good as to listen to these words at this late hour.     6012.970778576716
mr president, i too would like to congratulate the rapporteur, mrs cederschiöld, on her very serious work.     6056.8715869628995
she has clearly identified the problems created by the approval of the world intellectual property organisation' s treaties.     5282.743173168065
their approval is undoubtedly an historic occasion because for the first time the european union is participating as a contracting party in an international treaty that governs the field of intellectual property.     7492.889804698312
it is a fact that the road has been a long one, mainly because of the legal problem that arose in connection with the extent to which the community could be considered to have absolute competence in matters of intellectual property.     3042.205406687424
the problem has been overcome.     5119.77093416615
today we are asked to give our assent in effect to two treaties: the treaty on intellectual property and the treaty on interpretations, performances and phonograms.     11795.540505206447
as regards the first of these, it seems to provide an effective solution for the long-standing demands by the creators of artistic and literary works, while at the same time creating a framework of protection in relation to issues such as distribution, rental and the presentation of their works to the public.     7864.217066764882
as regards the second treaty on performances and phonograms, it must be stressed that for the first time an international treaty signed by the community as well recognises exclusive rights for performers, interpreters and artists for reproduction, distribution and rental to the public, and the right to fair remuneration for radio broadcasts.     7250.3639066346395
in that context a noteworthy and important step forward is the treaty' s treatment of the right of reproduction.     8005.799827581887
even though, as the commission says, the texts of the treaties do not contain special provisions to regulate all the respective issues raised by technological development, it can nevertheless be said that within the legislative framework created, performing or interpreting artists enjoy broader protection than that accorded by the treaty of rome or even by the community directives.     8743.41872820745
interpreting article 7 of the treaty, we can conclude that the application of protection for reproduction does not only mean protection for the reproduction of the interpretations or performances themselves as such that are embodied in recordings, but also protection against copying, whether in whole or in part and whether permanent or temporary, and protection against any other form of production.     4477.395556074192
it would be appropriate, however, for us also to mention some points which should be at the focus of future wipo work or the work of other diplomatic conferences.     3911.0103249990807
first, there is the right of distribution as referred to in article 8(1).     3090.5841356911064
the option accorded to states by the next paragraph of the same article to regulate the exhaustion of the above right is dangerous and is one of the treaty' s most negative points, since there is nothing to prevent states from enacting special conditions for the exhaustion of the right, a thing that would be to the disadvantage of artists.     8333.562757488407
as regards rental rights, on interpretation of article 9, we can conclude that the exclusive right of rental belongs to artists, interpreters or performers, only if this is laid down by national legislation.     22572.963386605923
i think this conflicts with article 13.
to conclude, i want to say that ratification of these treaties opens a horizon for property rights.     14558.223789015294
in the 21st century, the century of knowledge and the invisible economy, intellectual property will be the dominant form of property and will, in many cases, be more important than traditional property.     4981.843995109915
we are at the dawn of an age in which new legislation will be created, where the right to utilise intellectual property will clash head on with the public interest in terms of its dissemination and exploitation by the public at large.     6118.67987750921
mr president, i should like to thank our rapporteur for this excellent report which, nonetheless, concludes that, as a community, our accession to these two wipo treaties must not boil down to a mere institutional development, however important that may be, i.e.     2593.609191604847
the opportunity for the european community and the wipo assembly to work together.     3474.3178245175395
this ratification is also necessary in the light of the enlargement of europe.     3881.2791768326183
however, an objective is emerging behind this issue of community accession, one i find as yet too theoretical but one that some member states, nonetheless, find too offensive.     4587.998601559403
this is the affirmation of a european cultural policy.     1424.024114149824
in this connection, i expect our three institutions, parliament, the commission and the council, to be in agreement.     2662.7415660948695
cultural policy means, in particular, defending the role of the arts, recognising the role of authors and setting high cultural standards.     6693.433265971548
in addition, at a time when europe faces the task of defining a new policy on copyright and neighbouring rights, there is an urgent need - as has just been pointed out several times - to define and redefine what exactly an author is.     8522.071365199577
our definitions are still too vague and new technologies now make it essential for us to clarify them.     4080.411538174073
mr president, commissioner, the adoption of the wipo agreements was a very positive step in the direction of the global information society.     3442.4088302357945
the agreements crystallise the decades-old legacy of copyright, and they represent a precisely considered and balanced solution for the regulation of copyright and neighbouring rights.     7940.554641313189
the entry into force of the treaties and their final form is, however, in addition to this recommendation, dependent on implementation by the parties themselves.     5556.512089672188
in the united states the wipo treaty has already entered into force, having been enacted into law by means of the digital millennium corporate act and there they have managed to retain the delicate balance of the treaty by means of this legislation.     7965.094419986916
the eu too must proceed in its legislative work in accordance with what is agreed in the wipo.     10716.20122478238
it is worrying that the position adopted by the previous parliament does not support this balanced wipo system.     4905.558271679265
the opinion on the copyright directive at first reading was very distorted, and, furthermore, the balance is moving in a harmful direction from the point of view of europeans.     4582.623282560438
the recommendations that have been made will not improve the status of culture in europe, on the contrary.     2000.6102504055286
parliament' s amendments would mainly improve opportunities for the media enterprises that are well established in the market to move their old, in excess of 80% market share to a new environment with rigid, excessive copyright regulations.     8421.325682535971
i am not now speaking of charlotte cederschiöld' s recommendation, which is an excellent paper, but of the matters of copyright that the previous parliament discussed.     8971.696807500042
on one point of detail, i would like to thank mrs cederschiöld for having clarified, for example, the concept of so-called temporary copies in her opinion.     9967.964824658211
there is no consciously adopted position in the wipo treaty on temporary copies.     17237.648834342366
if these, which technically speaking are copies, were covered by the protection in force it would make the transfer of information - as the recommendation quite rightly states - expensive and complicated for no good reason.     5460.1648247086405
it would be as if the deliveryman had to pay a copyright fee whenever he carried a book to be read by the purchaser.     2124.51702228318
the real benefit of culture is that art and knowledge can be transferred safely and easily from the artist directly to the user, the consumer.     4283.099993692977
the new operational environment is, above all, a huge opportunity to create and spread culture in a different way than ever before.     3354.4805653275153
it is essential that the interests of all parties are taken into account.     1696.9920775708133
parliament has tried to take account of this in its own recommendation.     7316.660382924204
.     1000.0
(nl) mr president, i would like to start by congratulating the rapporteur on the work that she has carried out.     8351.874270691216
the commission is under the impression that she has worked fast and effectively.     3305.696418368528
this is important.     1491.8874807696254
it befits the commission to express its gratitude towards the rapporteur.     36249.16031074535
i would like to present the commission' s conclusion to you without any further ado: it agrees with the conclusions of the draft recommendation and, therefore, with ratification by this parliament.     4876.807747626993
i would like to stress the importance of both treaties.     9513.596502102357
both the wipo copyright treaty and the wipo performances and phonograms treaty constitute progress in the international protection of copyright and neighbouring rights and are, as such, a considerable improvement on the berne convention and the treaty of rome.     19618.219233719527
both treaties contribute towards a high level of protection for works and other matters, but offer the public access to the contents of these via electronic networks.     4440.878796118702
the two treaties explicitly permit the community to become a party to the treaties and to play an important role in managing them.     3052.625002997964
consequently, approval of the treaties by the community is of major importance, because it illustrates that the community sets great store by intellectual property rights.     5789.315069183246
in addition, assent leads to more widespread international recognition of the european union' s role in terms of copyright.     56290.77887378964
at international level, there is a great deal of support for treaties entering into force more swiftly.     5706.0524900729415
these have now been ratified by twelve or thirteen wipo member states, including the united states.     37425.44495979031
the entry into force of treaties largely depends on the european union, because thirty ratification acts are required in order for treaties to enter into effect, and these acts that are signed by the european union and the member states, including associated countries, are of vital importance in order to reach this number.     9330.188518738982
the developing countries, too, expect the european union to bring about ratification swiftly because this would give a strong signal worldwide.     7681.410890391205
in this context, parliament has already played a key role in the discussions on the draft directive on copyright in the information society, which led to the approval of the barzanti report in february 1999.
this directive is the necessary counterpart of the proposed decision and largely embraces the key principles of the wipo treaties.     3647.4715915995844
the european parliament must now follow once again the exceptional assent procedure of article 300(3).     3227.053810413018
it is vitally important to give the correct signal to the outside world, in the form of this recommendation.     5267.125349977854
in a nutshell, i would like to reiterate the commission' s conclusion here: it supports the draft recommendation and hopes that it will be of key importance to anyone with an interest in intellectual property.     10254.54949620033
thank you, commissioner.     696.3226540346473
the debate is closed.     4906.850855896859
