mr president, mr president-in-office of the council, commissioner, we are in actual fact holding a debate amongst the converted, which is a pity.     4390.023953889316
we should really talk about coherence in the development policy in the presence of mr prodi, for example, who has missed a great chance right from the outset, for he has allowed the development cooperation policy to fragment over three commissioners - and this is better than four, i have to admit - but this does not really make for a coherent policy.     3654.7118463517436

there are a lot of people who will be watching further developments with great interest.     9495.470787033564
mr president, the seattle demonstrators argued that free trade is bad for the less- and least-developed countries, it is purely a rich country selfish interest.     5682.740271005636
yet we might consider the rate of economic progress and the rate of eliminating poverty in countries such as india and tanzania over the 40 or 50 years since they became independent.     4276.850158997969
there the theory went that protectionism and state intervention would preserve jobs, allow industry to adjust gradually and without pain.     10918.444854301599
it did not work; poverty became worse than ever.     5254.905345775911
the medicine may not taste too good in the short term, but opening one' s economy to competition at home and abroad forces optimal application of resources and leads to a higher standard and quality of life in the not so longer term.     5994.195287019449
is that not the lesson of the european union itself and of the single market in particular?     938.8939733365488
as mr nielson said, this is a fine example to those countries and regions aspiring to greater prosperity.     4057.0286835418647

it is quite clear that a lack of coherence of development policy with trade policy diminishes our development efforts and must also diminish the political regard of the developing countries for europe and our fine words.     2740.895670755909
mr president, in the last couple of days we have heard fine words about the need for greater coherence, coordination and effectiveness in the external aid programmes of the community and of the member states.     6485.144611107847
there is one relatively small area of activity with enormous public visibility which would be a litmus test of the union' s ability to match words with action.     11400.128016730472
i am referring to the continuing tragedy of anti-personnel landmines which present an obstacle to so many development programmes, to post-conflict reconstruction, and which ruin the lives of many of the most vulnerable in some of the poorest parts of the world, particularly in developing countries.     7228.760685125707
over recent years the community has spent some eur 200 million in mine clearance, assistance to victims and related activities.     3319.1701579391547
the member states have spent similar amounts.     10048.419317038544
but there is still enormous inefficiency and a lack of will to implement a strategy agreed with other members of the international community to overcome the anti-personnel landmine problem within a finite time span.     5601.02765286703
mr nielson identified the problems of lack of concerted action and the need for better complementarity with member states.     9702.047325794652
this has been talked about for years.     7656.753569274469
the commission needs to carry out internal organisational changes, strengthen the coherence and effectiveness of its actions, ideally with a properly resourced central mine action policy cell.     15412.141386758416
it would also need more rapid contractual and implementation procedures and an enhanced multiannual budget line and much improved management of its programmes.     2845.5935541166946
a proper stock-taking of the effectiveness of mine action programmes needs to take place in each of the countries seriously afflicted by landmines.     8555.214140817518
it seems to me that the landmine issue encapsulates so many of the organisational, procedural, financial management and operational difficulties which have afflicted the commission and the union.     2843.221950084308
are these afflictions of the past, or will we find that in this relatively compact and limited area the european union is still unable to meet its aspirations?     3097.7916720642347
mr president, commissioner nielson, the conclusion of the new acp-eu convention is an important step towards greater coherence.     4796.848137783377
and it is to be welcomed that criteria such as human rights, democracy, rule of law, and good governance are more firmly anchored in this new treaty than they were previously, and that earlier colonial relations no longer shape most of our relations to quite the same extent, rather, we now set greater store by these political criteria in this respect.     5342.192345950087
for these are prerequisite to any development.     7289.850405890234
if these criteria are not adhered to, then development does not stand a chance
the same goes for cuba, mr modrow.     5090.547764613135

nonetheless, these are important criteria for the european union.     1460.8650383079644
there is a second issue i would like to raise.     5299.06356569455
we are discussing coherence here.     5887.020993505517
many speakers have cited examples of the extent to which the various policies are not coherent.     4435.889863007607
in my view, however, the development policy itself needs to be more coherent too.     2484.6217199935327
there is no coherence because there is still no effective coordination with the member states and there are some projects being carried out by the member states and the european union in the developing countries which are very much at odds with one another.     3030.8745352367105

mr president-in-office of the council, if in future you succeed in making this eur 13.5 billion subject to the supervision of the parliamentary committee on budgetary control, and to bodies such as olaf, then i believe you will raise the level of acceptability.     5025.427485739296
this, to my mind, is also something that we should call upon the intergovernmental conference to do, i.e.     2283.863083366286
establish the edf firmly in the budget of the european union.     3803.1645185856487
mr president, we certainly must concede that, on balance, this text is full of good intentions, but we did not vote in favour of it in committee, nor shall we do so in plenary.     4915.815150754884
we had two reasons for this, both of which gave rise to amendments which i should like to explain here very briefly.     2329.0759503332924
first, the committee on development and cooperation advocates the communitisation of the edf; clearly that is misguided.     12237.310456049725
in our view, cooperation is, and should remain, an intergovernmental exercise.     3343.7148895408964
with europe preparing to enlarge, communitising the edf would make it easy to hide a lack of commitment behind the screen of the community budget, quite apart from the fact that we would be wasting a fruitful and promising opportunity of associating our southern partners with our cooperation policy.     10429.033235256937
lastly, the commission has, in the recent past, had trouble managing the funds for development and humanitarian aid transparently, as mrs maes has just reminded us so opportunely.     5578.437306220432
why should we add to them?     2500.4550645438285
secondly, we find it most regrettable that mr corrie' s report should mention the trade and development agreement with south africa because, in the final analysis, this report is typical of the amateur diplomacy to which the european commission is taking ever more frequent recourse.     7849.688075500313
it is perfectly astounding that the union could have negotiated an agreement on behalf of the member states in which a fundamental section is quite simply left blank.     6889.909945059991
and let me say in passing, it is even more astonishing that the council allowed it to.     2463.144297561018
as a result, we are now in the absurd position of negotiating from a position of weakness on vital aspects such as wine and agricultural production within the framework of an agreement which we have already signed.     5508.310088933122
the part of this agreement which blocks negotiations could mean that the union ends up paying producers to stop producing sherry, port and a whole host of products which are purely and simply copies under international commercial law.     5073.0339992766885
imagine the potential cost of such a precedent.     11338.642769285461
it is proof, as if we still needed it, of the fact that immature diplomacy, which defends no interests, unless of course they are individual interests, and is caught between the dogma of free trade and pressure groups and more or less friendly ngos, is not just inefficient, it is positively dangerous.     4174.6593103675
finally, a word on the general thinking behind the corrie report.     11347.784609893388
nowadays, let us face it, the joint assembly is seen as an obstacle to free international trade by the dogmatisers in geneva and washington.     6996.381845624772
it was a chance to reiterate that our original and exemplary mode of cooperation with the southern countries should be developed and strengthened.     6643.807226725211
the corrie report missed this chance and we must, regrettably, punish it for losing so much.     15023.350884865235
.     1000.0
(pt) mr president, by way of both a conclusion and a summary, i would like to highlight three key aspects of this debate.     9973.903479787374
first of all, many speakers stressed that globalisation accentuates regional imbalances and social inequality, and increases poverty.     11834.321102861384
this suggests that something must be done, failing which tensions will be aggravated and conflicts jeopardising international peace and stability will arise or worsen.     7886.944707523772
secondly, it will be important to approach development policies from a different angle in this context, taking advantage of their capacity for correcting imbalances and reducing inequality.     11475.075107853965
i believe that the importance that everyone has attached to the european union' s role in international development policies is significant.     9168.742004977108
everyone recognises, indeed this was the theme of today' s debate, that, if the european union is to play a more active and leading role in development policies at world level, it will have no choice but to enhance the coherence of its own various development-related policies.     5845.325408118752
i note that some speakers also emphasised that responsibility for coherence in eu policies is not a matter for the council, for the commission or for parliament alone.     1869.078082231757
quite the contrary - it will have to be the subject of a concerted effort in terms of discussion and legislative and institutional preparation, be it on the part of the commission or of the council, or even on the part of the european parliament itself, and some members have in fact already suggested that a monitoring centre or a working party could be established to monitor the impact of various policies in the specific area of development.     1976.2576983899112
finally, i would like to emphasise that it is also important - and this is one of the priorities of the portuguese presidency' s programme - to note the view that it is necessary to recognise the value of development in the european union' s policies, and in particular in the context of its foreign policy.     4007.473870461141
we believe that this is absolutely vital, failing which we will be unable to take any consistent action as regards the coherence of policies, and we cannot do this without giving development policies their rightful place as an instrument for upholding the principles and values underpinning europe' s status in the international system.     9179.286253285323
for this reason it may be necessary, as i have stressed, to change the model and move away from the post-colonial model towards a more european approach to development policies.     3849.9168144674827
in this respect i believe - and some speakers here have also commented on this - that the adoption of the new convention, which succeeds the lomé convention, is an important instrument of renewal, and represents a new approach to a partnership with such an important group of countries from the south at a time of considerable instability in the international negotiating process, as demonstrated by the first negotiating round in seattle.     4796.02522367793
.     1000.0
i sense some degree of frustration in many of the contributions to this debate.     7829.155343181412
i have given you a statement on behalf of the commission which i think provides a useful background to this discussion.     7368.148397149229
we will furnish more detailed material relating directly to all the issues concerning coherence.     7869.130899494135
this discussion is not new.     1525.7903532741232
we are currently working on it in the commission and when that work is done i will report on it, as many have been asking if it will be possible to see the report on coherence.     1337.5909536229342
i have no doubt that as a result of the increasing political attention attached to these problems - something which has clearly been reflected in the debate today - it will be easier for the commission and the dg on development to bring coherence to the forefront of our working agenda.     3825.525665367091
this also calls for the introduction of further new mechanisms in the commission system which may very well include, as suggested by some of the political groups in parliament, the setting up of a special intra-service working group and the establishment of a kind of coherence watch in the commission.     2732.1366603201004
i have read - and heard in the debate - that coherence is being used as some kind of a miracle formula that takes care of everything.     4752.680816057952
i think it is important to keep it as a relatively clearly defined discipline of trying to reduce inconsistencies where they really exist, and we certainly do have them.     6000.017782180772
much of this has to do with real disagreement between sector interests in each and every member state, as has been very honestly said here in this house: people more or less identify with their spheres of interest, and all this is legitimate.     3390.5834841910237
i would, however, warn against the illusion that incoherence is the product of some kind of a mechanical deficiency in the system.     7471.175308782519
this exists, but it is only a small part of what we are discussing.     1488.8985926812122
the major part of it has to do with well known established differences of interest between sexes and so on, and this is the problem.     2826.8592731014746
mr mulder mentioned that no farmer will start producing anything if the price is too low.     8412.322393584602
this is true in our part of the world, but our partners and beneficiaries in africa have a slightly different perspective.     5283.3462786942555
they farm, not just for a living, but in order to live, and this makes a difference.     3307.8459241408323
subsistence farming is not based on price calculations, it is based on the hope of rain in the next season.     9912.740772978239
this does not apply to all farming in developing countries but to the poverty-focusing which we need to deliver better than so far.     2670.208155293083
from that perspective, what i am saying here is of deep relevance.     6703.228082742207
other issues have been mentioned: cattle-farming in namibia, tomatoes and milk powder in the caribbean.     18089.890775705255
we should not be too masochistic.     5845.235688620945
there is a limit to how useful that is.     1801.658118507849
all these cases are characterised by the fact that they have been taken care of.     2491.7465418023885
the intervention prices have been regulated, and the problems we had with beef in western africa and in namibia were actually solved by reducing the intervention support.     6418.685551910703
these are real problems, but they have all been dealt with.     1734.4266607518389
as regards the issue of cacao, chocolate: i repeat that the 5% has to do with creating a basis for a living in the neighbouring countries.     4272.124629157201
what we are talking about here are the local nuts which are as important to the people collecting them and making a living out of them in burkina faso as coconuts for farmers in ghana.     1124.5286737385957
this is not a clear case of incoherence, it is more a case of a competition between suppliers.     3021.8134684860715
what was mentioned concerning sugar in the caribbean is more complicated.     10588.618799511305
in the overseas countries and territories framework the accumulation of some of these trade aspects represents a complicated problem, but it may be that what we are talking about here has more to do with promoting the interests of some european companies than with generating income in the caribbean.     4462.3232554719525
in any case, this is a matter which we are currently dealing with.     2186.971984660474
i am committed to try to create a balance in all these issues and i would mention the achievement of the trade development and cooperation agreement with south africa.     7302.35062633574
in all modesty, i would claim that this is something that documents that the commission is willing to walk or fly the extra mile in order to ensure coherence between the perspective of development cooperation and the perspective of trade interests.     8100.669001672916
we certainly have done our share to solve that problem, and i sincerely hope that we now will have a soft landing and a peaceful implementation in the forthcoming years of that agreement.     2881.3367619578976
finally, i thank you again for this debate.     1299.1914857503289
we will come back again with material on these issues, because these problems, in one way or another, are here to stay, simply because development cooperation represents basically doing something in the world that is different from taking care of traditionally-defined trade interests, etc.     3037.859993358707
we have to reconcile those different approaches.     12871.821251593934
thank you very much, commissioner nielson.     940.197016857905

next session of un human rights commission
the next item is the statement by the council on eu priorities for the next session of the un human rights commission on 20 march 2000.
mr president, ladies and gentlemen, as you know, the european union played an active role in the 55th session of the human rights commission held in geneva in march and april 1999.
it tabled resolutions on the human rights situation in iran, iraq, in the israeli settlements, in burma/myanmar, in the democratic republic of the congo and in sudan, and negotiated statements to be issued on behalf of the chairman on colombia and on east timor.     19349.672647444408
for the first time, the european union tabled a resolution on the death penalty, which was enormously successful, and led work on a resolution concerning children' s rights, which was a joint initiative with the latin american group of countries.     3432.8263061049743
the union will continue to play this role at the 56th session of the human rights commission to be held in geneva in march and april.     4079.9764131186894
in particular, as agreed at the human rights council group on 9 february 2000, the european union intends to table draft resolutions on the israeli settlements, iran, iraq, sudan, the democratic republic of the congo, burma/myanmar, a draft chairman' s statement on colombia, and, possibly, on east timor, together with a draft statement on the situation in various countries.     13432.471709875193
the european union will continue discussions on preparations for the 56th session of the human rights commission at the next meeting of the human rights group in march.     2278.061799920812
in particular, there will be a discussion of the possibility of tabling a draft resolution on the death penalty.     2639.145412338421
to date, the group has not reached agreement on the strategy to be adopted in this respect, and in particular with regard to formulating a compromise to be tabled in the last resort in case of any amendments to the contrary, including article 2(7) of the charter of the united nations, which refers to sovereignty.     3400.118055592572
we have tried to reach agreement in advance on a compromise text, in view of the difficulties that arose at the 1999 session of the third committee of the united nations general assembly, at which the member states failed to agree on an amendment introducing a reference to article 2(7) into the draft resolution sponsored by the european union on the death penalty.     4662.271043302912
as you know, at the 55th session of the human rights commission held last year, the united states decided, at a very late stage, to table a draft resolution on china.     2597.721028132261
this draft resolution was defeated by a chinese no-action motion.     7771.064801392138
the european union voted against that motion.     6192.526521778311
the european union' s position on the approach to be adopted towards china in the human rights commission was confirmed at the general affairs council on 21 march 1999: there was no european union initiative on china nor was agreement reached to co-sponsor a resolution on china, but an eu vote against the no-action motion.     7443.166367571362
it is by no means certain that the european union could have maintained a united front if this resolution had been put to the vote.     1570.1092045820567
the united states of america will be tabling a draft resolution on china at the 56th session of the human rights commission and are putting pressure on the european union to support this draft resolution.     2565.482720201928
this initiative has provoked a strong reaction on china' s part: the chinese authorities are making representations to the countries and institutions of the european union, in an attempt to persuade them not to support the united states' initiative.     10936.287964370058
this initiative is being discussed within the union, but with no hope of a decision in the near future.     3005.6877745065285
it is in the interests of the european union to keep all its options open for as long as possible, and in any case at least until after the eu-china human rights dialogue meeting is held on 25 february 2000.
in view of changes made to the membership of the human rights commission, we estimate the vote on the no-action motion will be close.     3458.141040466128
it is therefore important this year, too, that the countries of the european union should reach a consensus on the position to take in any vote on the resolution.     2465.269206750904
in this context it is important to note that the european union regularly opposes requests by china that the human rights dialogue should automatically rule out tabling a resolution at the human rights commission in geneva.     5069.497611308437
the european union unequivocally considers that although it is a very valuable instrument for exchanging views and for taking note of the overall position of the other party, the dialogue should also yield concrete results on the ground, and when this does not happen it may be necessary to present a resolution to the human rights commission, potentially whilst the dialogue is continuing.     3197.752371859884
the european union also regularly informs china that the human rights commission is the appropriate body for considering human rights issues, and whether or not it agrees with this initiative, this is in any case legitimate from this point of view.     3586.503724647478
the next human rights dialogue will take place on 25 february in lisbon, and will include seminars on legal issues and on women' s rights, and it is expected that representatives from the academic world and from civil society will take part.     6956.1227233346335
the next human rights seminar will be organised by the portuguese presidency in may 2000, and, during the last round of the dialogue in october 1999, the chinese side accepted the european union' s proposal for technical assistance to support the process of ratifying the united nations international pact on economic, social and cultural rights and the united nations' international pact on civil and political rights, both of which have been signed by the chinese government.     3998.3251176161184
the european union is discussing issues on which it hopes that china will take concrete measures, and on the basis of these discussions the presidency will convey to the chinese authorities the message that the european union hopes china will announce positive measures at the session on 25 february.     4168.411189783147
the presidency will also present to the chinese authorities a list of individual cases on which it intends to express its serious concern.     4320.176718878175
the meeting held in beijing in october 1999 confirmed that, on the one hand, the chinese authorities were willing to make concessions on issues relating to the dialogue itself, with a restructuring of the dialogue and of discussion of legal forms of repression such as the death penalty and administrative detention.     8167.879509885043
on the other hand, they will not give any ground on the pivotal questions of the single party system and territorial integrity.     3031.794303871357
discussions within the european union about the situation in china following the outcome of the dialogue rounds in 1999 have led the european union to decide to express its concern about human rights violations in china more firmly; the same applies to the absence of concrete outcomes from the dialogue, while recognising the positive steps that china has made on the international stage.     6932.510285069305
on various occasions the european union has reiterated to the chinese authorities its disappointment with the results of the dialogue, and has stressed that it hoped for better outcomes in certain more sensitive areas.     6856.35764176926
it was decided that the dialogue should continue, but in a more targeted and effective way, given that china has agreed with the european union' s proposal to restructure the dialogue by means of better use of experts and to establish a closer link with the seminars.     8429.352251529019
lastly, during the next human rights commission there will also be a debate on rationalising the work of the commission itself.     1454.9900844103256
a working party has been set up with the express task of revising the commission' s mechanisms.     4560.150165099281
this working party has worked hard to reach agreement that it is essential to enhance the effectiveness of the present mechanisms and to make them more rigorous, especially in the financial field.     2549.408019064391
the european union will oppose any spurious attempt to use this reform of the human rights commission' s activities to limit its capacity for intervention by restricting its sphere of influence and its ongoing monitoring of all human rights issues at world level.     10072.041691228289
.     1000.0
mr president, i would like to thank the presidency for the work it has done to prepare for geneva on subjects like racism, civil and political rights and the right to development.     3579.6331699934317
as the presidency said, much remains to be done, despite the understandable request by parliament and many ngos that the european union should set out its position well in advance of the geneva session.     3525.423935927766
that is easier said than done - we are still discussing some of the toughest issues.     2210.5947401153167
for example, discussion is taking place about many of the matters raised in parliament' s resolution and it is too early to predict the outcome in all cases.     7713.238045813154
it is not surprising that the debate is long and difficult.     2177.13397118573
that does not suggest any lack of commitment to the improvement of human rights but it does indicate the usual concern to get our approach right so that we really make a difference.     3867.6804199126127
however, let me reflect that concern by referring to three controversial issues: the death penalty, cuba and china.     23736.317387600397
i am going to be so unwise as to allow one or two personal reflections to creep into my remarks.     1869.5987452774427
we are currently reviewing the options and possibilities for introducing a death penalty resolution in the human rights commission.     4588.944162111152
this has always been a subject about which i personally have felt extremely strongly, i have always voted against the death penalty.     1840.6000059089863
spurred on by amnesty international and others, one of the first decisions i took as governor of hong kong was to abolish the death penalty.     15015.814769111508
we know what happened recently at the un general assembly.     10100.147233428146
we had to freeze our resolution on the death penalty or risk the passing of a resolution that would have incorporated wholly unacceptable arguments that asserted that human rights are not universally applicable and valid.     5379.255575111982
the new york stage is different from geneva.     14620.204162474682
last year' s debate in new york on sovereignty and humanitarian intervention was such a sensitive topic that it influenced all other issues.     11714.221555703481
it certainly influenced the debate on the moratorium on the death penalty.     7398.103789329763
the debate in geneva, however, is more focused on human rights issues.     4729.046620365567
we may therefore stand a better chance of making progress there.     4474.391478135221
secondly, cuba.     1096287.5463131987
i want to address this issue directly because we in europe are criticised unfairly for not taking human rights there sufficiently seriously.     5554.734619567947
we are extremely concerned about moves by the cuban government over the last year that have further curtailed public freedom, moves like changes in the penal code and the scope of the death penalty.     5503.336680480288
we are also worried by the recent increase in the number of political detentions.     9436.413077741588
we are monitoring the human rights situation through the reports of ngos and the work of our human rights working group based in the eu embassies in havana.     2413.2700036341585
we have regularly put our criticisms to the cuban authorities, and this parliament has done the same.     4241.402153768921
but i do not believe that the right approach is to try to isolate cuba.     14484.961479700341
we want a fair and open society in cuba, a cuba which respects the principles of the market, the sanctity of contract and the rule of law.     5165.6879259336465
we want to encourage a process of transition to democracy and respect for human rights and fundamental freedoms.     4441.156159368969
my views are exactly the same when it comes to the third subject with which i wish to deal, namely china.     4683.412347538754
quite simply, i want us to be engaged with china.     7220.707659366965
it would be self-indulgent and self-defeating, as well as insulting to over a fifth of humanity, to want anything else.     2507.870623082602
i also want to see a china transformed by peaceful change into a free pluralist society which does not lock up democracy activists or religious believers.     7062.587084280924
so what should we do?     841.0440569710612
naturally we should trade enthusiastically with china and hope that we can welcome china into the world trade organisation on sensible terms.     9800.792138932848
it is a misnomer to talk about a world trade organisation which does not include china - or russia, for that matter.     2723.530213109857
but i do not believe today, nor have i ever believed, that our burgeoning economic relationship with china - manifested, for example, by china' s eur 25 billion surplus with the european union - should silence us when it comes to china' s human rights record.     20799.86569017008
for me the question is not whether we should address the question of human rights in china - we have a duty to do so and a legitimate interest in doing so - but rather what is the most effective way of doing this?     2110.5742341703235
so what should be our attitude towards a geneva resolution this year?     4366.339082781726
as the presidency has noted, the union as a whole is considering its position.     3706.026823553136
but i strongly suggest there are a number of factors of which the member states ought to take proper account.     6446.962740257972
first, while some people question whether tabling a geneva resolution makes any difference, several of the most distinguished chinese advocates of democracy strongly believe that it is a valuable move, not least because of the message it sends to people in china itself.     7350.640473514395
that is a point that deserves serious consideration.     2147.4718556454845
secondly, we will all want to take proper account of what has actually been happening in china in recent months, for instance, the heavy sentences imposed on democracy campaigners, the detention and recent punishments meted out to members of christian churches and the arrests and sentences of the members of falun gong.     9832.236159924458
chinese leaders are in no doubt about our position on all these issues and on developments in tibet.     8696.820109762928
we have made our views very clear to them.     1262.9411649099993
while we all know that for many people in china there has been a real long-term improvement in their economic prospects and their social and economic liberties, there is a dark side to the picture as well.     2497.628724939918
critics of tabling a resolution say that at least we now have a human rights dialogue with china.     4215.395348637655
that is true and potentially valuable, although i have to say - and i have said to chinese officials as has the presidency - that there has not been nearly as much content to our dialogue as we would have liked.     2640.9389537964207
of course the dialogue has enabled us to identify and implement eu cooperation programmes in the human rights and legal spheres.     5224.853784974899
that is very welcome and i also believe that our village governance programme should help to promote democracy at grassroots level.     7043.716906141807
naturally any dialogue as complex as the one on human rights is going to be a long-term process, but i wish i could point to more real gains.     2909.928689363789
the next dialogue in lisbon on 25 february will be an important test.     5589.746134214809
it will show whether the dialogue is producing results or not.     3549.3333352118457
what, finally, do we want to see as the first steps?     2348.2848508461666
well, for example, we would like to see china move from signing the un conventions on civil and political rights and on social and cultural rights toward actually ratifying them.     5008.515576744966
i wish we were closer to that.     2972.9893930108415
where does all that leave us this year?     2686.6941692337496
i repeat that member states will have to make up their minds.     7073.070486594029
what i hope is, first, that we in europe will adopt a common position and stick to it, that we will not allow some to be picked off, that our position will not be based on self-delusion or pretence, that it will be a common position and a sensible position.     1259.029327013478
i know that some people say we must have a better way of pressing our concern about human rights than through tabling resolutions.     4581.611305676645
but if that is true, then at the very least we need to be more imaginative and honest with ourselves in looking for that way forward.     2227.08953144719
if we are to rest our position on the human rights dialogue, then we need to be able to convince you, the ngo community and the public at large that it is actually getting somewhere.     2966.881796027546
that, in my judgement, is the challenge for the next few weeks.     3931.389050082097
i am grateful to the house for its concern about human rights around the world, and particularly in china.     5710.424349443601
thank you very much, commissioner patten.     728.8734161947891
we will now interrupt the debate on the joint statement concerning the next session of the un human rights commission and proceed to the vote.     2913.7392877898587

the reason is that this concerns the approximation of a legal effect measure, as proposed by the commission.     3035.452279434763
we have accepted this but have proposed further approximations of the legal effect measures that affect this directive, in particular the adoption of a text - which has been approved by the commission overall - for the use and labelling of genetically modified organisms.     5320.0229890505625
we take the view that this should form part of this approximation at this time, but the commission will not adopt this.     3887.764127749944
i believe that, in accordance with article 69 (2), we must endeavour to enter into discussion with the commission, and i would therefore urge my fellow meps to decide in favour of referral back to committee.     16809.095626955375
(parliament decided to refer the report back to committee)

report (a5-0033/2000) by mr andersson, on behalf of the committee on employment and social affairs, on the communication from the commission on a concerted strategy for modernising social protection (com(1999) 347 - c5-0253/1999 - 1999/2182(cos))

explanations of vote- lienemann report (a5-0014/2000)
.     14193.432074698674
(fr) i welcome the quality and the ambition of this report on life.     9442.89815930963
i shall briefly remind you that life is a financial instrument used to support environmental policies within the european union and also in third countries, whether they be neighbouring countries around the mediterranean, countries around the baltic sea or applicant countries.     3308.336558059577
the committee on the environment, public health and consumer protection has proposed many amendments which have, for the most part, been retained in the common position adopted by the council.     2344.188014723244
as far as the third phase of life (2000-2004) is concerned, the instrument is to be divided into three sections, life-nature (47% of the resources), life-environment (47%) and life-third countries (6%).     2436.335765365348
in my opinion, the most important amendments concern the following:
life' s contribution to sustainable development within the community and also to the development of community policy in the field of the environment, particularly regarding the integration of the environment into other policies, as well as to the implementation and updating of legislation relating to the environment;
improving the efficiency, transparency and methodology of procedures for the implementation of life, procedures for information and the dissemination of such information to the public, and procedures for cooperation between recipients;
the objectives of life: this must have as its main objective sustainable urban development, in close association with the pilot projects carried out under the urban initiative, on which i have had the opportunity to speak elsewhere;
the contribution that the projects supported by life make to the sustainability of social and economic activities, and thus job creation.     3214.9190247045644
i consider it crucial that the life instrument should remain open to the participation of candidate countries from central and eastern europe.     5623.716175998724
the other candidate countries (e.g.     4405.694232276768
cyprus, turkey, malta) my also take part in life when agreements permitting such participation are signed with these countries.     9240.027060117936
the stumbling block in the negotiations with the council lies, of course, in the amount of the total appropriation allocated for the third phase of the life programme (2000-2004).     4085.0897971171134
the commission proposal, ratified by the council, is for eur 613 million, whilst the committee on the environment, public health and consumer protection considers that the budget should be eur 850 million.     3582.755248013798
i support mrs lienemann' s firm stance.     308962.22452445474
the financial allocation has not in fact been upgraded since the programme was created, even when the membership of the ec was increased to fifteen member states following enlargement.     3124.546992524166
if the report is adopted in plenary session, then conciliation proceedings will have to be opened, in accordance with the codecision procedure, in order to solve the problem of the amount of appropriations earmarked for the programme.     9846.822198565807
this type of procedure is common practice since the programmes or legislative measures are too often allocated budgets which do not live up to their ambitions!     3161.7145208423117
böge report (a5-0023/2000)
.     15356.916766424492
(fr) as an mep and, more importantly, as a consumer, i welcome the european commission' s proposal as a step in the direction of enhanced food safety within the european union.     8980.306757314997
this proposal has two objectives: first, to amend the 1991 council directive on animal health conditions by removing the provisions on scrapie and, secondly, to introduce a new regulation establishing rules for preventing and combating transmissible spongiform encephalopathies (tse) or bse-type diseases affecting sheep and other animal species.     5661.400604051123
what is interesting abut this new legislation is that a specific legal basis is being created in order to combat scrapie.     4954.144366450499
what is even more satisfactory is that this was one of our main demands in our follow-up report on the mad cow crisis.     3141.575911267042
i also consider it essential, for the sake of coherence, that existing legislation on scrapie, which is limited to the trade in ovine and caprine animals, should be incorporated in a single and complete set of new rules on tse affecting all animals throughout the european union, rules designed to prevent the consumption of foodstuffs or feedingstuffs.     8187.038521198241
these rules are all the more welcome in that doubts still persist as regards scrapie.     8532.445884300014
a number of scientific hypotheses maintain that scrapie developed into bse in cows and therefore caused the bse epidemic.     12505.799350737767
even if this proposal for a european parliament and council regulation to prevent and combat certain tse needs to be made more accurate and more effective, especially as regards detecting and fighting scrapie, it is still a notable improvement.     2128.332457252661
it is a pledge to european citizens, who have voiced their concern, as regards european food policy, on many occasions.     7178.045391641137
the european parliament has relayed their concerns and they have been heard.     3772.300006852552
as a result of the debates on the principle of precaution, traceability, responsibility and transparency, the european commission has presented a white paper on food safety.     6207.563179078993
this white paper sets out several areas of concern and proposes the creation of an independent european food authority as part of a european strategy to restore consumer confidence.     6971.7709932154
it is up to us, as responsible europeans, to prove that the single market and the free movement of goods and food safety are not mutually exclusive.     2713.0839002919934
cederschiöld report (a5-0008/2000)
mr president, i voted for this initiative to amend the copyright legislation because i believe it is one of the most important sectors within the remit of the european parliament.     4778.236687745838
in my view, products of the mind are far more valuable than tangible products, namely, those that can be seen and touched.     3058.6399600993627
the fruits of the intellect and science, which are intangible, are of inestimable importance.     6781.790218769256
therefore, the european community should be more energetic in providing practical encouragement, not just through upholding patents but through concessions and other forms of support for scientists who are striving, through the knowledge and experience they have acquired, to give future generations something better than that which they have today.     4822.453869070879
.     1000.0
(da) we oppose, on principle, the european community' s entering into treaties on copyright law on behalf of the member states.     56866.05419447255
we are nonetheless aware that we are concerned here with a sharing of powers between the member states and the community.     2921.786562449511
at the same time, we appreciate the fact that the wipo treaties are a significant step forward in the direction of guaranteeing the rights of distributors and practising artists.     4032.843603598382
we are therefore voting in favour of the recommendation.     7976.2701733198855
- (de) the group of the greens/european free alliance abstained from the vote on this matter because, in the plenary debate of the previous day, commissioner bolkestein did not respond to any of my questions regarding eu accession to the wipo treaties.     6658.124739547554
in institutional terms, there has been no clarification of what role the ep is to play in the further development of the wipo treaties.     7303.353625746605
in addition, there is a clear tendency, in matters of international jurisdiction, more and more often to obtain a pure eu jurisdiction.     5346.591907102805
when it is, then, still not clear to what extent the ep can participate in the decision-making processes, the question of legitimacy arises.     5180.195418441277
moreover, the concept of 'author' is not defined in the treaties.     1275.2197641119983
in this regard, too, the commission has not answered the question as to what definition it wishes to base its discussions on, and whether it wishes to settle foreseeable conflicts by imposing its own definition of the law on others.     5877.954380733272
one of the trickiest aspects is the public interest: will ratifying and applying wipo lead to the privatisation of culture?     7259.61838330539
and, finally, the matter of the relationship between trips and wipo must be clarified.     18799.098023209855
regarding this, too, there has been only silence by way of a response from the commission.     2696.734216708381
if nothing helpful is forthcoming in the course of a parliamentary debate planned for clarification and orientation, then we must at least abstain from voting.     8969.661865949556
graefe zu baringdorf (a5-0015/2000)
.     128230.67629890621
(pt) food safety issues are in the spotlight following the bse and dioxin scandals.     63573.06454568401
gmos are still the subject of great uncertainty, both in terms of their environmental impact and of their effects on human and animal health.     4218.740097997771
the precautionary principle must therefore apply in the absence of scientific certainty.     15685.885263821807
the use of biotechnology needs to be studied in greater depth and not just "packaged" for immediate sale to boost the profits of a few multinationals in this sector.     4645.651916057228
the profit motive is very much behind the present food safety crises.     3755.78607019316
this is the context that the rapporteur finds himself working in, making a legal link between the issue of gmos and feedingstuffs, and noting that the latter "shall be authorised only if ... safe for human health and the environment" .     4603.388931455004
furthermore, he considers that "genetically modified additives shall be clearly identified as such on any label or any document, official or otherwise, which is affixed to or accompanies the additive" .     5998.951670151734
this will facilitate greater control and traceability of such additives and guarantee the consumer' s freedom of choice.     5376.29711300849
in view of the montreal protocol on genetically modified organisms (gmos), i welcome the fact that the rapporteur has included these issues in his report, and i have therefore voted in favour of it.     14419.283461420055
stenzel report (a5-0034/2000)
mr president, i would very briefly like to give an explanation of vote, on behalf of the ppe group on the equal report.     5952.014239310003

.     1000.0

.     1000.0

equal stands for "equality" .     10575.054831710306

.     1000.0
(pt) the commission intends to use the community initiative equal to replace the community employment and adapt initiatives and various subprogrammes in these areas, such as now, on new opportunities for women, horizon, for disabled persons, youthstart, for promoting the labour market integration of young people, and integra, for groups threatened with exclusion.     9285.16912111777
unfortunately, this involves a significant reduction in appropriations.     3945.0827947972507
accordingly, the commission will use this initiative to develop what it considers to be new ways of fighting discrimination and inequality of all kinds in the labour market, based on the four pillars of the employment policy guidelines.     4293.78550032353
this is a great deal to build on such fragile foundations.     12403.874991876064
however, in order to give this new approach some chance of success, the commission should have opted for a less complicated proposal, a more simple and direct one, involving the ngos from the outset, so that partnerships could develop in a balanced way.     5336.783747625161
the commission did not do that.     1044.8441826800824
we hope that it will at least revisit the aspects that we have mentioned.     3283.094526859724
mr president, some of us in parliament will remember the successful film, "cocoon", in which some elderly people drank a special kind of water that made them young again.     2147.41413893167
in my view, it is, therefore, immensely important to exercise extreme vigilance over the water we all drink and, consequently, to study and approve the provisions referred to here.     5341.919180851512
for that reason, i approved these measures, but i should also like to express the hope that there will be particularly rigorous monitoring of water purity at the point where it enters our houses, to ensure that it is of the highest level.     2814.3733113441235
in the measures that have been adopted, i note that support for people who purify their water in their own homes has been omitted.     1580.562892805699
i trust that this will be remedied in the future.     3256.296395878657
apart from amendment no 76, which was an error, since i intended to vote in favour, the rest of my votes can be explained by means of the following fictional situation:
supposed draft environmental directive: "european parliament and council directive establishing the obligation to maintain, during winter, a temperature of no less than eighteen degrees centigrade in the streets, squares and parks of the cities of europe ".     7295.474098159448
guidelines of the directive: a) each city shall provide the means, in terms of energy and technology, sufficient for this objective to be achieved by 2012; b) the full costs shall be passed on to the inhabitants of each city by means of electricity prices.     4617.761787314107
opinion of an mep from the north of europe: "this directive would be unfair since, for example, the cost for an inhabitant of stockholm would be two hundred times greater than the cost for an inhabitant of naples ".     4203.073192287185
opinion of an mep from the south of europe: "nevertheless, this is a question of competitivity and equality of conditions".     5875.151615277397
(end of fiction).     499622.6172899903
in explaining my vote, i must say that it is positive that there is a community directive on water, but i believe that environmental dogma will not lead to the construction of europe, but will lead to legislative caricature.     2286.2175975019604
we cannot give equal treatment to countries which suffer from floods and droughts, such as the mediterranean countries, and countries with northern levels of rainfall.     7489.761682786813
furthermore, it would show a lack of solidarity to make transfers of water, between different river basins, practically impossible in the future.     10381.757180640732
once more we must remember that we are undergoing a continental project for political union.     7659.436630134542
.     1000.0
(fr) drinking, eating, washing, farming - water irrigates each act of our daily life and we treat it, wrongly, as an unfailing and inexhaustible resource.     6181.526795088776
a simple gesture such as turning on a tap seems to bring water straight from the source.     8627.131273770809
but the source is threatening to run dry.     18778.330892397466
scientific studies are now no more than a formality: water will be the main resource at stake in years to come, starting tomorrow.     2105.2900633549007
but tomorrow will be too late.     1504.2023808814522
we have to lay down the rules of play today, before we all lose.     4541.701048228705
in adopting the water directive on wednesday 15 february, the european parliament confirmed its wish to protect surface water, coastal wasters, inland waters and groundwater.     4957.300393834989
europe now has a framework which will allow it to prevent any deterioration in the quality of water, promote its sustainable use, protect eco-systems, help fight floods and droughts and eliminate the disposal of dangerous substances.     8862.196596052412
we have thrown water a life-belt.     15424.644819414194
.     1000.0
(fr) it is with the greatest satisfaction that i welcome this ambitious proposal for a framework directive on water policy.     4974.928765555083
the ecological disaster on the banks of the danube is a reminder, as if we needed one, that all too often human negligence jeopardises natural resources such as water.     4220.84681762041
the objective of this proposal for a directive, which dates back to 1997, is to put an end to the fragmentation of previous water legislation and protect surface water, coastal waters, inland waters and groundwater.     4130.831531657876
it provides a framework within which we can prevent any further deterioration, protect ecosystems, promote sustainable water use, help to fight floods and drought and gradually eliminate the disposal of dangerous substances.     12742.047674038296
specific directives will then be adopted within this framework.     2013.7616248105076
the procedure involved in drafting this text was lengthy.     8586.621054180405
the proposal for a directive was amended twice, first in 1997 and then in 1998.
a technical working party made up of council and parliament representatives met in january 1999 to try and reconcile the positions of the two institutions.     2647.4056110717092
however, the committee on the environment has tabled numerous amendments, most of which i support.     2371.469841270797
as far as the timetable and objectives are concerned, the council' s objective is to achieve good quality surface water within 16 years of the entry into force of the directive, while the committee on the environment, public health and consumer policy wants this reduced to 10 years.     5238.389897530315
the committee is also calling for an end to discharges or leaks of dangerous substances by 2020, the final objective being to achieve zero input by 2020.
the council hopes to achieve good quality groundwater within 16 years, but the commission wants no further deterioration in the chemical status of groundwater so that good quality groundwater can be achieved in 10 years.     7671.237953894499
all the standards and all the objectives relating to protected zones should be achieved within 10 years, not 16.
i must say that i support these reduced deadlines, especially as provision is made for derogations.     3695.656261318776
as far as costs are concerned, i approve the proposal whereby member states will have to ensure by 2010 that water pricing policies provide incentives for more sensible use of water resources.     7618.765667155984
i likewise expect the european commission to make a clear declaration undertaking to submit a proposal to ensure that water pricing policies reflect the environmental costs and the costs in terms of resources by 2012.
i will close by quoting one of the amendments tabled by the committee on the environment, which needs no further comment: "water is not a commercial product like any other but, rather, a heritage which belongs to the peoples of the european union and which must be protected, defended and treated as such" .     3452.5447201443667
.     1000.0
i wish to put on the official record my concerns with the lienemann report and also the reasons for my vote.     5455.583594265739
we have all agreed on the need to put in place proper controls for the protection of our water supplies and also for the users of water.     2511.704712639029
there is much to be praised in the approach to the problem suggested by the rapporteur.     2707.4000127234963
however, the question of taxation or charges for the use of water is a matter which, under the rules of subsidiarity, should be left to the member states for decision.     2357.855423880416
in my belief it is incorrect to use such a crude tool as a common european charge for this issue.     6760.771807107968
in ireland all the major users of water, commercial, industrial and agricultural, already pay for their use of water.     1858.1298367071108
this is a decision of national and local government, and this is where all future decisions should be retained.     1844.793741883268
these are the main reasons for my vote.     1913.6608541600626
.     1000.0
the european union is currently in the process of adopting new eu water legislation to update and to establish a long-term strategy to ensure the supply of clean drinking water and the protection of existing water resources including rivers and coastal waters.     2445.9195703806563
i support the objectives of this community action in the field of water policy.     2288.768637139904
however, i do not support the proposal that domestic water users be charged for water used.     3271.734323278061
water, in this context, is a commercial commodity.     6434.631880526226
it is an essential to life itself and people must have free access to sufficient supplies for normal daily life.     5296.200173890703
we see it as positive that an attempt is being made to reverse the trend towards water degradation, which is most alarming and which governments all too often tend to sweep under the carpet.     5031.528181477118
we all know that water is a commodity without which mankind cannot live and that it is imperative that its quality should be preserved.     4049.273190915011
but it is also essential to bear in mind that the various elements of water policy cannot be separated, and that action on this aspect will affect all the others.     3046.0619464365263
the "physico-chemical and ecological quality of water" was highlighted during the debate and in the proposals, and the adverse effects of other aspects was, at best, touched upon marginally.     3093.6766184183784
it is essential to be aware of the widely varying situations in different member states, such as specific climatic conditions, water regimes and fluctuations in them over the year, the social, economic and ecological effects of applying standards, and also of the failure to do so.     3120.389577572801
it was because of our concern about these issues that we disagreed with some of the proposals put forward by the committee on the environment, public health and consumer policy, and in particular those relating to an excessively restrictive timetable and to the scant attention paid to social and agricultural aspects, which are very important for a country like portugal, which suffers from water shortage problems and also inadequate socio-economic development.     2869.7256824088863
but we also believe that, in many cases, the proposals put forward by the commission and the council represent too much of a step backwards, which is why we would like to see a vote balanced between the various requirements in the proposals presented to us.     2231.1089588650343
.     1000.0
(fr) we are disappointed by the council' s common position on this proposal for a framework directive.     23439.90506429907
it is minimalist on a number of points, especially with regard to the dates of entry into force of far too many provisions and the number of, and conditions for, implementing the derogations.     6000.877597964024
however, the main problem is with the definition of the objective which the european union wishes to pursue with this text.     2982.0837508409404
the objective is of course to create, first and foremost, a clear general framework which will allow the law in this area to be simplified.     3136.3580270896873
however the union should be seeking, beyond that, to improve the quality of both surface and groundwater.     4595.941959367927
public health and the supply of drinking water are at stake, as are biological quality and diversity and the conservation of rural areas and species.     2388.018652947475
from this point of view, the common position is weak.     2756.804918186517
i should like to see our parliament defend a more ambitious position than the council' s position, but i should also like that position to be equally credible.     8782.1077368636
the "zero input" and "zero pollution" requirement, including for substances naturally present in water, is not realistic.     5031.396948317855
i am not one of those meps who thinks that it is essential to adopt "extremist" amendments at second reading with the sole aim of gaining room for manoeuvre in preparation for negotiations with the council of ministers.     8460.977898084802
on the contrary, i object to this systematically applied strategy.     15463.536655978041
it damages parliament.     92518.43418293489
our messages to our citizens and to the council are muddled and lack credibility.     10336.73253582782
overly strict, unrealistic environmental objectives will not be applied because they are unattainable.     4733.883972920468
is that the message we want to send out?     4699.058666158675
there is one other specific point which is dear to me: member states should be able, if they so wish, to set up trans-national water transfers.     3736.145228244202
this type of project complies with the principle of regional cohesion and solidarity, which we have always defended, and, as such, should enjoy the widespread support of the european union in the context of the promotion of trans-european networks and regional policy (through the structural funds).     2775.696230131581
i would like to remind the house that we adopted an own initiative report on the technical feasibility of trans-european water networks two years ago and i would be most obliged if the european commission could shortly tell parliament what action it has taken on that report.     4139.5144156203105
.     1000.0
(es) we have seen a report concerned with water quality in europe transformed into an instrument of commercial war.     8891.692335548163
we all agree that ensuring water quality is a hugely important objective, above all, given the adverse effects of developing a model of accelerated industrial growth on the natural environment.     5782.127976059028
it is clear that this model' s logic of private profit has been harmful, and therefore the 'polluter pays' principle is a suitable way of dealing with these consequences.     6478.7025341213675
however, apart from these principles, there is the consideration that water, as a common resource, cannot be subjected to the rules of the market.     2759.941108822789
surprisingly, i imagine with the best of intentions, this ecological objective has become an instrument of commercial war by making consumers, and especially the agricultural sector, responsible for the price of installations and the procedures for ensuring water quality.     4400.160579947807
in fact, water becomes simply one more product subject to the laws of the market.     2120.828151950266
this means that the neoliberal model now reigns in the field of water.     13976.105521899639
by failing to distinguish countries and regions where water is abundant, and is therefore not a problem, from regions where drought and low rainfall make it a scarce commodity, stifling the chances of any economic and social development, we are perverting the ecological principle and turning it into a weapon.     7003.181076916586
in the same way it is somewhat ironic that we are hindering the possibility of sharing this commodity, which is scarce in some areas, while regions where there is abundant water are giving advice on austerity with regard to its use, to those who live in drought areas.     8443.416428473694
as a result of this lack of sensitivity and solidarity, my vote has been consistent with what i have said, as an mep from one of the dry mediterranean agricultural regions - although one with prospects - namely, the region of murcia.     4014.8161360160734
.     1000.0
on behalf of the european parliament labour party, i wish to indicate that while we have supported the major part of the lienemann report, we have certain reservations.     10710.749017957274
the timetable set in the report of 2020 is unrealistic and impracticable, but the timetable set by the council of 2034 is too far in the distance.     4496.04044819798
we believe therefore that conciliation will set a more realistic date.     5044.718078953288
we have voted against amendments nos 25 and 72 which would require the achievement of minimal anthroprogenic pollution in all groundwater.     8202.402815939982
we believe this requirement is unrealistic in view of the very high cost and extremely long timescales involved in restoration to a near pristine standard.     3714.572996663142
the foregoing is the reason for our vote.     1245.6171651079176
.     1000.0
(fr) water is a renewable but limited natural resource and needs to be properly managed.     6214.70877770254
the framework directive on water policy will become the cornerstone of community policy in this area.     3912.67585560169
it has clear, ambitious objectives.     6433.382593596792
we share them.     3380.793517122284
it sets environmental parameters close to zero.     8210.809910480033
but we feel that it would be wiser not to set unrealistic deadlines which neither our governments nor our industries will be able to meet.     4983.868703389813
we need a timetable, by all means, but a more gradual timetable.     3618.9944338287705

such provisions may well cause serious problems in cross-border areas and in candidate countries, thereby complicating enlargement.     5138.152737089574

andersson report (a5-0033/2000)
the group for a europe of democracies and diversities has not been able to vote in favour of the andersson report.     14720.062797726483
this does, of course, contain a number of good observations.     19934.8058670054
for example, it is acknowledged on page 15 of the explanatory statement that "the different levels of development and traditions in the field of social security in the member states make it impossible to bring about uniform harmonisation of the member states' social security systems" .     4691.223184392691
now, the concept of "uniform harmonisation" is one of those muddled expressions which totally obfuscates the integration process in question.     7283.775976599496
it is also true, as it states in the explanatory statement, that harmonisation could be damaging as "it might place greater demands on weaker member states at the same time as competitive considerations might exert pressure on more socially developed countries to cut back on social security.     5052.757676634741
no-one would benefit from this type of harmonisation" .     4633.321810691049
the situation, however, is that a social model is being established whose real purpose is harmonisation, and uniform harmonisation into the bargain.     3869.203404782927
paragraph d points out that "the union may provide added value by adoption of proper binding and effective convergence criteria" , and the report goes on to emphasise that the development of this convergence process is to be promoted by means of joint talks between the ecofin council and the social affairs and employment councils.     4216.257742800216
finally, paragraph 7 emphasises that "the social convergence process must be accompanied by an effective and ambitious fiscal coordination" .     10316.656434130942
and, in this regard, we approach the heart of the matter.     5010.225854809159
this project, with its social harmonisation, does not constitute social policy.     4670.191833825613
instead, it is an extension to the eu' s emu project, which is of quite special interest to the three countries which remain outside emu and can rescue themselves from the sinking ship, namely great britain, sweden and denmark.     8875.058078740256
it is therefore important for us to emphasise that we cannot support a proposal whose essential rationale is to extend the emu project.
)     6115.788423764627
mr president, i voted for the council's strategy for modernising social protection because, in my view, it constitutes a step towards the establishment of european social and pensions legislation, although it does not actually go the whole way.     4846.250988362288
i was sorry to hear what was said in the previous explanation of vote, since i believe that, in essence, the report aims to provide much better protection for the elderly, despite the fact that, as we all know, the governments of the 15 member states would like to reduce pensions to the minimum.     4575.9392835299395
i hope that parliament will soon have the authority to legislate on pensions.     6336.0375467608055
the elderly are waiting for the european parliament to make it possible for them to live more comfortably and with greater dignity than they do now.     3629.3271367253424
