you will understand, mr president-in-office, that there was a point to my question, because the council is a political body of the european union and the citizens of europe, men and women, want to know how it views the democratic and social model of europe in the twenty-first century.     2111.748492337186

i would nevertheless like to say, with regard to the rights of the child, that we consider this to be an extremely topical issue, particularly for the council of europe and in view of the existence of a set of relevant international texts to which we have signed up.     2993.7584316882076
this matter should form part of the charter of fundamental rights.     7144.988092482122
this will certainly be the guideline that my government for one will advocate for the charter of fundamental rights.     3824.147599067744

i would remind members that, according to the guidelines for these sittings, questions must be concise and be worded in a way which will allow a brief answer.     5550.173264850595

subject: environmental problems caused by the drogheda port company development project, and the role of the court of auditors a major conflict between environmental conservation and structural development interests has arisen in the area of the boyne estuary, ireland, following a development project of the drogheda port company.     12458.239985593604

what kind of measures does the council plan to take in order to strengthen the review and evaluation powers of the european institutions in similar cases?     5051.3720612155375
.     1000.0
(pt) in response to your question, mrs mckenna, i would first of all like to highlight the fact that the role of the court of auditors was laid down in the treaty establishing the european community.     12763.188057065328
in accordance with article 246 of the treaty, the auditing of accounts is carried out by the court of auditors which, to this end, studies the accounts of all revenue and expenditure of the community as well as the regularity and the legality of the underlying transactions.     8136.34482699291
the court also guarantees proper financial management.     3814.8007312470386
this is indeed also stipulated in article 248.
the court of auditors, therefore, already has all the instruments necessary to carry out its role.     8547.921432829538
with regard to your suggestion for increasing the court' s resources, the council considers that all of the union' s institutions have, under the community budget, the necessary financial means for them to be able to properly carry out their respective remits.     10512.877177485689
in some cases, it would probably be desirable for their financial resources to be increased, but there are budgetary restraints of which i am sure you are aware.     3471.6237068051705
i would like to take this opportunity to remind you that the budget of the european union is approved by common agreement between the council and this parliament.     2269.5065020615634
the council therefore, has no means of changing the role conferred on the court of auditors by the treaty.     10322.99676693457
if extra resources were given to the court of auditors it would be money well spent, because it would ensure that eu funding was not going to something that conflicted with environmental concerns.     3184.5234415029913

address by mr vaclav havel, president of the czech republic
mr president in office, ladies and gentlemen, i have great honour in extending a warm welcome, on behalf of the house, to the president of the czech republic, mr vaclav havel.     86547.43468755433
i should also like to welcome mrs havel, who is sitting in the gallery.     7894.033246864721
you are most welcome.     3507.466034624139
(applause)
this is not your first visit to our parliament, mr president.     3034.9464022895736
you addressed plenary in strasbourg nearly six years ago, in march 1994, and many of us, who were already european members of parliament at that time, still have a vivid recollection of your speech.     2387.1635694479064
in fact, shortly after the maastricht treaty was ratified, you spoke in favour of strengthening european values, creating a european ethical dimension and opening the union to the countries of central and eastern europe.     8055.950951884527
in other words, for many citizens, and not just in your country, you personify the european values to which we constantly voice our attachment.     3972.9751746266866
there have been extremely dynamic developments in relations between the union and the czech republic following two european elections and a major new reform of the european treaties.     2997.580944650332
an association agreement has been signed and a joint parliamentary committee has been set up by the european parliament and the czech parliament.     1760.3747406980142
your country filed an official application to join the union in 1996 and accession negotiations were finally officially opened in 1998.
the process of european integration and enlargement has speeded up impressively since the fall of the iron curtain.     5610.963442204548
the end of this artificial separation of europe marked the start of a new era.     8073.759879639809
we now face an historical challenge with multiple prospects for all the citizens of europe.     6209.02190457633
mr president, you are a symbol of these changes.     4907.137543671792
you founded and signed charter 77, the human rights movement which represented and called for fundamental values during a dark period in the history of your people.     6765.694130620419
a communist regime sentenced you to five years in prison for defending human freedom and dignity.     9991.237293315378
but you never lost hope and history proved you right.     5177.257786871948
ten years ago, the "velvet revolution" proclaimed havel na hrad.     13060.405643153803
for ten years you represented democratic czechoslovakia and, later, the czech republic, as its president.     5839.636453015366
reconciliation with your neighbours was, and still is, a basic objective which you have defended with vigour and perseverance.     5735.707815439921
you are a first class european.     1153.843335719808
today, the czech republic is preparing to join our union.     12668.67385867811
i am sure that you will agree, mr president, and experience has shown us, that the process of enlargement of the union is not without its pitfalls for both parties and the czech republic will need to make a great deal of effort in order to meet the accession criteria.     4353.330070078032
the european union, for its part, will need to adapt its institutions and policies in order to prepare for enlargement.     8219.655061352612
it has already taken a big step forward with agenda 2000.
the next step, i.e.     2908.201074718337
institutional reform, will be taken following the inauguration of the new intergovernmental conference, in which the european parliament will be fully involved.     2411.2494279176926
our responsibility as members of the european parliament is to ensure that the process of enlargement takes place in an climate of optimum openness and transparency so that, when the time comes, the citizens of the european union and the citizens of the czech republic will approve the accession of the czech republic to the european union through their elected representatives.     3315.409601036003
mr president, it gives me great pleasure to give you the floor.     7838.203456483089
(applause)
mr president, allow me, on behalf of the european parliament, to extend our warmest thanks for the clear message which you have brought and of which the members have already shown their appreciation.     4298.31628330981
thank you.     2303.4935333465437
you are right to remind us that a national identity is perfectly compatible with a european identity.     2889.0518567646172
and you have used your personal experience to show us that your european identity was not decreed; it was a spontaneous and natural move.     2970.107925034207
i have taken note of your words; this is something which we all feel very deeply.     2361.514714825324
we find you faithful to the principles which have always guided you, which have always guided your actions; your attachment to fundamental values.     6439.165013776258
you reminded us of your speech in 1994, which you yourself qualify as slightly provocative, but there is such a thing as creative utopia and we have been able to gauge together how far we have come.     6695.659937717281
and you very opportunely highlighted the role of the civil society, the importance of the civil society, not just in the candidate countries, but in all the countries in the european union, in restoring our citizens' confidence, in restoring confidence in the social solidarity which we need.     2471.8245209108577
to summarise, perhaps what struck me most in your speech; above all, you wished to remind us that, beyond its economic aspects, the task of constructing europe is a spiritual one.     9604.61895352751
we of course look forward most keenly to continuing this task with you.     10193.824684385616
thank you, mr president.     971.9038500594517

acp-eu joint assembly
the next item is the annual report (a5-0032/2000) by mr corrie, on behalf of the committee on development and cooperation, on the results of the activities of the acs-eu joint assembly in 1999.
, rapporteur.- mr president, i had hoped to be rising at 9 a.m., not 9 p.m., to present this report.     23375.93628447945
i hope the number of members in this chamber is not an indication of the interest in overseas development work.     8548.107026932292
however, it is normal practice that the report on the work of the acp-eu joint assembly is put before members of this parliament and i have the pleasant task of doing that tonight.     2773.1604411287008
once again, i can report two highly successful assemblies having taken place, one in strasbourg and one in nassau.     4643.029704442469
in many ways this was the end of an old era and the start of a new one.     2896.275646698064
the acp-eu joint assembly is a unique organisation in world politics.     4276.913267486672
where else do 86 nations come together to discuss matters of mutual concern?     7477.0926978527805
the strasbourg assembly was the last chaired by lord plumb.     38144.20240483203
in honour of the tremendous work he carried out over a difficult period the joint bureau in nassau made him an honorary president, and we wish him well in his retirement.     7677.922807106156
the two assemblies have been dominated by the negotiations on renewal of the lomé convention and i look forward to the signature of a new convention and its rapid ratification by all the countries concerned.     6167.487337771758
i remember that at the last mid-term review, when i was rapporteur, the then commissioner marin said that review would be the last.     1471.0115303698233
well, he was wrong, and i pay tribute to philip lowe and his team who have worked so successfully behind the scenes to conclude a new convention in very difficult circumstances.     3970.348243879073
i was also delighted to hear the report this morning from commissioner nielson who has worked untiringly to get the right outcome to the negotiations.     2976.9680914410064
i pay special tribute to him for his success.     6761.661618514957
i also pay tribute to mrs kinnock and her working group for the huge amount of work they put in on the future of lomé.     4707.84500266388
i am quite sure that mrs kinnock' s report and the one on regional cooperation and integration had a major bearing on the outcome of these negotiations.     4377.197259515417
i also thank the working groups on the small island states and climate change and the follow-up group on fisheries for their excellent reports.     2303.0950155517085
we have had question times to the council and the commission and to the acp council, and i would like to thank those institutions for their interest in our work.     1687.9913136615698
the main thrust of our work in both assemblies was poverty eradication.     14242.677893524
the resolution passed went so far as to say that poverty amounted to a violation of human rights.     4191.232285311798
it also emphasised the importance of abolishing gender inequalities and as i travel through africa and see the work done by women i only wish there were more women in senior government positions and in presidential posts.     7282.7933805893945
we might then see an end to some of the fraud, corruption and mismanagement that seem endemic in some countries and that have to be stopped.     4039.6494108344978
we had, as usual, many debates on countries where there are war situations.     4029.8805735101614
i visited ethiopia and eritrea to gain first-hand knowledge.     51105.57974152001
the oau is doing its best to broker a peace deal there.     7300.197650993013
but ethiopia is still calling for clarification on certain issues and the humanitarian situation in both countries is serious.     3394.7660480732834
we were delighted to hear in nassau that a trade agreement was being reached with south africa after four years of negotiations.     8090.430356987599
this highlights the problems of coherence between the eu and the developing nations, which came to a head at the wto talks in seattle.     8371.462714284624
much work has to be done to convince the developing nations of the advantages of globalisation and world free-trade agreements.     6927.538970923714
in nassau, we had two interesting debates on east timor and cuba.     24191.89519223956
cuba has observer status but i understand has now shown a wish to join the acp.     16532.945655282783
all nations from within the confines of the acp territory who can live up to the requirements of democracy, human rights, governance and an independent judiciary are, i am sure, welcome.     7937.4403802272545
i await with interest developments in cuba.     64916.60114344486
when mr dalmau, the cuban junior minister for foreign affairs addressed the assembly, he did not hesitate to let his feelings be known.     23426.797201428788
on east timor, mr da costa, from the timorese national resistance council, gave a very moving address indicating that east timor would also like to join the acp when it becomes a state with a government.     9111.946640672739
we must give them all our support to reach that goal.     4127.53910112962
human rights is such an important issue within the acp-eu assembly that joint vice-presidents have been appointed with special responsibilities for monitoring human rights closely and reporting to the assembly.     4152.71279042861
a number of cases have been highlighted, including the child soldier issue in sudan, where many children have been abducted from neighbouring states.     21055.0307974656
i had the honour of being elected the european co-president at the beginning of this fifth term and mr clair from mauritius is the new acp co-president.     2294.7757142089354
i would like to see a number of changes to our assembly, particularly after the signing of the new convention in may.     5817.133908342864
we must have more equality between our acp partners and our european members and i want to get rid of the "them and us" syndrome.     6369.6930453970035
firstly, i would like to see a joint parliamentary assembly of elected members from acp countries and not ambassadors or government ministers sitting there as delegates.     6757.483586849312
this i think is the new convention.     2778.437186486013
secondly, the new convention allows for regional assemblies in the six regions, which would allow us to be much more specific in our work.     1932.9545711817452
these assemblies would report back to the joint assembly.     1756.3657848312835
we had a regional debate in nassau in the caribbean and this was extremely productive.     4023.089387955376
we are going to have one in abuja in west africa too.     1779.952677710213
i want the assembly to stop being just a talk-shop and become a work-shop for the betterment of all the people in developing countries.     2371.438958093174
i want to see civil society given a louder voice.     7369.002872362834
but to do that we have to see an end to corruption and we have to face this head-on.     2044.312135206612
we must reward those nations which are making the effort to live by the rules of good governance.     6324.968245101254
equally, we should come down hard on those who are corrupt or whose constitutions do not allow the pluralist governments that we demand.     3988.5378968858317
i am delighted to see the result in zimbabwe.     19185.37624214034
european taxpayers will no longer accept their taxes being fraudulently used.     22835.905399000483
a new wind of change must blow through africa and new standards must be set and lived up to.     4122.172364768696
the joint assembly should play a leading role in monitoring the enactment of good governance, human rights and democracy in cooperation with the commission.     6274.551070400136
i believe that as equal partners the eu- and acp-elected delegates are up to that task.     3563.1911369449217
i beg to move acceptance of this report.     33349.84082292259
mr president, let me begin by congratulating mr corrie on his excellent and comprehensive report.     7270.081074034444
our approach to development policy must always be based on respect for human rights and the fight against poverty.     2285.8097771105026
but there is a crass contradiction between fighting poverty and the cuts in development aid appropriations in many member states and in the eu.     2882.0376968891283
with regard to financing, we must look to the future and provide the joint assembly with additional funds so that, for instance, we can send delegations to the acp states to oversee electoral processes, at their invitation.     5918.475122271534
in future, the eu must take a far more multilateral approach when it agrees development projects and, furthermore, must coordinate more closely with the individual states in order to improve efficiency and use the funding more effectively.     3388.9524627695105
an example here could be the field of education, which will deserve and need special support in the acp states in future.     3207.6602123579714
for instance, the very rapidly growing educational and technological potential of the internet offers entirely new means of helping the developing countries, which at present still have modest resources, to move towards a global approach, as opposed to globalisation.     5522.891606493973
i see it as a future task of the eu to contribute in this way to the establishment of small and medium-sized enterprises in the acp states, which can promote self-help.     4456.181396999739
an example here is the problem in nigeria, which imports industrial finished products from all over the world and then has to spend substantial resources on product maintenance by foreign workers.     7547.978233977184
one way forward to promote the setting up of smes could be to train the nigerian population adequately.     3691.5618022977205
it will be easier to take account of the developing countries' very diverse problems, and eu aid could be prepared and implemented in a more targeted manner if, as the report makes quite clear, we take the urgently needed step of optimising our working methods, by introducing the proposed regional assemblies.     3124.6407139894477
thank you, mr zimmerling: if i am informed correctly, this is the first time you are addressing this house.     2485.86400221183
congratulations on your maiden speech: it went really well.     7858.846798334251
mr president, it cannot be repeated often enough: since 1975, the lomé convention has constituted an important pillar of european development cooperation and has a pilot role to play in many respects.     4324.385917705097
based as it is on the principle of partnership and equality, and on parliamentary control by the joint assembly, it is an example to the world.     2366.037396539942
most appropriately, given that this year we are celebrating 25 years of cooperation under lomé, we will shortly be signing a new and more advanced partnership agreement, which also upgrades the joint assembly into a genuine parliamentary assembly on both sides.     4220.426671133633
so i do not want to look back only to the year 1999.
the rapporteur, mr corrie, has already done so most expertly.     2178.0436755779033
much of what concerns us today is the outcome of fundamental processes of transformation in all parts of the world since the late 1980s.     4209.722400178537
they also had strong repercussions in the developing countries.     2891.9156742582195
the after-shocks can still be felt.     12029.313639003163
acp cooperation is a dynamic process.     4749.581277844608
the content of the agreements has progressed steadily between lomé i and lomé vi.     28776.78693933451
at the same time the number of partner states has increased considerably, now standing at 71.
all those involved were particularly moved by the enlargement to namibia and south africa.     5110.245648099385
cuba has just knocked at the door, and, if cuba is also accepted into the acp-eu community, that will be a momentous step and will bring in its wake processes of change in cuba itself.     8874.385716114355
so acp cooperation remains very exciting, and not only because of the new emphases introduced in the new convention.     3035.879319465211
fighting poverty and promoting sustained economic, cultural and social development are important objectives in the endeavour to build up democratic societies, based on respect for human rights, in the acp states.     4204.218976998774
that also means strengthening the democratic institutions.     2879.381525645225
the history of the joint assembly provides impressive evidence of a positive trend in that direction.     6983.90897434948
in the past, the representatives of the acp countries tended to be government members, government officials or ambassadors; but today at least three quarters of the acp delegates are elected members of parliament.     8799.933234769294
we ought to throw a big party when the figure reaches 100% in the near future, which is indeed what the new convention is aiming at.     5773.582881843006
unfortunately there is far too little appreciation of the steady advance of processes of democratisation which, unlike the various crises that arise, largely pass unnoticed.     16872.544812421576
one special aspect of multilateral cooperation with the acp states is regional cooperation and integration.     4348.366788365531
there is one wish on the european parliament' s long wish list that will be fulfilled: as we have already heard, the joint assembly will soon also be able to hold regional assemblies.     7056.960530807588
enhanced regional cooperation can help prevent the outbreak of conflicts based on ethnic, economic, social or religious factors, i.e.     7768.245468480465
have a preventive effect.     9341.254808896349
for the same reason the european parliament has always called for civil society to be involved in the decision-making processes and in cooperation management, and emphasised the importance of decentralised cooperation.     2037.5806988562222
the enhanced contacts between the joint assembly and the social partners provided for in the new convention are another step in the right direction.     2767.7579232266194
but i am a little sad that the european development fund has still not been incorporated in the community budget.     1820.9561461737476
i hope it does not take another 25 years before we finally achieve that too.     2833.244619290951
mr president, i congratulate mr corrie on his report.     8530.950840480256
the lomé convention has been an expression of a real partnership between the eu and the acp countries for over 25 years.     2703.711971345265
that it has continued at all in the face of overwhelming pressure against it from many quarters, including the wto, is a triumph of sorts.     7561.78688752734
but of course the new convention must be measured not by the fact that it exists but by the extent to which it can contribute to the eu commitment to poverty eradication and the achievement of the international development targets.     3110.9996056397226
measured by this yardstick there are a number of serious shortcomings in the new arrangements.     13726.791233446605
for example, the agreement is particularly disappointing for those acp countries suffering from short-term fluctuations in export earnings.     19214.584982497403
during a marathon negotiating session in december a compromise was reached which provided that, in respect of least-developed, landlocked and island states, assistance would be provided in the event of a minimum 2% loss of export earnings rather than the level of 10% for the other acp states.     4711.571665462712
but at the final negotiating session the eu claimed that it had never actually agreed to the inclusion of islands and landlocked states among those which would qualify for the support on the basis of the 2% threshold.     4354.648483912796
this change has been devastating for the windward islands in particular which may be the only acp state which will actually lose massively from the new arrangements.     7480.832320209319
this seems a particularly perverse outcome since the windward islands are one of the smallest and most vulnerable of the acp states and they are now going to have to pay the highest price for this new lomé arrangement.     4512.106605263088
meanwhile, on the trade side, pressure from the wto to ensure that any lomé convention is wto-compatible has meant that there is a huge momentum behind the idea of the so-called regional economic partnership arrangements or free-trade agreements.     6815.814101443705
non-least-developed countries are likely to face considerable pressure to accept these arrangements.     8488.907017800899
yet it is quite clear that even after a transitional period a number of countries will simply be unable to open their markets yet to the chill winds of free trade without wrecking their own domestic economy.     3680.081271633586
we therefore need to be particularly vigilant to ensure that genuine options are open to those countries which are not yet ready for free-trade arrangements.     2749.176440535215
we need to ensure that strategies for promoting regional integration within the acp are pursued in their own right.     3610.8087232595576
finally, during any new trade round the eu and the acp states should press jointly for a reform of gatt article 24 in order to explicitly provide regional non-reciprocal trade agreements between structurally and economically dissimilar groups of countries.     8845.39357125288
the lomé convention is a unique partnership; but if it is to be truly effective it needs to extend beyond the discussions within the context of lomé and involve a participation of the wto and a partnership in all other international negotiating fora.     4905.868216586119
mr president, what i want to do in this debate is condemn the fate reserved for the poor countries of africa, the caribbean and the pacific.     6497.054455652006
people have the gall to say they benefit from the lomé convention, but even looking at trading relations alone, the lomé convention has done nothing to protect those countries; their already feeble share in trade with europe has been halved.     2816.2609086836655
that reflects the growth of inequality between eu and acp countries.     8670.471601970363
the lomé convention essentially benefits a few large industrial or financial conglomerates, which continue to pillage those countries and perpetuate their economic dependence, notably on the former colonial powers.     12715.677690775314
when it comes to so-called development aid, given that underdevelopment in the majority of these countries is getting worse rather than better, development is clearly not what the aid is for, even when it is not just a disguised export subsidy for european manufacturers.     2508.556320103405
the aid is primarily a windfall for incumbent regimes, and the poor majority does not benefit at all.     3055.21353715507
even if it were properly redirected this aid would not be enough to finance the most basic infrastructure for health care, education or drinking water supply.     1885.427280582343
for some years now the great powers have been reducing the amount of this already derisory aid, and it is the height of cynicism when this is done on the pretext of human rights violations or corruption.     4633.868788293325
after all, who protects these deeply corrupt regimes, if not the governments of the rich countries?     7196.582227779669
they turn a blind eye to the masters of corruption, the big groups controlling oil, water supply, or public works.     3523.8833305705684
they could clamp down on them since their head offices are here in europe.     2077.1452834134593
the debate about either extending the lomé convention with its quotas and protectionism, or, instead, gradually winding down the special relationship between the european union and the acp countries in the name of free trade, is a warped debate between two ways of perpetuating the pillage and impoverishment of those countries.     4088.1010495194287
i protest against an ignoble and inhuman organisation which literally condemns hundreds of millions of human beings to a wretched death to enrich a minority.     6081.773432915282
a mere fraction of the fantastic wealth accumulated by the big groups could lift these poor countries out of extreme poverty, but, until we have the courage to take on those big capitalist groups, we are just covering up the real problems.     8158.03689543044
mr president, i want to thank the co-president of the joint acp-eu assembly, mr corrie.     5949.322905571603
it was no easy task to draw up a report on a complicated forum like the acp-eu assembly.     7793.299693156013
in this speech, i will confine myself to addressing certain points i consider fundamental.     11072.058664004846
i believe the european parliament should call for the assembly to become truly parliamentary, urging all acp countries to send members of their national parliaments as their representatives, so as to strengthen their own democratic fabric.     5602.398168445631
my next contribution to the debate is to turn my attention to globalisation and the wto, which is seen by the acp countries as an enormous danger to their development.     5093.151291068763
i do believe the acp countries need a transition period, but they also need support in the context of the new logic of regionalisation of aid, not only in agriculture and fisheries, but also in the exploitation of mineral and underground resources, some of which are being exploited, with slim economic returns to the acp countries themselves.     3288.945540907134
thirdly, the european parliament - through a joint working group within the joint assembly - must make a major effort to achieve stability in acp countries' budgets through the selection of budgetary items to permit economic and, above all, social development, and by monitoring income and fiscal policies in force in the acp countries.     5271.551789851333
we must put the people of the acp countries first in our relationship with their rulers, who need to be fully aware that development aid from the european union is intended for the people and not for the oligarchies that handle all the internal and external resources in some countries.     1999.745517487907
fourthly, and finally, the prevention of inter-ethnic conflicts between states must be our constant concern and to some extent this can be achieved either through a strong and authoritative diplomatic offensive or with the proposals i have set out.     4837.078587437584
mr president, i agree with the positive evaluation mrs junker has just made and i want to add that mr corrie has started his term as co-president of the acp-eu joint assembly with effective and impartial work deserving of our full appreciation.     4617.125232959483
i shall restrict my speech to one particular act which took place a few days ago, partly as a consequence of events alluded to in the report we are debating here.     5778.727274448223
i refer to cuba' s expression of intent, of 2 february, to become a full member of the acp group of countries.     19415.090182923417
a cuban delegation attended the last meeting of the acp-eu joint assembly in nassau, and vice-chancellor dalmau spoke in the plenary session, engaging in a lively debate, and afterwards held conversations with many of us.     20870.524970426843
following nassau, a delegation from parliament led by mr corrie and the chairman of the committee on development and cooperation, joaquín miranda, visited havana and a series of very illuminating exchanges of opinion took place.     13199.205868166377
it is my impression that this mission from the european parliament was the final push which convinced our cuban friends that the time had come to move forward on mechanisms of cooperation with the european union.     4372.251063527093
we are witnessing events of great political significance and i hope that we in the european parliament can promote the appropriate favourable view and a generous attitude on the part of the union, so that cuba' s aspirations can meet with a positive response as soon as possible.     7427.584967189072
that is important, for at least three reasons.     2440.982251797495
first, it is important for cuba, to bring its people development and prosperity and finally break the united states blockade, which has been repeatedly condemned by the european parliament and the united nations.     5055.978566792882
secondly, we must bear in mind that cuba' s application has the support of all the acp countries.     15373.856255581666
if the european union responds positively to the recommendation of our associates, we will be proving our respect and consideration for those associates and this will have positive effects on the whole framework of our cooperation with them.     5163.975552762862
nor can we ignore the value of such a decision in demonstrating the consistency and autonomy of the european union and the fact that it does not bow to pressure from any quarter.     7857.437314139919
by accepting cuba into the acp community we will be demonstrating that our europe is not involved in the shameful process of 'playing but not paying' , and that, when we get indignant about absurdities like the helms-burton act, we are not being hypocritical but are ready to act and take all the consequences.     2413.072539264638
the acp-eu joint assembly meets again in a few weeks time and i am sure this subject will be dealt with there.     3434.700864787449
ideally the european parliament' s representatives should be able to support cuba' s intent at those proceedings, as our parliamentary colleagues from the acp countries will no doubt be doing.     30687.6204102646
of course, cuba must meet all the standard conditions, just like any other member.     6094.947615233135
the cubans know that and i am sure they accept it.     7810.924008068915
it must also be made clear that cuba is not going to be asked for more than has been asked of our other partners.     1900.584443791778
cuba' s wish to join is welcome and cuba will be welcome in our circle.     72698.81308687931
i say that wholeheartedly, but also with the deep conviction that, just as the blockade has produced nothing but poverty and, as a consequence of that, extreme tension and a hardening of the cuban position, the integration we are supporting here, and the prosperity it will produce, will translate into the détente and openness we all seek and from which all of us, especially the cuban people, will benefit.     3714.065862918571
mr president, i also want to begin by thanking and congratulating the rapporteur, mr corrie, for an excellent report which i think very well describes and summarises the work which has been done in the acp-eu joint assembly in the course of 1999.
i do not have very long in which to speak, and i really only want to take up two questions.     2653.35299120741
the first is about the fight against poverty, which i think is becoming an embarrassment both for the eu and the member states.     2928.5171639556374
in spite of the fact that, both in the treaty and at the un' s meetings in rio and copenhagen, there was an undertaking to work to eradicate poverty in the world, the budget for development aid is, in actual fact, being cut back, when the express desire was that exactly the opposite should happen.     8735.898826912855
we must put a stop to this trend.     4926.430562131927
we must begin by writing off debts.     19127.257431471156
we must follow up words with action.     4441.176433118444
i should like us to take a leading role in this work.     5167.423077743366
my second question concerns the fact that, in many contexts, we talk about the acp countries having to learn the principles of democracy.     7285.840843716581
paragraphs 2 and 3 of the resolution demand, within the context of the new agreement, that working methods should be developed and optimised.     2232.879469063144
the acp countries are to be urged to allow a whole range of political opinions to come up for discussion.     6757.00517151696
i think that is a very good idea indeed, and i fully subscribe to it.     2345.7332209977912
however, we must also ask ourselves about our own democratic arrangements.     2164.7577501950564
how do these operate within the european parliament' s delegation to the acp-eu' s joint assembly?     28448.904477150216
in contrast to how things are done here in the european parliament and in parliament' s committees, where rapporteurs are nominated and appointed to produce reports, there are no regulations whatsoever applied within this delegation to the joint assembly.     6125.989112208826
the two large groups share all the reports and consultative documents etc between themselves.     4429.002157642365
in order to change this undemocratic arrangement, we, the group of the greens/european free alliance, have tabled amendment no 6 in which we request that the rapporteurs appointed to produce reports and consultative documents, together with the members of working parties, should be appointed in accordance with clear, democratic rules.     11773.994531168744
in this context, account ought to be taken of the smaller groups, and consideration given to introducing more representative arrangements.     4064.8062932153507
mr president, commissioner, firstly, i too would like to congratulate mr corrie on the quality of his report, which appropriately reflects the tenor of the debates in the joint assembly he so effectively co-chaired.     4002.8563877585384
the eradication of poverty was central to our work and many speakers highlighted the need to preserve an essential tool for achieving that ambitious aim, to whit, preferential agreements between the european union and the acp countries within the framework of the lomé conventions.     8534.776235238069
the challenge was immense.     26663.59423952147
globalisation, free trade, and the hegemony of the wto with its internationalist philosophy, were imperilling the very essence of the north-south dialogue.     2807.443007594791
so we can only applaud the conclusion of a new partnership agreement for development, and personally i am convinced that the work of the last joint assembly did have a decisive influence on breaking the deadlock in the negotiations, as did the new climate created by the events at seattle.     4138.220635552577
however, while i welcome the renewal of the agreements, what i find most interesting is the new wind blowing through our relationships and finding expression in the balanced nature of the negotiations now drawing to a close.     6972.1660435552685
the european union seems to me to have finally emerged from the old paternalist, or frankly neo-colonialist, logic where those giving aid are all too eager to start giving lessons, following a formula our friend michel rocard is so keen on.     8259.57814639851
the union has finally accepted the idea that dialogue cannot be genuine or effective unless it is the expression of a contract which respects the dignity of the partners.     4944.104293256077
by refusing to allow the idea of good governance to become a tool, in the hands of technocrats, for imposing new sets of conditions, the european union is demonstrating wisdom and modesty.     14373.999650938946
we have spent two centuries laying the foundations of a democracy whose fragility is constantly brought home to us by events.     11834.135623378925
what right have we to try to impose our model on people whose history, values and identity are sometimes so profoundly different from our own?     4747.038474138362
personally, i prefer the willingness that is clearly flagged up in these agreements to extend the partnership to new agents - local authorities, non-governmental organisations - which seems to me to represent the only possible way the people of these countries can progressively achieve ownership cooperation.     4521.112884014755
in short, this is the only way they can emerge from humiliating and childlike dependence on aid and take responsibility for themselves, the proof of success.     3322.942574993325
mr president, commissioner, firstly, i too would like to congratulate mr corrie on the accuracy and rigour of the summary in his report.     8835.277879605694
the european parliament' s opinion on the activities of the acp-european union joint assembly has always had a major impact, undeniably contributing to a consistent policy and role for the european union in development and cooperation.     4299.514733468696
the important role of the increasingly parliamentary joint assembly in strengthening relations between the european union and the acp countries needs to be stressed.     3720.411823850683
in view of this positive action, there is every advantage to be gained from according the joint assembly a budget compatible with improving the quality of its work.     5979.71736011525
we back the rapporteur' s proposal to introduce regional parliamentary assemblies, under the aegis of the joint assembly, in the six regions of the convention: the caribbean, the pacific and four african regions.     8428.621912659526
these regional assemblies would monitor action in their respective areas.     4851.803710547739
poverty has been the common denominator of all the debates in the joint assembly and its eradication is the priority for the european union' s development policy.     4620.5210088391195
in line with the resolution at the nassau meeting, we must concentrate on meeting the poverty reduction targets agreed by the international community.     7596.355147552584
consistency and action are needed.     4208.990086076997
the next lomé convention must specify the investment in education and stimulate regional cooperation and the participation of local bodies.     9496.534063553341
the participation of local bodies is absolutely essential.     5023.006461533744
in a wider context, the priorities, as i see it, are to convert poor countries' debt into programmes for poverty eradication and sustainable development, as well as control of fraud and corruption.     3096.8387599860703
we need to make progress with concrete and consistent action.     3685.1630313623755
people in the poor countries are beginning to despair.     9357.643056322147

(da) mr president, i should like to take this opportunity to thank the rapporteur for his excellent report and also to thank the joint assembly for its work in 1999.
i am also delighted to be able to confirm that i shall be participating in the joint assembly' s next meeting, which takes place in march in nigeria.     6598.565839344891
the commission is pleased to see the joint assembly playing a larger role, especially in connection with promoting a more extensive political dialogue with our partners in development.     6366.636243192387
the commission agrees with the rapporteur that combating poverty continues to be the central objective for development policy and will always play a key role.     3484.4016910143446
in order to be able to develop, the countries concerned must, however, become actively involved in the global economic system.     5114.750655485196
with regard to trade with our partners in development, the commission is quite prepared to guarantee that any new arrangement will take account of the acp countries' special economic and social limitations.     5092.783279038046
mrs lucas mentioned that the stabex and sysmin system ought to be continued with.     1984.5213656574726
i do not agree.     4924.588873210631
that system has proved itself to be inappropriate because it is, in actual fact, of use only to relatively few of the 71 countries concerned.     2116.668242051496
it operates too slowly, and it therefore stands to reason that the developing countries should have said yes to the new system of the future, because this will operate more flexibly.     2397.147895255447
regarding the future accession of countries such as east timor to the lomé convention, i can only repeat what i recently said to the european parliament' s committee on development and cooperation, namely, that the door is open.     8692.14559040253
it is the applicants themselves who are to decide whether they want to knock on the door.     6405.824558409241
once they have done so, we shall adopt a position on the matter.     3021.5423905573307
with regard to cuba' s application for accession to the lomé convention, the commission notes that, according to their recent declaration, the acp states support cuba' s application.     45479.822347091766
