i saw the newly-elected president georgievski a few weeks ago and i was extremely impressed, as others have been, by his commitment to multi-ethnicity and pluralism.     7940.816269386117
let me set out where matters now stand on these negotiations to which - as mr lagendijk said - other countries in the region are looking with very considerable interest.     3554.031430236214
the council adopted on 24 january this year its negotiating directives for a stabilisation and association agreement with the former yugoslav republic of macedonia.     37092.618442921295
this represents the most important step forward in the stabilisation and association process of the western balkans region initiated by the commission in may 1999.
let me make clear the main elements of those draft directives.     6826.394403115921
they cover the inclusion in the agreement of arrangements establishing a political dialogue with fyrom.     13588.304521948641
they cover provisions for enhanced regional cooperation including the prospect of establishing free trade areas between the countries of the region, though i must make it clear that there is no question of any attempt to recreate the state of yugoslavia.     4995.720452116777
they cover the prospect of the establishment of a free trade area between the european union and them within ten years of the entry into force of the agreement.     4686.042090169374
they cover provisions on the movement of workers, freedom of establishment, supply of services, current payments and movement of capital.     9162.611612809616
they cover a commitment by fyrom to approximate its legislation to that of the european community, notably in key areas of the internal market; and they cover provisions on cooperation with fyrom in a wide range of fields, including justice and home affairs.     6443.761230195392
we are now making in the commission the necessary preparations so that negotiations can start in march.     1978.3954536993224
i am due to visit fyrom in the second week in march.     16060.467627822023
i hope to be able to set the process in motion then, although the formal negotiations will start very shortly thereafter.     10270.161623006543
at the time of that visit i will also, as recommended by honourable members and others, be upgrading our representation in fyrom.     9314.115233220495
the target date for concluding the negotiations is december this year but this will depend to a very large extent on the goodwill and determination of the negotiators.     5988.77835403749
the agreement could enter into force in three to four years from now, given the time needed for ratification in member states' parliaments.     8312.845116105627
i should like to comment very briefly on a number of other points in the draft resolution.     3212.300899786789
we accept that the agreement should to a certain extent serve as an example for other such agreements, although obviously we will also want to take account of the fact that we are dealing with different countries, each of which has its own identity and its own concerns.     2391.6232559782156
i share the wide-spread concern that we should treat each country on its merits and not shackle everyone to the pace of the slowest in the development of relations with the union.     3536.906271195565
that has never been the intention.     1109.327528810208
the very fact that negotiations are beginning first with fyrom, before all the others, is evidence that we can and do differentiate.     7037.1449433469725
on the provision of regional aid referred to in the report we agree that our technical and financial assistance should go in part to projects having a cross-border or regional dimension.     2787.158234002411
we agree that there should be an evolutionary clause on the prospect of eu membership contained in the stabilisation and association agreement.     11059.22988623756
it will reflect the european union's position on its relations with the countries of the western balkans as set out in the cologne european council conclusions.     6499.5184640658945
we strongly agree on the need to decentralise our assistance programmes to the maximum possible extent.     6153.866517572581
as for the arrangements covering european union assistance to the western balkans, members will be aware that we are drawing up a new regulation to simplify and streamline their management and to put everything under one regulation.     2779.764752835713
i am aware that there is concern in fyrom and elsewhere at the end of the phare programme in these countries and, especially, at the loss of association with the phare name.     7955.823960279886
i note with considerable concern what members say on this score.     6554.457238311012
i will take another look at the issue, although i should point out that having two phare programmes up and running risks real confusion.     4726.948430324166
i want to stress, however, that whatever the programme is called, our commitment to the integration of fyrom and its neighbours into the european family is stronger than ever.     6989.272069334326
in general i trust that members of this house will share my pleasure that macedonia has reached this stage relatively rapidly.     14219.35136869161
i hope that the other countries of the region will redouble their efforts to make similar progress.     1398.1756293554513
i look forward to many similar debates to this over the next few years marking, i hope, the success of what we are trying to do in the balkans which, for me, remains the single most important litmus-test of what europe is able to do on its own doorstep.     4901.614446153839
thank you very much, commissioner patten.     728.8734161947891
the debate is closed.     4906.850855896859
the vote will take place tomorrow at 12.00 p.m.
agreement on comitology
the next item is the report (a5-0021/2000) by mrs frassoni, on behalf of the committee on constitutional affairs, on the agreement between the european parliament and the commission on the implementation of the new council decision of 28 june 1999 on comitology (1999/468/ec).     34830.99256087732
.     1000.0
(it) mr president, the approval of this agreement with the commission on procedures for implementing the council decision on comitology marks the end of a saga which has kept parliament busy for two years, along with a few enthusiasts scattered among the various institutions of the union, whom many people do not hesitate to call masochists.     3757.090718234385
until 1993, the way the commission exercised its executive powers, and in particular the mysterious and even suspect committee arrangements, was creating conflict and mistrust between the institutions, and threatening to have serious consequences on the legislative procedure.     5325.18663597325
that is why, in accordance with declaration no 31, annexed to the treaty of amsterdam, the union institutions have undertaken a complex task of negotiation and consultation, resulting in the new council decision, which revises the old system quite substantially, although we are not entirely satisfied with it.     5159.9946042268475
in particular, the decision outlines a form of parliamentary control whereby parliament can sound the alarm if an implementing measure, deriving from an act adopted under the codecision procedure, oversteps the implementing powers delegated to the commission.     7405.768426661371
during the protracted interinstitutional negotiations - which saw parliament actively involved, thanks to the work of the rapporteur, adelaide aglietta - it was understood that the european parliament and commission would conclude an interinstitutional agreement on the procedures for implementing certain aspects of the new decision.     3037.143167513102
on the one hand, certain points which the council did not intend to incorporate into the decision were to be made explicit and, on the other, the system for providing information about implementing measures and monitoring them was to be organised more precisely.     2187.6772196983648
during the trialogue held on 6 october 1999 in strasbourg, the presidents of the european parliament and the commission approved the idea of concluding this agreement soon.     3449.9678321850242
we believe we have respected that commitment, and indeed this was made relatively easy by the climate of trust and mutual respect evident throughout the negotiations with the commission.     5231.758244650622
essentially, the agreement deals with two points: one is the right of the european parliament to be informed, which is the sine qua non for the genuine exercise of its right to intervene pursuant to article 8 of the decision.     3462.24547489771
hitherto, such information had been supplied on paper and unsystematically, and was of no use to our services; and indeed this was often the fault of parliament itself.     921.4258698568628
from now on, the information will be provided by means of an electronic system called circa, to which the european parliament will have access.     1600.203068544558
this represents a real innovation, not only for us, but also - i am sure - for the commission.     1991.7783596540826
the other point is that article 8 of the council decision does not specify either the method by which the european parliament is to sound the alarm, or the time limit within which it must exercise its restricted power of control.     3507.4243876118107
paragraphs 6 and 7 of the agreement confirm that, as a general rule, the european parliament will adopt its resolution, stating its reasons, at a plenary sitting and has one month to exercise its right of intervention before the commission adopts the draft implementing measure.     4985.3252362222665
but we all realise that it is not always possible to wait a month before implementing a measure.     2406.3515703283792
therefore, the agreement contains explicit provisions on urgent procedure, including possible action by the committee responsible.     3285.1546958464646
i want to end by stressing that we cannot assess whether this is a good agreement or not, we must wait and see how it works in practice.     4511.044091217513
the european parliament needs to acquire the resources, which it currently lacks, to ensure efficient monitoring of implementing measures, and the commission must accept that some of its practices need reorganising to provide proper information to parliament and ensure that control can be effective.     3150.755755978493
finally, it needs to be borne in mind that - whatever happens - the real solution to the problems of comitology for the european parliament is still to change the procedure for implementing legislation laid down in the treaties, and gradually to dismantle the committees whose existence represents an anomaly that severely restricts the commission' s executive power and may prejudice the european parliament' s legislative powers.     6052.0950771529215
mr president, comitology is a concept most people do not know how to handle and hardly anyone knows what it means.     4394.250934858222
it makes them think of secret societies and conspiracies, which is the way things used to be.     3310.8166018006573
in fact, it implies nothing other than fixing the procedures for the exercise of the implementing powers conferred on the commission.     3745.2869742233775
the procedures for exercising these implementing powers take the form of a limited number of committee procedures.     6704.155302669553
up until the amsterdam treaty, there were about 20 variations of these committee procedures, which created neither clarity in the implementation of legislation nor transparency for the citizens.     4661.442903933361
on the contrary, for a long time even the european parliament was not informed.     1086.2751321242054
for the european parliament, the first step in the struggle for more transparency in this jungle of committees was to be informed, at least, of all that the european commission intended to regulate in the context of these various committees.     2613.3129081007482
agreement was reached in 1988 in the form of an exchange of letters between lord plumb and mr delors.     18425.904198548207
a further step towards transparency and the involvement of the european parliament came with what is known as the modus vivendi of 20 september 1994, which granted parliament the right not only to be informed but also to intervene in certain cases.     3938.7780904641186
it also gave it the possibility of scrutiny.     4873.741853058809
with the amsterdam treaty, the situation improved decidedly when the number of committee procedures was drastically reduced - as the european parliament had long wanted - to three procedures, the administrative procedure, the regulatory procedure and the advisory procedure.     2971.415534587151
now, following the amsterdam treaty, the rights of information and control granted to the european parliament in the past need to be enshrined anew in an interinstitutional agreement.     1777.8502889671756
mrs frassoni, our rapporteur, has done very careful work and identified the questions that have to be clarified in the context of that interinstitutional agreement and the administrative procedures that may have to be changed within parliament.     5138.7490280768425
a key problem is the time factor.     2957.1608343682387
when the european parliament is informed, it generally has to adopt a position within the very short timeframe of four weeks, should it so wish.     2550.368067904023
in matters relating to the health and safety of the people of the european union, the european commission, as guardian of the treaties and the only institution with the right of initiative, must be in a position to react very rapidly, if necessary within a few hours or days.     2483.665206648742
nevertheless, the european parliament must retain the right to be informed and where appropriate also to react.     2382.2142103667866
our rapporteur identifies very useful solutions to these difficulties, which i and my group can only support in full.     4314.505229347721
i just have one more request.     1014.1336453998131
in the german version, paragraph 2 of the resolution has been translated rather misleadingly and i urgently ask for clarification, which i would also like to see in the printed version later.     2414.471506358433
in my view, instead of "weitergeführt werden müssen" the wording should be "erhalten bleiben müssen" .     29110.531672253328
the german version is not very clear here.     1382.1518895882473
i would ask for that to be checked again.     3266.5013060547353
for the rest, i warmly thank our rapporteur.     9460.411284060523
it is a difficult subject and i hope we have managed to make it somewhat easier to deal with now.     4110.4717344654655
mr president, i congratulate the rapporteur on her report.     3759.097710781465
i must add that it is a welcome change to see mrs frassoni in such a conciliatory mood and willing to reach compromises with the other institutions.     13164.414246046224
when it comes to the igc, she is one of what the french call "les pures et dures" : no compromise, no deals with the other institutions; we would rather see the whole thing blocked than compromise our positions.     2996.539830200593
yet here she is willing to accept the steps forward that have been achieved, which are far from matching parliament' s original position on the issue of comitology.     4537.736665234436
let us remember what is at stake here.     13011.665891595103
parliament had four main criticisms of the comitology system as it existed prior to the new council decision supplemented by this interinstitutional agreement.     3854.352400439416
firstly, the whole system is obscure.     7640.719676973071
it is not transparent with hundreds of committees meeting with secret agendas and nobody knowing who is on the committees.     9464.070650939953
here is a real step forward, in the new system that has been agreed.     1016.5819928679549
we are to know who is on each committee.     2523.8401111329986
we are to know when they meet.     2923.592902484069
we are to get the agendas.     6435.340426023676
we are to get the documents that are sent to them.     1572.5480596942223
the whole system will be more open and transparent - if still rather complex.     1656.0288286559598
so that is at least a step forward.     1162.210715540979
our second criticism is that the system was very restrictive of the commission.     1988.9395098127868
we adopt legislation in the european union.     2504.707143244577
we expect the commission to implement it.     3353.4218470866326
and then we have a system designed to hinder the commission and make it more difficult - especially what was called the "contre-filet" system, under which the council could block the commission by a simple majority even when the council was not capable of finding an alternative to the implementing measure concerned.     2367.114922448852
here too there is at least some progress.     2745.6374416465105
the council will no longer be able to block implementing measures indefinitely beyond the three-month period unless it has a qualified majority to block it, in other words, unless there is substantial opposition among the member states represented in the council.     6824.788893467253
that is a more reasonable system.     907.2377012772652
that, too, is a step forward.     1091.7970956108113
but on our other two criticisms, we are less satisfied.     3835.1137748388205
the first criticism is of the system whereby the commission is monitored, scrutinised, controlled if you like, only by a committee appointed by member states or the council not by parliament.     3957.9736744577563
the legislative authority, council and parliament, should be equal.     1470.013908857242
we confer implementing powers on the commission, but then it is only a council or member state appointed body that can blow the whistle and say no to the commission and stop the implementing measure.     2937.5125557874494
parliament has no equivalent power.     7046.3083817860515
true, we are now given the beginning of such a power in this new system.     3176.0319381648483
we are to receive all the draft implementing measures forwarded to a committee at the same time as it is sent to the committee.     1596.3031802479838
we will have the chance to scrutinise, to debate, to question.     5457.935824448742
but we will only have the right to formally challenge the commission if we think it has gone beyond the delegation that we have given to the commission in the legislation.     2261.268052134266
in other words, we can challenge the measure for being ultra-vires but not on its content.     2498.2756889584493
and in a democracy, parliament should be able to challenge the content.     7305.2373859854115
not that we do so every week.     1299.9937804949702
we are not seeking to immerse ourselves in implementing decisions, but it is a democratic safeguard that we should be able to do so on those few occasions when it really would be necessary.     2540.7237339702947
that is lacking in this new system, or at least is only there in embryonic form.     2338.447640455304
finally, our fourth criticism is that if an implementing measure is blocked through the comitology system it is referred back, not to both branches of the legislative authority, but to the council alone, which has the right possibly to take an alternative measure.     3170.874738422019
that is not correct.     2392.6252374146443
both branches adopt the legislation - council and parliament - that confers implementing powers on the commission.     6455.218013337056
if such implementing measures are referred back, it should be to both branches, not to one branch alone.     3406.7048742942493
overall we have a system which has made some progress.     1876.7627614800563
the supplementary aspects that arise from the interinstitutional agreement with the commission are welcome, but that could not change the basic decision of the council.     2430.1358136768276
even here, we have renounced voluntarily - because this did not happen automatically - the plumb-delors agreement as part of the overall compromise.     5596.950142023882
we have been willing to relinquish the provisions in that.     2158.5606657563994
we shall have to be very vigilant on what used to be covered by that agreement and is not fully covered by the new decision.     1409.634578782102
we must be very vigilant.     3431.327379560082
so it is a step forward.     1232.1212960791916
my group reluctantly accepts it as a step forward.     10916.502195345087
we are not as enthusiastic about compromising as mrs frassoni.     8372.678191072331
we recognise that there are limitations and the issue will no doubt come back in a few years' time.     2231.4591767143856
if we want a union that is democratic and transparent to the fullest degree we will have to revisit this subject.     4664.607562185235
mr president, first i want to thank the rapporteur, mrs frassoni, for her work.     17945.09867031379
it is never easy working on comitology.     5163.501359153479
mrs schleicher referred to a kind of committee magic, something we have long talked about.     20798.317217537566
certainly the process has always been very complex, as a result of historical decisions, and these must eventually be rationalised.     2606.328073268494
in my opinion, the agreement reached between commission and parliament is a clear rationalisation of the process.     3473.7178460825357
from our point of view, it satisfies the fundamental interests of parliament.     8290.631558500845
mr corbett set out parliament' s concerns.     185256.40061949848
he says some of them have been satisfied and others have not.     2406.575698079413
i would say the ones the commission was able to satisfy have been satisfied.     11466.351669631416
mr corbett is raising issues the council decision does not cover, and unfortunately we can go no further on those.     3638.0206895934048
so how far have we come?     1399.288075628788
there is no doubt that we have reached a fundamental point, highlighted by mrs frassoni, given that we now have an automatic information system.     8169.171130855875
this will facilitate two activities parliament is especially interested in.     2753.5529184709544
one is knowing how the committees operate, about their composition, agendas, descriptions, and so on, and the other is the right of scrutiny over actual decisions.     4297.2483870152955
i think this is a good agreement.     1894.4378963415566
it is a good agreement which undoubtedly improves the transparency of the process.     1988.4302863900277
from here on things are going to be easier.     3410.1577531608987
but the agreement we are reaching today should not be regarded as a backward step compared with the plumb/delors agreement.     5446.527278863279
mr corbett referred to that, but the new model is certainly much more complex, different because the situation is now different, and, in our opinion, much more coherent.     2235.5072071995896
we have made a start, and we hope the work will produce some excellent results.     3356.929941312255
we think there are other positive advances too.     4611.126895986743
to a certain extent we have gone beyond agreements which are already dated and, by realigning old procedures with the new situation, we aim to clear up everything still outstanding from the past.     2898.200795871276
from the commission' s point of view, our conclusion is that this is a good agreement, and we want to thank the members of parliament who worked on it - especially mrs frassoni.     9316.43349436388
we hope that this new climate means we have achieved the result we were seeking, which is greater transparency and better information about the decisions of the commission as an executive.     3091.077063434193
thank you very much, commissioner solbes mira.     2761.5698056482393
the debate is closed.     4906.850855896859
the vote will take place tomorrow at 12.00 p.m.
protocol on the excessive deficit procedure
the next item is the report (a5-0013/2000) by mr knörr borràs, on behalf of the committee on economic and monetary affairs, on a proposal for a council regulation amending regulation (ec) no 3605/93 on the application of the protocol on the excessive deficit procedure annexed to the treaty establishing the european community (com(1999) 444 - c5-0174/1999 - 1999/0196(cns))
mr president, i am also pleased to welcome the spanish commissioner, mr solbes mira, whom i have congratulated more than once, inside and outside this parliament, on the excellent work he did in spain on the subject of european convergence.     12202.627309982441
the proposal for an amendment to this regulation on the application of the protocol on the excessive deficit procedure, annexed to the treaty of maastricht, raises fundamental issues concerning the switch to the 1995 european system of accounts, which, as you know, replaced the previous system of accounts adopted in 1979, and in particular the emergence of new financial product categories, consistency in the calculation of the ratios of government deficit to gross domestic product on the basis of esa 95 and, finally, recognising the importance of calculating interest expenditure and the consistency of the latter with this new methodology.     5701.058746743821
in our view, the main purpose of the proposal for a regulation is to update the definitions and the assessments of some of the economic operations involved in the calculation of deficits, as reflected in the treaty establishing the european community, the treaty of maastricht, which lays down the maximum percentages which the government deficit must not exceed as a ratio of gdp at market prices in the various member states.     4053.427149542399
to date, the member states have calculated this gdp in accordance with the criteria laid down in esa 79.
the most recent estimate of the deficit was submitted in september 1999 and it relates to the 1998 financial year.     9958.897396368035
therefore, if the regulation is adopted, the estimate which is due to appear in march 2000, the first estimate of the deficit for the 1999 accounting year, will be made using the new system.     1624.7337342625422
we know the member states have done their duty - if i can use that expression - doing their calculations and preparing their national accounts in line with esa 95, and they have already published their corresponding estimates.     9787.364693754418
in addition to incorporating a new methodology, this has meant making slight changes to most accounting aggregates and operations, on account of the aforesaid methodological changes, and also the improved statistical information included in the estimates.     13068.222293602888
these changes may of course alter the government deficits in terms of the member states' national accounting.     14204.768476384925
i will now describe, very briefly, the five areas where the most important changes may occur.     4099.401494346252
first, the adjustments to the scope of what is meant by 'government institutional sector' under esa 95.
this is important, since government deficit means the deficit accumulated by all forms of government.     18198.63127028158
the non-availability of precise criteria for the definition of government deficit would produce distortions in the estimates of the member states' government deficits.     13802.958005200544
secondly, the deficit - or, if applicable, the surplus - means the esa 95 government sector' s net borrowing requirement or lending ability respectively
thirdly, government investment means the gross fixed capital formation of the general government sector, as defined in esa 95.
fourthly, government debt is defined in accordance with esa 95, as are the flows or interest stemming from assets/liabilities.     25095.878239531914
fifthly and finally, the aggregate to which government debt will relate will be gross domestic product at current market prices, estimated in accordance with the 1995 european system of accounts' definitions.     7393.333457475819
to summarise, i consider that this proposal constitutes a satisfactory adaptation of the criteria laid down in the 1995 european system of accounts, and i therefore approve it in full, as did the committee.     4591.402994128076
mr president, commissioner, let me say at the outset that my group welcomes this proposal because it makes the basic statistical data for an economic and monetary union directed at stability and real growth more harmonised, directly comparable and more accurate.     3618.0260819764762
we have already heard about the various aspects involved.     1702.9269682879353
what i think is crucial is that henceforth the statistical data will be more reliable and comparable.     4871.6706832579575
we need that because the various european union bodies and all the member states within monetary union have to be able to depend on one another.     2877.6120308324357
it is important to adjust the procedure for two reasons.     4159.725312520721
firstly, for the single market.     1243.3776980972766
it is most important quite generally in a single market to have reliable and directly comparable data and statistics so as to give an accurate picture of the situation and development trends.     2748.162071591035
secondly, for economic and monetary union.     6887.364228585936
the regulation dating from 1993 on the application of the protocol must be brought into harmony with the methodology of the european system of accounts at national and regional level.     4583.756661224847
that is vitally important to the accurate and reliable calculation of deficits.     5760.648142312413
this adjustment will make it possible to estimate the deficits and debts in the member states more precisely.     8307.86921702733
these technical changes will have a very real impact on the actual position of the countries, since the data will automatically be amended in line with the public deficit.     3568.746223815667
i also welcome the fact that the estimates of the deficits for the 1999 financial year that are due to be published in march 2000 are already based on the new esa, i.e.     3202.1571382270345
the analysis is based on consolidated aggregates that are economically relevant.     10520.443073498192
let me also briefly use this debate as an opportunity to urge all the governments and political parties to do their utmost to ensure that this procedure never has to be initiated for an excessive deficit.     3987.934237841077
it is good that the procedure exists.     1317.9558192733184
we need it because it lends our criteria the necessary seriousness and credibility.     4175.265120191069
the citizens ask what happens if the criteria are not met.     3239.1262066973263
thanks to these procedures and other measures we can prove that we are taking the criteria and ourselves seriously.     2657.9785402237762
another important point is that we must do all we can, not just to prevent excessive deficits, but also to further reduce public deficits.     2005.5713333356155
i know what i am talking about and i will conclude by telling you why i am so glad we are holding this debate!     6504.0360469370835
we are currently holding a debate in my own country on whether the former minister for finance actually gave us accurate information on the deficit.     3566.6682670620357
in my country, austria, new elections had to be held in 1995 in order to meet the maastricht criteria and persuade our political partner to rethink his approach.     10645.803102663016
i must also tell you that in the year 2000, when there is so much talk about us, the coalition talks with the spö failed - and unfortunately so - because the socialist party brought down the negotiated austerity and consolidation programme.     4489.306668213348
that is a prime reason why the new government had to be formed in austria; for the austrian people' s party, the övp, wants to continue to act as guarantor of economic stability.     6240.149387754381
the övp wants to ensure that we continue to meet the maastricht criteria and reduce the public deficit, as we have agreed here.     5038.778351459897
mr president, the beauty of statistical data is that it is not only reliable but also comparable.     4158.184480986913
this is of great importance, especially in the light of european unification and the completion of the internal market.     5823.778650463695
this is why this report is also so politically charged: a report, by the way, on which we would like to congratulate the rapporteur warmly.     2883.098125314186
it also inspires a few political comments.     604.3634161371937
the stability pact is a given in europe.     2577.4554114365174
all countries participating in the euro have committed themselves, in the medium term, to striving towards a budget which is either balanced or shows up a slight surplus.     5654.072440679004
this, of course, does not mean that, if the economic climate is less than favourable, the budget could not fluctuate up or down slightly.     1222.0162812838996
it means that, in the long and medium term, there should be a balance or surplus.     2663.9993783365726
the stronger the position of the participating countries, the stronger the euro will be.     5429.5427128456595
as a result, the three percent standard will rarely be exceeded, not even in a bad economic climate.     2866.8215215888476
this is the theory behind it, anyway.     5499.395379680417
we have always been of the opinion that these criteria should be stringently met.     1286.4093054228003
that is of great importance in order to turn the emu into a success, now and in the future.     3582.9484881117887
strict compliance with the stability pact can thus contribute to a culture of stability within all emu member states.     8228.100286160072
various member states have taken one-off measures to be able to meet the criteria.     6004.853465710217
we are of the opinion that the member states' budget policy should be strictly supervised, to ensure that the measures continue to be taken in the spirit of the prescribed criteria at all times.     3809.919855014626
a drop in standards should not be allowed.     15062.47432827889
we are also of the opinion that a further reduction in the burden and reducing the national debt should be prioritised.     3136.7426237813506
indeed, we still have not managed to stop passing on the burden to future generations.     3628.022872505848
.     1000.0
(es) mr president, many thanks, first of all, to the rapporteur for his splendid work.     4399.395542302828
it was a subject which called for a large dose of technical skill and i think mr knörr has handled it with great precision and professionalism.     11118.132432353155
i would not presume to be able to summarise the content of our proposal better than he has done; i would just like to add one small point: in addition to adaptation to esa 95, there are two small technical changes which mr knörr knows all about, relating to interest and the valuation of debt in foreign currency.     8770.782312132469
i just mention that to complete the view of the process.     2216.9923001243255
secondly, i would like to thank mr knörr for his speediness.     7335.11652958342
he has done really good work in a very short time.     1081.539755269677
this is an important subject for certain references which we have heard subsequently.     4177.419230191788
what we are seeking with this legislation is not to make the statistics reliable and comparable - they already are - but to make them more reliable and more comparable.     2273.218571690382
obviously, the fact that parliament has taken its decision so quickly will mean that the march figures can already be calculated on the basis of these new criteria.     3086.4521686665876
that being so, i believe the final result will be better.     1569.7691329542874
the figures will be more reliable and more comparable and i think the concerns some members have raised about compliance with the stability pact can be better addressed.     4324.593812641571
this regulation is purely instrumental.     22167.72629419208
it does deal with the substance of the problem some of you have raised.     4731.655957669576
but we do need to think about how we deal with the substance of the problem.     3384.759586013199
the stability pact is in place and we will continue to press ahead with monitoring compliance by the various member states, despite the fact that the results are clearly positive in all the member states where we have analysed the position as regards the stability of the government deficit.     5484.427865301331
but we see no reason to lower our guard.     5640.556720868504
we must press on in that direction because we know it is vital.     2782.7868960943115
so, once again - and finally - i want to thank you for your help in allowing us to measure stability with greater technical certainty and better comparability.     2258.8721579840712

increasing penal sanctions against counterfeiting of the euro
the next item is the report (a5-0020/2000) by mrs cederschiöld, on behalf of the committee on citizens' freedoms and rights, justice and home affairs, on a draft framework decision of the council on increasing protection by penal sanctions against counterfeiting in connection with the introduction of the euro (5116/1999 - c5-0332/1999 - 1999/0821(cns)).     45846.32161933381
.     1000.0
(sv) mr president, ladies and gentlemen (all the more welcome for being so few in number), one might have thought that it was an unimportant subject which was to be discussed at this hour, but that is not the case.     4769.579699036554
it is a definite and concrete issue.     2273.775644003078
it is about our common currency.     1868.6906290156996
how many people here know that this currency is being counterfeited and that we have a quite unacceptable state of affairs?     3131.470887441031
we cannot permit our common currency to become weaker through its being counterfeited.     16144.546528151113
the ecb, ecofin, europol, olaf and the commissioner responsible for legal questions have therefore realised that common countermeasures must be taken quickly in order to close the loopholes left by the legislation.     4825.024050296826
it is easy to forget that it will be possible to exchange the old currencies for twenty years after the new currency has been introduced.     3998.128790100852
it is therefore important to include these currencies too in the section of the present programme of measures which deals with penal sanctions.     4617.240800501424
this is something which parliament has said it wants to see added.     2931.097902685977
the council ought to have given parliament the stipulated three-month period within which to consider, too, the last document produced on this matter, but we are all aware of how urgent it is to have these measures put safely in place.     2661.739856333834
because the issue is to be decided upon in march in the council, parliament has cause to adopt a position on the proposal now, so that the member states have time also to take due account of parliament' s points of view in the decision which has to be made in march.     2296.8300144462214
any country which began to make difficulties would incur a responsibility for the currency, and that country would be accountable, both morally and economically, if its actions were to contribute to a delay.     3868.2446407187476
a country which acted in that way would bring a lot of ill will upon itself.     5157.4785677159425
on 1 january 2002 the euro will be in circulation.     4967.019543400104
the whole of the union must have the same protection, a type of protection which prohibits counterfeiting, not only of currencies in circulation but also of currencies which will be in circulation.     4197.299141611559
it is important that, in time, there should be a more or less similar-looking form of protection under criminal law in all countries.     2089.2544616857695
the commission has proposed measures aimed at providing information, together with penal sanctions, preventive measures and the pooling of experience.     8708.560382976406
these measures do, in fact, entail a certain necessary harmonisation in as much as the lowest acceptable maximum penalty for currency counterfeiting is to be eight years' imprisonment.     9229.946147378581
trading with counterfeit currencies and the possession of counterfeiting equipment will also become criminal offences.     18965.600013381936
quite simply, we are to have a minimum level for the maximum penalty.     1856.9821569228686
the denominations of the euro are well known.     1035.2445224981034
modern computer equipment can be used to cause a very great deal of damage if we do not protect ourselves.     3151.6586742857903
parliament has tabled a number of amendments to the present proposal, for example in regard to jurisdiction, the idea being that a person may only be prosecuted and sentenced in the one country for the same crime.     3049.80812067125
there are also proposed sanctions against companies which are involved in counterfeiting, this being a measure which we would encourage.     2925.73069423098
the new applicant states are also encouraged to adapt to these regulations.     4513.13495568601
it has taken two years for the council and the commission to produce this proposal.     1666.5123189118517
it is essential that the decision should now be taken in march.     1175.2954476980394
to delay, or perhaps even risk damage to the euro, would be indefensible.     1543.8812830942322
it would also be a betrayal of the people who will be handling the new, unfamiliar notes.     1084.7087719263113
in particular, it is believed that counterfeiters will turn their attention to countries outside the euro zone, where people are less familiar with the new currency.     11126.774031546844
the protective measures cover these countries too, and the views expressed by these countries have carried a lot of weight.     6414.393601747546
the present proposal is therefore staunchly inclusive and reciprocal in character, accepting responsibility for a variety of interests in a way which imposes obligations upon others.     25401.096822783744
the council must now convene in march and make the relevant decision.     817.6410652039235
thank you for listening.     4855.00063694081
thank you, too, those who contributed to this matter' s being dealt with swiftly.     2151.260310174741
i am thinking in particular of the group of the party of european socialists.     3161.37535106278
mr president, first i want to compliment the rapporteur, charlotte cederschiöld, on drawing up this report, which is an excellent point of departure for increasing protection by penal sanctions against counterfeiting in connection with the introduction of the euro.     16331.44915633436
the euro will enter into circulation on 1 january 2002 and will become one of the world' s most important reserve currencies.     26915.498244831128
due to its importance on a world scale, the euro will be particularly exposed to the risk of counterfeiting and forgery.     10330.532972016137
this draft decision therefore lays down a set of minimum standards intended to harmonise criminal law in this field and make its application by each member state simpler and more effective.     4500.215371295775
it also provides for the euro to be protected even prior to its circulation, which will fill a gap in legislation in some member states.     5856.121917889561
i expressly support the rapporteur as regards the need to extend this protection to national currencies which can continue to be exchanged after they have been withdrawn from circulation.     12216.411751288131
this means that every member state must guarantee the establishment of effective, adequate penal sanctions, which act as a deterrent against the offences covered by the draft framework decision.     6662.773091151815
as to the proposal to establish a maximum custodial sentence of not less than eight years, i do not think that this will raise any problems as far as my country is concerned, even though the maximum sentence for counterfeiting under its criminal code is currently five years in prison, as it has already expressed willingness to make this amendment.     3226.274389830873
i also call on those few member states still harbouring reservations to resolve them so that a decision can rapidly be reached on this matter, for the reasons our rapporteur set out earlier when she called for this to be done.     2656.42720260631
mr president, this draft framework decision represents the first time a common proposal on crime has been put forward, with legislation to be applied by all fifteen member states at the same time.     2055.87782760676
we are therefore making progress towards the aim of developing and confirming the status of the european union as an area of freedom, security and justice, as expressed at tampere.     4223.445613167618
mr president, i need not repeat what the rapporteur said or the points mr coelho made.     1626.59755451191
all that is indeed very true and correct.     1630.8736736114142
it is easy to counterfeit the euro.     21529.3425056633
we are going through a dangerous transitional phase in which a phenomenon has appeared in criminal law that is perhaps unique in monetary history.     4415.492346400438
it is that a currency that does not even exist yet, that is not in circulation, but exists only as bank deposit money, can nevertheless be counterfeited already.     3772.1736644072307
yet because that currency is not yet in circulation as such, counterfeiting euros does not at present constitute a criminal offence.     6625.269496266431
it is a highly complex problem.     1829.2216559595895
if we do not act promptly - as mrs cederschiöld rightly said - anyone can produce a few counterfeit euros now and possibly not be prosecuted for subsequently putting them into circulation.     6476.514637683565
let me say in passing that this may seem an attractive prospect for a number of people.     12364.382493279996
so what is the european union doing about it?     971.1572663511128
it is doing a number of things that are right, as mrs cederschiöld has described; but, in the context of these things she described, parliament must look not so much at the content of the framework decision, not so much at the report drawn up by mrs cederschiöld, but more at the procedures that are being applied.     3003.492501517617
the manner in which the council, which is by far the most undemocratic institution in europe, consulted the european parliament makes a mockery of the concept of democracy.     4165.568666328865
we received a text for consultation which the council had been working on for a very long time, incidentally with only partial success - i will come to that in a moment.     1936.5620463644489
now the european parliament is supposed to state its position on this complex, difficult and, at times, contradictory council document in the space of three months.     5115.606358254173
it is only thanks to the immense efforts of mrs cederschiöld and to parliament' s decision not to stand by its democratic right to conduct its advisory procedure over an adequate period of time and to have all the documents available in all the languages, that we have now managed to give our position by the date set by the council, so that the decision scheduled for march can indeed be taken.     3626.801647043445
on a subject as important as counterfeiting and protection against counterfeiting and harmonising the necessary criminal law provisions we cannot really work at the ridiculous pace the council is demanding of us.     4693.425447092225
so let me state quite clearly here, on behalf of my group, that we discussed at length with mrs cederschiöld whether it is reasonable to work like this.     7650.150461377926
we concluded that it is not, but given the importance of the issue we nonetheless agreed to accept this procedure.     2870.6606471448754
this framework decision does without any doubt represent some progress.     6383.793972139282
only, as mrs cederschiöld pointed out, in the end it does not fully achieve the legal certainty the council wanted because various shortcomings have not been dealt with.     6004.505971478603
they relate to the minimum rules we are seeking to establish, the minimum rules relating to a punishable offence and in particular the application of the same criminal law norms in all member states, including those that have not yet introduced the euro but where it could theoretically be counterfeited.     6298.105925259234
one reason they have not been dealt with is that as ever the leopard cannot change its spots, i.e.     1947.4396864110408
in the case of this framework decision, which specifically relates to judicial cooperation, the council once again prefers to adhere to the level of government cooperation.     5465.480734068487
that is why, and i quote, this framework decision "is confined to supplementing the provisions of the international convention of 20 april 1929 for the suppression of counterfeiting and its protocol" , i.e.     8450.800235465895
the provisions of a timeworn international convention that is a good 71 years old.     1344.8711817863343
that means that the legal basis we are now seeking to establish to protect the euro does not derive from community legislation, as it should, but merely takes the form of provisions supplementing an international convention dating back 71 years.     5165.612767754037
i leave it to the assembled company in this chamber to decide whether that can be the future of a european union based on judicial cooperation.     2991.989182557949
thank you for your attention during our night sitting together.     5231.153008917716
mr president, the launch of the euro could well be a heaven-sent opportunity for counterfeiters.     21081.242434532152
the fact is people are suddenly going to lose all their currency references, and that means they will be at serious risk of easily accepting forged euros because they are not very familiar with the genuine article.     5601.978511291083
so the council is naturally worried and is seeking to establish sanctions, primarily criminal, applicable in all the countries of europe, against offences of this kind.     4839.8670991813215
no doubt the council could have been satisfied with a simple resolution recommending that member states adopt national legislation to that effect.     4300.5010551525365
that is what i would have preferred, personally.     3403.8968944606286
but, probably through lack of confidence in the zeal of certain members, the council has preferred to implement a legal instrument which is more binding at european level: a framework decision.     1923.2469434867573
now that is where the difficulties begin, because criminal law comes under national sovereignty and the treaty of maastricht contains no specific provision covering this situation for the euro - so as not to frighten the voters by over-egging the pudding.     2391.051346150324
the draft framework decision before us today tries to cover up this lacuna by referring to articles 31 and 34(2)(b) of the treaty.     2232.056678684174
however, i find these passports inadequate.     2201.2355875466665
article 31 only envisages minimum common rules for criminal acts in certain cases, specifically restricted to organised crime, terrorism and drug trafficking.     15527.22003001977
counterfeiting is not on the list.     7995.5792484509775
article 34, a product of the treaty of amsterdam, only lays down the new form of a framework decision, and clearly this only applies to the existing powers of the european union.     2647.541528014226
so we come back to the previous problem.     1687.4517266023604
in other words, the council has not succeeded in giving this text a credible legal basis.     2273.8265804739194
in a way, this is deferred punishment for its lack of honesty, at the time, in presenting the maastricht treaty to the voters.     8999.082987764297
i would also like to make a more general point.     1495.2680941465899
not so long ago we were dealing with one or more reports on questions of principle relating to the euro in virtually every part-session.     3752.36029302728
but there has been nothing for a year.     1268.2099040780859
just a few little technical reports like this one on counterfeiting.     1708.2950572279747
the commission' s communication on its strategic objectives for 2000-2005, which we have debated this week, contained absolutely nothing on this vast subject, except the reminder, in a single line, that the euro coins and notes are due to come into circulation on 1 january 2002.
however, there are many underlying problems yet to be resolved.     7829.786151913336
let us mention, for example, the disturbing lack of public interest, the definition of monetary policy objectives abusively reduced to the mere fight against inflation, the strange coupling of monetary federalism to a lack budgetary or fiscal federalism, the status of the present applicant countries, when they become members, vis-à-vis the euro zone, the confused reaction of the international markets to this currency with no homogeneous popular foundation, and so on.     5293.622352037984
do i also need to mention that investors who sold dollars to buy euros have lost 20% since 1 january 1999, 40% if they bought bonds, and no less than 55% if they sold yen to buy euros?     12085.950019348218
mr president, for those unfortunate investors even real euros are false money.     8005.7394519292575
these are gigantic problems but they seem to be taboo here.     4229.695757133376
i should like to take over the baton from the previous speaker.     2875.3578670606935
there are a number of questions of principle which are demanding solutions, because the point of departure is, of course, that the regulation of criminal law is a national concern.     4043.3189198285545
criminal law is part of each individual nation' s own cultural tradition, and it is very difficult to arrive at common definitions of what is to be understood by these basic legal concepts.     6003.648435393318
i appreciate mrs cederschiöld' s work, and my point of departure will be her search - in connection with the tampere summit - for common definitions and common regulations regarding what is punishable and what common sanctions can be applied.     17058.437290968606
she would like to devise these sanctions jointly so as to arrive at a coherent criminal justice system for offences which have particular relevance for the eu.     9203.579412831255
but the problem is that there is no legal basis for this, and with good reason, because the regulation of criminal law belongs to the sphere of national jurisdiction.     2799.132260387491
in that context, i want to ask the following question of the commission' s and the council' s representatives: is the legal basis which has been invoked adequate?     6883.952347606158
in my view, it is clearly in conflict with, and far exceeds the scope of, article 34, paragraph 2, point b where a legal basis is established for so-called framework decisions which are binding upon the member states in regard to the desired objectives, but in which it is left to the national authorities to determine the form and methods of implementation.     3979.415388742999
what of course characterises the present draft framework decision is the fact that the member states' obligations are laid down in detail.     6339.2023465664515
there is no freedom of choice regarding methods and implementation.     10792.87765115756
in my view, this exceeds the parameters of the legal basis.     10680.052734354933
i should like an answer from the commission and the council, for i consider it obvious that article 31 does not supply the relevant legal basis.     2552.053911263877
mr president, commissioner, let me begin by warmly congratulating and thanking the rapporteur and pointing out that, in her report, she does not just welcome the draft framework decision but also proposes a few changes, which have been referred to by various speakers but which we cannot discuss in detail now, to supplement and improve the text.     2837.720524254543
it is crucially important to continue the success story of the euro and we must make every effort not to spoil this success story or put it at risk by taking inadequate measures to prevent counterfeiting - and we are well aware of the initial attempts that are being made.     3220.2334521206867
we have all noted that the conversion phase will bring uncertainty and risks with it.     9128.003484282946
so, in addition to the efforts to combat counterfeiting, to ensure the sound implementation of the provisions, the planned information campaign will of course play a key role and here we still have serious work to do.     3688.299844258562
we are also concerned with the question of cooperation.     1795.7641232263209
we need to define the precise responsibilities of the ecb, the national central banks, the commission and europol pertaining to all matters relating to counterfeiting of the euro.     7205.085345325654
i also think we should perhaps consider setting up an interinstitutional or eu body in charge of coordinating the cooperation work.     4409.698117372529
in the same context, we could establish an early warning system functioning round the clock, together with a common information system for the rapid exchange of data between the responsible authorities.     3674.5757085408068
aside from the report, which we welcome, many questions have arisen that still need to be resolved.     5109.03108346986
i hope the debate we are holding here will bring some clarification.     5512.142958252891
mr president, mrs cederschiöld, ladies and gentlemen, how safe is the euro?     77460.94636591927
that is, after all, a question the people often ask.     1488.4911274945691
and that question will of course become increasingly pressing with the approach of 1 january 2002, the date when they will have euros in their pockets.     4915.2410337765
for the safety of the euro is not just a matter of the stability and external value of the currency but, in fact, also of protecting the euro against counterfeiting.     2759.611182835401
that too is a concern of the people, for, as has rightly been said, this will be a new currency for them, new in terms of how it looks and also how it is used.     1223.8927395993448
of course all the citizens of the eu member states will also be faced with the novelty of a currency that is valid over such a wider area, and even beyond europe, as a reserve currency.     2361.3939402347473
they will need time to get used to that too.     1728.4486837413765
so it is clearly necessary to ask what the eu is doing to protect the euro against counterfeiting.     3166.4754418770685
and that is what we are discussing at this late hour.     1741.7538800471516
this really is a subject that is close to the citizen, so it is a pity it is being debated at such a late hour.     3186.60266139412
but that cannot be helped.     6294.6976037311815
of course, the european central bank has been looking into the matter for a very long time.     1658.3151574302397
after all, it is responsible for technical measures to protect against counterfeiting.     3600.4700692170018
as has also been pointed out, in summer 1998 the commission submitted a communication listing a very wide variety of areas where measures need to be taken, such as information, further training - an important area, cooperation between the euro-states and of course even beyond them, and measures under criminal law.     3695.86436469416
that is what this framework decision is about.     976.4424242742004
mrs cederschiöld, let me offer you sincere thanks on behalf of the commission and, above all, let me also thank parliament for being prepared to give its position in the space of such a short time.     11456.259294965706
i certainly appreciate that.     5201.388813944642
the framework decision aims to ensure that the member states apply the same definition of counterfeiting offences.     6548.639276291256
of course the first condition for uniform action is always to have uniform definitions and, as was emphasised, what we are basically trying to do is to ensure that the euro is protected even before it is physically introduced.     3803.8863194293085
naturally, the commission is also glad that the european parliament' s prompt decision - and i hope the vote will be in favour - means the framework decision can enter into force very rapidly now, so that we can begin to transpose it.     5043.545553254929
for the commission has been asked to report, as it will in the first half of next year, on the progress in transposing it in the member states, on whether the serious nature of this issue is being matched by serious action.     2659.2041970151568
it has been pointed out that this framework decision is in fact a decision in the area of criminal law.     2087.0878108780735
in that respect we must recognise this innovation, this step, even if, as mr schulz just pointed out, simply supplementing a rather old convention is not really a particularly bold step.     5833.98528138177
in reply to the question whether the commission considers that the framework decision has an adequate legal basis under the treaty, the commission believes that the right basis is being applied here.     3056.6973999714637
the framework decision will, however, have to be supplemented by other legal acts in future.     2513.0708036548776
the commission will submit a proposal for a regulation on cooperation between all the responsible authorities and on cooperation with europol.     2146.9348966545595
that proposal will establish a duty to cooperate, and a duty on the part of the national authorities to notify europol of cases of counterfeiting and to present any confiscated counterfeit money to the european central bank for identification and classification, so that effective measures can then be taken.     4098.645061103057
on the question of coordinating the various measures, since early 1998, the commission, followed now by olaf, has been working with experts on the subject from the member states, the european central bank and europol on means of effectively combating counterfeiting of the euro in advance.     4095.4253981401907
as mr karas just pointed out, a few weeks ago the commission submitted a proposal for an information campaign on the euro, to which it proposed allocating as much as eur 32 million.     3796.375170870846
that is a volume of funding that can be put to very effective use, a large amount; and of course the information campaign will also focus very strongly on protecting the euro against counterfeiting.     3085.3970912268032
i believe that this debate shortly before midnight will also teach a salutary lesson to all those counterfeiters who believe their hour has come, because they think the eu is no longer awake at this late hour!     8007.221105643924
thank you very much, commissioner schreyer.     2221.079744546403

the purpose of the meeting, which was attended by several vice-presidents and quaestors, together with the architects who designed this building, was to discuss how we could improve our working conditions.     5481.784269446449
we discovered during the meeting that the lovely white curtain behind you, mr president, in fact conceals a huge window which, if the curtain were drawn back, would afford a wonderful view over the river.     13304.709554480332
we were told that the curtain was closed so that you would not have your back to the light and to improve the lighting during debates.     2148.509560972917
but what is to prevent us, before the sitting is opened or when you close the sitting, opening the curtains and enjoying the apparently magnificent view behind you?     1887.3049661213468
i am more than happy for our house to be illuminated by the brilliance of our debates but a little real sunlight on our bubble of democracy from time to time would not come amiss.     2452.232318070995
