ucits
.     799078002.6189679
(sv) mr president, commissioner, ladies and gentlemen, it is my hope that parliament is prepared now to make a decision to extend and strengthen the financial market in europe.     7532.160170184235
as a new member of the european parliament, it has been very interesting and instructive for me to be given responsibility for this important matter.     1751.2382010037295
i should especially like to thank my colleagues on the committee for their willingness to find compromises; the president and the bureau for lending a sensitive ear to this complex issue; the commission, and especially the commissioner, for being such worthwhile contacts; the council; and also the division responsible for servicing the committee, for its selfless work.     3024.174111461828
there was a good deal of agreement within the committee, apart from on a matter which, too, i hope it will be possible to solve.     4576.77255632736
the issue of ucits may appear difficult, technical and a political minefield.     18419.01208955277
i believe that some people do not even know what ucits stands for.     6332.64641766357
allow me, however, to state very clearly that this is a question which really does affect the citizens of europe in so far as it relates to how their savings are administered, what options there are available, how competition operates between countries in regard to different forms of savings, savers' demand for security, and the opportunities europe' s citizens have to save wisely for their old age.     3733.4412450018417
it is a question of weighing demands for liberalisation in the interests of a better market and higher yield against investors' need for protection and security.     2981.4161032289694
in the course of the 1980s, most markets in europe underwent drastic deregulation.     2902.5377702159567
as a result, the national money and bond markets in particular developed very quickly over a period of a few years.     1225.6819261368582
collective investment undertakings became an alternative to saving with a bank.     6416.121732041339
in my own country, more than 60 per cent of swedes save with a variety of funds.     14905.442276763075
the security afforded by this type of saving is attributable to the broad range of investment vehicles in which the funds are placed.     9185.578421905064
the capital and securities market has continued to be developed.     2955.2142516607037
the current directive on ucits has become antiquated and outdated.     4503.212656727202
new types of funds have been introduced in the various member states which are not permitted to market their units in other member states.     3530.447325417603
obviously, this inhibits competition and innovation and restricts the public' s range of savings options.     7870.516721336504
the commission' s proposal with which we are dealing today is in two parts.     10405.79174011422
the first concerns the product, that is to say the funds.     2109.8350710100126
the other concerns the collective investment undertakings.     2433.3313732424926
the main purpose of the first proposal is to extend the investment opportunities of collective investment undertakings by allowing them to invest in units in other collective investment undertakings, for example units in funds, standardised options and futures contracts and deposits in credit institutions and in certain types of money market instruments.     8196.778171411679
special risk-spreading rules are proposed in respect of such investments, involving both quantitative and qualitative criteria.     7689.946515652532
in addition, the commission' s proposal puts forward special risk-spreading rules for funds whose aim is to replicate a particular stock index, a so-called index fund.     21739.16829719763
the committee wishes to make the requirements in relation to the commission' s proposal more stringent.     31630.250402038768
to guarantee transparency and prudence, investments in non-harmonised funds should only be allowed on condition that qualitative criteria, for example a transparency requirement and investor protection, are introduced.     12551.398376159577
in addition, quantitative criteria (risk exposure) should be introduced.     31113.296218837168
moving on now, then, to the point where there is (or, i hope, was) a lack of agreement, namely otc derivatives.     7726.667176393868
the commission' s proposal makes a distinction between derivatives dealt in on regulated markets and derivative instruments not dealt in on regulated markets (so-called otc derivatives).     104696.5726513897
the commission' s proposal takes it as read that otc derivatives are not one of the general types of investment that ucits deal in, but that they should only be used to achieve efficient portfolio management and to hedge exchange-rate risks.     14629.282381133069
in recent years, the derivatives market has grown very rapidly in almost all the member states.     4808.71509195412
it is therefore important that regulation of derivatives is flexible so that the market is not tied down ahead of future developments.     3226.3666185765005
the definitions 'standardised futures contracts' and 'standardised options' should therefore be combined to form a new definition, 'financial derivative instruments' , which also covers otc derivatives.     28101.633264871045
investment in otc derivatives, however, should only be allowed on condition that both quantitative and qualitative criteria are introduced.     12607.199985052814
they are very important in this case.     1270.085287396107
they must be introduced to guarantee the protection of investors.     7246.171646703343
the commission' s second proposal focuses on the bodies which manage ucits, that is to say management companies.     15580.022592774274
the rules for management companies, for example the conditions for starting up and conducting business, are brought into line with the existing rules and regulations.     3092.6121172602825
a crucial point is that rules are proposed for so-called simplified prospectuses.     15546.62242654246
relevant information must be given to prospective unit-holders in a fund in a language which is readily understandable to investors in the host country.     5883.017875892696
in this regard, too, the committee is prepared to go further than the commission.     2225.936741991547
a question which has also been discussed in the committee is that of the capital contribution to be required from management companies.     1751.2947262161758
there are different views on this.     3147.4626884825766
one of the most important things, for me, personally, is that the common regulations should not cause smaller companies to go under.     1510.7180340150485
during the months that i have been working on this report, one very clear demand has emerged, both from industry and from those who represent the consumer.     2095.631011670178
the current situation is not good.     844.3967996638488
both savers and the industry need modernised regulations for the whole of europe.     5937.681589746197
i believe that today' s proposal strikes a good balance between liberalisation of the market and the desire for effective protection for investors.     12796.321817492886
i just want to conclude by saying that a number of members of parliament think that the present proposal goes too far, while others think that i am not sufficiently liberal.     3816.7470044642787
to them i just want to say, as the english expression goes, 'you can' t have it all.'     4838.973245576356
.     1000.0
mr president, at the outset i wish to place on the official record of the house my thanks for the tremendous work undertaken by mr schmidt, our rapporteur.     8425.726675813628
i was in fact the surrogate father of these reports during the previous parliament.     8305.377730240974
we failed over the last six years to get any compromise or agreement with regard to this matter.     3345.1348711215887
in a short space of time mr schmidt has managed to bring it forward to this final stage within parliament.     6842.255986305572
i thank him for that.     1228.0047324715968
as the draftsperson for the opinion of the committee on legal affairs and the internal market, i also wish to place on record my thanks to the secretariat of both the legal affairs committee and the economic affairs committee for their cooperation on this matter.     2803.1941994024264
i am speaking today wearing a number of hats: firstly, as the surrogate father of this report; secondly, as the draftsperson of the opinion; and most importantly, as one who sees the tremendous advantages which can be gained for the european union from a harmonised system of ucits investment to ensure that monies presently being earned in europe but invested outside the european union and not put to the benefit of the union economy can be retained within the economy for the creation of entrepreneurship, employment and also for guaranteeing future pension rights.     6165.586170518793
there is little disagreement amongst members with regard to the first proposal.     5880.301490003894
it puts into place a certain number of controls and mechanisms to ensure that we can have this kind of harmonised regulatory authorities.     4717.960074911765
however, it is in relation to the second proposal that there is most concern.     5352.936537730863
i have a number of ideas which should be put across.     6147.328179140667
firstly, the idea of an eu passport for management companies is to be welcomed; and the home-state approval rule is also to be welcomed.     5880.032741711158
however, instead of imposing a new regime, we could have used the investment services directive as the analogy for this regime.     2272.2332558616486
it seems unusual that we failed to grasp that opportunity.     18994.91153734435
many members have been working on the basis of trying to protect their own countries' national interests rather than improving the overall system for europe.     3459.9069816894867
we will have to revisit this issue again in the near future.     5189.7827642343955
i call on members to ensure by their votes today or that we can put europe on a sound footing for the short term, if not for the long term.     2464.1050827462295
mr president, i must firstly thank the rapporteur, mr schmidt, my colleague, mr goebbels, and mrs villiers and mrs lulling from my group.     21928.701274160325
despite their differing opinions on some points, they have consistently worked in an extremely constructive manner.     6759.888495347598
as indicated by the rapporteur, we are discussing probably one of the most important issues that we will ever have to discuss in this parliament.     2460.1725799703486
collective investment undertakings have for some time been the primary savings vehicle through which millions of ordinary citizens, without any specialist knowledge, have invested their life savings.     9203.748564436788
this is the main reason why we must proceed with liberalisation, albeit in a prudent manner.     7288.927824339682
i say this because the failure of a bank is a problem for which any government can find a solution.     2296.245591380614
however, the failure and suspension of payments of this type of undertaking would create a political, social and personal problem with incalculable consequences.     4908.663089680926
there is also a second reason why prudence is needed in the regulation of these undertakings.     3678.6845031672738
i believe in a social market economy based on a successful market operating subject to certain rules intended to guarantee the general interest.     3438.55532212592
specifically, at the moment, this means the protection of investors.     5095.836369081938
this basic principle of liberalisation, increased competition and greater investment opportunities, together with prudence to ensure the protection which investors deserve, has determined and inspired my group' s position on most of the controversial points which have arisen.     9683.435677664142
firstly, there is the problem of investment in non-harmonised undertakings falling outside this directive.     5733.6685653749655
it is clear that by extending the proposal under discussion, the options and the range of investments in undertakings for collective investments, those undertakings which remain outside the rules will be obliged to invest in high-risk funds.     6299.550515283565
it would be wrong to allow the regulated and protected undertakings to invest in other undertakings involved in such risky investments.     5981.605868947883
secondly, the problem of derivatives is one which has created the most divisions, both within my group and between the groups.     4351.464665273467
we have achieved a compromise solution.     3262.047894719054
everyone accepts that investments in derivatives must be legally limited in order to prevent a harmonised fund losing all its assets due to an investment in high-risk products.     10465.130948035656
it is clear that this risk is greater when the investment is in derivatives not dealt in on a regulated market, namely the famous otc derivatives.     9545.89697180423
we have achieved an acceptable compromise solution on this, or so we believe, which i welcome.     3070.5772493802097
as this issue will be returning to this house at some stage, i must highlight the working method used between the commission and this parliament.     5433.94900766699
the texts before us today are old and were already old when they reached this house.     3170.3479481996405
this was because they were substantially amended during the finnish presidency.     10241.763291388343
we are therefore faced with two options.     6151.061841654926
we can either return the proposals to the commission for the latter to present a harmonised text or we can continue the current process.     3736.9741755235627
this second option has won the day and i am pleased about this.     10833.793483479683
however, in the future i hope that the coordination between these two institutions and the council can be improved so that our work is more understandable and more productive.     1215.3226454152598
mr president, the socialist group supports the commission' s efforts to implement the action plan for financial services.     9527.276920957633
obviously, in the wake of the introduction of the euro, we need to create an integrated european financial market which is similar to the american and japanese markets.     4025.6281633557937
the debates on ucits gave rise to speeches on behalf of all sorts of lobbies and heated debate in the relevant committee.     28481.151423593234
i should like to congratulate the rapporteur, mr olle schmidt, and also mr garcía-margallo y marfil, for their constructive approach.     26305.52687960569
the socialists recognise the importance of the investment fund industry to the development of our economies and to the allocation of resources.     4802.538323757866
but the world of international finance is a dangerous one.     1322.3079613048233
the capital markets are still at the mercy of their excesses.     11937.645484681081
there is at present a surfeit of cash looking for a home.     2308.5083855941352
in 1999, the increase in the means of payment in the world resulted in over-stimulated stock exchanges and an explosion in the volume of transactions, pushing up the prices of long-term financial and real assets.     8438.185762577585
and as international financial activity speeds up, the risks increase.     3792.9289474558636
you do not need a nobel prize in economics to realise that the current working hypothesis of the financial markets, which anticipate annual growth in profits of the order of 15 to 25% is quite simply unrealistic in the medium term.     4230.95335236622
i adamantly believe that the main risk to the world economy in the foreseeable future is not inflation, but deflation generated as the speculative bubbles, created by what alan greenspan calls irrationally exuberant markets, burst.     2747.326437639541
the legislature has a duty to contain the risk inherent in the continuing deregulation of the european financial markets.     6670.855334643805
by definition, investment funds use other people' s money.     20216.759358701678
the legislator should protect the general interest and introduce safeguard clauses.     22363.77680259008
there is no freedom without rules.     5069.002711476738
we have done away with so-called master-feeder structures.     29761.4682231364
these feeder funds exist in the united states, which is an area with a single supervisory authority and a single legal system.     3488.486743028593
the european market is still fragmented and such structures would doubtless cause problems.     3196.8122871364963
the socialists, like the commission, question the advisability of allowing investment funds to invest in so-called over-the-counter derivatives, i.e.     5228.714230980944
products negotiated between professionals by private contract.     99930.66947936405
as these transactions are generally not subject to prudential control, the risks are all the more obvious.     1364.8571336720277
the socialist group has tabled amendments proposing both quantitative and qualitative safeguard clauses in order to contain the risks to investors.     15602.04487094049
by adopting these amendments, parliament will allow recourse to otc derivatives but will also limit the risk to private investors.     13990.740824894683
mr president, there is much talk at present of the principle of good governance.     4474.406716960432
this principle should not only guide the public sector, it should also guide the private sector, especially financial undertakings.     3335.3364774315073
mr president, our objective in setting a framework for unit trusts and other uctis is to secure the highest possible safe return for our savers, which is why i want to address the issue of derivatives in my remarks today.     6038.345734777107
i firmly believe that the prudent use of derivatives can help fund secure, safer, higher returns.     57899.62680700106
despite their image of danger, derivatives are usually used by asset managers as insurance.     33517.918753892234
a contract that, for example, pays a substantial sum if, say, the nasdaq or the cac 40 share index falls, causes an extra payment by the investor if the nasdaq or the cac 40 share index rises.     29757.648038743733
is this speculative?     10726.467998592296
not at all.     441.43212815538635
imagine you have spent some months building up a portfolio of shares in companies in which you are confident: they have good management, good prospects and they are good investments.     8211.47205396332
then you become worried that the whole stock market is going to collapse, as it did in 1987.
you have the option of selling off everything and getting into cash, but that would be to waste your research into the winners.     4654.285356457438
it would also incur all the transaction costs of selling your shares and then buying them again after the fall.     12066.250464534964
the solution is to take out a derivative which pays off if the nasdaq or cac 40 falls.     11307.701900668459
but even with a tailor-made derivative or otc derivative which would be cheaper, perhaps insuring you against a relative fall in your portfolio against the index, you would still have the portfolio and the shares, but you would have protected yourself against the risks of a collapse.     10120.313798571153
in exchange, you might also have given up some of the upside of the markets.     2650.4067546573624
in this case, which is perhaps the most common use of derivatives by asset managers, you reduce your potential reward but you also reduce your potential risk.     9305.953768091415
that is a desirable development in a soundly managed fund.     7647.4788554919605
history demonstrates that investors benefit from a liberal and light-handed approach to regulation.     9399.603880362021
heavy-handedness - a prescription of how private sector managers should invest and where they should invest - will merely reduce returns to savers.     7994.731027206254
pension funds which suffered from rules on where they could and could not invest, as required by of our government bonds, made returns of 5.2% a year, barely half the 9.5% annual returns made by free funds between 1984 and 1996.
these are of course the returns after deducting all losses.     5427.555174632654
do not confuse a real concern for the interests of investors with a populist fear of the new and the innovative and liberal approach that pays off.     2501.552949198113
this is what we should be concerned about here today.     1334.8201682746683
mr president, the report presented to us today seeks to complete the free movement of financial investments in europe, including the most sophisticated forms of investment.     4789.8280074545255
we do not, in principle, oppose this industry.     5219.560269044166
however, unlike mr christopher huhne, we feel that a continent which has proved incapable of controlling its oil industry properly should not overly deregulate an industry which may, in time, provoke the same type of crisis.     8149.392241958256
we shall therefore vote cautiously on all amendments limiting recourse to excessively derivative instruments and we shall vote in favour of all amendments which limit the excessive concentration of assets within a single financial group.     13432.992439031073
but we make no bones about the fact that we shall vote against the text as a whole.     2204.114123382066
we shall also vote in the future against any proposals to deregulate the financial markets and we call on all the other political groups to do likewise until such time as taxation on investment income is finally harmonised.     2794.1138062409836
for years certain countries, including great britain, have been blocking harmonisation and turning their financial centres into tax havens.     10199.385581102055
they even reject the strict minimum, i.e.     2461.659796146627
the monti package proposed by the commission in brussels.     7845.802933747923
this attitude is turning europe into a machine which exempts investment income from tax and shifts the whole tax burden to commercial income, with all the social and even moral consequences which that implies.     4868.180505160092
we must put an end to this.     3858.9043030982252
by blocking the demands of the financial industry, the european parliament has the means to ensure that countries which have been turned into tax havens end up with their back against the wall.     3951.872052791045
mr president, the 1985 directive introduced a sort of european visa for certain types of ucits which could be marketed throughout the union.     13309.425329405223
since then, the capital market and transferable securities have not just continued to evolve; thanks to unbridled financial globalisation, they have exploded exponentially and without restraint.     5605.757236039409
this brings us to the two proposals for directives.     2431.655460130187
the second proposal, on which the report is excellent, does not require any particular comment.     2056.0039077647125
it aims to strengthen consumer protection and security.     5264.529466732414
it is a step in the right direction, even if one wonders why in heaven, with what right and with what respect for our citizens, we are further bolstering the monopoly of the english language in all documents distributed throughout europe.     4018.8482548272073
that is the thrust of an amendment which we have tabled; it favours all the languages of the community and our agreement to the second directive will depend on it.     2520.2167670515464
the same cannot be said of the first proposal.     1528.6167996377778
we might have been able to agree to the original text insofar as it was informed by a legitimate concern to update current legislation.     6388.400263194556
however, we are unable to agree to the text of the report, although i welcome the work carried out by mr schmidt, for several reasons.     8174.718816077737
first, it leans too far towards products based on indexes which have no bearing on the real economy; derivatives which will help to swell the financial bubble which mr lipietz referred to just now.     2283.658293850763
secondly, like him, i think that we should not promote excessive concentration in funds which, in the final analysis, will prove to be a trojan horse for american pension funds.     3515.5066577028847
there is, of course, no reciprocity in favour of european ucits.     9523.435774633232
we shall also vote against the abolition of the facility for the member states to increase to 35% ...
(the president cut the speaker off)
mr president, this is a vital issue for europe.     2916.7954676168206
a europe that faces an impending pensions time bomb must take every step to encourage saving and maximise the return that the people of europe can obtain on their savings.     6477.1966232259465
i welcome many of the developments which mr schmidt' s report brings us.     19919.98031385215
ucits are a success story within the european union.     14151.07617497012
they are a safe product, traded cross-border.     35544.95946856033
but it is time for the legislation to be updated.     1730.3178636767584
mr schmidt has done an excellent job here.     10020.983626634774
it is no secret that i would have much preferred mr schmidt' s report not to have been altered so much as it passed through parliament.     6666.629602823602
but i appeal to this house to take a balanced and common-sense approach on this issue.     7103.164806053143
the radical step taken by this report is to allow ucits to use over-the-counter derivatives.     13636.251345769902
i appeal to everyone here to evaluate the products which will be used by ucits.     15556.271470526988
we are not talking about the use of derivatives for speculation.     17591.54689211317
we are talking about the use of derivatives to reduce risk and to increase the returns on the savings of ordinary europeans.     9943.677674964647
mr schmidt is to be applauded for having obtained the support of parliament for over-the-counter derivatives.     19289.917697105706
we must not be afraid to go forward, to react to changes in the market.     2855.37281175831
yes, we need to continue to ensure that ucits provide a balanced and protected product for consumers.     13473.551615327404
the report gives very adequate protection for consumers.     4326.405840845889
but a number of the amendments go too far in restricting the use of over-the-counter derivatives.     8973.240359274465
i feel these derivatives will be an addition to the portfolio of unit trusts, which can only help individual people around europe obtain a high return on their savings.     13005.918049986076
i salute mr schmidt.     606944.5366758312

however, i will make sure that we have a go then and see how the land lies.     2546.613818741737
mr president, the ucits directive has been an enormous success since it entered into force on 1 october 1989, mainly thanks to the excellent protection which it offers investors.     6043.239637759652
the directive and the reputation which ucits enjoy with investors have allowed the investment fund industry to multiply its assets in the european union fivefold.     21436.098306441207
there has been a strong increase in cross-border marketing of investments within the european union and the directive itself is used as a model for legislation throughout the world.     2970.964987179768
however, the directive is ten years old and, now that the markets have developed and more and more sophisticated financial products are available, it is somewhat outdated.     1673.4990682853902
the commission proposals being debated here will give it the face-lift it needs.     6936.301500761654
i share some of the concerns expressed here, especially by mr garcía-margallo y marfil, but this face-lift would be even more successful if the funds which simply reproduce a stock exchange index, and which are therefore managed either passively or not at all, and the funds which use an index as a benchmark for an actively-managed fund were treated in the same way, if only to allow medium-sized undertakings listed on the stock exchange but not included in the stock exchange index better access to the capital market.     4354.291025728325
in addition, investors often have better protection with an actively-managed fund.     12043.071307649252
my amendment no 47 is therefore a warning for amateurs.     810.4381620588717
as far as the proposal for a new provision limiting investments in the same group to 15% is concerned, i would like to stress that, apart from the fact that this limit is too low, the text does not define the concept of a group.     3796.896632630607
this concept does not exist under european law because it is impossible to implement and equally impossible to control in practice.     4068.27003583105
i shall therefore vote against this proposed amendment.     2488.7235742324533
to conclude, mr president, allow me to remind the house that the directive needs a makeover which will make ucits even more attractive, but it does not need to be strapped into a european corset which will squeeze the life out of it.     1588.5389730743973
- (de) mr president, the introduction of the euro and the further liberalisation of capital markets has already produced a larger and deeper european financial area, which is helping to open up markets and create price transparency, but also to increase competition.     4032.6320185913564
at the same time, it is important to appreciate the economic importance of financial business dealings, particularly those we are concerned with today.     2822.841206551242
at the end of 1999 there were almost 15 000 investment funds registered as ucits with a total value of in excess of eur 2 billion, thereby accounting for roughly 75% of all funds assets.     9081.44541650347
hence the commission rightly cited the economic importance thereof in its proposals for draft directives, and i must say that i actually have every confidence in the commission' s proposal.     6652.549199038324
we are all aware how difficult it is to improve a legal framework for investment funds, and how difficult it is to construct the european passport for management companies around particular standards which are to apply even-handedly across the board.     2651.6697621171934
this therefore represents a step forward in respect of the legal and supervisory stipulations for financial markets and financial products.     3733.7460956470595

however, i do not believe that we can assess the risk associated with the otc derivatives, and i also think that as a european parliament it is our duty to inform and impress again and again on consumers - who expect investment funds to be comparable with a traditional savings agreement when it comes to long-term security, and indeed that is why they were known as investment savings - that these products do not bear comparison and that is why we need a minimum level of protection.     3241.871174854899
why do we not take our cue from the minimum level of protection afforded by the trade in transferable securities directive, article 11 for example, which relates to good practice?     5040.498277276871

mr president, commissioner, the proposal for a directive now under discussion brings with it several welcome reforms to the current legal provisions.     2567.138581694566
the preparatory work has largely consisted of a viable balance between increasing the efficacy and growth of the european investment markets and preserving a sufficient level of protection for consumers who buy fund units.     8809.38590658057
the greater the trust consumers have in investment market operations, the more consumers will be prepared to invest on the open market.     5899.20013650593
and the more consumers invest, the brighter the prospects will be in the european business money markets.     2119.9093830208053
it is quite clear that when businesses can more easily have access to their own capital-based finances their financial structure will be more firmly based, and, thus, opportunities for growth and investment will also improve.     1658.317859858302
whether or not the money will come onto the equity market in the form of direct investment or via ucits is not relevant here.     7148.315194112404
although in the committee on economic and monetary affairs we were very much in agreement with the aims of the legislative amendment, the debate we had was not the easiest one.     3386.5606009640915

however, i would like to express my position with regard to a few detailed proposals.     4913.199261969172
firstly, i am very satisfied that the proposal for a directive as a whole increases the scope of investment funds for targeting capital at different instruments.     7136.156474131451
as regards derivatives, i consider the position adopted by the committee justified - unlike mrs randzio-plath, for example.     20180.47080613057
the directive should not treat over-the-counter derivatives differently from standard derivatives because they are used in many member states and were also used before.     10243.408794259027
with regard to article 5g of the other proposal for a directive, the so-called article on delegation, i think the commission' s original proposal on rules concerning authorisation was better than the one now approved in the committee, so i would support some of mrs villiers' amendments to this point.     5081.475466347258

it simplifies things and brings things up to date.     2983.887243875744

we support the schmidt report on proposal no 1, but we regret the fact that the directive sets a lot of quantity limits that restrict the ability to spread risks.     10600.960275011006
we believe that a more liberal market is the best way forward.     1562.9444262886184

the important thing is for money to generate more money.     4576.352409601971
this is the only opportunity that we have to see to it that the interests of economists converge with those of people who want to earn money and have their mind set on dollars, like disney' s scrooge mcduck, who has dollar signs for eyes, except in this case the symbol is that of the euro.     4304.71872736375
