company restructuring in europe
the next item is the commission statement - company restructuring in europe.     432439.04782575666
.     1000.0
mr president, industrial change, which can be gradual or sudden, is driven by new technologies, worldwide competition or consumer preferences.     8809.43188564042
it has economic implications and an impact on employment and social cohesion, but it also provides major new opportunities when anticipated, prepared for and managed.     3265.4496324004767
the acceleration of technological progress and the increasing competitive pressure of globalising markets are pushing companies to rapidly adapt their production and company structures.     9208.795932556179
companies must be able to anticipate and identify at an early stage what they need to do to ensure the continuity of their activities.     9219.627181238444
sometimes this calls for mergers of activities.     2823.4170270945783
the commission is acutely aware of this situation and of the need for people to feel basic security in difficult times of change.     2889.241124992234
we are convinced that the cornerstone of successful adaptation to change is dialogue at all levels and with full and transparent information as its basis.     5271.261810222417
we have had in the recent past different experiences and approaches on how to deal with large-scale operations of corporate restructuring, often affecting the lives of large numbers of european people.     4663.987171666748
in some cases these operations were prepared well in advance, fully involving workers' representatives, with due care being taken to search for the least damaging solutions.     5072.3768272720945
where necessary, workers likely to be affected were prepared through employability measures to face the challenge of sudden unemployment.     9193.243093629173
in other cases these operations were launched and implemented with less care and concern for those affected.     2245.4049179170447
on every occasion, and as recently as last october in this house, the commission has highlighted the following basic positions.     5020.268212985489
firstly, decisions on corporate restructuring remain the prerogative as well as the responsibility of management.     7890.585034823121
this means that, when contemplating actions likely to lead to serious social consequences, job losses for example, companies should always actively search for and choose the least negative and least damaging solution.     9428.111443962629
secondly, when dismissals prove to be inevitable, the utmost must be done to reinforce the employability of those concerned.     5248.2735040527705
this requires anticipation, timely preparation and careful management of these processes.     16595.393109557546
thirdly, nothing can justify the absence of timely, effective and comprehensive information and consultation of the workers' and employees' representatives before such operations are launched or publicly announced.     8549.828190985709
worker involvement is always, and particularly in the event of corporate restructuring, not only a basic right but a necessary precondition for the social acceptance of such operations.     4827.105596961881
in the end it is also a decisive factor of their success, as no collectivity can survive and prosper without trust, dialogue, partnership and solidarity between its different components.     7452.522452393576
these basic ideas meet with a wide consensus in europe.     5575.289650812408
this is what intelligent restructuring is about.     5476.582824444867
it is more and more rare to see companies embark on damaging restructuring operations ignoring these fundamental concerns, as unfortunately was sometimes the case in the past.     4159.615249297056
we have built a wide range of instruments which meet these concerns in recent years in the european union.     4371.785482989191
we have set minimum requirements at european level, which all member states and all companies must observe when contemplating job losses, especially when they are massive, or preparing other decisions likely to affect workers.     5220.439065467867
the collective redundancies and transfers of undertakings directive and the european works council directive constitute our legal framework, protecting the workers' rights and ensuring fair standards in the field of information and consultation at national and transnational level respectively.     4656.726313222074
as regards the national and/or local level of employee involvement, the commission wishes to underline again the need for proper information and consultation of employees' representatives before any redundancies are decided or implemented, as provided for in the collective redundancies directive and in other national provisions.     4449.640448017165
i wish to recall that it is, in principle, up to national courts to deal with possible violations of these rules, if and when they are brought before them by national representatives.     3554.625840203894
however, even when existing rules or arrangements are formally respected this does not mean that management' s attitude is socially irreprehensible.     2040.5594244362803
in fact, the commission has argued for some time now that the rules we have established are not sufficient to deal with the challenges of today.     2666.903973258788
in a world characterised by permanent change and in the context of high levels of unemployment, the rules in force seem to us to be outdated as they do not allow or promote the key ideas behind our employment strategy and our approach to change, anticipation, risk prevention and employability.     4440.593990765494
that is why in november 1998 the commission presented a proposal for a directive on worker information and consultation which has since then received your strong support.     3288.110550880212
we thank you for that.     753.7614534277635
unfortunately, the other branch of the legislative power, the council, has not yet - more than one year after the presentation of the proposal - begun working on it.     2051.2049071048286
i hope that this debate contributes to reminding member states of the importance and urgent nature of this initiative.     5929.067431114079
we have recently heard from the portuguese presidency that a serious debate on this proposal will soon be launched.     2446.5993053724055
the forthcoming french presidency also seems willing to give this dossier the high priority it deserves.     8557.924046002412
the commission sincerely hopes that parliament and the council quickly finalise this important dossier which constitutes, in our view, the most effective answer to the concerns raised by cases such as the ones which brought us here today.     2505.0787871366756
as for the transnational level of employee involvement there is some indication that at least in one case the companies may have breached transnational information and consultation agreements which they had concluded with their european workforce.     6681.790818042784
they may have at least exploited some ambiguity in the legal and contractual framework in force.     5843.700087066673
the commission will contact the relevant national authorities to check this.     2521.065759995877
in any case, i wish to say to those who believe that their rights in that respect were ignored that adequate means of enforcement and redress are available to them, notably through relevant national jurisdictional instances.     4386.251379860733
the commission is aware of your request to proceed immediately with a revision of the european works council directive.     3588.7510243606075
as recognised in the report, which we will adopt in the forthcoming weeks, we accept that the directive contains some loopholes.     3667.673040572677
nevertheless, as my colleague, commissioner diamantopoulou has already underlined on other occasions, when deciding whether or not to revise the directive immediately, different considerations must be taken into account.     3916.159229677371
first, any revision of the directive would have little immediate effect on existing agreements, unless the fundamental principle of respecting the autonomy of the negotiating parties no longer applies.     5964.663904979237
secondly, an immediate revision would make things much more complicated as regards progress in respect of the other pending dossiers in this field like european company statutes and the national information and consultation proposal.     5006.5491939242
the successful outcome of these other dossiers could greatly facilitate the subsequent revision of the ewc directive and even provide some degree of a solution to the problems identified above.     5463.001408986835
that is why we tend to consider that it might be more appropriate in this context to proceed with the revision of the european works council directive only after the conclusion of those two dossiers and following a more complete evaluation of the application of the directive.     2566.940806434465
action is not limited to legislation.     1771.8013668397048
to encourage and support dialogue as the cornerstone of the successful adaptation to change the commission is also developing the recommendations of the lillehammer report by preparing the setting up of a european observatory on industrial change.     3833.276752227908
we are also developing another suggestion of that expert group, the managing change report.     4539.895045599162
let me finish by addressing some of the other concerns expressed in the draft resolution submitted to this house.     5262.6778691385825
as for the enforcement of the links between competition policy and social policy, let me remind you that there is a provision in the merger regulation which enables workers' representatives to be heard by the commission in the framework of the relevant procedure.     3488.8007889350424
generally speaking, we recognise the need for proper consideration to be given in different instances to all aspects surrounding these kinds of operations, including their effects in terms of employment.     4784.465817308731
as an example of this i mention that public authorities, notably the commission, have already taken measures through guidelines on national regional aid that came into force on 1 january this year so as to ensure that public funding remains associated with the recipients of regional aid and consequently with the jobs attached to the investment.     3114.5627085528135
to this end all regional aid is made conditional on the maintenance of the investment in question for a minimum period of five years.     2820.2569035071538
the same provision was included in the structural funds regulation which means that it applies to all investments cofinanced by the structural funds.     2500.44141129113
social cohesion and the interplay of policies at eu-level is the focus of the forthcoming lisbon summit.     2138.535326425813
i know we will be coming back to these issues again in the future to see how we can best make sure our policies are adapted and reformed to underpin economic and social progress in europe.     3326.596297547598
thank you very much commissioner.     434.5214474481301
mr gorostiaga atxalandabaso, i understand you have a point of order.     14353.412806304357
if it is a personal statement, would you be prepared to wait until the end of this debate, at which stage i would be happy to call you?     4169.902492469217
mr president, the problem is one of public honour in this parliament, it is not a personal statement.     2502.9700180891723
this morning, the president of parliament, mrs nicole fontaine, repeatedly refused to allow me to make a point of order.     9974.06037719216
unfortunately, this is not the first time that madam president has shown this inadmissible behaviour towards me.     4263.329598744532
in doing so, madam president has breached rule 142 that states that points of order shall take precedence over all other requests to speak.     7444.0659081291515
she has also breached rule 122 that gives the right to all the members of this house to make observations, observations which in this case refute the remarks made by another deputy, mr vidal-quadras.     9701.077482708848
this partisan behaviour of our president should be criticised most firmly.     8350.351515486904
i will willingly accept madam president' s apologies any time.     615854.1018061988
thank you for your statement.     2114.9990340881513
we will certainly make sure that the president is informed of your statement.     2433.5180166040927
i can assure you that as far as i am aware the president never likes to have a debate on issues in the middle of a vote.     6652.729511245945
therefore the house was probably quite willing to go ahead at that stage.     6244.161974580119
but thank you for your remarks.     4033.2972220281126
mr president, i welcome commissioner liikanen' s words, and i am contributing to the debate - we are all contributing to the debate - because, although the subject is broad in scope, it also concerns a specific fact.     2921.278918624326
the subject in question is of great political and social importance.     2264.5243314354916
free enterprise, free movement and free competition are essential pillars of the political and economic structure of the european union.     3465.8439974363987
company restructuring is part of the principle of free determination of investments, and must therefore be defended.     4175.6359404851
however, all too often we witness events which are in stark contrast with these principles.     4634.16443221422
today, we are witnessing one of these cases, which, on the surface, may seem to be of minor importance and affect only the specific industry, but which, if it is allowed to pass without an authoritative intervention by the european union, will become an unwritten rule which will cause problems for many of the member states and the union as a whole.     2585.0737719693298
an american company - goodyear - with production sites in italy, germany and england, is reviewing its production and relocation, justifiably, in its own view.     18360.419316227024
however, it seems to be doing it without observing the relevant community legislation, for it is certainly not giving reasonable notice to employees or heeding the obligation to render an account of their use of italian and european public finances.     2831.161953018106
in italy, commissioner liikanen, goodyear is not carrying out restructuring but closing a plant whose production covers 14% of the market and which goodyear itself defined as, 'having the most highly-skilled workforce of all its plants' , a plant which was opened with a total of eur 85 million of european capital.     4366.060621746992
no notice was given of the closure, and no intention was shown of discussing the fate of the plant or its workers, despite the fact that the public funds invested in that plant make it much more public than private.     4250.550639305568
it would be a serious matter if every non-european company were to learn to use europe as a bus where you do not have to pay for the ticket, you do not have to pay for cooperation, you benefit from using the facility and you leave without being accountable to anyone.     2950.1335661866037
europe has to guarantee every sort of freedom, including freedom to use one' s own money for the purposes of one' s own freedom and not to let others use it at will, others who make demands and then, when the break is made, refuse even to discuss the terms of the break, despite the fact that this is a break which has been declared unilaterally.     5914.013119828136
just now, commissioner liikanen was talking of restructuring which must not be uninformed.     1760.6643173594007
sadly, in this case, he has not taken into account those who are affected and seems not to have considered those carrying out the restructuring.     2305.0069341520402
i would also like to point out to commissioner liikanen that it is not easy to take a matter to a national court.     2851.876202672751
of course, it is always possible, but when jobs have been lost and factories closed down, the idea of going to court when proceedings are as lengthy as they are in italy is not very reassuring.     3222.2097107301865
commissioner liikanen, we expect an authoritative intervention.     9342.280778890708
as parliament, we are, of course, expecting to be able to contribute to the resolution you mentioned - we are ready and waiting - but we expect an authoritative intervention on this, which can on no account be allowed to reach its conclusion and be forgotten, as has almost happened already.     6254.483661164072
mr president, commissioner, ladies and gentlemen, for the second time within the space of four months, we find ourselves debating the restructuring of companies in europe.     10368.885082631388
this is - as it was the first time round - sparked off by the reorganisation of large companies, which is linked to a drop in employment.     2243.9642006847807
i would like to make it quite clear that the liberal group is against any political interference in companies, in terms of both finance and management.     3006.618581357898
our task is to draft legislation and to monitor to ensure that it is observed.     2381.053932010795
it appears that goodyear in italy received italian and european subsidies to the tune of approximately eur 40 million.     8302.973696356043
the company went under despite this.     5384.067919369283
this goes to show that jobs cannot be bought.     5387.100470896662
the market is a law unto itself.     5404.211385471433
as such, state aid costs the government twice: first in subsidies and then in unemployment benefit.     3556.8757400430154
surely this cannot be the role of your, my and the citizens' tax money?     2411.997878980464
all this, of course, does not mean that our group does not share the concern of the italian members regarding the effects of the closure on the region concerned.     3243.657373718261
my group is also opposed in principle to the naming of specific companies in this resolution, for two reasons: firstly because this creates selective responsibility.     5235.2778398361
a company is pilloried one day and not the next.     2102.8162369831707
why should this be the case?     1569.6236880496972
michelin caused a great stir.     430750.1262786827
but i did not hear a squeak when nissan and ibm were discussed, where, similarly, a great number of european employees were involved.     1862.6819328000302
secondly, this is not fair towards employees of other companies where reorganisations also take place and where it hurts too.     1999.4228032946419
my group has signed a joint resolution with epp and uen, in which, however, we made it clear right from the outset that we are against the naming of company names.     6531.538707221346
this is why the liberal group will vote against paragraph 8 and the second section of paragraph 9.
if these paragraphs are nonetheless adopted, my group will abstain from the final vote.     4376.500409947905
i do hope that this will not be necessary.     1297.2210845972465
mr president, the portuguese presidency and the commission' s strategic objectives for 2000-2005 both talk of full employment for which two essential conditions are required.     4349.147412364872
the first is to create new jobs for young people entering the labour market and the second is not to lose jobs.     3323.2009600506876
yet the current situation in europe provides us with clear examples of how jobs are being destroyed, particularly, and most seriously, by companies which are making huge profits.     2865.8262713126305
in the past, workers were asked to show their solidarity with companies in order to ensure their survival.     11607.439365393979
now the companies are making huge profits but are also making workers redundant.     2454.2802476725483
examples have been given in spain of michelin and abb-alsthom and my colleagues will give examples for italy.     5089.921460499413
there is also another hidden way of making workers redundant, as practised for example in spain by the ericsson company under the term 'externalising' .     3403.6463028570256
note that sensitive words like 'redundancy' are not used.     2067.444075132446
externalising services by subcontracting these to contract companies results in stable and permanent jobs being turned into precarious jobs without union or social rights.     5879.465512752417
this is just an underhand way of making workers redundant.     1739.1255367372917
commissioner, if the european institutions really believe in what they say about full employment, they must take clear steps to secure this.     2472.8439268396583
they must revise the directives in order to guarantee the participation of workers in these negotiations.     15483.693847997742
they must revise the directive on collective redundancies, reduce the working day, link european subsidies to a clear commitment to maintain employment and include social and labour clauses in the international agreements signed by the european union.     4468.971317522651
mr president, sadly, once again, parliament is having to discuss the restructuring of companies in europe.     2670.077310357484
yet again, workers are likely to lose their jobs.     11525.285440766036
this time, i am referring to the workers of the company goodyear in cisterna di latina.     55861.411965119434
as has already been pointed out, in one month' s time, 600 people and as many families could be deprived of their sole source of income.     3209.5109979345493
despite the commitment they have shown to the company and despite their high level of professionalism - which has made the cisterna plant one of the most productive in europe - these workers are likely to be removed from the production cycle, while goodyear, after years of benefiting from state and european subsidies, is totally free to walk out.     5777.213025268098
i do not think this is at all fair, either to the workers at the cisterna plant or to all those italian and european citizens whose taxes have, albeit indirectly, enabled goodyear to benefit from the state aids i have just mentioned.     4384.756272441135
the european commission and the council must, at last, assume all the necessary responsibilities, both with regard to the adoption of a common position on the information and consultation of workers and with regard to community and national aid, which must only be granted to companies who undertake to establish long-term agreements to protect jobs.     2476.1560511034454
today, we ask the commission to start by protecting the workers at goodyear in cisterna, and therefore to put as much pressure as possible on the company for it to come to an agreement as soon as possible, thereby ensuring the continued existence and operation of the cisterna plant.     4183.562595479112
mr president, commissioner, i would also like to remind the house that there is very little time available, and therefore that any measure - including those already cited by the commission in its introductory report - which we adopt must have an immediate, timely effect.     2644.7838437229534
mr president, commissioner, as far as restructuring goes, there is no doubt that european companies must adapt so that they are better placed to face new challenges or go under.     2765.3564905140715
we are well aware of this current economic context and that the purpose of restructuring is to rationalise corporate management.     3336.9311513933003
although it is laudable from an economic point of view to make structural changes in order to be more competitive and win market shares, this approach may be reprehensible if it is made at the request of financiers, often shareholders, with the sole unavowed aim of increasing the financial standing of the company in order to raise profits which they can then withdraw from the company.     4948.2875429712385
the various recent announcements referred to by previous speakers, be they by michelin or goodyear, are symptomatic in this respect.     20893.73439883536
rationalised management and improved profits should, in my view, serve the company and hence its employees first and foremost, and any financial benefits should follow.     7498.660312012556
however, what we witnessing today is the phenomenon of concentration and corporate regrouping and relocation, with all the familiar consequences, both on the quality of products and at the social level, especially in terms of unemployment, difficult working conditions and increased flexibility: consequences which have a drastic effect at the human level, especially on the family unit.     3562.569117244077
there is a very real risk of seeing a two-headed monster emerge on this planet, with poor countries providing the labour and rich countries providing the capital.     6155.955716824531
surely europe should act as a safeguard, maintaining a certain equilibrium between increased profitability and social welfare?     7642.704196548865
we must not forget that it is human resources which make a company function at its base and guarantee it good results.     2844.947543654483
finally, allow me to remind you that the third letter of the edd group to which i belong stands for 'difference' .     3370.225820461043
europe has a duty to make its voice heard as an alternative to savage restructuring for profit at all costs.     11896.317039948859
without doubt, the voice of difference in this monotonous flood of restructuring.     8491.731286454424
mr president, last month in the uk, in a region that neighbours mine, the michelin group announced significant redundancies at its stoke-on-trent plant due to company restructuring and increasing global competitiveness in the world market for tyres.     5729.250338144958
i, like all members present, am saddened by this loss of jobs.     15464.273525866924
equally, i am saddened by the announcement made by goodyear that has prompted this resolution.     44288.40971107116
but i doubt very much indeed whether a european works council would have been able to change this company' s decision.     2927.764764406725
this house is very good at producing resolutions and legislation and we sometimes, in this institution, forget that europe cannot survive alone.     4344.301533248589
we need multinational companies to base their businesses within our continent.     12324.8779114788
in my few months here, i have found it astonishing that whilst we all talk about the need to encourage businesses to grow within the eu, nearly every resolution we pass actually encourages businesses to leave.     5018.70120386298
in our last plenary part-session in brussels we voted on the end-of-life vehicles directive and managed to make the legislation retrospective, adding huge costs for the european motor manufacturers that they could not have predicted.     4330.14391042015
two days later, toyota, which employs several thousand people in my region, stated that this directive would not only increase the cost of cars but could well also mean redundancies in their derbyshire plant.     2038.3877101426533
in a world where surely less regulation is the key, we in this house seem to believe that we can add more regulation and costs on business and that they will stay within the european union, untempted by other locations elsewhere where there are fewer burdens.     2986.82378862787
we meps speak often about smes and have now come up with a unique way of creating them.     4020.046486033943
take a large successful business, add european regulations, and within months you have created a small one.     2488.9163975982324
perhaps before we criticise anybody outside this place we should take a good look at ourselves and what we do here.     4006.0292642575023
mr president, i will not use my speaking time by going into the individual cases debated here today.     2634.0618229650318
in this respect, i share mrs plooij-van gorsel' s view that we cannot talk about these in detail.     4309.454830397624
but maybe next time, we ought to discuss corus, the merger which has now taken place between our nederlandse hoogovens and british steel.     2738.088997989753
i would like to place this discussion in a somewhat wider context and examine the link between policy in the field of company mergers within the european union and policy in other fields.     3827.0001730540325
in this light, i am pleased that mr liikanen is present and we do not only have to hold this discussion, where the interests of employees are concerned, with mrs diamantopoulou but we can involve the commissioners who are active in other fields.     5522.328952841843
i would also like to advocate coherence in the commission' s policy from these different vantage points, unlike my predecessor, who holds the belief that there is no need for rules.     14085.561582009925
we need to gain an insight into what companies are like now.     5204.580479275479
at present, we are witnessing a number of large mergers between companies which originate in an anglo-saxon tradition and companies with a rhineland tradition.     5397.5732981485335
what remains of the concept of what a company should be like now is naturally very relevant: whether it is merely the property and toy of shareholders, or whether it is a more broadly based stakeholder society, which is meant to guarantee continuity for staff, consumers, suppliers and the local population and has, in addition, a social function.     2728.176698737331
as such, i would like to ask these various commission directorates and these various commissioners to provide a wider vision regarding corporate governments.     4563.353656479751
i think that it would also fit in well with the plans which have been presented over the past couple of days on good government in europe, as company policy forms part of this.     4794.324524984709
the government is talking to trade union and goodyear delegates in italy as well, in an endeavour to find a solution to the closure policy unilaterally adopted by the north american multinational in cisterna di latina, an area of central italy which has already been affected by other restructuring operations.     10697.998384947776
the closure means the direct or indirect loss of more than a thousand jobs and hundreds of families being abandoned to insecurity and sudden poverty.     6568.889160244113
the goodyear workers are putting up a magnificent fight, in the knowledge that they are defending not only their own interests but the interests of the goodyear workers in the different european countries as well, and there are many of them: france, germany, the united kingdom, poland, luxembourg and slovenia.     3751.439029290813
today, a small delegation of workers is here with us in the gallery, anxious to find out what their future will be, what solidarity the european union will offer them and what policy it will adopt to prevent the multinationals acting solely and exclusively in the interest of their own market and profits.     2982.9348309921675
the european union, in all of its aspects, must use all of its powers to resolve this and other disputes.     2646.5240534023333
mr president, earlier this week president prodi announced that one of the commission' s strategic objectives for the next five years would be to aim for full employment.     3957.542824064268
he acknowledged that 15 million europeans are currently looking for work and that unemployment is the main source of poverty and social exclusion.     2998.640947379398
what is more, the portuguese presidency sees the fight against unemployment as top priority.     5943.349859051261
yet, all too often these political priorities are invisible in everyday policies.     4570.967189453358
the commission' s decision to allow the abb-alsthom merger to go ahead is a case in point.     9123.32580468695
the new company has announced its intention to initiate a restructuring plan with the aim of making savings.     8450.525375150748
this global restructuring plan could lead to the loss of up to 12 000 jobs.     4238.363790946233
in my region of the uk in particular the employment consequences of this merger have been severe as a result of abb-alsthom having announced that it was cutting 270 jobs in britain with 95 jobs being lost in stafford and 112 in rugby.     3458.1428215893316
the company' s failure to inform the works council about the proposals for the merger is particularly worrying.     9285.25108364062
it is especially important in the case of large international companies where decisions are taken far from the employee.     2213.155744424075
point 17 of the community charter of fundamental social rights of workers stresses the need for consultation, adding that this shall apply especially in companies having establishments in two or more member states.     5610.325371921277
i do not think it is simply a matter of a community charter.     2046.6150554796534
eu legislation on this issue could not be clearer.     3291.2654676544917
the commission would do well in this sort of situation to make its decisions not simply on the basis of whether a merger will distort competition but also on the basis of the social and economic consequences.     2297.4959157248127
we need more joined-up policy making on these issues.     4123.966768142715
what we also need urgently is an impact assessment study on the effect of merger in every situation.     4419.236063396022
this is the only way of respecting article 127 of the treaty.     2042.981662240274
i welcome the statement made by commissioner liikanen, but i also look for the support of this parliament to ensure that the legislation which we have passed on workers' rights is implemented rigorously and to ensure that impact assessments on the workforce for proposed mergers are undertaken as a matter of course.     3519.9298553756707
i have received seven motions for resolution tabled pursuant to rule 37(2) of the rules of procedure.     5008.203687487528
that concludes the debate.     8638.001648140955
the vote will be taken later this evening.     2793.284437005088
the sitting was suspended at 3.50 p.m. and resumed at 4.05 p.m.
topical and urgent debate
the next item is the debate on topical and urgent subjects of major importance.     486282.75209073763
racist and xenophobic incidents in el ejido (spain)
the next item is the joint debate on the following motions for resolutions:
b5-0149/2000 by mr galeote quecedo and others, on behalf of the group of the european people' s party (christian democrats) and european democrats, on outbreaks of racism and xenophobia in the el ejido area in spain;
b5-0159/2000 by mr gasòliba i böhm and mr sánchez garcía, on behalf of the group of the european liberal, democrat and reform party, on racist and xenophobic incidents in el ejido (spain);
b5-0162/2000 by mrs díez gonzález and others, on behalf of the group of the party of european socialists, on the outbursts of racism and xenophobia in el ejido (spain);
b5-0169/2000 by mr puerta and others, on behalf of the confederal group of the european united left/nordic green left, on outbursts of xenophobia in el ejido (spain);
b5-0175/2000 by mr bautista ojeda and others, on behalf of the group of the greens/european free alliance, on xenophobia-related incidents in el ejido (spain).     26684.102838442894
mr president, i am reassured by the fact that the first three speakers in this debate are intimate with this situation given our african origin.     10619.996637339382
i must firstly congratulate the groups in this house on the high degree of consensus achieved in the motion for a resolution.     4717.3955826442825
i am aware of the efforts made by members to avoid using an issue such as this for electoral purposes.     7135.89015431847
secondly, i must also assert my total conviction that neither spain nor andalusia is xenophobic or racist.     16276.217969831037
of course this does not mean that i do not totally condemn the violence used by individuals or groups who deserve to be prosecuted firmly and rigorously by the law, and by the law alone.     3542.90038473498
we should be tackling the issue of immigration in a comprehensive and rigorous manner.     2967.1161653992103
these events, which are unfortunately overshadowing the political decisions in this area, confirm without doubt that the development of the tampere agreements and title iv of the ec treaty must be an urgent priority.     3932.543639646043
today' s problems cannot be solved in five years.     160554.39870817523
they will take a long time, as we are finding out.     1947.0539057447518
the legislation of the member states must be brought into line with the agreements of the governments in tampere, as paragraph 7 of the motion states.     4932.4588094838755
legal immigrants must be integrated socially without any excuses or delays.     13421.878853026617
furthermore, the labour market must be organised realistically so that jobs held by third country nationals are subject to labour legislation on wages, social security and social protection in terms of equality.     3354.4524196312977
this will eradicate the labour exploitation and markets in marginalised people, which are resulting in the events condemned by the motion, and which are the real basis of the current social problem.     3470.5999526840233
solidarity, dialogue and agreements between local, regional, national and european institutions and employers, unions and the immigrant communities themselves, whose countries of origin must also undoubtedly assume some responsibility, are essential.     5695.88538211395
collaboration with the countries of origin on this issue is vital.     15549.056919723387
in this respect, the action plans approved by the high level group and the tampere european council must be allocated a budget.     1994.954342958364
specifically, i must stress in this debate the plan for morocco.     9444.239036427032
this is a country with which the european union has an effective relationship and common interests and which should take particular priority in mutual cooperation and collaboration.     2353.806009193564
finally, this house must tackle the phenomenon of immigration with sufficient calm to avoid increasing tension in a debate which must go much further than the appropriate words and condemnations prompted by such behaviour.     5273.498839484678
this is a very important social debate in the creation of a european area in which the right to freedom, security and justice will be recognised in the future charter of fundamental rights.     1921.8575426401771
mr president, commissioner, whenever a serious social problem occurs, it always has a political basis.     2132.9119797068865
this is true of the disgraceful events which have recently occurred in el ejido.     12176.686058470537
the proof of this lies in the fact that the groups in this house have reacted and tabled several motions for resolutions.     5429.556163870444
we all regret the deaths which have occurred and the violent and disproportionate reactions against the immigrant workers.     5979.942012888065
we all, i believe, agree in our condemnation of the xenophobia and racism which was apparent in certain actions.     6009.2231025613455
these events certainly have their origin in, among other factors, the absence of a clear immigration policy allowing the affected communities to live together respectfully and peacefully.     5679.529051805534
we hope that the events in el ejido will serve as a lesson to everyone and will not be repeated.     6267.619423819348
yet it is not enough to regret the situation.     1832.1853699146775
we do not want africa to become our cross, in fact quite the contrary.     3405.692789930585
we must therefore act.     2649.303157936541
i should like to highlight certain measures which we are trying to introduce in another european region affected by immigration - the canary islands.     10975.376634960974
firstly we are creating a network of reception centres for minors.     2760.4985874734834
secondly we are conducting a programme of social integration.     4754.66639723836
thirdly we are carrying out education programmes on tolerance and respect.     6147.38599544321
finally we are producing a census of immigrants.     7785.159208766173
i hope that these measures will help towards ensuring compliance with the tampere agreements on immigration and the new spanish act on aliens.     6263.422673011532
in particular they must ensure that people arriving in europe do not lose their dignity.     6312.279232394331
mr president, last week, in the andalusian town of el ejido, we saw scenes which were inappropriate to the european union in the year 2000.
this was acknowledged by the commission president, romano prodi, on 9 february when he confirmed that what had occurred in el ejido went against the very principles of the european union.     6641.644463228796
these events have caused us great pain and shame and have awoken our fears given the extreme racism, xenophobia and intolerance demonstrated.     13861.741869244737
it is incomprehensible that ways of coexisting and ensuring mutual respect cannot be found between two communities which are mutually beneficial.     5969.025264354241
the only way to prevent these events happening again, in a place where there are large communities of immigrant workers, is through social integration.     2716.7748295936103
this requires a firm economic, political and social commitment from the competent authorities.     2421.522995724085
we are all immigrants.     2291.1359422128753
for generations human beings have not always remained within the borders of one nation state but have constantly migrated.     5748.247921505092
geographical mobility is not an invention of the twentieth century and neither are its economic and work-related causes.     3244.740108795117
we are all immigrants, if not ourselves personally, then through one of our relations or ancestors.     1241.810952447543
however, instead of producing these events which we are condemning, migration must generate social, cultural and economic enrichment.     8774.96689229388
what happened in el ejido is unfortunately not exclusive to this one town of the european union.     4177.7993025785245
we must therefore firstly condemn clearly and emphatically the brutal racist and xenophobic outbursts of certain inhabitants of el ejido against immigrant workers in the area.     22510.257565533313
a whole community should not have been held responsible, criminalised and attacked for one regrettable but isolated incident.     2141.086556945026
secondly, all the authorities must act immediately.     1986.0133097905255
coordination between them is the key to combating this type of xenophobia and racism.     3295.227287071005
in particular, local authorities and mayors have a direct responsibility to prevent any demonstration of racism or xenophobia, especially when this involves violent and aggressive acts against people and their property.     5252.424448965565
these authorities must also help to achieve difficult agreements between the parties involved and must not hinder these, as has happened with the mayor in question.     4026.197058536212
thirdly, the political parties must assume direct responsibility for the political actions of their public officials.     4994.069350063248
they must prevent any individual action or declaration which is contrary to the principles and values of coexistence and tolerance expressed by the european union.     3776.790842292946
finally, the european union must be responsible for monitoring the application of its principles.     1845.3315216783685
it must prevent any racist or xenophobic demonstrations in its territory and support and collaborate with national, regional and local authorities and non-governmental organisations and associations in the application of these principles.     2767.016407907452
this must occur within a framework of constant action to combat racism and xenophobia and to encourage the integration of all immigrants resident in the eu.     4312.076529991412
mr president, initially our group did not fully understand the need to table this motion for a resolution on events which seemed to be a local phenomenon.     4123.957137887071
however, the incidents in el ejido are a symptom of a phenomenon which is present throughout europe and which deserves our full attention.     7458.937613870491
we thought we had eradicated the terror of intolerance which racism had unleashed in europe.     8757.515508834784
yet, over the years, social disintegration in europe has been combining with an increase in migration from surrounding countries and increased awareness of human rights to develop into this climate of racism and intolerance.     5786.505503719577
this is why what seemed to be a local and isolated phenomenon in el ejido is actually a worrying symptom which is coinciding with the emergence of parties like haider' s.
this house therefore proposes the adoption of urgent measures encompassing all the relevant factors and to be applied immediately.     7049.707771807322
we must ensure respect for human rights, tolerance and multiculturalism and we must obtain an increase in budgets and action lines for social and health issues.     2756.535342583112
in this respect we must not forget that the original victim' s attacker was suffering from a mental illness for which he had been inadequately treated.     2788.453244404774
the current difficulties must also be taken into account so that this town can adopt the necessary measures, as indicated previously.     2073.4158587609218
finally, as our nobel prize winner, josé saramago, has said, we are all in each other and as jesus christ said, although saramago disagreed with this, we should not just love one another, more importantly, we should respect one another.     7079.345365288966
mr president, commissioner, andalusia is one of the most tolerant and multiracial regions of the european union.     2224.3564393111533
since time immemorial it has welcomed all those who have decided to move there and regarded them as andalusians from day one, whether or not they were born in our region.     4147.057830297547
therefore, as an mep, but primarily as an andalusian, i can feel only sadness, repugnance and condemnation for the xenophobic and racist events which have occurred and which are foreign to this tolerant community.     3982.240726948942
yet a worrying situation is apparent.     2885.0380857740747
all decisions which are or are not taken and all agreements which are or are not negotiated by the community institutions with regard to the economy of the principal sectors in disadvantaged areas like andalusia may indirectly lead to these serious incidents.     2875.9283968413615
i am referring, for example, to the lack of agreement with morocco and to the lack of solutions for the fishing industry, part of whose fleet is moored in almería.     5725.572517001151
i am also referring to the erratic management of the import scheme for fruit and vegetables by the eu which is dominating relations between almería and morocco.     8555.906956899014
i am also referring to the immigration policy of the regions, member states and european union which, due to actions or omissions, is leading to a few territories like almería having to support this migratory pressure from north-west africa on their own.     6128.264204089358
all these factors have unfortunately been accumulating over time and are now allowing those who are cruel, intolerant, racist and destructive, of which all our countries have some examples, but thankfully a minority, to light the fuse of xenophobia.     1584.6359189705604
anyone, whether european or not, who lives and works in andalusia, is an andalusian and deserves our consideration and respect.     6385.661722374057
the eu can and must speak out.     1404.7656830492704
for you, the south is andalusia but for us andalusians it is africa.     5226.585602536947
we must therefore ensure that the eu provides every possible resource so that the economy of the south can prosper and enable a society to develop with more tolerance and respect for one another.     2695.150962071366
this has not been achieved with the solutions proposed to date which have quite simply been based on looking the other way.     1317.9669372684016
mr president, commissioner, i speak from experience acquired during many visits to el ejido over the years.     10118.018712990002
i was therefore aware how the situation was developing.     2928.8917566195696
here in the european parliament, as in many other institutions, we have watched this situation with concern and were beside ourselves when we heard what had happened.     1852.7316273483452
this is because we felt the problem to be ours, as it was.     3188.463843854177
in el ejido there is a serious social problem, problems of integration and employment and also a grave problem of public safety which acted as the detonator.     2421.868027063992
these events have also acted as a wake-up call to our consciences.     11297.007769498807
here in this house, in spain and in many other countries, we have all fixed on el ejido.     3309.4561685477847
yet what we do and say here and the concern we express will really serve no purpose unless this leads to the adoption of a much more determined attitude to finding out what is really happening, not only in el ejido, but in many other places where this type of problem may be occurring at any given moment.     3406.323873322151
we all agree in our condemnation of intolerance and we also agree that we must look forward.     3971.158662099924
we will not find the guilty parties in this house or in many other places.     3023.548479742388
those responsible for what happened in el ejido are a minority who set out to satisfy their desire for vengeance on the immigrants.     10997.404190730706
we must bear in mind that vengeance is not a solution.     9217.846255729644
these problems can only be solved by close cooperation between all the authorities in order to achieve social integration and respect for all working there.     4565.182227308678
this therefore requires a determined fight against illegal immigration and profound respect for the rights of immigrant workers who are legally working in el ejido and throughout europe.     7718.684147999359
mr president, this resolution expresses solidarity with the democratic forces in spain fighting for human rights.     3985.783121999021
all the spanish democrats wounded by what is happening in their country have our full and complete support.     4072.3876614038954
it was not only immigrants who were the victims of atrocities; the democratic system in spain has been held up to ridicule, despite having demonstrated its tremendous capacity to evolve over the last 20 years.     2790.8530973415263
employers would be lost without immigrants.     8756.909532769738
working conditions are so hard and so deplorable that no spaniard will accept them.     4101.969936391502
low wages, the lack of housing - some are living in cardboard huts - the lack of access to social services and the de facto system of apartheid are abominable and unacceptable and are not worthy of the respect which every human being deserves.     4249.291528724304
what happened at el ejido is the result of inadmissible racist and xenophobic practices in europe today.     12429.99063250328
for four days, the immigrants were collectively hounded.     3022.51168828204
women and children were beaten.     1402.6525945478572
fleeing from all quarters, hundreds of immigrants took refuge on a hill on the outskirts of the town.     8188.563035265899
we are right to blacklist haider' s austria; but we cannot allow little local haiders to practice policies of racial exclusion and merciless economic domination.     4112.415186430403
what happened in spain could happen in any country in the union if we are not careful.     2727.022281764372
we all have a duty to put our own house in order.     4758.427468231046
i support this proposal for a resolution, especially the points which refer to the application of article 13 of the amsterdam treaty, and point 11 of this resolution establishing a link between the structural funds allocated and the fight against exclusion.     3749.090175786242
it would be to our credit were the european parliament to roundly condemn these events and support all proposals tabled in order to ensure that the rights of immigrants in spain and throughout the union are respected.     4851.959803386482
mr president, acts of racist and xenophobic violence are taking hold at the heart of europe.     12155.612936266321
everywhere this violence is exacerbated by the spread of extremist movements.     35131.00422169417
we must take practical steps today to put an end to all attacks of a physical nature.     3917.2159481348676
no european society can truly flourish when it harbours the risk of seeing one section of its population fall prey to the hatred of the other.     5177.932019753819
having received a delegation from the austrian human rights association here yesterday and having seen what has happened in the province of almería, it is easy to understand that we could easily have prevented this resurgence of racist hatred.     8610.1176769401
these violent scenes at el ejido are so similar to archive pictures of alabama in the 1950s, when the kkk terrified the black population, that it is hard to tell them apart.     2743.8080463958154
they also put us in mind of germany in the 1930s, when the nazis were persecuting the jews.     6295.599485305164
without wishing to lump everything together hysterically, because each period is different, we have failed to learn the lessons of history.     6875.86436881483
that is my conclusion today.     1626.520390894682
because this witch hunt which broke out in the province of almería should have been foreseen.     8823.898356633
it is the result, first and foremost, of the ghettoisation and exploitation of immigrant labour.     2499.2772933866368
three annual crops of moroccan and african workers support 100 000 people and manage to bring in an annual turnover of nearly eur 2 million; and yet 60% of them have no running water in a region where, as we know, the temperature may reach 40 degrees in summer.     7719.7306305860675
we also know that many of them only earn eur 30 a month, while their fellow european citizens can earn three times that.     3078.932896712905
this precarious wage-earning position weakens these immigrants and their families and makes them vulnerable.     5663.13792907221
the irresponsibility of certain elected representatives and the barely disguised complicity of the authorities have contributed to this racist explosion.     9480.921449551299
we should counter such acts of terror, mr president, by setting up a parliamentary committee of inquiry as quickly as possible so that we can decide how we can succeed in preventing other conflicts of a similar nature elsewhere.     3939.772457606601
my fellow member, mrs roure, was right to remind us just now that we all need to put our own house in order.     2555.9818815514623
finally, and i think this is important: why not have the honesty today to relay what was disclosed in a recent un study explaining that, if spain wishes to preserve its work, retirement and pension potential, it is no good just giving 30 000 immigrants a year legal status; it needs to be prepared to accept 12 million immigrants between now and the year 2050?     3853.8799454659884
we must insist today as a matter of urgency on the right to education and the duty to remember, to recall the dates in history when our countries teetered on the brink of horror.     7090.607511009533
we need to educate our children on these matters and finally have the courage to recognise that we will need to be prepared to accept many more immigrants in years to come.     5759.799803486838
it just remains for me to urge you all to attend the demonstration on saturday.     7670.752219750612
we must take to the streets of europe to protest against these alliances with extremists which are a disgrace to europe and which lead our youth astray.     8107.923301108706
mr president, since the signature of the treaty of amsterdam, the european union has had responsibility for combating racism and xenophobia.     6152.020913742973
two types of measure are required for this purpose.     9691.869401604845
the first should seek the integration of people of non-european origin and the other should aim to organise immigration.     4341.425191575669
with regard to the first, the commission has sent us a series of proposals, contained in two directives and an action plan, which aim to apply the following decision taken by the heads of state and government in tampere.     5232.7121633620845
this states that 'the european union must ensure fair treatment of third country nationals who reside legally on the territory of its member states.     6142.6617870043665
a more vigorous integration policy should aim at granting them rights and obligations comparable to those of eu citizens' .     3740.536067860649
this is totally contrary to what we have seen and found recently in el ejido where there are irregularities, labour exploitation and indecent living conditions, which have been condemned in the past.     4032.3417845083845
i hope for prompt implementation of the measures to achieve integration.     9160.23959691924
i also hope that the governments which produced such wonderful words in tampere, including spain, will work diligently to apply these.     10986.499396646224
i have less hope about the development of an effective and fair european policy in accordance with the statement made by the council on immigration.     3920.5731713619734
the only proposal which has been made is for the directive on family reunification.     2125.8258717795293
this is a key issue, as we have seen recently in the media that there are many men on their own in el ejido.     2584.182280171424
yet we do not know whether this has been accepted by the governments.     1708.7711917464653
outside the community arena, some governments are making efforts to renew their immigration policies.     5640.080666406378
this is occurring in my country, despite the government, the party in power being the only one which does not like the new law on aliens.     3190.5021412426813
the objective of combating racism and xenophobia, explained in the treaties, should be the objective of all european institutions and politicians.     2579.584890849466
on 28 february 1998 the european political parties, including the spanish popular party, signed the charter of european political parties for a non-racist society.     4625.385300503357
this requires coherent behaviour, yet local leaders of the popular party are throwing more fuel on the raging fire and boycotting the attempts of other institutions to put this out.     3870.9989752388037
the party to which the mayor of el ejido belongs should, according to the commitments signed in this charter, act forcefully.     17757.12382685395
it is only, as stated by mr hernández mollar, through unity, responsibility and firmness that el ejido, spain and europe will be able to overcome these events of which we are deeply ashamed.     10441.964341776873
mr president, one cannot but feel disgust and indignation at the scenes of lynching, no less, of moroccan day labourers which took place in el ejido.     9452.309819820513
we do not the extent to which far-right commandos were to blame and how far the common people stupid enough to follow these commandos were to blame.     7068.92668905551

.     1000.0
i would like to begin by congratulating the honourable member on the way in which he introduced this extremely important debate.     4166.678195713453

racist and xenophobic acts are wholly unacceptable within our community, wherever they take place.     11096.34750195871
they fly in the face of the very principles on which - as mr havel said yesterday - the european union was founded: principles of liberty, democracy and respect for human rights.     3955.632616006157
of course progress has been made in recent years but we still have to work together towards a climate of tolerance in which racism and xenophobia are regarded as completely beyond the pale, completely unacceptable, while at the same time we deal very robustly with incidents such as the ones we are discussing this afternoon.     2311.1026611273473
we need, in our judgement, to focus on a package of measures including: first, the introduction of effective, anti-discrimination legislation at union level; second, implementation of a common immigration policy with regard to the nationals of non-member states to promote, in practical terms, the integration of the immigrant population in member states' societies; third, the introduction of measures to combat social exclusion.     7373.469148781142
the european union has already laid the basis for community legislation on combating discrimination on the grounds of race.     3404.444820127851
the insertion of article 13, which was referred to during the debate, dealing with non-discrimination in the treaty establishing the european community, is one of the most significant changes of the last revision of the treaties.     3618.6767472898864
since the treaty of amsterdam came into force the commission has presented a package of proposals for the implementation of article 13 including, in particular, a proposal for a directive on the application of the principle of equal treatment regardless of race or ethnic origin.     5497.986395765495
this proposal is under examination by the council and the european parliament.     1164.5252441034781
the commission hopes very much that this stage will soon be complete.     1889.3868160908723
the conclusions of the tampere special european council, as mr hernandez mollar said, clearly illustrated the will of the heads of state and government as regards the introduction of a common policy on immigration and the principles on which it should be based, with particular emphasis on the union' s policy on the respect and promotion of the rights of migrants.     5652.323083724333
at tampere the heads of state and government also emphasised the importance of approximating national legislation on conditions for admission and residence of third country nationals based on a shared assessment of the economic and demographic developments in the member states as well as the situation in the countries of origin.     5169.149385385213
the european council also considered it important to step up the prevention of illegal immigration.     2457.7591381214884
its objective here is to secure the rights of victims of criminal networks organising illegal immigration, with particular reference to the rights of women and children.     4815.941197175944
those who organise illegal immigration should be prosecuted with the utmost vigour.     17377.860630302635
the word that is used about them in chinese society is 'snake-heads' .     2005.3700840964175
it seems to me a fitting description of them.     4699.842553160551
let me also draw attention to the work on drawing up a european union charter on fundamental rights, which has now reached the drafting stage.     5161.778298800384
those rights are, by definition, universal.     12869.718826757873
the charter will extend to third country nationals in member states even though certain rights will be reserved to nationals of member states of the union.     4919.77843487446
the el ejido incidents were shocking, as several members have said in their speeches.     23824.260072251658
but trying to take something positive from them, i hope they will spur us on to translate our aspirations about tolerance, fairness and freedom into real and effective policies that will help to transform the lives of all those families and individuals in the european union who cry out for our understanding and our help and who deserve it, not at some indefinite moment in the future, but here today.     4607.907439600744

we should stop financing this regime and we should certainly stop supplying arms for the war.     6065.2060925612495
mr president, the facts are well-known.     2377.529332659968
on 24 december, the angolan court sentenced a correspondent of the portuguese paper, expresso, to a year' s imprisonment and a fine of usd 20 000.
the journalist had brought to light a corruption scandal in the president' s cabinet.     26067.024307520896
the trial took place behind closed doors and the defendant was only allowed one witness.     17255.999627300487
another journalist was threatened with his life during a parliamentary debate last month by a member of parliament of the mpla government party.     9487.149408515317
these are only two examples of a trend in angola to silence any government critics.     9357.030675489885
over the past year, more than twenty journalists have been arrested for alleged slander or criminal offences against state security.     9116.350175986176
their only crime was to criticise what human rights organisations have been denouncing all along, namely the large-scale corruption and disappearance of hundreds of millions of dollars, paid by the oil companies to the angolan government.     8383.63673831144
even the british minister, peter hain, conceded this recently.     6329.149179789419
we should ensure that the oil revenue is not used merely to finance the war or to line the pockets of prominent members of government, he told president dos santos last month.     10172.924975894008
but, at the same time, we note that great britain and also portugal are continuing to channel military aid to the government.     2485.441334257408
there is indeed a great deal of hypocrisy regarding angola.     22714.582368695555

in my opinion, angola is a test case.     15280.551824444708

native americans in the us - dineh
mr president, the american leaders do not seem to have learnt a great deal from the criticism of how they treated the indians and other indigenous peoples.     18656.687079140327
we have to admit that our european sense of responsibility is no more developed in this respect.     2611.9008456288097
once again, an indigenous people' s right of existence is being threatened.     32066.168799269853
once again, the individuality, dignity and health of a people are being sacrificed to the higher economic interest.     4044.845682440699
once again, we are losing a piece of our planet' s cultural and ecological heritage, despite the many treaties and resolutions on human rights, on the protection of minorities, rights of indigenous peoples, etc., which have already been ratified.     7658.794036865198

there are hardly any reintegration programmes for people who have been relocated to the so-called new lands.     4350.415111997554
it very much remains to be seen whether these new lands will be safe in terms of public health.     3767.6281789062305
it is of key importance that the american authorities provide greater clarity regarding the way in which they are trying to offer these families a dignified alternative in keeping with a traditional way of life.     3621.77842118486
it is also important that a debate is held where the long-term effects of mining in this region are discussed and weighed up in economic, ecological and social terms.     1905.7575530131394
the credibility of the industrialised north is at stake.     19633.34853492251
indeed, what right do we have to demand of developing countries that they give their minorities a voice, that they protect their valuable natural areas and that they respect human rights, if it transpires that we are unable to meet these demands in our own countries?     3278.5048747865267
mr president, as mr van hecke rightly pointed out, this is about human rights and about the rights of indigenous peoples, and parliament must speak up, especially vis-à-vis good friends, and the united states is a good friend.     8742.568729178129
this is about the repression of a minority which unfortunately goes back a long time.     1837.5876401770615
it is up to the european parliament to protect vulnerable people.     1600.6420951555085
so we need to do this.     953.4326342175397
this is also about respecting a culture, a language, a way of life and a people' s religion, a vulnerable culture which has nearly disappeared.     4917.721002576632
it is important to point this out.     1491.677141384796
this is why it is necessary - and mr van hecke has already mentioned this, and this is also what we clearly ask for in our resolution - that the role of mining in this vulnerable desert area is reassessed.     3933.6467189383065
we have just approved the water directive here in parliament.     1290.2116336711647
the water which these people need to exist is under threat because the groundwater strata there are being drilled by a mining company, and this is why the traditional way of life of the people who live there is being threatened.     3827.426792269611
the forced relocation scheme must be stopped unconditionally.     85726.62024939327
people should no longer be forced to move.     12693.991677771754
over the past 25 years, no less than 25 000 people have been forced to move and this must stop.     2571.740431799972
if people need to move at all, they need to be given a viable alternative.     2988.978496963879
this is not the case here.     1048.0100240446916
the area where the people in question are now being taken - and this is also the reason for this resolution - is a polluted area which is economically not suitable.     1488.4912878922269
this is why i think that we as european parliament need to ask the american authorities in any case to suspend the relocation scheme under discussion and, if necessary, abandon the scheme altogether.     6096.183356985913
mr president, ladies and gentlemen, this is my maiden speech and i am delivering it with a great deal of pleasure and conviction as the topic is human rights.     13313.026700803339
in terms of human rights, europe has a very eventful past and this is why we have become to some extent the cradle of these human rights.     1353.5408266924032
we need to continue to stand up for these rights, especially in our own member states and societies, but also elsewhere.     2491.5894113478653
as mr sterckx said, we certainly need to do this vis-à-vis the united states which, after all, is close to us in terms of democratic values.     2943.016533187704
the treatment of the native americans in many respects flies in the face of the most fundamental human rights.     5516.626746504582
the account of the dineh indians is telling.     34627.0777129753
with the discovery and, certainly, the exploitation of black gold - and, later on, uranium and oil - all possible means were used to remove these people from their land and to send them to regions with few prospects other than poverty, hardship and contamination.     6765.708203450526
the area in arizona which is to be the new land for the dineh indians was hit in 1979 by the worst nuclear disaster in us history.     4912.76468832537
it is now the intention to send the last 4 000 dineh indians to this land.     13841.626938405756
the fight of these indians is being backed by more than 250 ngos and, thanks to the efforts of the belgian 'mother earth' organisation, we are being kept informed of this situation.     3593.743419060063
this too is characteristic of american society, which hears little about it.     3747.787308984986
they get het up about wars and genocide on other continents, but wrongly keep quiet about the genocide taking place in their own country.     4238.107882148441
today, the dineh indians are facing their final fight for their right to land, for their right to dignity and, above all, for their right to their way of life.     6121.816844083751
there are few of them left and they are up against a very powerful, economic adversary.     1049.7176911919646
mr president, commissioner, ladies and gentlemen, i endorse the many comments which have been made by the previous speakers, but i would like to emphasise a few things.     5278.93687114672
the dineh people belong to the north-american navajo indians.     97215.60794257243
they are facing expulsion and have been fighting a battle of survival for dozens of years.     11356.024087637126
they have been living in harrowing living conditions for years.     3718.7130039106023
the deprived conditions in which they are living at the moment is forcing them to compromise against their better judgement under the financial and economic pressure exercised by the coal industries.     4604.879852614065
the relocation act, which was introduced in 1974 and aims to re-distribute the land of the navajo reservation, authorised the relocation of the peoples.     26930.53464987339
this relocation is, of course, unacceptable because human rights are being violated in economic, social and cultural terms.     4337.16101832449
the dineh indians are being moved like pawns and, in that way, lose not only their past and the land which is so extremely important to them - the land of their forefathers - but also any prospect of work and sources of income.     3587.0999411754506
the relocation act was pushed through in congress by a group representing the coal mining industry.     51588.03677805738
congress never questioned where the people living in the area would have to move to or how relocation would impact on their lives.     7886.780800248842
the population was not allowed any input.     10909.279782144136
this resembles a kind of trade in human beings.     25973.56332860861
the relocation scheme was implemented and the dineh people were moved to an area contaminated by uranium.     14852.42731493691
after the collapse of the dam in new mexico in 1979, which resulted in the spill of 370 000 cubic metres of radioactive water and eleven hundred tonnes of pure uranium, this place is one of the world' s most contaminated areas.     19727.952418362296
the united states administration, the coal industry and all of us need to acquit ourselves of our responsibility and provide a sound alternative: either the soil is cleaned or the dineh indians are allocated a proper place where provision is made for all kinds of support, be it in the psychological, social, cultural or budgetary field.     4820.537664750849
it is high time we respected the rights of the indians.     6742.800779185384
this is why we ask you to vote for this resolution: we in europe should not be intimidated by american interests which are being pushed by financial, religious and industrial groups.     2344.7879242269773
so let us contact the united states now!     5331.852047894483
madam president, despite the protests by the international press, we still do not know the fate of mr andrey babitsky, the courageous russian journalist who has worked intensively to ensure free and independent information on the ordeal of the civilian population of chechnya and the destruction of the city of grozny.     11998.068915051412
among the contradictions in the accounts that have been given, there has also been an attempt to reassure international public opinion over mr babitsky' s fate, by confirming the theory that there was an exchange of prisoners with chechen fighters which, inter alia, if this did actually take place, would constitute a breach of international law, which certainly does not provide for the involvement of civilians in such circumstances.     11232.667572869139

cambodia
mr president, commissioner, it is because of my experience in the balkans, when i was there on duty, that i call on you today to support the efforts of the secretary-general of the united nations to bring those responsible for the genocide in cambodia to trial before an independent court under the aegis of the united nations.     5664.037832119019
i do not think that reconciliation is possible after such murderous tragedies, unless those responsible for starting them are brought to trial and sentenced.     3915.8918811573003
nor do i think that the people who were their victims can give them a fair trial.     4425.395598948832
i became convinced of this in sarajevo when i had to negotiate the periodic exchanges of prisoners which accompanied each successive ceasefire agreement, none of which unfortunately lasted, between the parties to the conflict.     7861.767783808071
every time, just as the exchange was about to take place, we found men missing from the meticulously drawn up lists.     3041.260735104327
every time, when we asked why, the reply was the same, 'we cannot release him: he is a war criminal' .     6086.440494065342
in the emotion, pain and fury of the agreements, it was impossible to break the vicious circle of blood and vengeance.     11050.898917013757
if we want to help the cambodians with the task of reconciliation on which they have embarked, we need to try and sentence those responsible at the highest level and thus exonerate the simple underlings and all those who were willingly or forcibly dragged along behind them.     4306.814729735042
i expect ta mok and his main lieutenants to be tried and sentenced by the establishment of a tribunal whose impartiality is guaranteed by the united nations.     7592.16479709099
this will be the only way for the cambodians to pardon those who simply followed them and to achieve lasting peace.     6824.555663260288
mr president, it became known this week that two prominent cambodian opposition papers have been threatened with closure because they are reported to have insulted the king and prime minister.     13404.831932283074
these papers actively support the opposition politician, mr sam rengie.     54212.554445778056
