reforming the commission
the next item is the commission statement on reforming the european commission.     35254.09315360951
i shall give the floor straight away to the president, mr romano prodi.     5601.996314091849
- (it) madam president, ladies and gentlemen, last july, here in your presence, i made a solemn commitment to reform the commission.     14882.826850342279
i would just like to take a moment to recall the events of last july: the outgoing commission had been in crisis, i had just been nominated and you gave your preliminary approval of that nomination.     3551.3283294548223
the climax of the chain of events was my undertaking to reform the commission, to reform it thoroughly, completely revolutionising the commission' s way of working.     4510.580970622632
today, that commitment has been fully discharged: you have the reform here, before you, in the form of an institutional white paper which has just been adopted by the commission.     1887.7253851936646
i would like to emphasise that, on that occasion too, the commission voted unanimously, with one voice.     4454.1718103899175
briefly, these are the main points of the reform.     1541.474123032259
one: definition of political priorities and translation of these into practical measures through the focused use of resources.     3504.754377621892
this is at the heart of the system, and it is this, the setting of political priorities and the focused use of resources, which will enable the political sphere to assert its primacy and to take its responsibilities fully on board.     3331.2735997809013
in order to do this, we have created a completely new policy-planning mechanism, a system which will allow the college to set annual policy guidelines and to establish the budgetary procedure on the basis of these, to assign well-defined objectives to the directorates-general and to keep the relationship between available resources and activities assigned under control, which is of fundamental importance.     3720.5301804486257
in this context, activity-based management is the key to ensuring coherence between objectives, actions and resources.     3144.3268502146034
the second point is human resources.     1521.6163990531397
it is the central part of our reform.     2300.941578420753
our staff are the commission' s best asset, its richest resource.     43410.19000818219
they are extremely highly qualified, of a calibre which it is difficult, if not impossible, to find in the individual national administrations because of the nature of the recruitment methods and criteria used.     1800.4475977290879
this was the impression i received when i first became president of the commission, and it has been confirmed by observation during my daily work.     3535.584111013909
however, at the same time, i have found that morale amongst the commission' s staff is very low.     8231.204875821824
previously, there was pride and pleasure taken in being part of a universally admired institution, but now staff are frequently demoralised by the unceasing criticism.     5718.784677470778
all this has to change, and this is one of the main reasons for our reform.     1246.4770012153967
recruitment, career development, mobility and disciplinary measures are all being based on completely new criteria: there will be greater emphasis on individual service, credible and, above all, verifiable performance appraisal systems, lifelong learning and a greater degree of management responsibility.     7385.437159678155
these are the areas in which changes will be made.     1124.2911917392196
the third point, which was also rather a sensitive, key issue in the recent crisis, is financial management.     1439.6992177359655
this is the hub of the administration which has to ensure efficiency and security.     1297.5751583022552
we are going to create a management and control system in every directorate-general.     2610.074077767059
at the moment, our control system is completely centralised; with the reform we will switch to a control system which combines decentralisation of management control with an efficient central audit system.     4471.11509156424
this means that we will have a central financial service to provide financial management guidance and advice, decentralisation in every directorate-general of the entire chain, that is of programmes, undertakings, contracts, expenditure and the related ex ante control systems, and an external auditing service which will direct these operations.     2938.0154370922355
this is a complete revolution of the commission' s way of working; it is a totally different method of organisation and it will require a totally different way of thinking.     5010.10236597539
we are familiar with the problems associated with this transitional stage, and it is this transition which has caused the greatest concern among our staff: we will therefore adopt the necessary security measures and put the necessary safeguards in place.     4248.950479775855
in fact, the commission desires this reform in order to become a model of excellence for the whole of europe and the world.     2861.6017118384257
we also want to be exemplary in our advanced use of information technology.     5453.206292181767
we want to be a model of a completely computerised administration.     6550.471632216604
the commission has been accused of being buried under piles of paper, and so we are going to become a paperless commission.     2080.045018907791
this will make the commission itself more efficient and encourage the use of new technologies outside the commission.     2381.304714310634
this is not just a technical revolution, but a complete organisational revolution as well.     3234.1512032372557
i shall leave to neil kinnock the task of explaining the details of the reform, but i would first like to thank him for the intelligence, enthusiasm and energy he has brought to this exercise.     4340.783871540291
we would both like to thank all those who have worked on the project and all the commission staff, who took part in the broadest debate ever organised at the commission.     2448.3142087500714
i would stress that this style of debate is something very new and, in my opinion, it has forced us to radically change our way of thinking as well.     4034.947626488487
the white paper before us is also the product of contributions from the commission staff: they knew that the commission would have to undergo substantial reform in order to become, once again, that internationally-admired institution which was at the heart of european history.     7566.0138885950855
the adoption of the white paper marks the end of a chapter of history - and i hope that it will remain closed forever.     10515.77743722161
now we can focus on the future, on the challenges awaiting the union and on the programme for 2000-2005, the contents of which we have outlined to you over the past few weeks.     3468.5969717242174
the very political essence of the reform is mirrored in the objectives of this five-year plan.     4875.834945517114
we need - the whole union needs - a strong commission, a commission which has regained its ability to act as a political entity and is recognisable as such in all its actions.     6719.487588974636
this is why we are redefining all our work priorities.     4694.279355587059
this is why we are offloading all the activities which it is no longer appropriate for us to carry out - another important area of reform - in accordance with a principle of decentralisation and subsidiarity.     4256.297297379539
for this reason, we are reforming the administrative machinery in order to create the necessary conditions for us to be able to carry out our work efficiently.     8366.93188947733
we will make the best use of the resources at the commission' s disposal, but they are very limited and we have almost exhausted them.     7340.306721374462
we may need to come back to you and ask for additional resources to fund the new tasks which have been assigned to us and those which will be assigned to us in the future.     1483.3081124293
in september, we undertook to present a white paper on reform, and today we are fulfilling that undertaking according to the conditions established in your presence.     7489.148036283114
the commission wishes to be judged on results and results alone - on what we achieve.     8197.487888205087
i would, however, remind you, and this is my final point, that the commission is not the only community institution.     1363.96058673143
parliament and the council are also called upon to take up all the challenges presented by these reforms.     2020.568081954702
the commission has resolutely forged a path, and i hope that all the institutions will follow it right to the very end, in the service of the citizens of europe, to achieve an ever-stronger and ever more transparent europe.     2086.547812676513
i am glad to be here together with president prodi and my colleague, michele schreyer, to present the white paper adopted by the commission this morning: "reforming the commission" .     5126.424632011125
as honourable members will see from the document, which will be available to them today, the unprecedented consultations with the staff of the commission, with this parliament and with the council, which have taken place since the adoption of the consultation document in january, have been very productive and resulted in useful refinements.     1785.0219651280827
together with romano prodi, i express my gratitude to this house and to the very large number of people who have put time and effort into giving us their constructive responses.     6705.888041284519
the white paper puts reform in its proper political context.     21952.622610086084
in the commission, systems and structures have built up over forty years in which successive enlargements have occurred.     5231.891901125342
the treaties have been revised, new managerial tasks have been attributed by this parliament and by the council to the commission, and many other changes of great significance have occurred in our union, in our continent and in the world.     3054.300204510689
those realities, together with the approach of further enlargements, all counsel strongly for modernisation of the commission, so that this essential institution can fulfil its primary tasks of policy conception and development, of treaty application and of management of the public resources and do so with maximum effectiveness, maximum responsibility and maximum accountability.     4388.577729386485
the sustained independence and strength of the commission are, of course, basic to that mission as this house has repeatedly recognised.     4085.867389587855
reform and renewal are essential for that purpose too.     3232.7920270893596
the commission is not and will not be a secretariat of any kind, and that is clear to all of the institutions - it is well understood everywhere.     1768.3552201332316
but the commission does exist to serve the union and its peoples with high standards of performance.     3275.93901337912
that is what the public has the right to expect and it is what the people who work for the commission want to provide and will provide.     1146.5381796907782
the house will be aware, of course, that the commission cannot fulfil these objectives alone.     3490.0957908262085
all institutions in the european union will have to face up to the issues posed by the reform and face up to them honestly and responsibly.     3024.1594566318336
i know that my colleagues and i can count on the support of many members of this house across the spectrum.     6958.057055683751
one of the biggest challenges that may come from the essential efforts to match the tasks of the commission with the resources of the commission will confront this house.     5406.477432247512
last month, as romano prodi has just said, we launched a thorough evaluation of activities and resources throughout the commission and we will fully report on that in september.     3606.24946128113
our aim is to focus the commission on essential policy priorities and on core activities in line with our strategic policy objectives for the next five years.     3566.276691647733
inevitably, that will mean identifying activities that could and should be reduced or ended.     2755.3140179296925
it means taking a very hard look at how the use of internal and external resources can be most effectively balanced in the interest of the union.     2230.2986919904183
it means allocating staff to areas of high-policy importance within departments and across the commission.     5781.638182447203
and when that assessment is complete, we will be able to establish whether the resources at our disposal are commensurate with the tasks attributed to us.     11739.9043800983
if they are proven not to be, we will put the case for more resources to the budgetary authority.     1764.1840005000192
if that does not meet with a positive response, the difficult issue of which of our lower-priority activities should be discontinued will then have to be addressed, not just by the commission, but by this parliament and by the council too.     2007.204882724731
i do not make this point because of any desire in the prodi commission to retreat from activities or responsibilities.     2180.450587143882
on the contrary, as many in this house understand, i am setting out these considerations because we want to meet our responsibilities and meet them with full effectiveness and full accountability.     6471.247758684733
that means closing any significant gap between obligations and operational resources either by reducing activities or by increasing means or by a combination of both.     7468.582880140394
in each of the three pillars of reform, the active support and cooperation of this house will be absolutely vital.     3522.8159797011554
the new activity-based management system, that the president just mentioned, will link political priorities to the availability of resources and consequently to the budget for the first time.     1670.0096503926654
the role of the budgetary authority in the operation of abm will, therefore, be vital.     2272.4407944846844
attempts to micro-manage the commission' s operations are clearly in no one' s interest.     82881.57169754748
a resilient and practical interinstitutional agreement will, therefore, be a prerequisite for the efficient operation of activity-based management.     2536.270306991667
in human resources policy, the involvement of parliament will unavoidably have to be as an employer of civil servants as well as a democratic institution to which the commission is accountable.     5001.555340417915
again, that will be a very demanding challenge.     1522.7601863655248
and finally, in the crucial area of financial management where the court of auditors, the committee of independent experts and other external and internal analyses have strongly advised radical change, the support of this house in implementing the recommendations for full security of money and value for money will be of prime importance.     3502.939524275015
in the limited time available for this statement, it is obviously impossible to go into the full details of our proposals.     2771.3214393130256
i have no doubt that we will do that on several occasions in the coming months and years, and i certainly look forward to the questions of honourable members this afternoon.     3064.6505248612843
it is clear, of course, that in many ways the really hard work starts now, with the implementation of our proposals.     3251.4157028660397
i look forward to working with this parliament, with the council and with the staff of the institutions to ensure that the reform strategy is put fully into effect for the benefit of the union and for the advantage of all of its peoples.     2253.4379630871836
thank you, commissioner kinnock.     3174.1278313360212
we shall now move on to question time.     3423.1558034399945
madam president, the commission proposals include the waiver of immunity of officials.     16698.551665663803
recently, the immunity of a former commissioner was lifted and proceedings are currently pending before a judge in brussels on the basis that brussels is the seat of the community institutions.     5581.599553216292
yet these institutions have other seats and, given that the community' s actions affect the whole community territory, there are in fact thousands of courts which could judge officials, commissioners and so on.     6336.817080363626
is the commission aware that, in order to lift immunity, it would be necessary to adopt a new legal instrument to regulate the procedure for holding commission officials accountable, including former and current commissioners?     6503.635785120317
there is effective legal precaution.     10062.505852221018
in the particular case that the honourable member has in mind, not only did the commission easily and quickly come to a decision to waive the immunity of a former commissioner, but the former commissioner herself advised the commission of her desire that the immunity should be lifted.     5152.519024418973
i can see the complexities that concern the honourable member, but i say to him that in the last five years there have been ten cases in which there have been requests from national legal authorities for the immunity of 26 officials to be lifted, and every single one of those requests has been met, and met with alacrity.     1995.2007623919396
i think that the house can therefore be confident that not only are the civil rights of individuals who may be accused or required to produce evidence safeguarded, but so is the interest of the properly constituted legal bodies of every member state in seeking to get information, or the right to interview or investigate anybody associated with the commission.     5274.355413496983
as the parliament' s rapporteur on the staff elements of your report, mr kinnock, i very much welcome the fact that we are now starting the process, and if i may say to all colleagues here, we have a very important task to do, which will be absolutely central to the future of the commission and its effectiveness.     2949.6359108472207
and i make the point, before i move to my questions, that what happens concerning the staff regulations in the commission has a fundamental role on our responsibility also as employers of staff because, of course, the european parliament staff operate under the same conditions.     2730.7918332843055
that will form part of the work that we will have to do and i sure that all of you today will want to engage in that process.     1739.2708105707359
now there were two points i wanted to pick up with mr kinnock, if i may.     11023.169772155963
the first one concerns the timescale of these important reforms.     7178.116555115924
mr prodi said in his very important opening statement that he had identified severe problems in the commission about low morale and low motivation, and clearly that is a major concern.     4202.777733024184
big expectations are built up for these reforms both within the commission and outside.     5250.287639522834
and if we are to move forward, i suggest to you that the reforms on the personnel side, early evidence of change of culture, empowerment and motivation of staff have to happen sooner rather than later.     3874.0546928362546
and if we look at the original proposals - and i suspect that we will see the same in the new ones - it was not until 2002 that we were going to complete overall reform of the staff regulations.     2210.896915790094
indeed, it was not until 2001 that some of the key elements of appraisal were going to be introduced.     2618.1889614625056
i suggest that is too long, and we really must move forward much more quickly than that.     1472.704109096811
the second point that i want to make concerns information technology.     3957.059219012489
have you really taken into account the changes in staff structure that new information technology will bring about, because those will be fundamental in the new direction of these reforms.     2450.9075781736387
on the second part of mr harbour' s question, he will see in the course of this year how with greater precision we will be showing how the application more extensively of information technology will facilitate change, not only in terms of the working practices and conditions of the staff but in the operation of the commission, as the president briefly outlined in his statement.     2787.450259342805
could i thank mr harbour for the energetic commitment that he has shown to the issue on which he is rapporteur and respond to him by saying that we firmly believe that the morale, the motivation and the professional commitment of commission officials, which is already very strong, will be enhanced by the introduction of comprehensive training, the allocation of responsibility, the upgrading of management and a number of other changes which we will be introducing.     4659.929075928821
cultures are products of structures and systems, and the structures and systems have become old.     2712.3740982447353
by renovating those structures and systems, in some cases very radically, we will assist the people of the commission who are strongly committed to the european ideal and have high quality of capability to release talents and manifest themselves as never before.     3044.0474683822704
i would like to make one last point in answer to mr harbour.     4311.769385819488
i share his impatience to secure the full implementation of the mechanisms of reform and change practices.     3371.0832933226734
i wish i could honestly say to him that we will be compiling and providing in the form of proposals communications before 2001 and certainly be putting them into effect before 2002, but he will be aware there are legally required consultation procedures and periods which we must respect.     2946.874579206967

can i thank mr cox for underlining the virtue of following absolutely the recommendation of the committee of independent experts and introducing a system which will end definitely with the revision of the financial regulation and the end of centralised ex ante control.     5609.643808151887
i would emphasise, too, that the intention is not only to liberate the personnel of the commission - that is certainly an objective - but it is simultaneously to "responsibilise" - it is an appalling word but we all know what it means.     2284.2261691402614
in undertaking such a development in our organisation i am certain that we can match or even advance upon the success and increase in job motivation which has come as a consequence of similar developments in several administrations, both in the public sector and in the private sector, in the last decade or so.     3413.59003614947
i think that the end of the centralised system will certainly not result in any relaxation of effective financial control but will increase its effectiveness and its relationship to operational tasks.     3030.9519130267763
financial control will be absolutely secured, both because of the way in which we will introduce the changes, and because we will introduce a completely professional, independent internal audit service.     2183.250507445864
as i said earlier, our ambition is total security for money and value for money.     2546.7633514476047
we are also interested in value for the people who work for us.     1823.8016596120772
the changes in financial management and control will greatly enhance the possibility of doing all of that.     1927.2749098975771
i am also grateful, at the risk of being repetitive, to mrs theato for the consistent attention that she has given to these matters and indeed the way in which, if she will allow me to say so, she played midwife to many of the changes which we are now contemplating.     3047.0145642216858
i hope that she will come to take pride in the child for which she has at least part parentage.     2560.5411589402147
first of all, i am not sure if i got the interpretation properly, but i do not think it would be possible for someone as expert as mrs theato to talk about the abolition of independent financial control.     7425.068796865296
there is a degree of independence obviously allocated to the current financial controller by the financial regulation.     5061.346347088504
however, because of the mixture of roles of both financial management and control, and audit under the present terms, it is very difficult to think of this as an uncompromised role, no matter how great the integrity of the person actually holding it.     2682.351598062255
so, the changes that we seek to make - as i say consistent with the very strong and clear recommendations of the committee of independent experts - are not negative in any way at all.     1814.3321208017555
coherence, consistency and universally good standards in a decentralised system of financial control are guaranteed by a series of measures.     7576.8681274374285
one is to ensure high quality training, another is to ensure that no directorate-general can take over financial control responsibilities unless and until the auditor service is absolutely certain of the robustness of the system, another is the establishment of a central financial service, as honourable members will see from the white paper, to ensure that those standards are set and supervised, and there is also the additional belt and braces and boilersuit approach of having an effective and independent internal audit service.     2170.4862562150242
i will not go into all the details in the course of a parliamentary answer of this kind but i am certain that on examination mrs theato will be able to see that in the details of the white paper, there are very strong assurances.     6386.33156115435
there will also be evidence of the fact that we have taken heed of the representations made in this house particularly in the committee on budgetary control, and that is significant in respect of the use of ex ante visas in directorates-general.     4360.123317896793
if that is felt in a directorate-general to be a relevant and effective way of enhancing financial control, the ex ante system can be used.     1965.9398688186502
naturally, there are diverse requirements across the commission depending on the number and nature of the financial transactions in which particular directorates-general have to engage.     6211.04743037668
account is taken of that too in the white paper but where there is a desire and a demonstrated need for the use of such a system, it most certainly can be used.     2438.733466804915
lastly on transition, i share the reservations of mrs theato over so-called "soft law" .     42118.08739810172
we have had this discussion before and she knows i make a distinction between various forms of codes which can have a very positive effect and eventually be turned into what i suppose we could call "hard law" .     4500.561921358664

there are absolute assurances of that and provisions made for it in the white paper.     5356.427960695578
in addition, however, and very importantly, because it is a transition period, there will be training, there will be advice, there will be support, there will be assurances.     3649.536592715826
the new head of the audit unit will be in place by 1 may and with that and an accumulation of other assurances i think that we can be as certain as human beings can be that there will be a totally secure system even during the period of transition.     3373.133929219836

irresponsibility because the feeling exists in some quarters that there will not be sufficient control and discipline.     7074.57773015674

in so far as it is at all possible with all of the advice at our disposal and all of the design capability that we have, i am certain that we have installed, or that we will install on the basis of our proposals, a system that will provide the union, its taxpayers and this house with complete security.     2292.5890998804107

statement by the high representative for common foreign and security policy

certain important steps have been taken to secure progress in the summit between europe and africa in which i was pleased to participate in cooperation with the portuguese presidency.     3620.313665589145
a vital meeting has also taken place in portugal, in vilamoura, with the eighteen countries of latin america to try and develop a deeper relationship between the european union and latin america.     6432.19959672385

however, i realise that you asked me here to talk to you more specifically about foreign policy and particularly security policy in europe since helsinki.     2022.3548550510832
i will speak of this briefly.     3465.319035802251
if you wish to ask questions at the appropriate time, i will be pleased to answer them.     3580.201371616712
the helsinki summit under the finnish presidency took place a little over two months ago.     4528.647344048434
decisions were taken at this summit which are fundamental for foreign and security policy in our continent.     5652.40258575185
these were essentially of three types which i will describe.     10982.420532609967
the first was the decision to establish within the european union three committees for decision making relating to crisis management.     2464.4456778107533
the first is a political and security committee, the second is a military committee representing the chiefs of staff of the fifteen member states and the third committee is to make decisions on the european union' s relations with third countries and other organisations.     4842.504491353437
this basically involves relations between the european union and the atlantic alliance and relations between the european union and those countries which, although geographically in europe, are not part of the european union, or at least not at the moment.     2003.508588748562
i will restrict myself to these three points in order to bring you up to date with events.     5690.3756868712435
firstly, with regard to the decision-making bodies, the political and security committee met for the first time this morning, 1 march 2000. this committee was established by the last general affairs council.     1975.9089847250348
therefore, two months after the helsinki declaration and decision, a political and security committee meeting has taken place for the first time within the european union.     1796.180714165533
i was able to attend this meeting this morning and met with the representatives at lunch.     32994.39260939713
you can be satisfied with the progress made.     5780.00524892623
the fifteen countries have appointed diplomatic experts at ambassador level in order to tackle one of the most important issues which we will face in the coming months.     7324.793786788309
secondly, the military committee, which represents the various chiefs of staff of the fifteen countries, will hold its first meeting on 7 march which will be just over two months after the helsinki declaration.     5140.448654165606
this military committee, as you can imagine, will have various duties.     4182.633698891942
in the interim stage, its basic duty will be to advise the council and myself and to take initial decisions on how to set up the final bodies which we will, of course, try to have in place by the end of this year or the beginning of next.     3562.59217467657
thirdly, the military staff will represent senior military personnel from the fifteen countries.     11935.014772278264
together with myself, this staff will form the civil and military secretariat whose task will be to prepare, with the assistance of the military committee, any operation needing to be organised in due course in the context of crisis management within the european union.     5078.536510961284
this crisis management will not be a strictly military affair but the military representatives and the military committee will be responsible for advising on the logistics of any humanitarian operations which may require the involvement of military forces.     5452.342254928421
mozambique is surely an excellent current example of how intervention may be required in terms of military logistics, not for military purposes, but with strictly humanitarian ends.     11674.538299982887
we are therefore working diligently in this respect.     7104.2832060001965
madam president, if, a few months ago when i had the pleasure of appearing before you for the first time, i had told you that i would be able to announce on 1 march 2000 that the first meeting of the political committee had been held today, that the military committee would be up and running within a week after that and that tomorrow the first members of the military staff would start to arrive at the building which we are to use, many of you would surely have thought that the high representative was going mad.     2533.4220206106784
we are not mad.     2340.801276998368
what we have done is to work long and hard, with determination and, without a shadow of a doubt, with the support not only of yourselves but also of the heads of state and government of the countries which you represent.     3039.28262344442
few initiatives within the european union can have been progressed with such determination, drive and commitment as has been achieved in crisis management since helsinki.     3327.5256350360814
in less than two months we have established the mechanics for cooperation with the commission.     1405.1981549724042
i must thank commissioner patten who is here today for his support and the tremendous energy that he has also invested in trying to ensure that all these issues could be settled in such a short time.     1730.446857237575
i will now move on to the second issue which i should like to discuss with you.     3778.374885223693
we have already looked at the bodies which we have set up but we also have a fundamental obligation in terms of our credibility.     3239.6051756643287
europe must be able to provide the military capacity to act in times of crisis.     8795.133308939376
i must repeat that the european union will not be using this military capacity to make war but to bring peace.     3123.8599037945337
it will participate in crisis management in order to help where there is war or situations of conflict, to re-establish calm and to take the heat out of these situations and also to avoid them.     4576.633135248306
as you may recall, it was in this respect that the helsinki summit intelligently agreed to form a force of 60 000 soldiers from all the member states.     2990.486814665209
it will be possible for this force to be deployed at any given moment within 60 days, or two months, to prevent a crisis and for it to remain in action to prevent conflict for a period of one year.     2510.7026555867005
this is a major operation and an important objective but i can say that we are in a position to meet this challenge which the heads of state and government posed in helsinki.     3464.2166769960986
on monday, just two days ago, in sintra, a beautiful city very close to lisbon, the defence ministers of the fifteen member states met and took some important decisions towards implementing this headline goal defined in helsinki.     12749.795165654668
the proposed timetable is tight but essential and will require great determination from all of us, including this house.     5165.4590760183255
i will set this out in five points.     2729.066160051844
firstly, before the next general affairs council which will take place this month, we shall be able to define precisely the detailed and specific meaning of the headline goal, bearing in mind the views that the military planners may consider appropriate.     7060.367128204845
to say that we will deploy 60 000 soldiers in a peacekeeping operation is not the same thing as organising an operation to achieve peace.     5208.863379240293
we must therefore have considered all possible scenarios so that we can do this job and do it well.     1437.40503161505
before 24 march we will therefore have established this timetable so that the general affairs council can, on this date, do its job and do it well.     2268.6192128400817
secondly, we must look at the capacity which each country has so that this total force of 60 000 soldiers is distributed in an orderly and efficient manner between the fifteen countries.     4527.982524436361
thirdly, we must look at the differences between the headline goal and what each country can provide.     9389.467958558707
we may not have enough between the fifteen countries which will have to make an additional effort to meet the proposed needs.     3283.8400259073505
how can we do this?     991.3897781156385
this should be decided by a conference of the fifteen countries to look at the gap between what we want and what we have and to see how to distribute what we need among the fifteen member states.     4820.614359852224
madam president, the timetable must ensure that, by december of this year during the french presidency, the process can be completed with a major conference of forces from all the countries to ensure that the needs and capacities match.     3783.1164148527187
we will then be able to say truthfully that we have a plan of what we must do between the end of the year 2000 and the year 2003 which is the date we have set for having this capacity available.     1720.9226949820327
this is what we are doing and we are committed to this.     1463.7332658093644
we have worked hard in recent months and weeks and will continue to do so for the rest of this year during the portuguese presidency, which is clearly carrying out excellent work, and during the french presidency which will undoubtedly continue working with the same speed and skill as it has demonstrated on other occasions.     3035.946845662217
i would like to say that in the meantime there will be at least four european councils, those in lisbon and feira and two during the french presidency.     2192.75962346769
on these four occasions, the heads of government will also be ready to continue examining these issues so that we do not lose the momentum and so that, by the year 2003, we are able to deploy what we have agreed, if necessary.     5466.953740479996
madam president, we are therefore working on this issue with determination, energy and the full support of all the governments and all the heads of government.     3023.949026305417
i have two further points to make on this process.     2044.0922543759145
the european union must carry out this whole operation in collaboration with the third countries to which i have referred which, although geographically in europe, are not members of the european union.     4411.629845215638
we have to work with these countries and involve them so that if such a crisis arises, which i hope will not be the case, all forces and all countries will be more than welcome.     1411.6943818601628
secondly, we have to be able to establish a smooth and transparent relationship with nato.     9505.801715670523
the operations to be carried out in the future will be of three types.     2886.4818990303575
the first type will be operations in which european forces are involved within the framework of nato.     2209.7456029780783
the second will be operations involving european forces and directed by them, but using the means and infrastructure of nato.     2326.4784450832517
the third will be operations in which the european forces act alone.     1231.0008164028734
in the first and second types we will need to cooperate with nato and must therefore establish a transparent, positive and confident relationship between ourselves and nato, as appropriate, which will be good for both the alliance and ourselves.     3416.859855966294
madam president, i have talked about the military capacity which we are ready to provide, the timetable which we want to apply and what has been achieved in recent months.     4269.994477819817
however, we must not forget that these crisis operations must be tackled from a civilian front as well.     5632.800644959525

there are times when a crisis which started out with a military need develops so that, after a few months, a police force is needed rather than a military force.     2124.9938969377818
then, in the final moments of the crisis, it is perhaps courts or bodies to create democratic institutions which a country may need more than police.     3774.946228266843

women in the decision-making process
the next item is the declarations by the council and the commission on women in the decision-making process.     9147.854106784444
mr president, two centuries ago, during the french revolution, a very famous woman, olympe de gouges, was executed and proclaimed mad because she had supported equal rights for men and women.     2457.4083433906862
two centuries later, we have achieved a great deal in europe.     4440.502244168301
the last century was marked by three periods.     4924.669688604075
the first period was a period of institutional change, change in the legislative framework and huge constitutional victories in all the member states as a result of the fight by the women' s movement, as a result of major decisions by progressive parties and as the result of action by enlightened men and women.     3709.207517206766
this was followed by a second period, in which the worth of female models was recognised.     4238.930801097575
there were women everywhere.     4353.275907196086
prime ministers, members of parliament, ministers, in the professional arena, doctors, lawyers, pilots, generals.     5755.019671051892
we are now in a third period: in this third period, the composition of the bodies which wield political and economic power needs to be changed.     3033.7709439241153
the composition, or rather the change in composition, will also bring about changes in policies - policies which need to take account of the merits and experience of both sexes.     6047.824787201069
ladies and gentlemen, the first few months of the new millennium have been marked by two important events: the first was the election of the president of finland, mrs halïnen.     5272.149133933126
for the first time in finland, the women candidates outnumbered the men.     1204.303636615367
and a woman was elected.     3008.8639129177814
the second event took place in france, which passed a law on the equal representation of men and women as a result of the declaration made in april 1999 at the 'women and men in power' conference, at which the ministers responsible undertook to implement specific measures and policies to change the power ratio.     4778.256711753791
we must congratulate prime minister jospin for ratifying this commitment in the clearest and most categorical manner.     11492.932034061492
a great deal has been said and numerous policies have been implemented in the member states.     2430.08829748496
but the figures tell the true story.     6127.254002429274
and the figures still give europe cause for concern.     2944.953650467419
24% of members of government are women and there are an average of 22% in parliament, although this fluctuates from 6% in some member states to as many as 40% in others.     1730.1764909997257
the number of women on committees who wield power and frame policies is very small.     7582.974444298516
even in countries such as germany and belgium, which have specific legal commitments on the representation of the two sexes, the involvement of women on various committees is no more than 18%.     3502.735158535906
what we need, therefore, is to promote specific policies.     1945.1364648771432
we need a policy mix which includes long-term political commitments, as in sweden and finland; they now have governments in which women account for 56% and 44% of members respectively.     2827.955172303006
we need organisation and statistical monitoring.     6014.672618548359
we need controls and suitable structures which reflect the culture, mentality and specific problems of each area.     3330.2697197655625
and, of course, these policies need a budget, they need to be given financial teeth.     3625.2050693934184
increasing the number of women or, as the french prime minister put it, increasing the influence of women in the decision-making centres, is the best way of renewing the policy, of reshaping the approach to the question, of injecting political thought with new ideas and new experience: the new ideas and experience which women can inject by reason of the different way in which they live and grow up.     2645.617350431086
the recent initiative by the finnish presidency to develop indicators recording and reflecting the representation of women in the decision-making centres is extremely important.     8195.962207422366
these indicators include national parliaments, regional councils and town councils and give a complete picture of the degree of representation of women in every member state.     8678.57441690341
i think that creating and supporting statistics is a very important political issue and that the facility to set targets, compare performances and bolster our efforts is an important political tool.     3642.9642228561793
the programme which i shall present for the fifth women' s programme includes a similar initiative for systematic monitoring.     12338.392104112228
although legislative measures and institutional intervention by member states have been effective as far as the decision-making centres of the parties in power is concerned, the same cannot be said for the private sector.     1940.9509021478937
my contacts with large european and multinational companies in europe have brought tragic results to light.     4120.286443116336
there are companies at european level which employ thousands of workers, sometimes hundreds of thousands of workers throughout the world and yet fewer than 2% of the directors and managers are women.     4970.951926844668
nor must we underestimate the representation of women in economic decision-making centres.     6613.010131696972
here too, specific efforts will be made of course; naturally they cannot be of a legislative nature, but they can operate on the basis of an exchange of best practices; in all cases, my initial contact with the chairmen and directors of these large companies has had positive results.     3667.5547709858147
as you know, europe is endeavouring to solve the problem from within.     8985.925046602526
a decision has already been taken on quotas of 40% for each sex on all teams and on all research and technological development programmes.     2135.4574483070683
it was approved by the house in may 1999, when the need for women in research and technology was stressed.     2620.4999145782876
we in the commission are obliged to set a specific and clear example and all the commissioners are working together, as can be seen from the reform of the commission, to achieve 40% representation of women on all the committees of the european commission.     2736.3270334326967
ladies and gentlemen, there are often objections, crypto-progressive objections, as to whether there should be a binding undertaking on the representation of women at the centres of power.     12301.286572321253
no one ever asks in a federal state if all the members of the federation should have their say.     2271.4058102928375
and at the level of the european union, the question of whether the 15 member states need to be represented on every committee will never be asked.     2201.9930351339194
why then is it curious and why do we have reservations when it comes to a legislative commitment on the representation of half the population.     4560.6224341705465
i should like to be absolutely categorical.     10709.53411009937
there should be no reservation, no reservation whatsoever about a legislative commitment on the representation of women at the centres of power.     6993.944788352954
(applause)
women make up half the population, half the strength of the planet.     10608.013267023132
excluding women from the centres of power means, first, democratic failure and, secondly, under-exploitation of human resources.     6466.307930712814
we face a unique challenge and the time is ripe for combining economic and political expediency with respect for basic democratic principles.     8649.238964147604
i am optimistic that we shall succeed.     20998.001528604524
the bet before us is a bet with time.     15304.67656625169
future generations of women must not be sidelined.     9678.402156486822
if need be, we must use all possible means to force the situation.     1347.8822387974196
(applause)
mr president, firstly i must thank mrs belém roseira for taking part in this debate and for her interesting speech on the subject.     2924.748231481075
i must also thank the commissioner for being here and for her words.     1418.543003520829
the various political groups generally agree on the easily verifiable observation that women are under-represented in the decision-making process, in both the political and the economic and social spheres.     2201.710130834168
nobody nowadays disputes that women should participate in political life, in the parties and in the institutions but it is not easy to agree on how this participation should be achieved.     4044.1579547006445
the equality between men and women which most affects the lives of women and therefore society in general is equality in work.     3450.411312820146
this involves both equality in accessing work, which implies equality in training, and equal pay for the same work, so often defended and yet far from being achieved.     2562.5306846531093
we are convinced that the participation of women in the decision-making process will be beneficial to society as a whole.     3628.172921213628
women always have a different viewpoint from men.     4046.923899648826
this serves to enrich and complete the picture of the best solutions to the problems in daily life which people like us, who are dedicated to active politics, undertake to solve.     5572.322465219593
the difficulty lies in finding the right solution to allow women to have a greater presence in positions of responsibility and therefore in decision making.     3524.043203869835
in some countries of the european union a compulsory quota formula has been adopted which immediately produces a more equal presence of women.     2424.7341995756783
however, this is not always the best solution as there are not always enough women ready to enter a difficult and competitive world which they regard as essentially masculine because it has traditionally been reserved for men.     3115.147841556036
women are not participating in political parties and in the working and social world at a level of fifty per cent.     3447.9733929155595
the real percentage is much lower and it is this participation at the grassroots which we must promote in order to ensure a greater presence of women.     5922.463166493439
there must also be a clear and real will to allow more women, who are ready and able, to assume responsibilities and commitments in political life and therefore in the decision-making process.     2452.316858063815
there is no clear agreement within the ppe-de group in support of the compulsory quota system.     2652.8961591218913
some members have even come out against this.     3307.3980205059834
personally, i would not want to feel like a quota, to think that i was in a particular job because i was a woman.     2330.704862104234
this is a feeling which men will never have and it is an important difference.     1749.7719756525032
mr president, i should also like to thank the president-in-office of the council and the commissioner for good speeches on their part which i think provide hope for real optimism, especially given the will and commitment radiated by both women.     2881.719742825653
i think it was an excellent example the commissioner gave when she said that no one would have dreamed of having a situation in which the fifteen countries of the european union were not equally represented but that it was a different matter when it came to equal representation for women.     2624.013112782603
it could hardly have been made more abundantly clear, what we are concerned with here.     1673.0867388310426
i do hope that the commissioner will have the necessary perseverance to also produce the necessary reforms within the commission - but then, of course, she is young and full of energy.     1963.6884955807773
i am convinced that it will be hard work, when all is said and done.     1906.6958197469073
if the commissioner asks for support from parliament, she can in any case count upon the support of the group of the european liberal, democrat and reform party.     5388.759925943702
we have a very strong interest in seeing equality introduced to the commission and in seeing the necessary basis for decision making secured so that the commissioner can implement her views on equality.     2652.309236265455
i would say to the portuguese minister that i completely agree with her that what is at issue here is the fact that you cannot obtain equal rights without equal power.     2857.0853325089574
that is absolutely crucial.     2128.421154291087
it should also be remembered, however, that no one voluntarily relinquishes power, and that applies to both men and women.     1607.0890305473652
men have never wished to surrender positions of power when they had them, but there are also very few women who have been willing to give up their positions of power, and i am thinking here, in particular, of positions of power in relation to children.     5445.317191209137
moreover, i would also say that this may also be one of the reasons why there are so few men here today.     1047.586746560622
inevitably, we should like, for the same reasons of power politics, to be allowed to have this debate more or less to ourselves.     3177.444011019441
what i want to emphasise by saying this is that it is a matter of both sides' surrendering power.     3648.8537473207302
the commissioner emphasised that what is needed is a very broadly-based political mix, and she specifically mentioned finland and sweden.     1575.0184619028762
just for the record, i would say that she might well also have mentioned denmark.     1500.480050435089
and i say that, of course, because i come from denmark.     2195.8066741276466
in fact, 45% of the danish government is made up of women, and that is, in reality, a touch more than in finland.     1791.5501656112672
we still do not have a female president but, on the other hand, we have a queen, and that must no doubt, in certain contexts, amount to the same thing.     1587.3974591598371
i should like to broach some of the points the commissioner mentioned, notably the question of financial resources.     2211.5604678209993
it is crucial we accept that there is a price to pay if women are to have equality in the short term.     1980.469868636105
in the short term, we also need to invest in making sure that children are properly cared for and, of course, also properly educated.     3596.136990135434
the issue is therefore one of appropriate structures.     1709.7189580260617
one might well wonder if traditional industrial culture has been a particularly good thing, and i am therefore very happy that the portuguese ministers are to participate very actively in the lisbon summit, which is, of course, to be partly concerned with it developments.     2587.5178777378037
i think that this field offers some quite distinctive opportunities for women, specifically because it is characterised by flat, rather than hierarchical, structures.     9273.863834076854
in denmark, we have, in any case, seen developments to suggest that quite a lot of women entrepreneurs are on the way up.     4060.447629288722
there are two points which have not been seriously mentioned and which i should just like to mention here finally.     1312.8555008798637
the first is the issue of the media' s influence.     1254.3809452849305
i think it is very important that we should investigate what kind of stereotypes the media establish.     5531.759653289598
do we have a special problem in this regard?     1954.0754621795168
i have to say that i do not know the precise answer to that question.     1983.8985562200562
i have a suspicion that this is the case, but i am not certain.     2167.557622949845
the second point is the enlargement of the union.     2777.8842165356327
i think it very important that we should emphasise straight away that a number of demands are to be made in connection with enlargement as well, specifically that the new member states should arrange to be represented by both men and women so that in that way they are improving the status of women in the countries concerned.     3339.8666484648993
i would also like to thank the minister and the commissioner for their statements and i agree with the commissioner' s statement; it is time that fine words were translated into action.     6885.673541629181
we all know to what extent women are under-represented in decision making at all levels because we have the statistics and they speak for themselves.     1736.713463129025
i agree that accurate statistics too are essential because it is only when we see the figures changing that we will know we are beginning to overcome the problems.     3585.486732352277
it is systematic monitoring which will reveal this.     4971.702952831196
mainstreaming is a long-term commitment and process.     5065.586874767062
in the meantime, we need positive action and this means the use of quotas and other measures.     1951.5107289233142
we can learn from the successes mentioned, not only by looking at the actual numbers of women that were elected, but the effect that has had on the nature of government to illustrate that everyone benefits from a truly representative system.     3329.5297343607485
i am pleased to say that in wales, which i represent, 40% of our national assembly members are women as the result of positive action by the political parties but, on the other hand, we have only 4 women mps from wales in the house of commons which is only 10% of the total; so statements of principle, of support in principle, are not enough because they have not worked.     2737.193289476996
if we believe in the principle of equal representation of women and men in decision making we need to commit ourselves to the actions that are necessary to make it happen.     3925.179623879828
mr president, at the second international conference of socialist women held in copenhagen in 1910, it was decided that the main demand of the time, that of a woman' s right to vote, should be linked to the women' s question as a whole and that (as also stated in the relevant resolution) women' s day must be accorded an international dimension and be thoroughly prepared for.     4320.0312988833175
it is also worth noting that international women' s day has since been banned in all those countries in which fascism and racism came to power during and between the war years.     4474.100662585781
in 1910, a woman' s right to vote was on the agenda.     6041.441414518877
today, we are focusing upon the ways in which more women might be able to participate in the decision-making process.     4037.550498106454
women are no homogenous collective.     20209.135197593972
women belong to different social classes.     15829.470500468899
women have different individual needs and interests.     2587.2479649935635
irrespective of where we are and notwithstanding our class, race or religion and whether we stay at home or go out to work, it is nonetheless a fact that, in all areas of society, the structures are such that we are thoroughly subordinate to men (albeit with a few individual exceptions, it is perhaps best to add).     4196.851633966664
in my own country, sweden, a number of political parties have, in recent years, voluntarily introduced quota allocation by gender as a feature governing the ways in which political assemblies are constituted and run.     7021.592266722701
this has led to an increase in the number of women involved in political decision making which, in turn, has provided opportunities for further research.     2642.483033230556
what happens when more women take an active and equal part in political decision making?     1949.4709208816635
what has so far emerged is that the agenda has changed - and when the agenda changes, so too of course do the types of decisions made.     3185.3996470068896
when the talk turns to quota allocation by gender, the word "qualifications" also crops up.     8852.644737871564
what is interesting is that the qualifications demanded are rarely defined.     7110.00134866315
it often looks as if men today, as in the past, always have the least defined qualifications.     1848.7559152201131
being a man therefore seems, in itself, to be synonymous with maximum competence.     5862.744599778631
this way of seeing things i consider to be proof of spectacular incompetence on the part of many of today' s decision makers.     17340.621342338694
earlier this year, we requested, here in this chamber, that 8 march be made into an international holiday.     3772.7827749828275
in anticipation of this request being met, i now want to take the opportunity of wishing all socialist women success with the "women' s world marches" which are to begin this spring on international women' s day.     13347.982057604364
this is a world-wide activity which will focus upon violence against women and female poverty throughout the world.     4992.8844424687195
finally, i want to say, as commissioner patten said when he drew the previous debate to a close, that the ideal police officer would be a black grandmother.     4212.783730579571
we do not have any such officers, but black grandmothers are in fact to be found.     1603.0236900310986
mr president, the place of women in politics is an important matter that is worthy of more than our trying to outbid each other in terms of political correctness.     5216.508965503706
undoubtedly there are too few women involved in politics.     2181.8024188989057
it is not absolutely certain that the predominance of men in the party machinery alone is responsible.     1099.899454689611
in france, 95% of elected representatives are town councillors.     2675.3515259984697
the vast majority of them are not members of any party.     3574.685866358531
in the authorities of the french departments, the leading french party is that of independent elected representatives, in right-wing or left-wing categories.     2768.577343482058
in order to be elected as a local councillor, you just have to want to be elected.     2620.905280268727
but access to national responsibilities often depends upon the introduction to politics provided by such local mandates.     4471.594937584563
our group wants to encourage the fair representation of men and women in the institutions of the european union.     4901.34292406483
that is why we reject the policy of quotas and positive discrimination, whether it is in favour of women or supposed social minorities.     5713.023863443022
the policy of minorities, while neglecting the deep-seated unity of the human family, to establish superficial divisions, is a dangerous threat to the cohesion of the social fabric and the equilibrium of our democratic system.     8949.46071092399
on the one hand, if we establish quotas for women, why do we also not create quotas according to age or ethnic group, quotas of catholics, of workers, of those who wear glasses.     4093.0319468217676
the establishment of social quotas in favour of the very smallest categories would be no more ludicrous than establishing quotas of women.     5937.123613238491
with such a philosophy it would no longer be necessary to organise elections or administrative competitions.     3581.3894924189835
it would be sufficient to determine the composition of the ministries and assemblies by means of a statistical calculation.     14416.522849774516
the policy of quotas can only increase the disaffection of the voters with regard to politics by once again giving them the feeling that everything takes place behind their backs.     5130.232957524896
on the other hand, such a policy shows a deep contempt for women.     3977.878831351436
a woman must not attain political or administrative responsibilities automatically thanks to the implementation of the quota but by having her own merits and skills recognised.     4152.049637881525
ladies, have you so little pride that you would be prepared to hold a ministerial office or wear the sash of an elected representative by virtue of the mathematical odds of the quota?     11877.089309671022
women deserve more than a quota.     1792.5995045029915
it is by reviving the principle of meritocracy that we shall improve in quantity and quality the presence of women in the decision-making process in quantity and quality.     3426.162066431082
minister, commissioner, everything you have just said has been very well expressed, even if a few male colleagues, if there are still any in the chamber, perhaps gnashed their teeth.     1612.5148740728664
i know that there are also some women who do not wish to hear of quotas or even of positive action, because they believe that this positive discrimination humiliates them and marks them forever in their careers.     3746.414390110848
but the statistics prove it: the positive action, even the quotas, - if they are not applied blindly, without distinction - have increased the number of women in the decision-making process.     3169.880033567715
that is why we are justified in stating that the use of quotas, as a transitional measure, contributes to balancing out the participation of men and women, particularly in political life.     4007.1042275530103
i should like to take this opportunity to remind the commissioner of some requests and initiatives of this parliament which her predecessor did not follow up and which are closer to our hearts than the quotas.     5298.313978138234
first of all it concerns our report, which was adopted last year on the occasion of international women' s day in strasbourg, which requests the commission to submit to us a new proposal to amend the 1976 directive on the implementation of the principle of the equal treatment of men and women.     5012.347958765408
according to the treaty of amsterdam, there was no longer any reason to maintain the positive action as a tolerated departure from the principle of equal treatment which only recognises an individual right, and not a right that belongs to the society that wishes to achieve this equality democratically.     6537.555558889411
things being what they are, nobody should dare any longer to dispute that the right of equality of men and women is a fundamental right of a democratic society, requiring equal treatment and equal opportunity be achieved by a package of active measures, including positive action.     5707.342257613885
therefore, commissioner, you can go ahead.     1215.0276329214641
you do not need to wait for a new amendment to the treaty.     2017.4616662831597
may i just remind you once again of our request for an amendment to the 1976 directive which has been watered down and misapplied with regard to the equal treatment of self-employed people, including assistant spouses.     3067.4648313603875
no action has been taken on our report which unanimously proposed a european framework status for assistant spouses.     2798.9102585871833
here again, commissioner, the ball is in your court.     4744.732728021161
do not take too long to send it back.     2861.0484386635685
i would like to thank the minister and the commissioner for their speeches and for committing themselves before the european parliament on the crucial matter we are discussing today.     1626.1663112122324
the presence of women in decision-making bodies, in particular, is a question of democracy.     3425.8448116198283
all citizens, both men and women, should be able to see, to feel that they are genuinely represented by those they have elected and those who rule.     3250.1971304951617
if we consider the fact that more than half the population is made up of women but that that there are many more men than women present in these decision-making bodies, it is clear that we are still far from true democracy.     1697.308828925841
however, it is also a question of political agendas.     1846.341245482476
in addition to making up the majority of the population, women are also the most dynamic element in society: it is women who have made the greatest contribution to the creation of new enterprises in europe in recent years; a greater percentage of young women obtain irregular work contracts than men, and in many of our regions, they are the main feature of the innovation, training, performance and flexibility development model.     4431.961910900216
out of a sample of women who work in and around milan, where i live, 90% said that they do not have enough time to do all they need to in a day.     2777.2233251580033
women work an average of 60 hours a week, which is 20 hours longer than men.     3980.4899840075273
the price paid by women for following their desire to be protagonists in all spheres is that they never have enough time.     3903.1564159273908
if this is true, and it is, any appropriate policy which aims to find positive solutions to these problems must start from this point.     2664.7303105092224
laws, programmes and interventions must be implemented which are well-constructed, coordinated and audacious, in order to encourage the formation of new cultural, economic, social, institutional and political ruling classes whose modernity is characterised by the involvement of women.     4173.909609719727
much has been achieved on many issues in europe, but we are still far from resolving the question of equal representation.     3112.23433044167
i agree with the commissioner that it is time for the european political sphere - the entire european political sphere - to represent women and men on an equal footing, and for decisive action to be taken in this matter.     2947.9622811017953

this means that we need to amend policies in various areas if we are to guarantee that women can participate on equal terms in the decision-making process.     2858.689622212656

the truth is that the scant participation of women in centres of political representation and decision making demonstrates a democratic deficit which is incompatible with genuine democracy.     8295.29279748088

madam president-in-office of the council, commissioner, i am a right-wing member of parliament but i have the same battles as my colleagues in supporting and assisting fellow citizens in my country to gain power and access to decision-making posts, which should be feasible not only in the political arena but clearly also in all sectors of society.     3249.566950096992
quite simply, i would say that there are several ways in which to attain positions of power and i am particularly against the system of quotas, because i believe it is especially contemptuous of women and i come from a country where parity has just been achieved.     1975.3695949730557
it is quite logical, it is consistent!     4819.435484475186
it should have existed for a long time because women represent more than half of society and it seems quite clear to me that it is natural for them to represent at least 50%, if not more, of those within decision-making bodies and, inter alia, in the political arena.     2439.3716062843014
so, there should be no positive discrimination to include women.     2929.2190108059444

that simply amounts to acknowledging their inferiority.     431.8797119089242
but i do know that we have never felt inferior to men, particularly in politics.     1777.736821898177
so i believe that we are conducting this battle to assist our fellow countrywomen to achieve decision-making power, to gain responsible posts in the political arena, but i believe that it will only be achieved if we completely change the way people think.     4054.657668176809
we shall all have to be sufficiently strong to force our political leaders to voluntarily impose women' s participation in the political decision-making process.     5558.678705729469
mr president, minister, commissioner, "women are half of heaven" , a successful man once said.     4824.007832165571
but when it comes to important decisions and priorities, there is a lack of women' s voices.     15140.445552056719
women, who constitute more than half of the population, are heavily under-represented within political, economic and social fields.     3679.9047236455026
that is unacceptable in a democracy.     1672.0628578644814
where equality is concerned, the gulf between the actual and the ideal is nowhere more apparent than in the area of decision making.     3091.3655299351753

i propose that the eu' s objectives for women in the area of decision making should be based upon the averaged-out achievements of the three most successful member states within each area, with the member states each submitting a report annually which will then have to be evaluated.     4475.308011534114
women are the foremost victims of human rights abuses such as rape, sexual offences, domestic violence and the trade in women, but they are under-represented on the legal and legislative bodies which pronounce upon these crimes.     4576.870397361792
the latter are consequently accorded low priority, and the perpetrators are often not prosecuted.     5897.530878833716
women are the prime victims of armed conflicts but are under-represented in peace-keeping efforts, conflict resolution and reconstruction work.     6200.603634527111
in the resolution, we therefore urge the member states to increase the proportion of women involved in all aspects of conflict resolution and peace-keeping.     6473.961671743183
the call for women' s participation in decision making on an equal footing with men is not only a demand for social justice and democracy; it is also essential if women' s experience and knowledge are to be utilised to the full.     6957.034286804038
not to use women' s talents in decision making is an unacceptable waste.     16168.060317183808
women should not only be "half of heaven" but should also be entitled to take their share of responsibility for decisions which affect our entire planet.     4661.952651857519
mr president, i would like to congratulate the president-in-office of the council and commissioner diamantopoulou on the excellent speeches they have made today.     2547.1348216097585
we have high hopes for what you will achieve.     2707.019318753931
we are at the start of a millennium and a century which we hope will be decisive for women and for their opportunity to occupy decision-making positions.     2267.020397021652
we want this opportunity to bring substantial changes to our lives in society at large, we want democracy to be synonymous with equality between men and women, and we do not want women to be excluded socially or economically as is still happening today.     4470.202533862447

we know from our own experience that freedom and the ability to decide are not given freely but must be fought for.     4080.756182291367

mr president, commissioner, the reason why i was so pleased with your intervention was that you had the courage to list the member states where the political representation of women is a success, such as, for example, in finland, france and sweden, and equally, you had the courage to list the member states where things in this respect are not going so well, such as, for example, my own country and greece too, which you forgot to mention.     3297.206098231441

i do not believe that any of the meps will be able to prove that i am any less competent as a result.     5036.062302394918
it appears that the meritocracy in a number of our european countries is still so biased in favour of males that women simply do not stand a chance.     4303.5985823028395
it is true that a number of countries in the european union have already introduced quotas, and you yourself made reference to belgium, commissioner, where it has not really taken off.     2867.224675705939
hardly surprising, would you not agree, mrs avilés perea, not because we failed to find the necessary candidates, but because recent scientific research, instigated by the commission, has shown that quotas are, in fact, only useful if women are placed in the appropriate safe seats.     10619.575255468453
this is why we need to be sensible about applying quotas.     5570.949468732774

mr president, i too should like to congratulate the commissioner and the president-in-office of the council for their solid recommendations and, above all, for their proposals.     2596.810647065818
because it has been said that men do not usually get involved in discussions on women' s rights, i thought i might contribute the views of a contemporary politician and intellectual, francis fukuyama, to today' s debate.     14724.17453640183
in an article entitled "if women ruled the world" , he maintains that if women were more involved in politics, the world would certainly not be so wedded to violence.     2965.9403372612883
athena, the goddess of wisdom and war, may have been a female model in ancient greece, but the influence exercised by women today can act as a catalyst to stop militarisation and promote peace.     7042.380349754772
there are, therefore, serious grounds, over and above justice between the sexes, for distributing public offices and political and economic power fairly and evenly between men and women.     2531.071183843385
women devote their entire lives to the endeavour to protect life and the environment through dialogue, reconciliation, an understanding of different views and resolving differences by peaceful means.     13818.067304634063
